title: Spatiotemporal Gap-Filling of GOCI-II Chlorophyll-a Using Attention-Based Partial Convolution Model
authors: Chae-Eun Kim, Bo-Ram Kim, Ki-Yeong Mun, Junhwa Chi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Ocean color remote sensing provides continuous observations of marine environments, with the Geostationary Ocean Color Imager-II (GOCI-II) offering high temporal resolution data for waters surrounding the Korean Peninsula. However, cloud cover and atmospheric interference frequently create data gaps that compromise the continuity and reliability of chlorophyll-a concentration measurements—a critical indicator of marine ecosystem health. This study proposes the Spatiotemporal Attention Partial Convolution model to reconstruct missing chlorophyll-a values in multi-day satellite imagery. The model extends two-dimensional partial convolution to three dimensions, enabling simultaneous exploration of spatial patterns and temporal dynamics. A 3D attention mechanism guides the network to focus on informative spatiotemporal features during reconstruction. To ensure robust training, we developed a comprehensive artificial cloud mask generation strategy using Gaussian Random Fields with parameterized Cloud Density Index and Missing Pixel Ratio, creating diverse missing data scenarios that exceed the variability found in observational data alone. Rigorous evaluation using actual GOCI-II cloud masks from 2024 was conducted to evaluate performance across varying missing data conditions, achieving mean absolute error of 0.036 mg/m³, root mean squared error of 0.068 mg/m³, and R² of 0.865. Long-term spatiotemporal consistency analysis across ten selected regions showed substantial improvements in spatial and temporal data coverage, with valid pixels increasing from 54% to 97%. Qualitative assessments confirmed that reconstructed images preserve natural spatial continuity without artifacts, significantly enhancing the practical utility of GOCI-II data for marine environmental monitoring and research applications requiring high temporal resolution.

title: A relation-geometry enhanced network for stereo matching of optical satellite stereo images
authors: Yang Lei, Wanshou Jiang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The height information of complex urban scenes is essential for urban understanding and 3D reconstruction. Fine-grained stereo matching results provide detailed height relation of urban scenes, which is vital for localization, mapping, and 3D object modeling, yet existing methods face challenges such as limited scope of disparity prediction and low geometry consistency. To address these issues, a relation-geometry enhanced (RGE) network is proposed for stereo matching of optical satellite stereo image. To be specific, a pre-trained monocular depth estimation model named DepthAnything V2 is applied to generate the relative relation of the images as well as to obtain features focused on the depth information. Given the produced depth map, a relation guided geometry fusion (RGGF) module and a relation constraint feature optimization (RCFO) module are introduced to generate the relation-geometry enhanced information. Moreover, a relation-geometry gated recurrent unit (GRU) (RGGRU) based disparity update module is designed for precise stereo matching. The evaluation results of the proposed method on the US3D dataset and WHU-Stereo dataset, demonstrated the effectiveness of the RGE-Net. The RGE-Net outperformed other stereo matching algorithms on the six cities by obtaining both the accurate disparity results and precise details of different regions.

title: OCR-3D: Occlusion-Robust Context-Guided Relative Depth for Monocular 3D Object Detection
authors: Jiasheng Su, Chenchao Hu, Yayan Xu, Siyu Chen, Yang Sun, Jinhe Su
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Most existing monocular 3D object detection methods primarily rely on object-centric foreground regions to infer object depth. However, the estimated depth often becomes unreliable under occlusion, leading to inaccurate 3D detection. Notably, real-world robotic scenes are not composed solely of foreground elements. Context regions such as roads and buildings contain rich and relatively stable structure cues, including the global depth layout and distance-dependent scale gradients, most of which can provide valuable information for object depth inference under occlusion. Motivated by this, we propose OCR-3D, a context-guided relative-depth framework that assists depth estimation from foreground regions. Specifically, we first use the foreground mask predicted by a region decoupling module to decompose global features into foreground features and context features, thereby preventing the foreground from dominating subsequent context modeling. Building upon this, we introduce a relative-depth prediction branch that learns relative-depth representations of the foreground from context regions, and injects them into foreground depth prediction via a depth cross-attention mechanism. Experimental results on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods while maintaining an efficient monocular inference pipeline.

title: Feature Transformation Effects in PolSAR Images Based on a Time-Varying Active Polarization Conversion Metasurface
authors: Yuehan Wu, Junjie Wang, Ran Sui, Dejun Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Electromagnetic metasurfaces, using electronically reconfigurable components, can dynamically modulate electromagnetic wave properties including amplitude, phase, and polarization. This capability shows great potential in wireless communications, radar systems, and target feature reconstruction. Although recent studies on time-modulated metasurfaces have explored feature manipulation in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR), the polarization dimension has been scarcely addressed, limiting their application to single-polarization SAR systems. This paper investigates the impact of Active Polarization Conversion Metasurfaces (APCMs) on Polarimetric Synthetic Aperture Radar (PolSAR) imaging and establishes a comprehensive electromagnetic scattering model that integrates polarization-time joint modulation. This work systematically reveals the cross-polarization modulation mechanism of time-varying polarization control on the radar signal's polarization scattering matrix. Theoretical derivations and numerical simulations indicate that the time-varying polarization modulation function of a precisely designed APCM can quantitatively redistribute scattering energy between co- and cross-polarization channels, simultaneously generating harmonic components with controllable polarization characteristics. Furthermore, by optimizing modulation parameters such as frequency and duty cycle, the imaging displacement of harmonic components can be accurately controlled. This study overcomes the technical limitations of traditional single-polarization SAR feature control, presenting an approach to simulate multi-polarization radar target features and reconstruct polarization scattering characteristics.

title: How to Ask the AI: A User Perspective Survey for Large Language Model Prompting
authors: Yiqun Zhang, Yunfan Zhang, Mingjie Zhao, Sen Feng, Yiu-ming Cheung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: AI tools like ChatGPT and DeepSeek, powered by Large Language Models (LLMs), allow users to obtain instant and effective content responses simply by typing requests, such as “plan a three-day Vienna trip”, “solve the attached mathematical problem”, “draft an email to inquire review progress”, etc., which are also known as LLM prompts. Crafting clear and wellstructured prompts leads to more appropriate LLM feedback, which effectively bridges human-LLM interaction. Although prompting appears accessible to non-expert users, precisely organizing effective prompts is a highly systematic and skillful process, presenting potential challenges even for experienced users. This survey explores the principles, taxonomy, and organization of prompts from a user-centered perspective. Differing from the existing surveys that primarily focus on technical principles and application scenarios of LLMs, this paper provides actionable guidelines for formulating effective LLM prompts across diverse real-world tasks and specifically contributes by: 1) developing an intuitive evaluation strategy for prompt efficacy, 2) providing prompting workflow demonstrations on representative applications, and 3) maintaining a dynamically updated open-source project to ensure the core takeaways remain up-to-date. These measures lower the threshold for users to correctly understand and craft prompts that align with evolving application scenarios. This work will be maintained as a living GitHub project <ext-link ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://github.com/Yunfan-Zhang/TAI_Guideline-Table" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">here</ext-link>.

title: Effects of Environmental Factors on Photovoltaic Module Temperature Monitoring Using BOTDR
authors: Zhiniu Xu, Yuedong Chen, Lijuan Zhao, Ziming Hua
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: To ensure the stable operation of photovoltaic (PV) panels, this work investigates temperature monitoring using Brillouin Optical Time Domain Reflectometry (BOTDR). Optical fibers were first installed on the back surface of the PV panels, and outdoor experiments were conducted under varying ambient temperature, solar irradiance, and wind speed to measure panel temperature. Then, a temperature field model of the PV module was then developed in ANSYS under the same conditions as the experiment. The simulation results closely matched the experimental data and the model is validated. Using this model, the effects of wind speed, solar irradiance, and ambient temperature on the temperature of optical fiber and panel were systematically investigated. The difference between the average panel temperature and the optical fiber temperature was defined as the measurement error. Results show that measurement error increases with rising ambient temperature and solar irradiance, while it first decreases and then increases as wind speed increases. At higher ambient temperatures, variations in solar irradiance have a more pronounced effect on measurement error, whereas the influence of wind speed becomes weaker. In contrast, as solar irradiance increases, the effect of wind speed on measurement error becomes more significant. When the ambient temperature ranges from 5 to 35 °C, the solar irradiance ranges from 300 to 1200 W/m², and the wind speed ranges from 0 to 6 m/s, the measurement error varies between 0.62 °C and 3.07 °C, with an average value of 1.72 °C. This work provides a reference for improving the accuracy of PV panel temperature monitoring using BOTDR.

title: Outsmarting the Smart: Intelligent Jamming Strategies Against AI-Empowered Anti-Jamming Frameworks
authors: Muhammad Shahzad Arif, Yuhang Shen, Sami Muhaidat, Paschalis C. Sofotasios
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key enabler for realizing adaptive and autonomous decision-making in next-generation AI-driven wireless networks, enabling real-time optimization of transmission strategies to counter jamming attacks. However, this adaptability also introduces critical vulnerabilities since the reliance of RL agents on environmental feedback renders them susceptible to deception, particularly when adversaries manipulate the environment in order to mislead the learning process. Yet, even though prior research considered adversarial jamming with white or grey-box access to the RL agent, the challenge of black-box jamming, where the jammer adapts without explicit feedback on its impact, remains largely unexplored. With this motivation, the present contribution addresses a practical adversarial scenario where a smart anti-jamming agent does not just resist jamming but actively exploits jamming signals to increase its throughput, especially as jamming attacks intensify. Defeating such an adaptive agent is particularly challenging in black-box settings, where the jammer has no knowledge of the link’s internal mechanisms or reward structure. In this context, we systematically benchmark a variety of advanced reactive jamming strategies, including both interaction-driven and optimization-driven approaches, under these realistic constraints. The achieved results indicate that adaptive, learning-driven jammers can reliably force even intelligent anti-jamming links into suboptimal operation, causing substantial throughput loss while consuming significantly less jamming power than conventional reactive jamming attacks. These findings reveal a fundamental vulnerability in RL-driven cognitive networks and highlight the urgent call for more resilient learning frameworks in order to secure next generation wireless systems.

title: Identifying Critical Breakpoints and Latency Effects During Remote Backup Driving_supp2-3691492.pdf
authors: Anthony D. McDonald
publish: N/A
abstract: As Level 4 driving automation progresses, remote driving remains a critical fallback for commercial trucks operating at the edge of their operational design domains. However, network latency presents a major challenge, impairing driving performance, increasing cognitive workload, and reducing trust in the remote driving system. This study investigates how latency affects human factors and driving performance in remote driving scenarios through a within-subject driving simulator experiment. Results showed that latency significantly increased perceived workload and reduced trust, with no direct effect on fatigue or situation awareness. Driving performance was also affected: median speed decreased, steering corrections intensified, and steering entropy increased under higher latency. Segment analysis revealed that lane-keeping tasks—particularly in curved road segments—were most sensitive to latency, exhibiting the strongest degradation in control stability. In contrast, lane-changing maneuvers were less affected, likely due to their discrete and anticipatory nature. Segmented regression identified critical latency breakpoints (347–480 ms), beyond which trust in the remote driving system and driving performance exhibited substantial changes. These findings highlight the need for latency-aware, context-sensitive design strategies, including predictive assistance, and adaptive feedback interfaces, to support safe and efficient remote truck operation under fluctuating network conditions.

title: Huygens Dipole-Enabled Broadside and Endfire Beam-Switching Transparent Antenna
authors: Zhao-Qi Cui, Shu-Lin Chen, Ming Li, Maral Ansari, Feng Tao, Pei-Yuan Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Transparent reconfigurable antennas have garnered increasing attention owing to their popularity in the rapidly growing field of transparent electronic platforms. To date, very few transparent antennas have been reported that can achieve desired beam-switching while maintaining high transparency, primarily due to multifaceted design challenges. Moreover, the achievable beam coverage for transparent reconfigurable antennas remains limited. To address these issues, this paper develops a highly integrable transparent antenna with switchable endfire and broadside beams. By employing a planarized Huygens dipole structure deployed on a single-layer transparent dielectric substrate without using any vias, the designed antenna achieves beam reconfiguration in the elevation plane while maintaining high optical transparency. With the aid of the metal-mesh technique, the fabricated prototype achieves an optical transparency of 87.6%. Moreover, the antenna demonstrates three beam-switching states, comprising one broadside beam and two endfire beams. The measured performance exhibits an overlapping bandwidth of 5.60–5.93 GHz for the three states. The corresponding peak realized gains are respectively 4.85, 5.76, and 5.50 dBi, with the gain variation being less than 1 dB. These beams collectively provide a very wide beam coverage of around 270◦. The measurement results agree well with the simulation ones, confirming the effectiveness of our developed antenna.

title: Virtual DC Motor Driving Synchronous Generator Control for Dual-Port Inertia Support Based on Flying-Capacitor Three-Level Inverter
authors: Xinyang Su, Minghao Wang, Junyu Chen, Yonghui Liu, Xinbo Ruan, Zhao Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Virtual synchronous generator (VSG) method is increasingly employed in DC/AC interlinking inverters. However, conventional VSG approaches primarily address AC frequency stability and inertia enhancement, while neglecting their dynamic influence on the DC side. In this paper, a virtual DC motor driving synchronous generator (VDMSG) method based on flying capacitor three-level (FC-TL) inverter is proposed to provide inertia support for both the DC and AC sides of an interlinking inverter. Firstly, the external operation characteristics of the DC motor driving synchronous generator system are derived, and the VDMSG control scheme is designed accordingly. Besides, a FC voltage decoupling control scheme for FC-TL inverter is proposed. Based on it, the FC voltage is regulated for providing virtual inertia energy for both DC and AC sides. Finally, experimental results demonstrate the bilateral virtual inertia enhancement performance of the VDMSG method under various disturbances.

title: Co- and Cross-Polarized RCS Imitation Based on Amplitude and Phase Controlled Metasurface
authors: Kaiqi Zhang, Yuxi Yan, Binchao Zhang, Zhen Tan, Weidong Hu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: This letter presents a method for co- and cross-polarized radar cross section (RCS) imitation based on an amplitude and phase controlled metasurface. By establishing the quantitative relationship between the polarization scattering matrix (PSM) and the metasurface's reflection coefficients, an L-shaped patch element is designed to achieve quasi-continuous amplitude control and 1-bit phase switching for both polarization channels, by rotating the element within <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$45^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$90^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$135^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> ranges. A genetic algorithm (GA) is employed to optimize the amplitude and phase distributions of the metasurface to imitate the co- and cross-polarized RCS of targets. Experimental results of three <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16\times 16$</tex-math></inline-formula>-element prototypes demonstrate that imitation errors remain around 1.5 dB within the experimentally validated range of 9.5–10.5 GHz and elevation angles within <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The results validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach for polarimetric RCS imitation in the joint frequency–angle domain.

title: Design and Validation of a Crate-Independent 1GbE DAQ Backend for Medium-Scale Detector Readout
authors: Fatima Bzeih, Benjamin J. Raydo, Evaristo Cisbani, Paolo Musico, Paolo Gastaldo, Saverio Minutoli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science
abstract: Detector readout systems in modern nuclear and particle-physics experiments must handle a large number of channels and high throughput while maintaining well-defined timing. Trigger and data acquisition requirements are also becoming more diverse, motivating architectures that support multiple acquisition modes and remain maintainable at the system scale. This work presents MPD-Eth, a crate-independent, detector-agnostic, Ethernet-connected multipurpose digitizer/DAQ backend implemented on a Xilinx Kintex-7 FPGA. The system couples a register-mapped control plane to a fully hardware TCP/IP data plane for 1-GbE event transport. The firmware implements front-end timing/control, digitized capture compatible with an ADS5281 eight-channel, 12-bit ADC’s source-synchronous LVDS interface, clock-domain buffering, and self-delimiting event framing supporting triggered, self-triggered, and streaming operation. The first demonstrated configuration targets the Jefferson Lab tracking program using APV25-instrumented GEM/μRWELL detectors. In the fully populated configuration (16 APVs, 6 samples/channel), the payload is 24.96 kB per trigger, yielding a sustained rate of ∼4 kHz at an effective 1-GbE payload throughput of ∼100 MB/s. These results demonstrate a compact network backend suitable for experimental deployment and provide a validated baseline for a forthcoming 10-GbE extension.

title: Efficient and Effective Personalized In-Context Learning for On-device Large Model Services_supp1-3697569.pdf
authors: Tao Qi
publish: N/A
abstract: Recently, on-device large models (e.g., 7B parameter LLMs and LVLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world services such as mobile assistants, edge computing, and IoT systems, where low latency and resource efficiency are critical. Since large models trained on general-domain data are typically suboptimal for downstream services, In-Context Learning (ICL) technique is widely used to enhance their task-specific service performance without extra tuning. However, effective ICL methods generally necessitate a sufficient amount of supervised data to provide abundant service-related information, which usually leads to a long context (i.e., more texts or images input) for models to process. This long context issue can significantly degrade the real-time inference efficiency and also impact the information utilization effectiveness, particularly for on-device large models with limited long context modeling capacities. To tackle this challenge, we propose a Personalized Knowledge Refinement (PKR) framework to achieve efficient and effective ICL for on-device large models. Specifically, we first introduce a personalized knowledge extraction module, which analyzes the inference behaviors of the target model on small-scale supervised data and then convert these behavioral patterns into personalized service-specific knowledge as the instruction context. Furthermore, we propose an adaptive knowledge filtration mechanism to model the informativeness of the extracted knowledge and eliminate the redundant ones, further improving the knowledge encoding efficiency per unit of context length. Experiments based on 14 benchmarks spanning both textual and visual task services, and 4 large models, demonstrated that PKR consistently improves task accuracy while reducing context length and inference latency, making it a practical solution for real-world on-device services. Codes are released at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/wanghl21/PKR</uri>.

title: Performance Analysis for Rate Splitting Multiple Access Enhanced Pinching-Antenna Assisted Integrated Sensing and Communication Systems
authors: Shiv Kumar, Keshav Singh, Chih-Peng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: The novel flexible-antenna technology, known as pinching antennas, has recently attracted significant research interest. By embedding discrete dielectric materials, a pinching antenna can be dynamically activated at arbitrary points along a dielectric waveguide, enabling flexible control of large-scale path loss and on-demand coverage extension. This paper investigates the performance analysis of a downlink rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA) enhanced pinching-antenna assisted integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system. Specifically, we consider a sensing-centric scenario in which a single pinching antenna simultaneously serves two communication users and performs target sensing, where the sensing target and one user are co-located in the same room and are served via a line-of-sight (LoS) link, while the other user resides in a separate room and is connected through a non-LoS (NLoS) link. We derive novel closed-form expressions for the outage probabilities (OPs) of both communication and sensing tasks. The communication OP is obtained through an information-theoretic approach, whereas the sensing OP is evaluated using a mean square error (MSE) based approach. In addition, the system throughput for the communication task and average sensing MSE for the sensing task are also evaluated. To gain further insights, asymptotic OP expressions are also derived for both communication and sensing tasks in the high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) regime. Monte Carlo simulations are performed to validate the analytical results. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed pinching-antenna-assisted RSMA–ISAC system outperforms pinching-antenna-assisted non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA)–ISAC and space-division multiple access (SDMA)–ISAC in terms of communication reliability and sensing accuracy. Furthermore, compared with a conventional antenna-assisted RSMA–ISAC benchmark, the proposed framework achieves significant sensing performance gains due to reduced effective sensing distance and near-field spatial focusing enabled by pinching antennas, while exhibiting a controllable sensing–communication trade-off governed by power allocation and antenna positioning. Finally, we analyze the impact of pinching-antenna height, room size, and other key system parameters on the performance of both communication and sensing tasks.

title: BRR: A Multi-Armed Bandit-Based Relay Selection Algorithm for Post-Disaster DTNs
authors: Zhe Yuan, Long Tan
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In post-disaster scenarios, communication infrastructures are often severely damaged, leading to intermittent connectivity and dynamic network topology. Delay Tolerant Networks (DTNs) provide an effective solution for data transmission in such challenging environments through opportunistic communication among mobile nodes. However, due to the randomness of node encounters and the lack of global network information, selecting appropriate relay nodes remains a critical challenge for DTN routing. To address this problem, this paper proposes a Bandit-based Relay Routing (BRR) algorithm based on the Multi-Armed Bandit model. In the proposed approach, candidate relay nodes are modeled as decision arms, and the Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) strategy is adopted to balance exploration and exploitation during the relay selection process. By continuously learning from historical forwarding outcomes, nodes can gradually identify more promising relay nodes without relying on global network knowledge. Simulation experiments are conducted using the ONE simulator to evaluate the performance of the proposed algorithm. The results demonstrate that the BRR algorithm achieves higher delivery ratio and lower routing overhead compared with several classical DTN routing algorithms. The proposed approach provides an efficient and lightweight solution for relay selection in dynamic DTN environments.

title: Design of Airport Low-Altitude Traffic Control System Architecture Based on Cloud-Edge Collaboration and Deep Learning
authors: Yu Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This study addresses the real-time and intelligent needs of low-altitude airspace traffic management in airports by designing a system architecture based on cloud-edge collaboration and deep learning. Analyzing the high latency and resource bottleneck issues of traditional centralized cloud architectures, we propose an edge computing layer using Huawei Atlas 500 nodes and the KubeEdge platform, combined with federated learning to optimize the flight path model in the cloud, achieving efficient data processing and model updates. The system utilizes OpenEuler and GaussDB for domestic software compatibility, and achieves Level 3 security certification, ensuring compliance and security in a domestically-developed environment. Experimental results show that this architecture reduces latency from 50ms to 8ms, improves model accuracy to 95%, and achieves 85% resource utilization, significantly enhancing the performance and reliability of lowaltitude traffic management. This provides a feasible solution for intelligent air traffic management.

title: Attention-Augmented BiGRU for Mental Health State Classification from Reddit Conversational Data
authors: Shadaan Saiphy, Km Meenakshi, Deepshikha Bhargava
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The social media and online forums have emerged as a place where individuals can talk openly about their mental health struggles in the online forums. The Proposed system is capable of automatically identifying the signs of depression, anxiety and bipolar disorder through what people post on Reddit. The model, based on a particular variant of neural network, BiGRU, with an attention mechanism, was able to reach 94.87% accuracy on 11,717 real posts on Reddit in mental communities. This model accurately diagnosed cases of depression 97.2% of the time, anxiety 96.8% of the time and bipolar disorder with impressive accuracy of 99.4%. The peculiarity of this work is that we can even observe which words and phrases the model uses in making a decision, such openness is vital in case physicians are going to rely on and operate with such tools. We gathered information in an ethical manner such as subreddits such as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{r}$</tex> /depression and r/Anxiety, ensured that the privacy of all people is at stake and we had actual clinical psychologists to have proposed labels checked. This technology may assist in finding individuals in need of mental health assistance earlier, particularly those who would not inevitably consult assistance in any case, owing to stigma or unreachable professionals.

title: Conditional GAN Based HF Channel Modeling with Historial Continuous Wave Signals
authors: Haotao Peng, Yutao Jiao, Li Cui, Yuping Gong, Fandi Lin
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: High-frequency (HF) channels are highly susceptible to abrupt changes induced by ionospheric variations, while traditional HF channel models struggle to adapt to time-varying dynamic environments. To achieve real-time and accurate modeling of HF channels, this paper proposes an HF channel modeling method based on conditional generative adversarial networks (CGAN) with historical data as conditional input, which fully exploits the temporal correlation characteristics of HF channels. Two types of ablation comparison experiments based on measured continuous wave (CW) data from the Hainan–Hebei link show that the proposed method achieves higher modeling accuracy in real-world HF propagation scenarios and exhibits excellent adaptability and generalization ability.

title: Spatiotemporal Feature Aggregation for Radar AMC: A Comparative Study of Stacked GRU and Optimized CNN-GRU Hybrid Architectures
authors: Bibo Liu, Zhenyu Zhang, Chengkun Cao, Xuejiao Gong
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Automatic Modulation Classification (AMC) is central to cognitive electronic warfare, where both recognition accuracy and latency matter. Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks are widely used for temporal modeling but often exhibit parameter redundancy and slow convergence. This paper evaluates two deep architectures that challenge this default: a 3-layer Stacked Bidirectional GRU (BiGRU×3) and an optimized Hybrid CNN-BiGRU with dual-stream pooling. Using the DeepRadar2022 dataset, BiGRU×3 attains 89.21% accuracy (SNR ≥ -10 dB: 92.17%), surpassing BiLSTM×3 (87.40%) while reducing training time by 53.7% and parameters by 24.1%. The hybrid CNN-BiGRU, featuring large-kernel spatial filtering and Max+Avg pooling, improves low-SNR robustness relative to standard CNN-BiLSTM models. These results support GRU-based designs for efficient processing of radar I/Q sequences.

title: An Innovative Approach for Aircraft Damage Detection via FasterNet-Modified YOLO11
authors: Zhisheng Liang, Wanyi Yu, Jiankun Bai, Yongzhou Cai
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Aircraft surface damage detection is crucial for aviation safety and structural integrity. This paper introduces a novel approach that combines FasterNet with the YOLO11 framework to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of damage detection. YOLO11, known for real-time object detection, is improved by FasterNet, which accelerates feature extraction and enhances detection precision. The proposed model integrates dynamic convolution kernels, adaptive feature pyramids, and multi-head attention mechanisms to handle complex and varied damage patterns. Experiments on a custom aircraft damage dataset show that the enhanced model outperforms existing methods in both detection accuracy and inference speed. Additionally, dual-label assignment strategies optimize training, allowing better generalization across different damage types. This approach offers a promising solution for automated aircraft maintenance and safety inspections, contributing to safer and more efficient aviation operations.

title: Research on The Method of Train Insulator Stain Detection Based on Deep Learning
authors: Zaitong Tian
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Real-time and accurate detection of train roof insulators is very important for the safety of high-speed rail. Aiming at the challenges of complex background, dynamic blur and scarcity of defect samples, this study proposes a two-stage detection framework for deep learning: YOLOv11 is used to locate the insulator target area, and the PatchCore unsupervised algorithm is used to extract features and identify defects to overcome sample limitations. Experiments show that the proposed method has high detection accuracy and can accurately reflect the location of insulator stains.

title: Implementation of a Lightweight and Customized Signal processing system based on RFSoC
authors: Liangyu Li, Bo Wang, Ruimeng Zhang, Bo Zhong, Qing Shen, Yide Zhao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This paper presents the development and performance evaluation of a lightweight radar signal processing system based on an RF direct sampling architecture. TDistinct from common approaches utilizing existing commercial RFSoC development boards, this work implements a compact, dedicated hardware platform based on the Xilinx XCZU28DR RFSoC. Through meticulously optimized hardware design, significant reductions in size and weight were achieved without compromising performance. The platform supports synchronized waveform generation and data acquisition for 5-channel RF front-end, with a maximum sampling rate of 4.096 GSPS. Experimental results demonstrate that the system possesses excellent phase noise performance and superior multi-channel synchronization, validating its effectiveness for high-precision pulsed radar detection applications.

title: Research on Intelligent Strategy and Digital Operation of Automobile Industry Based on Artificial Intelligence
authors: Chao Ma, Grace R. Tobias
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: With the deepening digital transformation of the automotive industry, artificial intelligence (AI) technology is becoming a core driving force for industrial intelligence and operational optimization. This paper constructs an AI-based integrated framework for intelligent strategy and digital operations in the automotive industry. This framework aims to achieve digitalization, intelligence, and collaboration across the entire automotive manufacturing and service process through data-driven, algorithmic optimization, and intelligent decision-making. This study first analyzes key application scenarios of AI in the automotive industry chain, including intelligent manufacturing, supply chain forecasting, production scheduling optimization, and customer experience management. Secondly, this paper designs an intelligent decision-making model that integrates machine learning and reinforcement learning for production line scheduling and operational decision optimization. By leveraging digital twin technology to achieve virtual-reality mapping and feedback correction, a closed-loop mechanism is established: strategic intelligence, operational execution, and performance feedback. Experimental results demonstrate that this model effectively reduces production downtime by 18.7%, improves scheduling efficiency by 22.5%, and significantly enhances the accuracy of operational data predictions. This research provides a feasible path and technical support for automotive companies to achieve AI-driven strategic collaboration and digital operations.

title: Evaluating Forecasting Accuracy of Artificial Intelligence Models for Smart Grid and Building Energy Demand
authors: Rajinder Vir, Jaswinder Singh Dhillon, Parvinder Singh, Makul Mahajan, Raghu Raja Sharma, Salil Batra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The ability to predict electrical loads as accurately as possible is essential for optimizing the operation of smart grids and building energy management systems. All of the optimizing functions of a power system unit commitment and its scheduling, dispatching, demand-side management require as accurate forecasting as possible for the time horizons in question. In recent years, forecasting power system loads through artificial intelligence (AI) has gained popularity due to its ability to capture the intricacies of the relationship between energy demand and the related factors. The main goal of the research presented in this paper is to offer the most precise, comprehensive, and systematic, review focused on forecasting loads in smart grids and building energy systems using AI. The author evaluates the forecasting precision and the different models of AI, the methods of AI training, the choice of input features of AI, and the certain, convertible, measurable, and definable features (exogenous variables). On the whole, the analysis results lead to the conclusion that artificial intelligence (AI) has surpassed other methods in almost every aspect and also attempted to answer the question regarding the diverse quality of data and the designing of the model that was used in the analysis. The final part of the paper, the author summarizes the most pertinent research questions that remain to be answered and describes the proposed actions for the subsequent research.

title: Distribution Network Topology Identification Using Graph Neural Networks
authors: Liang Ma, Jinshan Luo, Shigong Jiang, Liqi Liu, Xiaohan Li, Shangke Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of distributed generation, accurate topology identification has become essential for the reliable operation of modern smart grids. Conventional manual approaches are no longer adequate for large scale and complex network structures. This paper proposes a graph neural network framework with an enhanced graph attention mechanism for topology identification in heterogeneous medium and high voltage networks. Key components, including substations, power sources, lines, and switches, are represented as graph nodes to enable efficient structural modeling and feature extraction. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves identification accuracies of 98.93% and 97.86% for high voltage and medium voltage networks, respectively, outperforming conventional fully connected neural networks. The proposed approach provides an intelligent and efficient solution for topology identification and contributes to the reliable operation of power systems.

title: Community Driven Optimization for Fair Impact Maximization
authors: Uttam Kumar, Shivam Kumar, Navin Chanchal, Aditya Dayal Tyagi, Rajneesh Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Influence Maximization traditionally focuses on maximizing total reach, often concentrating influence within highly connected communities while neglecting peripheral groups. To address this imbalance, we propose a Community-Based Evolutionary Algorithm (CBEA) that integrates community structure throughout the evolutionary process to promote fairer seed selection. Experiments on real-world networks show that CBEA achieves about 94% of greedy influence spread while reducing inequality by nearly half at a modest cost.

title: Tourist Flow Computational Modeling and ABM Simulation Framework for Smart Tourism Big Data Analysis
authors: Aoyun Li, Shiyu Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Smart tourism big data analysis requires highly efficient real-time signal processing to manage crowd movements within complex dynamic systems. However, traditional static analytical methods struggle to process multi-source heterogeneous sensor data continuously, often leading to delayed congestion warnings and suboptimal spatial management. To address this, this paper proposes a real-time computational modeling and Agent-Based Modeling (ABM) simulation framework. The proposed architecture integrates an edge-processing layer for real-time trajectory signal filtering and feature extraction with a micro-level simulation and monitoring engine to replicate adaptive tourist behaviors. Empirical experiments using the real-world GeoLife GPS trajectory dataset demonstrate that the framework effectively processes high-concurrency heterogeneous signals. The quantitative analysis reveals that the data-driven ABM approach significantly stabilizes peak crowd flow monitoring and reduces the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) to 0.0276, substantially outperforming baseline static models and providing robust computational support for intelligent spatial decision-making.

title: TADAM: An Artificial Intelligence Based Sustainability Assessment Framework with Community Engagement
authors: Hina Gull, Raghad Alotaibi, Wafa Alqahtani, Shams Alfouzan, Yasmeen Alyami, Jana Alajmi, Sardar Zafar Iqbal, Maryam Abdullah Alnasser, Rawan Hamed Alghamadi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Environmental sustainability involves managing Earth's natural resources carefully to meet current needs while preserving the potential of subsequent generations to satisfy their own needs, focusing on ecological equilibrium, reducing pollution, preserving resources (water, air, soil, biodiversity), using alternative sources of energy, and fostering circular economy principles in order to ensure long-term global health and human welfare. Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 promotes ecological sustainability as a key component of the Kingdom's transition strategy, emphasizing its commitment to the highest possible standards of living and the next generation. For a long time, the nation relied solely on groundwater supplies, whose levels have been significantly reduced due to extensive exploitation, particularly for agriculture. Because of this, it has long been evident that the current path of action is unfeasible and that new, long-term solutions must be put into place. More than half of Saudi Arabians were unable to comprehend the concept of sustainable cities, according to early public awareness assessments. Although there was a minor rise in awareness between 2010 and 2015, the slow rate of development suggests that more focused measures are required. Saudi Arabia's present sustainability assessment methodology is limited in its capacity to support evidence-based policy and community action due to its fragmented nature and lack of localized data integration. This study uses a conceptual, design-oriented research methodology to provide a framework/platform for community-level sustainability evaluation. The framework will predict the environmental conditions of a particular area using AI algorithms on user-submitted environmental reports or data, weather data, and sector attributes. It will also calculate the sustainability score in a given area and offers suggestions to improve the neighborhood environment using machine-learning algorithms. The platform will also act as a knowledge and guidance hub for the residents to educate them about sustainability.

title: GCAF-Net: A Gated Cross-Attention Framework for Multimodal Retinal Disease Classification
authors: Qian Yu, Panpan Li, Junhao Ru, Qingwei Shi
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Jointly analyzing Color Fundus Photography, or CFP, and Optical Coherence Tomography, or OCT, images is essential for the comprehensive assessment of retinal diseases. Using age-related macular degeneration, or AMD, as a highly challenging clinical benchmark, we address the critical bottlenecks in automated multimodal retinal diagnosis. Existing approaches often suffer from limited training data, severe class imbalance, and suboptimal cross-modal feature fusion. In this paper, we propose a comprehensive multimodal framework that addresses these challenges through three key contributions. First, we design modality-specific data augmentation pipelines tailored to the distinct physical imaging characteristics of CFP and OCT. Second, we introduce a label-aware loose pairing strategy combined with class-balanced oversampling to effectively expand the training set and mitigate class imbalance. Third, we propose a Gated Cross-Attention Fusion module that dynamically regulates cross-modal information flow via learnable gating mechanisms, enabling adaptive and fine-grained multimodal feature integration. Extensive experiments on the public MMC-AMD dataset demonstrate that our method achieves 93.01% accuracy, 95.74% F1-score, and 90.45% Cohen’s Kappa, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.

title: PanCo-SLAM: A Robust Dynamic RGB-D SLAM System via Panoptic Semantics and Depth Residual Collaboration
authors: Jin Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The static world assumption of visual Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (vSLAM) is readily violated in dynamic scenes, causing feature mismatches and cumulative pose drift. Existing methods struggle to balance localization accuracy and real-time performance, limited by poor generalization of semantic priors or high computational redundancy of geometric constraints. To solve this, we propose PanCo-SLAM, a dynamic vSLAM framework with collaborative panoptic semantics and depth residuals, built upon ORB-SLAM3. First, OneFormer panoptic segmentation is adopted to acquire dynamic instance masks and static background priors for initial dynamic region screening. Then, a sliding-window multi-frame depth residual check is built to capture unknown dynamic targets beyond semantic priors. Finally, a refined dynamic mask is generated by bidirectional fusion for dynamic feature elimination, with only high-confidence static features used for pose optimization. Experiments on the TUM RGB-D dataset show that PanCo-SLAM reduces the RMSE of absolute trajectory error by over 96% in high-dynamic sequences versus ORB-SLAM3, while maintaining good real-time performance, effectively improving accuracy and robustness in dynamic scenes.

title: Parallel CNN for Accurate Classification of Aquatic Crab Species
authors: Xuegang Ma, Zicheng Huang, Junling Huang, Xiaofei Ma
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This study develops a parallel convolutional neural network with multi-scale feature fusion for accurate classification of Yangcheng Lake and Changzhou crabs. Designed with three parallel branches to extract microscopic and macroscopic features, the model effectively tackles variable crab postures and complex image backgrounds, featuring fast convergence and high accuracy across different learning rates. Comprehensive experiments verify its superior performance, providing a novel technical solution for aquatic product classification and demonstrating deep learning’s application potential in agricultural informatization.

title: Lightweight Object Detection Model for Early Fish Disease Identification in Aquaculture
authors: Yunxi Cui, Xiaona Xiu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In modern aquaculture, the rapid spread of fish diseases demands early intervention, but existing visual detection methods face dual challenges: weak early lesion features obscured by complex water noise, and the high computational cost of edge device deployment. Addressing the difficulty of traditional models in capturing fine-grained pathological features under hardware constraints, this paper proposes SCA-YOLO, a lightweight object detection model for early disease recognition. Unlike conventional deep object detectors, SCA-YOLO first utilizes ShuffleNetV2 as the backbone to reduce model parameters and computational overhead. It then couples a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) to adaptively suppress water reflection noise, alongside an Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP) module to capture multi-scale features, ranging from macroscopic ulcers to minute parasites. Finally, we further discuss a conceptual spatial-temporal extension that could translate static individual detections into group infection risk indices for future graded warnings. Experimental results on a hybrid lesion-level benchmark constructed from manually collected aquaculture images and the re-annotated SalmonScan dataset show that SCA-YOLO outperforms baseline detectors such as YOLOv5s in mAP@0.5 and F1-score while maintaining only 3.24M parameters, demonstrating a favorable balance between detection accuracy and model efficiency, highlighting its potential for edge deployment.

title: Alignment-Aware Dual-Stream YOLO for RGB-IR Oriented Object Detection in UAV Traffic Scenes
authors: Jun Yuan, Kun Wei, Haiyun Gan
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Wide-area, real-time, and fine-grained traffic monitoring is essential for improving road safety and intelligent transportation management. However, most existing Red-Green-Blue-Infrared (RGB-IR) unmanned aerial vehicle(UAV) object detection methods assume accurate cross-modal alignment and thus remain vulnerable to unstable fusion in weakly misaligned scenarios. To address this issue, this paper proposes AD-YOLO, an alignment-aware dual-stream framework for RGB-IR oriented object detection. The proposed framework preserves independent RGB and IR feature streams and incorporates an Alignment-Aware Semantic Gated Fusion (ASGF) module for feature-level alignment compensation and adaptive multimodal fusion. Experiments on the DroneVehicle RGB-IR dataset show that AD-YOLO achieves 65.9% Recall, 66.4% mAP50, and 55.7% mAP50:95, outperforming the dual-stream baseline by 3.3%, 3.1%, and 6.1%, respectively. Moreover, AD-YOLO exhibits stronger robustness under weak-misalignment perturbations. These results demonstrate that AD-YOLO offers an effective framework for RGB-IR UAV traffic object detection in complex environments.

title: Improved Mamba-DetectNet with Lightweight Attention and Feature Fusion for Weld Defect Detection
authors: Yuming Yin, Hongjun Zhou
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Weld defect detection is a critical task in industrial manufacturing and structural safety inspection. As a new generation of efficient detection model, Mamba-DetectNet combines the State Space Model (SSM) with a lightweight detection framework, providing a promising baseline for real-time weld defect inspection. However, the original structure still has limitations in multi-scale feature fusion and fine-grained defect feature extraction, especially for small, weak, and irregular weld defects.This paper proposes an improved Mamba-DetectNet for weld defect detection by optimizing the backbone feature extraction and introducing a dual lightweight attention mechanism. The backbone is enhanced with C2f and RFB modules to improve multi-scale feature expression and expand the receptive field, while a hybrid attention mechanism combining ECA and SimAM is embedded into the feature pyramid network to suppress background interference and focus on defect regions.Experimental results on a real weld defect dataset show that the improved model outperforms the baseline. Compared with the original Mamba-DetectNet, mAP50 increases by 1.84% and recall rises by 3.425%. The proposed model maintains a favorable trade-off between accuracy and efficiency, making it suitable for real-time industrial defect detection applications.

title: Machine Learning Model Design for Two-Dimensional Photonic Crystals with Point Defects
authors: Chu Guxuan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: The discovery of photonic crystals has opened up a brand-new path for controlling the propagation of light and electromagnetic waves. Its unique photonic bandgap characteristics enable photonic crystals to effectively restrict and regulate the behavior of photons, similar to the bandgap effect of electrons in semiconductors. This study constructs Back Propagation (BP) neural networks, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, leveraging their nonlinear mapping capabilities to accurately predict the bandgap structure and defect mode frequencies of point-defect two-dimensional photonic crystals, significantly enhancing design efficiency. In photonic crystals, by adjusting the parameters of the dielectric column, the band gap structure, band gap position, defect mode, and the intrinsic field distribution of the defect mode can be adjusted. The plane wave propagation method (PWE) and the finite-difference time-domain method (FDTD), which have developed in recent years, as effective numerical calculation methods, have been widely applied in the design and analysis of optical waveguide devices. However, both of these calculation methods require a very large amount of computation, usually necessitating traversal calculations of design parameters, and the calculation time depends on the size, structure, and precision of the device. This study employs BP neural networks, CNN, and LSTM models to predict and optimize structural parameters of point-defect two-dimensional photonic crystals, comparing the performance of different neural network architectures in capturing nonlinear relationships of bandgaps and defect modes, in order to achieve accurate prediction and optimization design of relevant parameters of two-dimensional photonic crystals with point defects.

authors: Elsa López Pérez, Thomas Watteyne, Cristina Onete, Dhekra Mahmoud, Pascal Lafourcade, Vaishnavi Sundararajan, Mališa Vučinić
