title: Trust Region Search-Based Bayesian Safety Evaluation Method for Accelerated Testing of Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Hao Wu, Yan Wu, Lifang Wang, Junzhi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Conducting efficient and accurate safety evaluations within the context of naturalistic driving environments is critical in the design and large-scale deployment of Autonomous Vehicles (AV). However, existing closed box safety evaluation algorithms suffer from inefficiency and are challenged to achieve rapid exploration, precise identification of safety boundaries, and accurate estimation of the AV’s failure probability simultaneously. To address these issues, we propose a Trust Region Search-based Bayesian Safety Evaluation (TuRS-BSE) algorithm. It combines Gaussian Processes (GP), the logit function, and the logistic function to construct a surrogate model for the AV, enabling the prediction of test results under different test scenarios. It also introduces three acquisition functions to enable efficient and comprehensive exploration, precise identification of safety boundaries, and the generation of risk scenarios for the AV. Meanwhile, the Trust Region Search (TuRS) algorithm is used to solve for the optimal candidate test scenario iteratively, enhancing the search efficiency. Additionally, the innovative integration of likelihood weighting with an efficient surrogate model eliminates the dependency on parameterized proposal distributions, enabling efficient and accurate estimation of the AV’s failure probability. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, we evaluated the AV system in both 2-dimension and 4-dimension test scenarios. The results demonstrated that the proposed approach can get an unbiased estimation of the failure probability and improve the efficiency of safety evaluation.

title: Classification of Brain Tumors Using a Fused Multi-Feature Stacking Ensemble Model
authors: Akash Pandey, Suvendu Rup, Sudhakar Pandey
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The lives of people are seriously threatened by brain tumors, especially if they are undiagnosed in their early stages. Tumor type has a significant impact on mortality rates, which highlights the importance of accurate early detection and classification. Although traditional diagnostic methods are frequently employed to detect malignancies, their classification accuracy and predictive performance remain insufficient for making trustworthy clinical decisions. This method uses a hybrid multi-feature stacking ensemble approach for various categories of brain tumor classification, utilizing publicly accessible MRI datasets in order to overcome these constraints. The suggested approach combines a number of preprocessing techniques, such as data augmentation to boost sample variety and weighted fractional filtering to improve anatomical features, edge sharpness, and noise suppression. Deep features retrieved using a pre-trained Mobile-Net architecture are combined with manually created descriptors, such as Gabor filters for localized texture characteristics and Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) for global frequency information. In order to minimize dimensionality redundancy, the fused feature space is reduced using probabilistic Principal Component Analysis (PPCA). Lastly, robust tumor categorization is achieved by using a stacking ensemble classifier. On a publicly accessible brain tumor MRI dataset, experimental assessments show that the proposed framework overwhelms conventional approaches and provides significant gains across a number of performance parameters.

title: Unpaired Underwater Image Synthesis with Cycle Consistent
authors: Xiangjie Meng, Qing Hong, Xiaomin Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: This study introduced a cyclic consistency generation counter-network to achieve effective desorption from paired data dependence. By integrating the Scene Parsing at Exceptional Scale (ADE20K) land scene dataset and the Enhancing Underwater Visual Perception (EUVP) underwater scene dataset, the model can deploy depth training under noncollapsing conditions, and a relatively robust image translation mechanism can be established through the discovery of intrinsic correlations between land and underwater cross-domain features. Experimental results showed that this model is excellent at improving image quality. The UCIQE score of the generated images is 0.51, and the UIQM score is 4.20. These data demonstrate that this method can effectively simulate realistic color and clarity. This work provided high-quality simulation data for underwater target exploration. This has significant technical implications for promoting the intelligence of underwater robotic systems.

title: Research on Detection of Chinese Phoneme Pronunciation Errors and Interpretability Feedback Teaching Based on Self-Supervised Pretrained Models
authors: He Wei, Lihua Ma
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of automatic diagnosis and interpretability feedback of phonemelevel pronunciation errors of Chinese as a second language learners, this paper proposes a hierarchical analysis method that integrates the structured knowledge of Chinese phonology and Self-Supervised pretrained models. By taking linguistic categories such as the place and method of initial consonants, the tongue position and lip shape of finals, and the contour and range of tones as explicit learning objectives of the model, this method guides the Self-Supervised speech encoder to extract more discriminative features, and then realizes finegrained error classification and attribution, and generates visual pronunciation physiological guidance and personalized training paths accordingly. Experiments on a pronunciation diagnosis dataset containing 320 learners show that the proposed method achieves an F1 score of 85.4% in phonemelevel error detection, a 6.8 percentage point increase compared to the optimal baseline model, with an accuracy of 84.2% in detecting errors in the place of initial consonants and an accuracy of 94.3% in tone recognition. A fourweek controlled teaching experiment further verifies that learners using this interpretable feedback system have a 12.5 percentage point higher increase in the pronunciation accuracy of target phonemes than the control group that only uses binary feedback. This study confirms that deeply integrating domain knowledge is an effective way to improve the performance and teaching utility of pronunciation evaluation models, providing a new solution for intelligent computerassisted pronunciation training and medical speech rehabilitation intervention.

title: Detection of Attacked Nodes in Computer Network Using Machine Learning
authors: Gaurav Joshi, Vivek Kumar, Neeti Taneja
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) are essential tools of detecting malicious traffic within the computer networks. Advanced IDS use machine learning to prevent complex attacks to both conventional networks and IoT nodes. This paper suggests a hybrid solution that will implement Bi-Directional LSTM (BiLSTM), a Sparse Autoencoder, and a Random Forest classifier to identify attacked nodes. The popular CICIDS2017 dataset is utilized.[1] that consists of benign and multiple attack classes) and a generated dataset of an IoT traffic. The autoencoder is sparsely trained to learn compact features, the BiLSTM is used to learn the time dependence of the data and the Random Forest is used to do the last classification. Our model is indeed extremely high in terms of detection performance: at test data, the accuracy rate is 99-99.7 percent and the loss concentration ranges between 0.021 and0.019. The ROC AUC is close to 1.0 which means it has good separability. The obtained results are above the majority of existing models of the IDS. [2], [3] Training curve (loss and accuracy vs. epoch) figures are provided, and it is shown that convergence and stabilization are fast. We have offered the design of this hybrid model and its implementation, empirical validation on standard and custom data, and the open sourcing of the methodology.

title: GS-DetLite: Efficient Geometry-Guided Lightweight Detection for Autonomous Driving
authors: Zhehao Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Real-time autonomous driving requires high-accuracy detection under strict computational constraints. While YOLO-based detectors are efficient, they often lack geometric awareness and long-range directional modeling. We propose GS-DetLite, a lightweight framework based on YOLOv11. It integrates Geometry Self-Attention (GSA), which leverages monocular depth priors for spatial structure modeling, and StripConv for capturing directional features of elongated objects. Experiments on KITTI and BDD100K demonstrate that GS-DetLite consistently outperforms YOLOv11 in accuracy while maintaining real-time speeds, offering an optimized balance between efficiency and precision for edge deployment.

title: NLESO-Based High-Fidelity Reconstruction for Noisy Magnetic Grating Signals
authors: Yifei Bai, Deren Kong
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In artillery recoil testing, conventional filters compromise phase integrity for noise suppression, leading to delayed peaks and erroneous velocity calculations from magnetic grating sensor signals. This paper presents a high-fidelity, zero-phase reconstruction method by applying a Nonlinear Extended State Observer (NLESO) to this extreme dynamic engineering challenge. The NLESO leverages adaptive nonlinear feedback to effectively attenuate intense high-frequency noise without introducing phase lag. Validated on both generic transient signals and real-world 10 kHz artillery recoil data, the proposed method achieves near-zero delay, the lowest RMSE, and enables direct extraction of smooth, high-quality recoil velocity. This work provides a robust, real-time capable solution for accurate displacement and velocity measurement in harsh shock environments.

title: A Secure Decentralized Time Updatable Policy-Based Chameleon Hash for Redactable Blockchain in IoT
authors: Qifu Zhang, Yewei Yin, Mengyuan Fan, Dailin Li, Haoyu Wang, Tianqi Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Blockchain is widely adopted for its decentralized and immutable features, yet this immutability impedes its development—for instance, illicit or erroneous sensor data is permanently stored in the Internet of Things (IoT). Redactable blockchain can endow blockchain systems with the ability to rewrite on-chain content, addressing the aforementioned issues of illicit data storage and regulatory compliance challenges. Recently, Huang et al. proposed a time-updatable policy-based chameleon hash (TPCH) scheme, which leverages the time-updatable chameleon hash (TUCH) primitive to achieve fine-grained redaction authorization, publicly verifiable accountability and identity traceability. However, a rigorous analysis of this scheme reveals a security vulnerability in its underlying TUCH primitive: an attacker can forge valid time-update proofs through a single legitimate redaction record, thus undermining the accountability of the scheme. In addition, the original scheme adopts a centralized architecture, which not only faces the risk of single point of failure but also cannot be deployed in distributed IoT networks. To address these flaws, we first design a security-enhanced TUCH (STUCH) construction, and integrate it with multi-authority attribute-based encryption (MA-ABE) technology to propose a decentralized TPCH scheme (DTPCH). Based on this cryptographic primitive, we further construct a high-security transaction-level redactable blockchain system, which ensures that the requirements of public accountability and identity traceability are met in a decentralized IoT environment. Performance analysis shows that the DTPCH scheme incurs an acceptable overhead while maintaining high security.

title: Image Colorization and CCTV Image Restoration Using Deep Learning
authors: Shreshtha Gupta, Shruti Kirti Singh, Yatharth Bhushan Tripathi, Sahil Chaudhary, Kalpna Sagar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The challenge of predicting accurate color information from grayscale images, where multiple correct color interpretations can relate to similar brightness patterns, is called picture colorization. Colorization systems have become much more automated and realistic because of recent improvements in deep learning, especially convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and generative adversarial networks (GANs). A deep learning framework is proposed for the simultaneous colorization and restoration of CCTV images in this paper. The technology tackles common issues in surveillance, including noise, blur, compression artifacts, low light, and grayscale limitations. A U-Net-based neural network is trained using images from the intentionally degraded COCO dataset to learn both restoration and color prediction in the CIELAB color space. This approach separates color and brightness learning for improved stability. The system combines reconstruction, perceptual, and adversarial losses to strike a balance between color constancy, visual realism, and structural fidelity. Surveillance-style photography is evaluated both quantitatively and qualitatively using PSNR, SSIM, and perceptual similarity criteria. Real-time inference capabilities for important surveillance applications can be demonstrated with a Flask-based deployment pipeline. By keeping structural integrity and improving the clarity of damaged footage, the framework aids forensic investigation. The discussion points out transparency, the preservation of original evidence, and the risks of AI-generated visual content when considering ethical and legal issues. The suggested method offers a practical balance between restoration quality and the ethical use of AI in forensics and security.

title: Analysis of Dual Agent Single Machine Scheduling Problem and Optimal Strategy with Truncated Learning Effect
authors: Lan Qing, Wang Ji-Bo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This study tackles the dual-agent scheduling problem on a single machine, integrating truncation learning effects to optimize agent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R$</tex> costs. It examines the problem's NP-hard complexity and proposes a hybrid heuristic algorithm combining genetic algorithm's global search with simulated annealing's local optimization. Experiments show the algorithm reduces cost deviation by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> compared to standalone methods for 20-100 tasks, with robustness to learning parameters <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\gamma$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex>. Sensitivity analysis indicates higher <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\gamma$</tex> lowers costs by 15 %, while high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex> raises costs by 12 %. The model offers theoretical and practical value for production scheduling, with potential for multi-machine extensions.

title: Cyber-Resilient Green AI: Intelligent Scheduling Under Energy and Security Constraints
authors: Arth Bhardwaj, Pragya Siddhi, Arun Kumar Takuli, Birendra Kumar Saraswat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: With the emerging development of artificial intelligence (AI) workloads and massive cloud systems, the electricity resources of data centers have increased exponentially, harming the issues of sustainability and performance in terms of environmental friendliness. Meanwhile, contemporary AI-enabled computing systems are getting more vulnerable to cybersecurity offenses, threats, which have the potential to interfere with the combat of scheduling systems, affect system resource allocation, and decrease the credibility of a system. Although the current studies have investigated energy-conscious workload scheduling and secure cloud computing separately, few studies have been considered in terms of combining energy efficiency and cyber resilience in managing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A I}$</tex> infrastructure. The current paper is a proposal of Cyber-Resilient Green AI conceptual framework that is intended to aid in intelligent scheduling of workload under energy and security limitations. The proposed paper combines energy tracking, security risk assessment, and intelligent scheduling schemes to assign AI tasks effectively to the existing computing resources. The framework will aim to reducing the use of power, resource use, and possible risks to security so as to prevent system operation as insecure and unreliable. The paper also evaluates what research has already been done in the areas of green AI, energy-aware scheduling, and secure cloud computing with the aim of determining its current limitations and future research opportunities. The suggested framework argues about the necessity of integrating sustainability and cybersecurity into AI-powered infrastructures and suggests knowledge about the ability to work on the creation of resilient and energy-efficient schedules of future cloud and AI computing environments.

title: Improved Lane Detection Algorithm Based on Ultra Fast Deep Lane Detection Version 2
authors: Qiang Li, Wankun Wang, Boxuan Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Lane detection is a core task for environmental perception in autonomous driving. To address the limitations of the Ultra Fast Deep Lane Detection Version 2 (UFLDV2) baseline model in road scenarios—such as poor local curvature adaptability, occlusion-induced topological disconnection, and training oscillations triggered by distant hard samples—this paper systematically optimizes it by integrating multi-dimensional spatial feature enhancement and temporal optimization mechanisms. Specifically, the architecture introduces deformable convolution and strip pooling modules, enabling the model to adaptively fit irregular physical lane edges while effectively endowing the network with long-range context aggregation capabilities. Furthermore, an exponential moving average strategy is employed to smooth historical parameters, significantly enhancing the detection robustness for distant features. Extensive benchmark experiments on the TuSimple dataset demonstrate that the improved method achieves an accuracy of 96.12% and an F1 score of 96.77%, substantially reducing both false positive and false negative rates . These improvements overcome baseline limitations, comprehensively enhancing detection performance.

title: RelicFormer: Digital Rebirth of Ancient Murals via Semantic Prior Distillation
authors: Cailing Wang, Peng Huang, Xingjia Tang, Hongwei Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The restoration of painted cultural relics is crucial for cultural heritage conservation, but existing deep learning methods are mostly oriented towards general images, making it difficult to meet their special requirements of faithfully restoring materials, colors and cultural semantics. To this end, we have constructed the first benchmark for painted cultural relic restoration, and proposed the RelicFormer method, which guides restoration by distilling the global semantic prior of the pre-trained Vision Transformer (ViT). On the test set containing 889 murals, this method significantly outperforms existing technologies in metrics such as PSNR (29.87dB) and SSIM (0.9287). In particular, it exhibits stronger structural fidelity and semantic coherence in moderately and severely damaged areas, providing an effective solution and evaluation benchmark for high-fidelity digital restoration of cultural relics.

title: Research and Application Case Analysis of Flexible Control Technology for New Energy Output and Energy Storage Based on 3D Digital Twin Model of Photovoltaic Energy Storage Power Station and Particle Swarm Multi-Objective Algorithm
authors: Lanrui Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: Based on the characteristics and application scenarios of the Chongqing region, this project plans the 400kW/1000kWh multi-element integrated energy storage device, including the one set of 100kW/400kWh grid type lithium battery energy storage, one set of the 100kW/200kWh grid type lithium battery energy storage, the one set of 100kW/200kWh grid type sodium ion battery, and one set of 100kW/200kWh grid type sodium ion battery. Typical experimental scenarios include supporting and off grid switching, combining the long and shortterm operations, new energy consumption, peak shaving and valley filling, voltage support, and backup power supply. Within 20 seconds, the battery current exhibits the three stepped waveforms, and SOC value shows decreasing trend with time. Additionally, the voltage value of the capacitor unit exhibits the periodic RC component charge and discharge characteristic. The 8760 hour time series was learned using the neural network, with the neural network set to the 70 iterations and the VMD-SSALSTM algorithm set to 30 population times. The paper shows that VMD-SSA-LSTM hybrid model based on the 3-dimensional digital twin model of the energy storage plant has the higher prediction accuracy of the digital model than the single LSTM model and the VMD-LSTM model. The specific error is limited to 2, and the prediction error of the traditional single model is about 8. Therefore, the hybrid VMD-SSA-LSTM model has better fitting and prediction ability.

title: Validation-Driven Adaptive Augmentation with Minimal Overhead for Image Classification
authors: Ye Zhu, Yifei You
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Data augmentation plays a crucial role in improving the generalization capability of deep convolutional neural networks. However, conventional augmentation strategies typically rely on manually designed and fixed transformation pipelines that remain unchanged throughout training. Such static policies cannot adapt to the evolving learning dynamics of neural networks. In this paper, we propose a validation-driven adaptive augmentation framework with minimal computational overhead for image classification. The proposed method maintains a pool of candidate augmentation policies and dynamically adjusts their sampling probabilities using validation feedback during training. After each epoch, validation accuracy is used to update the policy distribution through a lightweight normalization mechanism, enabling the model to progressively emphasize more effective augmentation strategies without reinforcement learning or expensive search procedures. Experiments on CIFAR-10 with ResNet-18 demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms fixed augmentation baselines under both limited and extended training budgets, achieving 84.45% accuracy at 30 epochs and 87.35% at 100 epochs.

title: FSDR-SMOTE Driven Class Imbalance Mitigation for Robust CNN-Based Rice Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Vishan Kumar Gupta, Mukesh Kumar Singh, Kireet Joshi, Shivani Agarwal, Paras Jain, Sarvesh Vishwakarma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The accurate detection of leaf diseases in rice using deep learning is usually affected by a significant level of class imbalance that inherently exists in agricultural datasets, thus contributing to imbalanced learning and a lack of generalization. To overcome the shortcoming of this technique, a novel classification framework that combines Feature-Space Density-Ratio Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (FSDR-SMOTE) and sophisticated techniques of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) has been proposed for efficient recognition of rice diseases. This technique allows the adaptive over-sampling of the minority class in the deep space of feature points, based on the estimation of local density ratios, thus maintaining the natural attributes of the disease and minimizing noises and over-fitting. Varieties of CNN designs are employed for balanced feature points to classify the five types of leaf diseases in rice, which include bacterial blight, brown spot disease, leaf blast, sheath blight, and normal leaves. The efficacy of the proposed FSDR-SMOTE-CNN technique has been verified by conducting simulations upon a genuine agricultural dataset of rice leaf images, and comparative analysis indicates that the technique outperforms traditional over-sampling approaches and traditional CNN designs in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-measure, especially for the minority class of rice leaf disease.

title: Research on NIHSS Score Prediction for Stroke Patients Based on Sentiment Mining from Chief Complaints and Multimodal Fusion
authors: Jia Ning
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Rapid assessment of NIHSS scores for stroke patients in emergency settings faces challenges. This study extracts sentiment features from chief complaints to construct a multimodal model for predicting NIHSS grading. We collected data from 1850 patients and built a three-stage cascaded model: a RoBERTa multi-task learning model to extract 9-dimensional sentiment features, a BiLSTM-CRF model to extract 20-dimensional symptom features, and an XGBoost model to fuse sentiment, symptom, and medical history features for three-class NIHSS prediction. After incorporating sentiment features, the weighted accuracy improved from 0.612 to 0.658, and the macro-average AUC increased from 0.731 to 0.782. Experiments verified that the performance of multimodal fusion significantly outperforms single-modality approaches, and sentiment features from chief complaints provide incremental predictive value, offering decision support for emergency triage.

title: A Secure and Stable Trust-Aware Clustering Scheme for IoT-Integrated Vehicular Ad HOC Networks
authors: Anil Kumar, Avnish Kumar, Sunil Kumar, Abhay Bhatia, Amit Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Recently, next-generation transportation systems that communicate intelligently through the Internet of Things embedded into Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks have been developed. However, network stability and cluster trustworthiness are still threatened by frequent node mobility, dynamic topology, and security weaknesses. This paper presents a Centralized Trust-Aware Clustering Framework designed to improve cluster stability and secure communication in IoTenabled VANETs. Trust values are determined based on knowledge, experience, and reputation using Roadside Units as centralized authority. Selected CHs are bootstrapped based on these trust parameters and cluster nodes with malicious or compromised nodes are evicted from the cluster. A dynamic trust evaluation process of direct and indirect feedback ensures the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C H}$</tex> is continuously adapts to the evolving node behavior. Across all performance metrics studied, including throughput, end-to-end delay, packet delivery ratio, and cluster stability, CTCF significantly outperforms existing schemes 3VSR, R2SCDT, and AODV. It can be concluded that CTCF integration of Trust management in a centralized clustering architecture is highly robust, secure, and reliable.

title: A Semi-Synchronous Federated Learning Scheme for Internet of Vehicles
authors: Lin Wei, Dan Peng, Lei Shi, Huijuan Lian
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Federated Learning demonstrates significant potential in Internet of Vehicles for facilitating collaborative task execution among vehicles while preserving data privacy. However, the direct application of conventional Federated Learning systems to Internet of Vehicles scenarios faces critical challenges, including inherent Federated Learning security risks and Internet of Vehicles environmental heterogeneity, which lead to inefficiencies in aggregation. To address these challenges, our work proposes a Semi-Synchronous Federated Learning (SSFL) scheme. To navigate the complexities of heterogeneous vehicular environments, SSFL implements a multifaceted defense and optimization architecture. The scheme adaptively manages the server’s response cycle through a dynamic waiting technique, effectively neutralizing the straggler effect. It employs a malicious gradient exclusion algorithm to preserve model integrity. Consequently, this approach ensures efficient convergence and robust training performance by addressing performance bottlenecks and security threats in a unified manner.
