title: Research on SPWGAN Generative Enhancement Method Based on Imbalanced Tool Data
authors: Xin Fang, Hui Ma
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Accurate identification of tool wear status is the core of achieving predictive maintenance for computer numerical control (CNC) machine tools. Aiming at the problem that weak faults are prone to missed detection caused by the extreme long-tailed distribution of vibration signals in tool wear monitoring, as well as the defect that traditional generative adversarial networks (GANs) tend to produce physical artifacts under extremely small sample conditions, this paper proposes a generative oversampling framework (SPWGAN) integrating spectral normalization and frequency-domain physical priors. This method first uses spectral normalization to stabilize the gradient evolution of adversarial training, and simultaneously introduces cutting dynamics constraints (FFT physical anchors) into the adversarial mechanism to force the network to learn real vibration laws, thereby achieving high-fidelity manifold reconstruction in an extremely sparse feature space. Experiments under the extreme imbalance condition of 1:100:1 based on the PHM2010 dataset show that the proposed method not only effectively eliminates frequency-domain artifacts of generated samples, but also accurately repairs the collapsed decision boundary of the classifier. After optimization, the overall diagnostic accuracy of the model is stably increased to 91.47%, and the recall rate of initial weak wear is sharply raised to 0.8360. This research provides a new mechanism-data dual-driven paradigm for highly reliable tool condition diagnosis.

title: An adaptive multi-label feature selection method based on granular space partitioning
authors: Yuan Li, Xin Wen, Kun Bi, Tao Zhang, Ke Zhao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Feature selection, as a crucial step in multi-label learning, can effectively reduce the computational complexity of learning and enhance the generalization ability of the learning model. Neighborhood rough sets have become an important theoretical foundation in the field of multi-label feature selection. However, the inability to adaptively determine the neighborhood radius often significantly affects the performance of multi-label learning. To address this issue, this paper proposes an adaptive multi-label feature selection method by integrating the ideas of granular ball space partitioning and neighborhood rough sets. First, the K-means algorithm is employed to cluster samples, and data objects are partitioned into different granular balls based on the label dissimilarity and purity among multi-label data. Subsequently, the radius of each granular ball is adopted as the adaptive neighborhood radius for the corresponding samples, based on which a novel neighborhood rough approximation operator is constructed, and a multi-label feature selection algorithm is further proposed. Finally, experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Research on Mobile Robot Navigation Method Based on Fusion of A*and DWA
authors: YuQing Mao, Yanbo Yang, Jiawei Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the challenge that a single algorithm struggles to balance global path optimality and local real-time obstacle avoidance in mobile robot navigation, this paper proposes a navigation method that integrates an improved A* algorithm with the Dynamic Window Approach (DWA). First, an obstacle safety distance penalty term is introduced into the cost function of the A* algorithm to maintain a safe distance between the global path and obstacles. Meanwhile, redundant nodes are removed through polyline optimization to enhance path smoothness. Subsequently, using the optimized global path as a reference, a DWA evaluation function that incorporates both local directional cost and global path deviation cost is designed to guide the robot in effectively following the global path while achieving local obstacle avoidance. The experimental results show that the proposed fusion algorithm can effectively generate a safe and smooth global path and adjust the local trajectory in real time in the presence of unknown obstacles, thereby achieving a balance between path optimality and real-time obstacle avoidance capability.

title: Hybrid Optimization Algorithm and Transformer Design for Low-Resource Chinese-African Neural Machine Translation
authors: Jianjun Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: With the continuous development of China Africa economic and trade relations, the demand for multilingual information transmission on translation tasks is also constantly increasing. Traditional translation methods have shortcomings in efficiency and accuracy, making it difficult to cope with complex economic and trade exchanges. This article proposes an algorithm architecture for intelligent management and optimization of multilingual resources based on a machine translation framework. It carries out cleaning, denoising, matching and other work at the data level to ensure resource quality; Enhance cross linguistic semantic modeling ability through neural network layers and fusion attention mechanisms; Realize self optimization iteration through deep neural networks and hybrid pattern structures. The experiment aimed to verify economic and trade texts, and the results showed a significant improvement in translation accuracy and efficiency. It can effectively solve the problem of multilingual corpus and achieve excellent application effects in practical translation, model stability, and application. It can provide technical and methodological support for cross-border e-commerce, contract translation, and government policy interpretation in China and Africa.

title: Joint Optimization of Energy Efficiency and Performance for Mobile Edge Computing in UAVs Assisted by Intelligent Reflective Surfaces
authors: Xueyang Zhou, Xu Zhao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The integration of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) and Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) offers flexible deployment for compute-intensive IoT applications. However, dynamic non-line-of-sight (NLoS) environments and UAV energy constraints severely limit performance. To address the challenges of unreliable links and low energy efficiency in UAV-assisted mobile edge computing systems under dynamic non-line-of-sight environments, this paper proposes an intelligent reflecting surface-assisted collaborative architecture and a joint optimization scheme. By constructing a four-dimensional joint optimization model encompassing task offloading, resource allocation, UAV 3D trajectory, and RIS phase shifts, and designing an efficient iterative algorithm based on block coordinate descent, the problem of coordinating communication, computing, and control resources in complex environments is effectively solved. The proposed approach significantly enhances the overall system energy efficiency while simultaneously reducing task delay and drop rate, thereby providing an efficient and reliable solution for low-altitude heterogeneous computing networks. Simulations show our algorithm reduces system energy consumption by 23.1%, lowers average delay by 22.9%, decreases task drop rate by 61%, and improves user satisfaction by 16% compared to benchmarks, demonstrating significant gains from synergistic UAV-RIS optimization.

title: Heart Attack Prediction Using Machine Learning: A Systematic Review
authors: Krishna Varshney, Kartik Rana, Nepali Singla
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases are still the leading cause of worldwide mortality and myocardial infarction represents a significant share. Optimising patient identification for elevated cardiac risk is therefore both a clinical and computational neces- sity. In this paper we summarize recent developments in machine learning for predicting heart attacks, derived from twenty peer- reviewed studies that have appeared between 2021 and 2025. We find that ensemble approaches perform the best, with Random Forest and XGBoost showing consistent high discrimination with area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUC) greater than 0.92 across multiple studies. Deep learning models trained on ECG signals demonstrate classification accuracies of nearly 93.5%, with high sensitivity and specificity for in- feroanterior and inferoseptal ischemic patterns as compared to traditional diagnostic criteria. Consistently reported predictive findings in the studies encompass patient age, serum troponin level, electrocardiographic changes, lipid assessments and arterial pressure. However, many challenges remain: imbalanced class distribution in the training datasets, different pre-processing techniques and few external validation studies as well as low interpretability of models to translate it into daily clinical practice. Utilizing multimodal sources - including EHR data, genomics and continuous home monitoring from wearable technologies holds promise for improving model predictive accuracy. Future research should emphasize algorithmic fairness, robust crosspopulation validation, and interpretable decision explanations in order for these computational tools to be incorporated in routine cardiovascular care.

title: Fatigue Detection with Interpretable Multi-Feature Machine Learning Using Webcam
authors: Krishna Kant Yadav, Khushi Ahuja, Khushi Tomar, Khushi Srivastava, Yasha Istwal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The study examines the real-time, non-invasive computations of cognitive fatigue and attention based on eye-movement behavior, which is recorded by traditional RGB webcams. The issue of cognitive fatigue in safety and performance in transport, education, and work environments is of high level of concern. However, the current fatigue detection mechanisms are costly and depend on intrusive devices. In order to overcome these shortcomings, we suggest a machine-learning-based gaze analysis system that can identify ocular biomarkers in video feeds with no infrared cameras or special equipment. The circulated method employs facial landmark detection to extract features such as blink rate, gaze stability, PERCLOS, fixation stability, and movement of the pupil. These features are temporally aggregated to a Random Forest classifier, achieving 98 percent accuracy, with ROC-AUC of 0.9805. The feature importance analysis identifies PERCLOS as the most important feature, contributing about 35 percent to model decisions. The system runs at 30 frames per second on consumer-grade hardware and is estimated to reduce 80 percent cost of infrared-based eye-tracking systems. The findings indicate that ocular analytics using a webcam with powerful feature engineering and learning algorithms can offer an alternative approach to fatigue monitoring continuously in the real world, which is scalable and cost-effective.

title: Artificial Intelligence-Driven Marine Communication Edge Computing Signal Processing: Technical Roadmap, Applications and Challenges
authors: Jiahui Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Research on edge computing signal processing for maritime communication environment, aiming at the efficiency and reliability problems under the conditions of cross-domain link time-varying, energy instability and heterogeneous computing power. This paper elaborates on the characteristics of the marine communication environment and investigates the challenges in signal processing such as high latency, energy constraints, and unstable network conditions. The issues in traditional centralized processing are analyzed and highlighted that edge computing is an effective solution. The need for incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) to improve system performance is also emphasized. The edge computing architecture of sea-air-ground collaboration is constructed, and the delay-sensitive and data-intensive tasks are moved forward to the edge to compress the end-to-end delay. The paper investigates core signal processing issues such as high latency with high energy consumption, network connectivity and reliability challenges, and the complexity of data processing and task offloading. Furthermore, an AI-based technical aspects such as deep learning-based edge signal processing, federated learning for collaborative modeling, and intelligent optimization algorithms for task offloading were discussed. The present technological challenges and future research directions are also discussed. The joint optimization of hybrid energy supply and task offloading achieves a trade-off between delay and energy consumption, and improves system stability and operational economy.

title: An Efficient Point Cloud Grasp Detection Method for Multi-Size Gripper Generalization
authors: Ziqiang Li, Haiyang Huang, Wei Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Robust grasp detection for parallel grippers of varying widths in complex unstructured environments remains a critical challenge in robotic manipulation. Existing methods such as REGNetV2 adopt a three-stage cascaded architecture with PointNet++ as the shared backbone, achieving competitive performance in multi-width grasp detection, but suffer from high model complexity, limited inference efficiency, and limited feature extraction capability. To address these issues, this paper introduces PointNeXt as a replacement for PointNet++ within the REGNetV2 framework, combined with point cloud downsampling and lightweight channel design, improving inference efficiency while maintaining grasp detection performance. Experimental results show that under the same hardware environment, the inference time is reduced from 116.67ms to 82.87ms, a speedup of 28.97%, with multiple grasp evaluation metrics surpassing the REGNetV2 baseline. Furthermore, two cross-width generalization experiments are designed, where models are trained on three known gripper widths and tested on unseen target widths. Results demonstrate that despite a moderate performance drop compared to the standard setting, the model retains competitive grasp detection capability, exhibiting generalization ability to unseen gripper sizes. Experiments on a multi-width grasp dataset built upon the YCB object set and MuJoCo simulation validate the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed method.

title: Design of Tent-Chebyshev Cascaded Composite Chaotic Sequences for Underwater Acoustic M-CSK Secure Spread-Spectrum Communications
authors: Yingjian Wu, Ping Liu, Yangfan Qian, Zhongwei Shen, Xuefei Ma
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: M-ary direct-sequence spread-spectrum and M-ary code-shift keying (M-DSSS/M-CSK) underwater acoustic communications often require a large spreading-code library, while classical PRN families are limited in scalability and may exhibit structural regularities. This paper proposes a scalable codebook generation method based on a cascaded Tent–Chebyshev composite chaotic map. The Tent map output is used as a switching/secondary initialization source to drive the Chebyshev map, producing multiple subsequences that are further serpentine-reordered and binarized (bipolarized) to form a large candidate library. A cyclic-correlation decision model is presented to clarify the detection requirement of M-CSK and the role of sidelobes. Simulations show that the generated codes achieve low aperiodic sidelobes and cross-correlation levels with length-dependent improvement, and provide comparable or slightly improved BER over an AWGN + multipath channel compared with single-map chaos and conventional PRN baselines, indicating suitability for secure underwater acoustic spread-spectrum applications.

title: Comprehensive Study of Deep Learning Architectures on EfficientNet, DenseNet, VGGNet and ResNet on CIFAR-10 Dataset
authors: Jaspreet Kaur, Kuldeep Singh Sidhu, Ankur Srivastava, Narottam Sahu, Akanksha Singh, Vipin Kumar Chaudhary
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Recent years have witnessed the fast development of convolutional neural networks (CNN) with better architectures for image classification. In this paper, we study these four popular CNN architectures-EfficientNetB0, DenseNet121, VGG16, and ResNet50 -on the CIFAR-10 dataset. Accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score were applied to evaluate the quality of classification, and the computational efficiency was evaluated by the complexity of models and the number of parameters and training time. Accuracy was the best for VGG16 (93.5 %) with balanced precision, recall and F1-score and was the second best for ResNet50 (91.24 %) among the pretrained models as mentioned in the results. EfficientNetB0 found a balance between accuracy (77.8 %) and the amount of computation that was required, while DenseNet121 did not perform well (68.5 %) despite its small parameter size. The results agree well with recent literature claiming EfficientNetB0 has the best accuracy-efficiency trade-off; DenseNet121 being parameter efficient, ResNet50 being flexible, and VGGNet16 being lower on the efficiency scale. In this paper, we emphasize some trade-offs between speed and accuracy for CNN selection and we propose deployment of models tuned with application requirements. In additional work, the authors will research hyperparameter optimization, transfer learning and experiments with more complex datasets, such as CIFAR-100.

title: Design of Miniaturized Ultra-Wideband Equalizer Based on Gallium Arsenide Integrated Passive Device Technology
authors: Peng Wan, Wenhao Yin, Shuaishuai Li, Pengfei Dong
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Significant fluctuations are often exhibited by the amplitude-frequency characteristics of radio frequency (RF) systems operating over a wide frequency band. To improve the transmission quality and stability of broadband RF signals, this paper designs an ultra-wideband equalizer operating from 1 to 18 GHz based on the topologies of LC series resonant circuits and LC parallel resonant circuits. In addition, Gallium Arsenide (GaAs) Integrated Passive Device (IPD) technology is employed to achieve significant miniaturization, resulting in a fully integrated ultra-wideband equalizer for use in phased array antenna systems. At the theoretical level, the feasibility of the circuit was verified based on the ABCD network analysis method. The realizability and validity of the proposed device are confirmed through full-wave electromagnetic (EM) simulations and experimental measurements. Finally, excellent electrical performance is achieved by the circuit over the 1-18 GHz bandwidth (corresponding to a 178% FBW). Measurement results show that the return loss at ports 1 and 2 is better than 15 dB across a wide bandwidth, with an equalization amount of 4 dB, and the dimensions are 0.8mm×0.5mm×0.1 mm, making it suitable for use in the feed network of phased array antennas.

title: A Single-Configuration Velocity Ambiguity Resolution Method for 2TX TDM-MIMO FMCW Radar
authors: Shuwen Li, Zhongxiang Zhang, Haoran Zhu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Velocity ambiguity is a fundamental limitation of single-configuration FMCW radar due to its restricted unambiguous Doppler interval. This paper presents a velocity ambiguity resolution framework for 2TX time-division-multiplexed multiple-input multiple-output (TDM-MIMO) radar under a fixed waveform configuration. A finite set of candidate true velocities is first generated from the aliased Doppler estimate. Each candidate is then evaluated by compensating the TDM-induced phase offset on the virtual array and measuring the corresponding angle-spectrum focusing quality. The final ambiguity branch is selected over multiple frames through dynamic programming with range, angle, and velocity continuity constraints. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method can recover velocities beyond the nominal unambiguous limit while preserving stable angle estimation, and remains effective in multi-target scenarios after track association. The framework avoids dual-PRI switching and is compatible with conventional range-Doppler-CFAR processing.

title: Helmet-Det: An Efficient Helmet-Wearing Detection Algorithm for Electric Vehicles Based on Deep Learning
authors: Siyan Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Helmet-wearing detection for electric vehicle riders is crucial for traffic safety but remains challenging due to small targets, occlusion, and complex environments. To address these issues, this paper proposes Helmet-Det, an improved YOLOv12-based algorithm. The method integrates a Multi-Scale Helmet Feature Fusion module (MSHFF) to enhance small object perception, a Rider Context-aware Attention mechanism (RCA) to improve helmet state discrimination using contextual cues, and a Helmet Small Object Enhancement module (HSOE) to strengthen feature representation of distant targets. Experiments on a self-constructed dataset show that Helmet-Det achieves an mAP of 96.9% while maintaining real-time performance, outperforming the baseline and demonstrating strong effectiveness in complex road scenarios.

title: Design of Electromagnetically Transparent Antenna Based on SIR Structure
authors: Menglong Han, Hongning Liu, Yin Li, Shuo You, Xinyang Zhu, Yongliang Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This paper presents a novel electromagnetically transparent base station antenna. The antenna uses a Frequency Selective Surface (FSS) as the radiator. By loading the Stepped Impedance Resonator (SIR) structure in the square loop, it gives the antenna electromagnetic transparency in the high band (1.7–2.7 GHz). Through the cancellation of scattering by reverse induced currents, it can effectively suppress the interference to high-frequency antennas. It has a wider operating bandwidth, covering the Low Band (LB) of 0.65–0.96 GHz, and a profile height of 0.18λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> (where λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> is the wavelength at the lowest operating frequency). It forms a dual-band antenna array with the antenna in the high band. The high frequency radiation pattern has no distortion, the gain is stable, and the dual frequency isolation performance is excellent.

title: Experimental Study on Partial Discharge Signal Denoising and Feature Analysis Based on CSSOA-Optimized VMD
authors: Yifei Ma
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) signals are important for insulation condition assessment in high-voltage equipment. In practice, noise often distorts these signals and weakens later analysis. This paper uses variational mode decomposition (VMD) with parameters optimized by the chaotic sine cosine optimization algorithm (CSSOA) to denoise PD signals. The method is compared with PSO-VMD, GA-VMD, and WOA-VMD on 200 simulated samples. Signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), root-mean-square error (RMSE), waveform similarity, convergence iterations, and running time are used for comparison. The results show that CSSOA-VMD gives stable denoising performance under the simulated noise setting used in this study. After denoising, six time-domain features are extracted and analyzed by principal component analysis (PCA). The samples processed by CSSOA-VMD show clearer separation in feature space. This study focuses on method verification under controlled conditions. More complex noise and open or field data will be considered in later work.

title: A Survey on Joint Optimization of Computation Offloading and Resource Allocation in Mobile Edge Computing
authors: Xiao Zhang, Tong Lu, Zhiwei Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the deep integration of 5G-A/6G and the Intelligence of Everything, computation-intensive services pose severe challenges for ultra-low latency and high energy efficiency. MEC alleviates the high-latency bottleneck of traditional cloud computing by pushing computing capabilities to the network edge, and the joint optimization of computation offloading and resource allocation is key to enhancing system performance. This paper proposes a three-dimensional classification framework driven by optimization objectives, systematically reviewing research progress from three dimensions: latency, energy consumption, and multi-dimensional trade-off. The paper focuses on surveying the applications of frontier technologies such as NOMA, RIS, semantic communication, integrated sensing, communication and computing (ISCC), and deep reinforcement learning (DRL). It analyzes the differences among various methods in terms of constraint conditions, performance tradeoffs, and applicable boundaries, and envisions three future directions toward 6G: deterministic and value-driven optimization, deep integration of sensing, communication, computation, and edge intelligence, and trustworthy and sustainable MEC ecosystems.

title: AI-Enabled Multimodal Medical Image Fusion: Recent Advances
authors: Rajeev Kumar Rai, Prabhishek Singh, Akansha Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Multimodal Medical Image Fusion (MMIF) is a technology of the modern era in the field of diagnostics that combines complementary data of anatomical (CT, MRI), and functional (PET, SPECT) sources to eliminate the drawbacks of single-source imaging. This review gives a detailed account of how MMIF has developed in terms of methodology over the years, starting with the classical Multi-Scale Transforms (MST) and sparse representations up to deep learning systems. We are critical about the transition of standard Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), which is prone to information loss in downsizing, to Invertible Dense Networks and Edge-Enhanced mechanisms that do not lose fine details of the information. Moreover, the review also presents the recent paradigm shift in the direction of Generative AI, namely analyzing how Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPMs) are better than GANs in the context of noise and super-resolution. The development of linear-complexity Global Context models based on State Space Models (Mamba) and bio-inspired Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) is a substantial part of this work, and this type of works solves the computationally expensive nature of Transformers in 3D volumetric fusion. In other areas, outside of architecture, we discuss obstacles to clinical deployment that are also critical such as the requires alignment of data during registration, the Register-Fusion Gap, and the need to have mechanism-aware constraints (additive vs. multiplicative). Lastly, we speak of the industry paradigm shift in the visual fidelity metrics to taskbased evaluation protocols, which are aimed at streamlining downstream segmentation and classification.

title: MBDA-Net: A Multi-Branch Dual-Attention Network for Breast Histopathological Image Classification
authors: Junpeng Cui, Xiaoli Wang, Guowei Wang, Shuai Li, Qingxing Su
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems are important tools for early breast cancer screening. To address challenges in multi-scale breast tissue structures and blurred lesion boundaries, this paper proposes a Multi-Branch Dual-Attention Network (MBDA-Net) for breast cancer histopathological image classification. The model integrates a Multi-Branch Convolution Module (MBCM) for multi-scale feature extraction, embeds Coordinate Attention (CA) in residual blocks for spatial awareness, incorporates CBAM in bottlenecks for enhanced channel-spatial responses, and uses Dual Attention Loss to guide lesion focus. On BreakHis, MBDA-Net achieves accuracies of 98.78%, 97.32%, 96.76%, and 98.30% at 40×, 100×, 200×, and 400× magnifications. Visualizations confirm accurate lesion focus, and t-SNE analysis shows strong inter-class separation and intra-class aggregation, demonstrating excellent performance for automated breast cancer diagnosis.

title: Retraction Notice: A 3-D Magnetometer-Aided Low-Field Electromagnetic Tracking System for Clinical Surgery Applications
authors: Oluwole John Famoriji, Thokozani Shongwe
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Magnetic field distortions caused by metal objects or other magnetic materials interferes with the accuracy of high-field electromagnetic (EM) tracking systems. Additionally, the effective range of the EM field or the working volume may be limited. In this study, a low-power generator and magnetic sensors exhibiting high-performance are introduced as a substitute for high-field electromagnetic tracking systems. To generate magnetic field gradients that uniquely encode each spatial point, magnetic fields are varied over three locations. These gradients are detected using millimeter-sized sensors with quality resolution, and are able to measure their local magnetic fields with accuracy. The sensors are integrated into surgical instruments (e.g. catheters and brain electrodes). By utilizing a low-field generator and low power consumption, the incorporation of electromagnetic systems in surgical rooms is significantly improved. Using advanced 3D-axis magnetoresistive sensors, the system achieves a mean absolute error of 3 mm at a distance of 42 cm from the field generator, thereby enabling precise and orientation-independent spatial encoding. Following sensor calibration procedure, localization along the Z-axis showed substantial improvement. The developed low-field EM tracking system, which does not require a line of sight is ideal for real-time navigation in complex clinical environments.

title: Empirical Evaluation of Situational Awareness and Its Cognitive Correlates Using EEG
authors: Vishal Pandey, Nikhil Panwar, Byung-Gyu Kim, Partha Pratim Roy
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Situational Awareness (SA) is critical for performance in dynamic environments, yet its neurophysiological basis and continuous assessment remain poorly understood. This study presents an integrated behavioral, electrophysiological, and computational investigation of SA and its cognitive correlates using EEG. Thirty participants performed a computerised Situational Awareness Test while 31-channel EEG was recorded, and twelve established EEG indices were computed to quantify attention, engagement, workload, and fatigue. Behavioral analyses showed that SA varied systematically with reaction time, task type, and fatigue, and exhibited stable individual differences across participants. Spectral analyses revealed robust SA related modulations in theta, alpha, beta, and gamma bands, with frontocentral and centroparietal regions emerging as key substrates. Correlation, ANOVA, PCA, and hierarchical regression jointly showed that SA is best described as a composite cognitive state: attention, engagement, workload, and fatigue together explained 69% of the variance in SA performance. For three-class SA classification (Low, Medium, High), we introduce EEG-STORM, a compact deep model that combines spatial projection, multi scale temporal filtering, and an ordinal regression head that respects the ordered structure of SA levels. Under leave-one-subject-out cross validation, EEG-STORM achieved 86.8% accuracy and significantly outperformed EEGNet, DeepConvNet, ShallowConvNet, and BiLSTM baselines. Gradient based saliency analysis highlighted dominant contributions from frontocentral and centroparietal electrodes, consistent with attention and executive control networks. These results provide proof-of-concept that multi-dimensional cognitive dynamics captured by EEG can support accurate, subject independent, three-class SA classification in a controlled laboratory setting, and offer a principled basis for developing adaptive human machine systems.

title: TRUST-Planner: Topology-Guided Robust Trajectory Planner for AAVs With Uncertain Obstacle Spatial-Temporal Avoidance_supp1-3695224.mp4
authors: Jingliang Sun
publish: N/A
abstract: Despite extensive developments in motion planning of autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs), existing frameworks face the challenges of local minima in complex dynamic environments, leading to increased collision risks. To address these challenges, we present TRUST-Planner, a topology-guided hierarchical planner for robust spatial-temporal obstacle avoidance. In the frontend, a dynamic enhanced visible probabilistic roadmap (DEV-PRM) is proposed to explore topological paths for global guidance rapidly. The backend utilizes a uniform terminal-free minimum control polynomial (UTF-MINCO) to enable efficient predictive obstacle avoidance and fast computation. Furthermore, an incremental multibranch trajectory management framework is introduced to enable spatial-temporal topological decision-making, while efficiently leveraging historical information to reduce replanning runtime. Simulation results show that TRUST-Planner outperforms baseline competitors, achieving millisecond-level computation, higher success rates, and faster traversal in tested complex environments. Real-world experiments further validate the feasibility and practicality of the proposed method.

title: A Single Reference-Guided Adaptation of Foundation Model Predictions for High-Performance Image Segmentation
authors: Yizheng Chen, Sheng Liu, Junyan Liu, Lequan Yu, Ruogu Fang, James Zou, Michael Francis Gensheimer, Lei Xing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Foundation models (FMs) in AI have garnered significant attention and demonstrated substantial potential across a broad range of applications. However, applying them to specific domains like biomedical imaging often necessitates further fine-tuning to achieve desirable prediction accuracy. This process typically requires large labeled datasets and substantial computational resources, posing a major barrier to their widespread adoption. Here, we introduce a fundamentally different strategy, called reference-guided adaptation (RGA), which enables ultra-data-efficient and interpretable FM prediction adaptation for a specific testing (a.k.a. inference) sample using only a single training (a.k.a. reference) example. RGA aligns the reference and inference sample by leveraging their semantic relationship and trains a lightweight refinement model to enhance the FM prediction while keeping the FM intact. We demonstrate the potential of the RGA framework through a series of medical image segmentation studies in different anatomical sites using three popular FMs: SAM, MedSAM, and SAM2. Our results indicate that RGA helps narrow the performance gap between generic FM predictions and task-specific segmentation requirements under limited-data settings by effectively leveraging task-specific knowledge from a single reference, addressing the “last-mile” challenge in FM deployment and paving the way for ultra-data-efficient and explainable AI modeling.

title: AgenticOTA: An Agentic AI Pipeline for Context-Aware over-the-Air Update Deployments in Connected Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Shikhar Vashistha, Neetesh Kumar
publish: IEEE Transactions on Services Computing
abstract: Connected Autonomous Vehicles (CAVs) rely on software code for functioning, which requires continuous maintenance and real-time over-the-air (OTA) updates to ensure optimal performance in dynamically changing environments. However, current OTA update systems operate on pre-defined schedules or reactive diagnostics, failing to adapt deployment strategies based on contextual insights such as subsystem performance degradation and user demands. Furthermore, existing approaches lack support for on-demand decision-making across heterogeneous CAV stacks, treating vehicle software as a monolith and limiting fine-grained interventions at individual functional modules. To address these limitations, this work presents AgenticOTA, a novel agentic AI pipeline for context-aware OTA update deploy ments in CAVs. To the best of the authors' knowledge, this is the first method that enables context-aware, fine-grained, and autonomous OTA deployments in CAVs. AgenticOTA utilizes LLM-driven agent integration to autonomously select validated container images and determine deployment viability based on real-time contextual information. The pipeline implements a modular, fine-grained software management layer that enables targeted updates and the selective replacement of individual functional modules in the event of failures, through containerized microservices, without impacting the entire software system. The effectiveness of AgenticOTA is demonstrated through comprehensive experiments against the state-of-the-art baselines. Experimental results demonstrate significant improvements, including a higher update success rate with fewer failures and rollbacks, as well as an orchestration initiation latency of 0.008 seconds, with a full end-to-end latency of 2.34s, across baseline methods.

title: Impacts of AIS Temporal Asynchrony on False-Negative Risk in CPA-Based Near-Miss Detection
authors: Jinwan Park
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Automatic Identification System (AIS) data are widely used for maritime traffic monitoring and near-miss detection. Most detection approaches based on the closest point of approach (CPA) implicitly assume that AIS reports from different vessels are temporally synchronized. However, in real maritime environments, AIS messages are often received at irregular intervals. Despite this, the influence of AIS temporal asynchrony on the reliability of CPA-based near-miss detection has not been systematically investigated. This study investigates how temporal asynchrony in AIS reporting affects CPA-based near-miss detection using real-world AIS encounter data. The distribution of time differences between AIS reports of vessel pairs was analyzed, and detection outcomes were evaluated under multiple temporal synchronization thresholds (DTMAX) through encounter-level analysis. The results show that AIS report time differences exhibit a long-tailed distribution ranging from several seconds to several minutes. To clarify the evaluation framework, false-negative near-miss events are defined as encounters detected under the baseline condition without temporal filtering and missed under DTMAX filtering. Detection experiments indicate that stricter temporal synchronization constraints lead to an increase in missed detections. For example, under a moderate CPA threshold (0.5 nm, 30 min), the false-negative rate relative to the baseline condition increases to approximately 36% when DTMAX = 60 s, but decreases to about 5% when DTMAX = 240 s. These findings demonstrate that AIS temporal asynchrony is a critical factor affecting the reliability of CPA-based near-miss detection under real-world irregular reporting conditions. The results further indicate that DTMAX = 240 s provides a practical reference point, maintaining 94.2–95.7% detection reliability depending on the CPA threshold. Incorporating temporal synchronization into AIS-based detection algorithms is therefore essential for improving the reliability of maritime traffic monitoring, navigational decision-support systems, and Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships (MASS).

title: Average-Reward Baseline Centering: A Plug-in Stabilizer for DDPG and MADDPG in C-V2X Platooning
authors: Vasimbhai Vohra, Vinay Thumar, Hiren Mewada, Sagar Patel
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Fast and reliable data exchange is essential for connected and autonomous vehicles, particularly in platooning scenarios where vehicles travel in close formation and depend on timely updates for safety and traffic efficiency. Actor–critic reinforcement learning can exhibit unstable learning signals and suboptimal policies under dynamic vehicular network conditions, especially in continuing (non-episodic) resource-allocation tasks, where slot-level fast fading, bursty CAM arrivals, and mode-switching between V2I and V2V links amplify reward variance. We propose a plug-and-play Baseline-Centered Target (BCT) update for DDPG-family methods. The core idea is straightforward: before forming each temporal-difference target, a sliding-window estimate of the long-run average reward is subtracted from the sampled reward, with no other change to the training loop. Theoretically, BCT preserves the fixed point of the Bellman equation and reduces TD target variance without biasing the actor update. BCT is evaluated across four coordination paradigms—centralized DDPG, independent-learning DDPG (IL-DDPG), CTDE MADDPG, and Hybrid MADDPG—each paired with its corresponding BCT variant, with TD3 as a single-agent reference. BCT variants consistently improve steady-state global reward across all paradigms (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$+11.5\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$+26.0\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and produce more stable training curves. In the Centralized paradigm, BCT and TD3 achieve comparable gains (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$+17.9\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$+19.0\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> respectively), confirming the mechanisms are complementary. In the Independent paradigm, IL-TD3 degrades below its base while BCT-IL-DDPG still improves by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$+11.5\%$</tex-math></inline-formula>, demonstrating BCT's robustness under multi-agent non-stationarity. Results are validated across multiple random seeds and window sizes (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$W \in \lbrace 5,\ldots,20\rbrace$</tex-math></inline-formula>), and an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N_\rm{RB}$</tex-math></inline-formula> ablation (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lbrace 3, 6\rbrace$</tex-math></inline-formula>) confirms BCT-Hybrid retains its advantage, with mean AoI dropping <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$83\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N_\rm{RB}=6$</tex-math></inline-formula>.
