title: Multi-Objective Path Planning and Energy Efficiency Optimization Method for Industrial Robot Cooperative System
authors: Sitan Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: Aiming at the collaborative optimization problems of efficiency, safety and energy consumption faced by industrial robot collaborative systems in complex scenarios, this study proposes a multi-objective path planning and energy efficiency optimization method. A multi-objective path planning model is constructed, and the improved NSGA-II algorithm is used to solve the Pareto optimal solution with the goal of task completion time, path safety and collision avoidance. A hierarchical energy efficiency optimization model is designed, in which the motion parameters are collected in real time through the energy consumption monitoring layer, the trajectory is adjusted by the dynamic energy consumption function in the path optimization layer, and the balance between energy efficiency and efficiency is realized by the multi-objective particle swarm optimization algorithm in the decision control layer. The experimental results show that the success rate of path planning is increased from 76.5% to 94.5%, the system energy consumption is reduced by 18.9%, the production efficiency is increased by 21.6%, the operating cost is reduced by 15.3%, and the return on investment cycle is shortened by 37.2%. The research effectively solves the multi-objective collaborative optimization problem of industrial robot collaboration system, which provides theoretical basis and technical support for the green and efficient development of intelligent manufacturing.

title: Research on Braking System Control Method for Electric Logistics Vehicles Based on Energy Recovery Optimization
authors: Qiliang Luo, Min Cai, Yu Wang, Jiangchuan Zhang, Shaofeng Yin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Facing the fact that utilization rate of regenerative braking during braking process of electric delivery vehicle is limited by vehicle speed, battery state of charge and tire adhesion condition, this study discusses a braking system control method based on energy recovery. Braking energy flow model and dynamic braking force allocation model of electric delivery vehicle are established. On this basis, a model predictive control method is introduced, in which energy loss, slip rate deviation and variation of braking force are included in the objective function. Recursive least squares method is used to realize online identification of key control parameter in the process of braking. Braking process with different load, initial speed and state of charge are tested on real-vehicle experimental platform. Research results show that when the method proposed in this paper is used, the electric delivery vehicle can get enhanced energy recovery efficiency while satisfying requirement of braking stability. Under typical conditions, the recovery rate reaches up to 39.1 %, with braking distance reduced to 29.8 m and peak slip rate controlled at 0.184, demonstrating improved coordinated performance. These results indicate that the proposed method provides an effective solution for integrated energy recovery and stability control, contributing to intelligent braking system optimization within advanced electric vehicle technologies.

title: PSO-Optimized CNN-LSTM Model for Efficient Early Diabetes Prediction Using Feature Selection
authors: Anu, D. Mangala Gauri, Kaushinder Singh Raghav, Sanjeev Kumar Prasad, Harnit Saini
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Accurate and timely diagnosis of diabetes is essential to treatment and improved patient outcomes. This study shows a learning algorithm where Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is integrated with a Convolutional Long Short-Term Memory network to enhance prediction accuracy by the use of feature selection. Diabetes Health Indicators Dataset of Kaggle is used to test the method. With PSO, it is possible to select a sub-set of features, thus yielding a model with less complexity and the same predictive power. The achieved experimental results demonstrate that the PSO-enhanced CNNLSTM model has AUC of 0.87 when using 12 features. It outperforms the baseline in the precision, recall and aggregate accuracy. The results presented herein demonstrate that a combination of optimization techniques and deep learning can significantly improve the medical diagnosis system and can enable a scalable and effective system to detect early diabetes in clinical environments in real-time..

title: Deep Learning Approaches for Hindi Text Summarization and Offline Signature Verification: A Systematic Review with Dataset-Level Performance Analysis
authors: Tejinder Kaur, S.L. Jany Shabu, J. Refonaa, N. Anusha, Pooja Chaudhary, Jotesh Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The amount of advances made in automatic text summarization are many. However, much of this work is for high resource languages (e.g., English). Therefore, many low resource languages (i.e., Hindi) have seen very little development with respect to summarization. In particular, the language characteristics of Hindi (e.g., free word order, extensive morphology, limited availability of training data), present significant barriers to developing effective and reliable summarization systems. Research has employed a variety of extraction, abstraction and hybrid approaches, including statistical/graph based techniques as well as neural/transformer based techniques such as BERT, BART, mBART and IndicBART. Despite these advances, the performance of the summarizers is frequently limited due to the lack of training data and/or poorly designed evaluation methodologies. This article provides an overview and analysis of recent developments in Hindi text summarization. Additionally, it outlines three new lines of research: linguistically-informed fine tuning, multi-lingual transformer-based modeling, and the design of Hindi-specific semantic evaluation metrics. Finally, the authors note that the key to developing robust, semantically faithful, Hindi text summarization systems lies in addressing linguistic diversity, evaluation limitations, and adapting systems to specific domains.

title: Global Optimal Solution Search Method for Non-convex Optimization Problems Based on Deep Learning
authors: Wenzheng Zhao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Local extrema and saddle points in non-convex optimization problems severely restrict the efficiency and accuracy of global optimal solution search. To address this, a hybrid solution method is proposed. The sampling step size is dynamically adjusted based on local curvature to generate high-quality training data covering global terrain features. Residual convolution and a multi-head self-attention network are used to map the contour map into a candidate solution distribution. A ranking contrast loss and variational lower bound loss are introduced to jointly train the network, using a low-energy region-priority supervision signal to guide the generated solution to approximate the global optimal basin. Finally, the truncated Newton method is used to locally refine the candidate solutions. On the Rastrigin, Ackley, and Egg Crate functions, the candidate solutions obtained have the lowest initial objective values, and after local refinement, the absolute error is as low as 8.32E-08, with an average of only 452 function evaluations required for convergence. This study provides an efficient and robust new intelligent solution approach for large-scale nonlinear optimization problems.

title: Adaptive Lightweight Convolution-Based Algorithm for Strip Steel Surface Defect Detection
authors: HongFeng Huang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Existing methods for strip steel surface defect detection face challenges in computational complexity, accuracy and real-time performance. To tackle these issues, this paper proposes an adaptive lightweight convolution module called AdaConv, aiming to achieve a balanced improvement in these three aspects. It uses a dimension-aware mechanism to dynamically switch between depthwise convolution and partial convolution in its main branch. It also adds low-cost linear operations to supplement feature information. This design cuts down computational cost significantly while boosting representational capacity. Based on AdaConv, this paper restructures the network architecture of YOLOv5 and incorporates the EIoU loss function to enhance bounding box regression, resulting in the Ada-YOLO. This paper also applies channel pruning with BatchNorm scaling factors to remove redundant structures. Experiments conducted on the NEU-DET dataset indicate that the pruned Ada-YOLO attains an mAP50 of 87.1% with only 2.05M parameters and 7.2 GFLOPs, corresponding to reductions of 74.3% and 66.4%, respectively, compared to the baseline model. The proposed model significantly reduces model complexity while maintaining high detection accuracy and real-time performance, offering an effective solution for defect detection in edge deployment scenarios.

title: Automated Brain Tumor Classification in MRI Images Using a Phased Fine-Tuning VGG19 Framework
authors: Neetu Faujdar, Davesh Kumar Sharma, Richa Sharma, Pooja Baliyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The most common technique used by experts to detect brain tumors is MRI scans. Therefore, it is essential to design the process that could help us to identify the brain tumors in the early stages rather than waiting for end results. In major issue with the existing approaches is the overfitting to overcome this challenge the model is designed with custom dense layer and has dropout regularization. The model's learning capacity is driven by approximately 140.9 million trainable parameters, capturing a wide range of spatial features. The training dataset consists of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 0 8 4}$</tex> tumor-positive and 980 tumor-negative images, which were augmented to address class imbalance. The definition of the loss function was done using categorical cross entropy, the model was trained and improved using stochastic gradient descent (SGD), yielding encouraging tumor detection results. This method establishes the foundation for upcoming advancements in automated radiological diagnosis and demonstrates the potential of transfer learning to advance automated medical imaging. From the results it can be identified that the VGG19 is having comparative good results in comparison to VGG16 and Resnet.

title: Super-Resolution Brain MRI Tumor Images Using Deep Learning for Improved Tumor Assessment and Management
authors: Natansh Kapil, Gaurav Agrawal, Manya, Rani Pal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Medical imaging plays a vital role in identifying and treating brain tumors, but MRI (Magnetic resonance imaging) scans are very limited by resolution. In cases where the image is low-res, all the most important details become blurred and it becomes very difficult for doctors to see tiny tumor spots or even those tricky structures in clear view. This can affect the accuracy of diagnosis and delay the treatment of the patient. The idea behind super-resolution is essentially to make MRI scans sharper and finer, in order that radiologists and automated systems can more readily diagnose something. In simple words, it is about turning ’unclear’ scans (low-resolution scans) into ‘clear’ ones (high-resolution scans) which can help the doctors to make their decision better and efficiently. Scanned Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) images are very difficult to analyze. For radiologists, diagnosing the brain tumors and identifying the exact shape from the low-resolution (LR) clinical scans are difficult, as these scans have a lot of variation and lack exact anatomical data which make them significantly different. This limitation can impact diagnosis and treatment planning. With AI (specifically deep learning) we will be able to enhance the resolution of MRI images. To deal with this, we have developed a new dual-path structure, which is based on 3D Latent Diffusion Models to perform medical super-resolution. It can maintain a good balance between the accuracy and speed, unlike the common methods, the fly between a diffusion-based inversion when the data of interest becomes super sparse and a simple decoder optimization when the sparsity becomes less than before.

title: A Smart Industrial Motor Protection System Based on Real-Time Electrical Parameter Monitoring
authors: Amit Kumar Saroj, Pooja Anand, Pranay Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Industrial motors are an important application in manufacturing industries as they are largely utilized in pumps, conveyors, compressors, and manufacturing machines. The constant operation of these motors in industrial environments results in a situation of overload current flow, overheating of the motor, a condition of high/low voltage to the motor, as well as a condition of a missing phase. Failure to detect these problems from the start can cause significant motor damage and financial loss. This research-based paper presents the development of a smart motor protection system that offers monitoring and automatic protection of industrial motors from faults. The proposed system is designed to continuously monitor important motor parameters like current flow to the motor, voltages supplied to the motor, and temperatures of the motor. The microcontroller receives data from the sensor and matches the information against set safe operating levels. In instances of an abnormal situation such as overload, overcurrent, or high temperature, the system automatically cuts off the motor supply by means of a relay system to prevent any further damage. In contrast to the traditional methods that require manual intervention or relay systems for protection, the designed system has advantages of fast response and high reliability. The smart protection system has display indicators for visual notifications of any abnormal situation. The experimental results prove that the system can effectively protect the motor under various conditions of faults and provide safe operating conditions. The cost-effective smart motor protection system for industries has been designed that is easy to implement for small to mediumscale industries. Further advancements can be made to increase the efficiency of industries by implementing the system based on IoT/predictive maintenance methods to provide enhanced safety at industries.

title: Hybrid EWT-VMD for Fault Feature Extraction and Intelligent Diagnosis of Rolling Bearings
authors: Zihao Yan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Aiming at rolling bearing fault diagnosis, this paper presents a hybrid approach that integrates Empirical Wavelet Transform (EWT) and Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD). As key components in rotor systems, rolling bearings directly affect equipment safety and operational efficiency. Bearing failures can lead to severe accidents. Although methods such as Empirical Mode Decomposition, VMD, and Fourier transform are widely used for fault feature extraction, systematic studies combining wavelet analysis and post-mode decomposition under heavy-load conditions (e.g., marine shaft systems) remain scarce. Accordingly, the proposed method employs EWT to perform adaptive frequency-band division of vibration signals, followed by VMD for secondary processing to improve fault feature separation and identification. Key formulas are presented, including EWT boundary division, wavelet construction, and the VMD constrained variational model. Experiments are conducted using the multisource bearing fault data-set from Jiangnan University, with vibration signals from N205 bearings at 800 rpm and a 50 kHz sampling frequency. It is verified that the proposed approach effectively identifies the fault characteristic frequency of the bearing outer race. Quantitative evaluation using time-frequency indicators such as margin factor and spectral kurtosis demonstrates significant improvements compared with the single EWT method, with the margin factor increasing by 253.3%. This demonstrates the effectiveness and superiority of the presented approach in strengthening fault characteristics and early fault detection. To sum up, this work provides a reliable and efficient technical solution for rolling bearing fault diagnosis under heavy-load conditions and offers valuable theoretical and practical references for the development of autonomous control systems in industrial equipment, which is highly consistent with the theme of the 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control and contributes to the advancement of related technical fields covered by the conference.

title: Research And Application of Artificial Intelligence Machine Learning and Deep Learning
authors: Yinshan Jiang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In the era of rapid digital economy development, Artificial Intelligence (AI) has emerged as a pivotal force driving industrial transformation and reshaping production and lifestyle. Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL), as key technological pathways for AI implementation, exhibit both hierarchical interdependencies and distinct technical frameworks with specialized application scenarios. This paper first defines the core concepts of AI, ML, and DL, clarifying their "holistic-local-subset" logical relationshi [6][10]. It then traces their developmental trajectories, analyzing the technical principles, core algorithms, and key differences between ML and DL [1][2]. Furthermore, it explores practical integration applications across multiple industries based on current technological implementations [7]. Finally, the study identifies existing bottlenecks in technological advancement and forecasts future trends, providing valuable insights for research and application in related fields.

title: A Hybrid Supervised Machine Learning Model for Extraction and Classification of Soil Moisture Index on Sentinel-1 Images
authors: Ajay Yadav, Mukund Pratap Singh, Anurag Goswami
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Soil moisture acts as an important indicator of surface water availability. It impacts the exchange of energy and mass between land surface and atmosphere. This study presents a hybrid ensembling technique to estimate the soil moisture index. Using the advanced features of Google Earth Engine, this research analyzes multi-temporal Sentinel-1 images obtained in vertical transmit and vertical receive polarization through Interferometric Wide mode for the time frame from January 2017 to December 2025. The study area, Prayagraj, India, was chosen due to its varied land and climate conditions, which provide a strong environment for assessing the model. The Random Forest and Classification and Regression Tree models evaluated separately showed classification accuracy of 97.79% and 97.94%, respectively. When integrated through an ensemble framework, the hybrid model reached a maximum accuracy of 98.78%. The results highlight the model's ability to effectively recognize changes in soil moisture over space and time, validating its use in agriculture and environmental observations.

title: Code-Mixing and Code-Switching Language Identification for English-Punjabi Word-Level
authors: Ramanjeet Kaur Dhaliwal, Sukhwant Kaur, Sharnjeet Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Code-mixing is now a natural phenomenon,and Punjabi and English code-mixing in Roman script is widely used on social media,this has created significant challenges in language identification, it is difficult for computers because of the presence of informal writing format styles and spelling variations. To address this gap, we constructed a word-level language identification. This study addresses code-mixed and code-switch text of Punjabi-English using machine learning and transformer-based models. Four models Logistic Regression with TF-IDF, a BiLSTM model, and two transformer models XLM-R and mBERT were evaluated. The experimental results show that Transformer- based models significantly outperform traditional approaches, XLM-R achieved the highest accuracy of 94.1 %, mBERT with 92.6 %, while Logistic Regression and BilSTM achieved 84.2 % and 88.9 % respectively. And strong performance for both languages English and Punjabi tokens class-wise analysis has shown strong performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6. 0 \%}$</tex> and 92.0 % using XLM-R. Even the simpler models performed reasonably, however, worse than others in the face of the irregular spelling of Punjabi words. Overall, the results show that sub-word based transformers are highly effective for handling noisy and irregular code-mixed text. and this work provides one baseline and practical insights for solid groundwork on future research on Punjabi-English natural language processing field.

title: Mechanism and Numerical Analysis of Limited-Frequency-Diversity Mitigation of Radial Detection Blind Zones in Ku-Band Radar under Evaporation Duct Conditions
authors: Yan Zhang, Tian Zhang, Liumei Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Evaporation ducts can significantly alter near-surface electromagnetic propagation and affect over-the-horizon detection of low-altitude targets. To address the problem of radial blind zones in Ku-band radar under evaporation duct conditions, this paper investigates a mitigation method based on limited frequency diversity (LFD) and investigates its mechanism and effectiveness using a parabolic-equation model. Comparative simulations under a unified strong evaporation-duct baseline scenario show that the Ku band exhibits the most pronounced near-surface energy trapping. Further analysis indicates that the deep fades caused by multimode coherent superposition under single-frequency operation are sensitive to frequency variation, thereby providing local complementarity for LFD. Simulations using a representative three-tone configuration of 17.8, 18.0, and 18.2 GHz show that the synthesized envelope can reduce blind-zone extent relative to single-frequency operation. Scans over the number of tones and frequency spacing show that the mitigation performance depends on both parameters, and that the three-tone configuration with a 200 MHz spacing can be regarded as a representative compromise between mitigation gain and implementation complexity. Continuity-oriented detectability metrics and sensitivity analyses further show that LFD improves both blind-zone extent and range-domain detectability continuity under the tested conditions.

title: Research on an Intelligent Detection Method for Peanut Quality Based on Improved YOLOv11
authors: Xinyue Dou, Ru Jiang, Qian Li, Nadirbek Yusupbekov, Azizbek Yusupbekov
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Aiming at the demand for rapid and accurate identification of defects such as mildew and breakage in peanut quality detection, this paper proposes an intelligent detection method based on improved YOLOv11n. This method adopts the ADown module to replace the traditional convolutional layers in the backbone network to enhance the feature extraction capability for small targets and fine details; proposes the PstarBlock to replace the Bottleneck structure in the C3k2 module to retain the key features of peanut kernels; and introduces the Dynamic head detection head to improve the detection accuracy in complex sorting environments. Experiments on a public peanut dataset show that the mean average precision (mAP) of the improved model reaches 93.8%, which is 3.5% higher than that of the original YOLOv11n and outperforms mainstream models such as YOLOv5n, YOLOv8n and YOLOv10n. This research provides an effective technical solution for the accurate and rapid detection of peanut quality.

title: Problem-Oriented Enterprise Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework
authors: Panyue Lv, Tongxin Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are developing rapidly and have demonstrated remarkable performance in dialogue and reasoning tasks, with important applications across various domains. Faced with vast and complex data, enterprises need to quickly and accurately extract the information they require. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) technology enables LLMs to leverage external knowledge, enhancing their performance on knowledge-intensive tasks. However, applying RAG to enterprise knowledge bases presents challenges, including complex document processing, low retrieval recall, limited answer formats, and poor answer quality. This study aims to address these issues. Based on an in-depth analysis of existing approaches, we introduce an improved RAG framework that incorporates a question classification module to handle different query types, replaces single-method retrieval with hybrid retrieval, and adds a Re-ranking module to significantly improve recall. During retrieval and generation, prompt engineering is employed to create templates that enhance the model’s comprehension of diverse question types and elevate answer quality. Additionally, a high-quality, expert-curated dataset consisting of question–answer pairs derived from enterprise documents was constructed. A series of experiments demonstrates that the proposed method achieves outstanding results.

title: Design of an Attribute Inference Attack Mitigation Algorithm for Sentiment Analysis Tasks in Federated Learning
authors: Hao Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: We, therefore, suggest a mitigation algorithm based on differential privacy and adversarial defense to prevent attribute inference attacks for sentiment analysis tasks during federated learning. Introducing perturbation of noises and attribute masking schemes locally at the client, the algorithm efficiently alleviates the risk of attackers guessing private attributes yet still ensures model performance of sentiment classification. Experimental results are indicative of how, compared to traditional schemes, our suggested algorithm immensely decreases attribute inference accuracy and shows fine resistance for environments of non-IID data. Optimizing techniques of gradient clipping along with automatic adjustment of noises enhance efficiency of training, strike a better balance between protecting privacy and model performance, and serve a stable solution for utilizing federated learning for applications of a sensitive nature regarding privacy.

title: Deep Learning Architectures for Enhanced Breast Cancer Detection: A Comprehensive Analysis of Multimodal Diagnostic Systems
authors: Simran Chugwani, Ajay Shriram Kushwaha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the most prevalent cancers and a leading cause of death worldwide in women. Most studies have shown that early detection is critical to improve treatment success and survival outcomes. Routine diagnostic imaging tools such as mammography, ultrasound, and magnetic resonance imaging provide important but limited information that often results in challenges to obtain complete and accurate diagnosis. Current studies have been examining multimodal strategies that combine data streams from different sources, including imaging, histopathological slides, genomic data, and clinical features, to improve early detection accuracy. Literature shows that these integrated strategies improve sensitivity, specificity, and diagnostic confidence; however, the studies reviewed here indicate persistent problems of data heterogeneity, limited publicly available, well-annotated datasets, inconsistent fusion methods, and limited clinical validation. This review summarizes existing methods, relative performance results, and most significant limitations of currently available multimodal models in breast cancer detection and highlights the emerging trends toward standardized data preparation, strong feature fusion processes, and user friendly interpretability, that may enable more accurate and clinically useful diagnostic solutions for breast cancer in the future.

title: AniTraxion: A Machine Learning-Based Framework for Real-Time Animal Detection and Collision Prevention on Smart Highways
authors: Himanshu Tyagi, Anju Bhatt, Reema Rallan, Shalini Verma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Highway animal-vehicle collisions represent a significant risk to the well-being of both humans and wildlife, highlighting the need for effective real-time animal detection systems that can enhance highway environments through the implementation of a smart highway system. This paper will discuss AniTraxion, a new machine learning based framework developed using the Enhanced Precision YOLO (EP-YOLO) model to assist in the accurate and efficient detection of animals to prevent collision with vehicles. In order to provide greater accuracy in detecting small and fast moving animals in a wide variety of environmental conditions, AniTraxion has incorporated three lightweight modules, including: the Detail Amplification Module (DAM), the Adaptive Scale Integration Module (ASIM), and the Contextual Noise Reduction Module (CNRM). The design of AniTraxion is based on a comprehensive literature review conducted in order to identify specific deficiencies within existing object detection frameworks, which led to the unique and innovative design of AniTraxion as a solution to the challenges associated with smart highways. The framework is capable of meeting its target mean Average Precision (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex>) of 0.80 as well as being able to achieve an inference speed of at least 60 Frames Per Second on resource-constrained edge devices, thus enabling interoperability within a real-time smart highway environment. The ability of AniTraxion to provide a scalable and high-performance solution to reduce the number of animal-vehicle collisions demonstrates that it represents a significant contribution to the development of safer and smarter transportation infrastructures.

title: Adaptive Multi-objective Interactive Decision-making Method for Underwater Communication Task Allocation
authors: Jingping Wu, Dong Yan, Hui Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Marine technological innovation is fundamental to the development of a maritime nation, and enhancing underwater communication capabilities is a prerequisite for the utilization of marine resources. Current approaches to underwater communication task allocation face several challenges, including the limited adaptability of static planning methods, insufficient quantitative support from intelligent algorithms, and deficiencies in interactive decision-making mechanisms. To address these issues, this study establishes a quantifiable mathematical model for underwater communication scenarios, proposes an adaptive improved multi-objective NSGA-III algorithm, and conducts tests on a simulation platform. The results show that the proposed model effectively characterizes the multidimensional constraints inherent in underwater communication environments. The improved algorithm outperforms traditional methods in both convergence speed and solution diversity, meeting real-time performance and reliability requirements for underwater communication task allocation. This research provides theoretical foundations and practical references for related fields.

title: Sign Language Detection in Real Time Using Computer Vision and Deep Learning
authors: Harsh Singh Pundhir, Farhan Khalid, Aaquib Akhtar, Aditya Kumar, Anju Gera, Amit Pratap Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The area of research concerned with the detection of sign language through computer vision has acquired considerable importance because of the increase in the necessity for communication technologies that are inclusive and accessible. Sign language is the main means of communication for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing, communication barriers exist with the non signers and between the signers. The developments in deep learning and computer vision have paved the way for the creation of advanced camera based systems for sign language detection that are completely independent of wearable sensors or specialized equipment. In this paper, we describe a computer vision based framework for real time sign language detection that employs deep learning techniques. The construction of the presented system is based on a systematic flow that comprises image acquisition, preprocessing, feature extraction, gesture classification, and output interpretation. The results of the experiments demonstrate that convolutional neural network based models yield excellent accuracy for the recognition of static hand gestures and provide dependable performance for dynamic ones even in real time applications.

title: Research on the Numerical Prediction Method of Output Characteristics and Life of 500kV Main Transformers for Typical Pumped Storage Power Stations Based on k-Means Algorithm and Grey Prediction Model
authors: Jiande Huang, Feng Zhao, Jianfeng Chen, Qin Chen, Bin Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: The paper analyze the output characteristic curve of hydro-power station, and use the 8760 hours of data from the certain hydro-power station to clarify a characteristics of hydro-power output, in order to determine the operating characteristics of the supporting pumped storage power station. The output characteristic curve of the pumped storage power station presents richer features, indicating that the curve exhibits multi peak characteristics within 365 days of the year, and the peak area of the curve has the significant impact on insulation life of the 500kV transformer. Lifespan of the transformer can be characterized by the Cole-Cole diagram, and the internal core temperature of the transformer can be quantitatively analyzed by the Cole-Cole diagram. Spectral data fitting was performed on the original data within the temperature range of a 70° C to a 110° C. Experimental data curve and the fitted curve are in good agreement, and there is deviation in the fitted data in the low and high frequency regions. This experimental phenomenon indicates that lifespan of 500kV energy storage power station transformer deteriorates rapidly under the high temperature conditions, while in higher frequency range, especially <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10^{6}\ \text{Hz}$</tex>, actual measurement data can better represent the actual operating status of the transformer. As pressurization time varies from 10 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 0 0}$</tex> seconds, internal insulation material of the transformer exhibits an average charge density around 100 seconds, suddenly rising step by step to 3 coulombs per cubic meter, and then maintaining the stable value of charge density.
