title: A Modal-Adaptive Twomey Algorithm for Retrieval of Aerosol Hygroscopic Growth Factor Probability Density Function
authors: Huiping Lu, Jiaoshi Zhang, Jianguo Liu, Huaqiao Gui
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Retrieving the aerosol hygroscopic growth factor probability density function (GF-PDF) from humidified tandem differential mobility analyzer (HTDMA) measurements is a typical ill-posed inverse problem that is highly sensitive to measurement noise. The conventional Twomey iterative method suppresses noise by applying a fixed three term moving average to the initial least-squares solution. However, this uniform smoothing strategy reduces the retrieval accuracy for unimodal distributions with sharp spectral peaks. In this study, a modal-adaptive (MA) strategy is proposed to dynamically adjust the smoothing strength according to the morphological features of the instrument response. Specifically, smoothing is disabled for unimodal responses, whereas the conventional three term moving average is retained for multimodal responses. A large synthetic dataset containing approximately 27,000 samples was constructed, including unimodal, bimodal, and trimodal GF-PDF with realistic Poisson and Gaussian noise. Quantitative comparisons among GF-PDF inversion methods are conducted, including least squares (LSQ), two Tikhonov regularization variants (L-curve and Hanke–Raus), and the Twomey methods. The results demonstrate that the proposed MA-Twomey algorithm achieves the best overall trade-off among mean squared error (0.1521), reconstruction residual (0.0966), and GF-PDF smoothness (18.6432) under all modal types and signal-to-noise conditions. In particular, the method remains highly robust under low-SNR scenarios. Results from real-world field data demonstrate that the MA-Twomey method exhibits practical value and applicability.

title: Diffusion Models for Medical Image Translation: A Comprehensive Clustered Review
authors: Yash Handa, Suhani Kumari, Subhash Chand Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Medical image translation plays an important role in improving disease diagnosis, supporting treatment planning, and connecting information from different imaging types. Earlier methods built on GANs and early diffusion models often struggled with high computational demands, slow processing, and loss of anatomical detail. The limitations of the earlier models were mitigated in the more recent diffusion models, which have proven to be more efficient and reliable, especially in the case of smaller or unpaired data sets. The contemporary models place more emphasis on maintaining the simplicity of the model, along with the consistency of the structural characteristics of the original images. Key progress includes the creation of faster and more efficient diffusion techniques, use of frequency-based image representations, memory-friendly 3D models, and better ways to transfer information between different imaging scans. In this study, we group the latest research in medical image translation into six main categories and examine their key ideas, strengths, and weaknesses. The findings indicate that diffusion-based models now offer better efficiency, stronger performance, and improved ability to maintain structural accuracy, making them promising tools for future medical imaging applications.

title: Temporal Hierarchies and Knowledge Graphs for Multi-Modal Video Language Understanding
authors: Hari Om, Anuj Attri, Avinash Kumar Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Multi-modal vision-language models have made significant progress in processing videos but there are still certain limitations. They usually prefer quick clips, struggle with time management and make imprecise decisions. This innovative video-language technique quickly addresses these concerns. This research offers a systematic knowledge-based framework for interpreting lengthy video files and producing clear results. The model employs external knowledge graphs and common sense reasoning to boost its comprehension. This enables it to uncover causal and contextual relationships that may not be immediately apparent. For example, it can explain how one event leads to the next. The model combines visual features and time transitions with simple language descriptions to explain how it reached its results. Diverse tests on a variety of datasets including a new task for answering questions about long movies show that this system surpasses previous multimodal models in terms of narrative reasoning and time consistency. This study highlights the benefits of taking a structured knowledge-based approach to building the next generation of video comprehension systems.

title: An Attribute-Based Searchable Encryption Scheme Based on Bloom Filter Index
authors: Weihui Zeng, Xinliang Zhang, Zhongcheng Wu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The demand for data privacy protection and efficient management in cloud storage environments is growing significantly. To support multi-keyword ciphertext search in secure settings, this paper proposes a Bloom filter index-based multi-keyword attribute-based searchable encryption scheme (BI-ABSE). BI-ABSE first employs a bloom filter to perform positional mapping of keywords, obtaining an initial bit array to reduce index storage overhead. This initial bit array is then permuted to enhance keyword security. Concurrently, attribute-based encryption is utilized to manage the permutation file. An aggregated index based on bloom filter polynomials is generated to improve search efficiency. Only users who pass attribute verification can acquire the permutation file and thereby access the data stored in the cloud. Authorized users also generate polynomial-based trapdoors. Leveraging the modular multiplication property of polynomials, the cloud server performs matching queries on the uploaded trapdoors. Finally, security analysis and performance analysis demonstrate that BI-ABSE achieves both robust security and high efficiency.

title: A Lightweight Group-Wise Attention Framework for Accessible EEG Emotion Recognition
authors: Ce Guo, Shujie Zou, Linkai Zhu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Recent EEG-based emotion recognition studies are dominated by complex temporal, graph, and attention-based architectures, yet many suffer from poor reproducibility due to reliance on restricted datasets, cumbersome preprocessing pipelines, or inconsistent evaluation protocols. This paper addresses this problem from a complementary perspective by exploring whether lightweight models can achieve strong performance on publicly accessible EEG emotion datasets represented as structured feature tables, without relying on expensive sequence modeling. We conduct experiments on the open Feeling Emotions dataset, which contains 2,132 samples, 2,548 handcrafted EEG features, and three emotion classes. We benchmark classical machine learning methods, lightweight neural baselines, dimensionality reduction variants, and our proposed Group-Wise Feature Attention Network with a softmax reweighting module (GFANet-Softmax) over automatically discovered feature groups. Under a unified repeated stratified 5-fold evaluation protocol with three random seeds for all models, Random Forest achieves the highest overall Macro-F1 of 0.9856, while GFANet-Softmax reaches 0.9787 and significantly outperforms a matched MLP baseline (0.9709). Our results demonstrate that lightweight group-wise feature reweighting is a competitive neural strategy for structured public EEG features, whereas PCA and 1D temporal convolutions provide limited benefits in this setting.

title: Crossmamba-Based Soft Robot Constrained Position Recognition with Multimodal Sensing
authors: Zhijia Zhao, Yuxuan Guan, Shouyan Chen, Zhijie Liu, Mingju Wang, Lusen Huang, Shitao Chen, Tao Zou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: This paper discusses the limitations of single-modal sensing in soft robots. It also addresses the dynamic variation of multimodal sensing weights under different working conditions. Then, we propose a constrained position recognition method for soft robots based on the Crossmamba architecture. The method explicitly models the coupling relationships among multimodal data through a full-dimensional state extraction layer. Experiments compare the proposed method with mainstream temporal modeling approaches, including Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTM), Transformer, and Mamba. Results show that the Crossmamba model achieves excellent performance and robustness in constrained position recognition tasks for soft robots. This provides a new direction for multimodal time-series data modeling. Results show that the Crossmamba model achieves a significant improvement in predictive accuracy, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> increased by approximately 3% and RMSE reduced by about 26% compared to the second-best model. This provides a new direction for high-precision multimodal time-series data modeling in constrained environments.

title: RWGCN: A Node Importance Evaluation Algorithm Based on Random Walk and Graph Convolutional Neural Network in Complex Networks
authors: Mi Wang, Ying Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This paper proposes a comprehensive evaluation model based on the fusion of Node2vec and graph convolutional neural network(GCN) to address the problem of traditional node importance assessment methods being difficult to integrate local structural attributes and global topological features of nodes. This method first determines the Node2vec random walk strategy based on node intimacy, and obtains local feature representations of nodes by graph embedding the walk sequence. Subsequently, the feature attention mechanism is introduced into the node feature matrix, and a two-layer graph convolutional neural network is employed to learn higher-order node features, thereby obtaining global feature representations of the nodes. Finally, the local and global features are concatenated along the feature dimension, and the weights are automatically learned by a multi-layer perceptron(MLP) to directly output the node importance sequence. Experiments on four real networks have shown that this method performs well in the evaluation metrics of monotonicity and Kendall correlation coefficient.

title: Geogenic Groundwater Contaminant Source Identification and Interpretation in Durg Block Using PCA and Machine Learning, with Special Reference to Uranium and Fluoride in Groundwater
authors: Pradhumn Soni, Mridu Sahu, D.C. Jhariya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Geogenic contamination of groundwater represents a critical challenge in hard rock and sedimentary basins where water-rock interaction mobilizes naturally occurring toxic ele-ments into drinking water sources. The Durg block is situated in the center of the mesoproterozoic Chhattisgarh Basin in the Central India. A fertile Precambrian basement that con-tains uranium-rich granites forms the unconformity between carbonate- and shale-dominated sedimentary formations. The present study incorporates an integrated hydrogeochemical and machine learning assessment of uranium and fluoride contami-nation in the groundwater of Durg block using pre-monsoon and post-monsoon data from the aquifer management plan, naquim 2.0 report, NCCR, CGWB of the year 2024. A total of seventy-eight samples, collected from hand pumps, bore wells and dug wells, were analyzed for major cations and anions, uranium, fluoride, and aggregate parameters including electrical conduc-tivity, total dissolved solids and total hardness. The mineralization is predominantly of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C a}-\mathbf{M g}-\mathbf{H C O 3}$</tex> type, slightly alkaline. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p H}$</tex> and high mineralization, showing intense interaction with carbonate aquifers. Uranium concentrations range from below detection up to as much as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.42 \mu ~\mathrm{g}-1$</tex>, while most of samples below the World Health Organization guideline of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 ~} \boldsymbol{\mu} \mathbf{~ L} \mathbf{L} \boldsymbol{1}$</tex>. Fluoride concentrations remain within the Bureau of Indian Standards limit of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 5 ~ m g ~ L-1}$</tex>, though moderate seasonal variation does exist. In fact, principal component analysis indicates that the dominant control on groundwater chemistry is carbonate weath-ering in the Chandi Formation limestone, while distinct component is associated with uranium mobilization from the underlying Sambalpur Granitoid basement. Machine learning models, particularly random forest and gradient boosting reggression emphasizes the prominence of structural pathways and buffering of carbonates in uranium distribution, and the role of aquifer lithology and seasonal recharge in modulating fluoride. A weak correlation between uranium and fluoride concentration indicates independent geogenic sources. The study establishes the baseline contamination patterns for Durg block and demonstrates the utility of combining classical hydrogeochemistry with machine learning.

title: A Correlation-Weighted Deep Framework for Multi-Criteria Recommender Systems
authors: Megha Gupta, Abhinav Tomar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: To create a recommender system that is able to account for multiple criteria used to evaluate both users and items, the recommender system must find ways to equivalently balance out the varying degrees to which each of these criteria are weighed and how they ultimately relate to users and user preferences in a nonlinear manner (particularly when there may be limited numbers of available items). The current methodologies being utilized to improve user/item profiles (using textual reviews) have promoted the use of static feature vectors; however, this leads to many of the current approaches failing to take into consideration finer granularity in user and item relationships, which can be detrimental to producing accurate recommendations. Thus, this paper proposes two separate baseline models as a starting point in this direction: the CCSD weighting model and the ADRS which utilizes a DNN approach to learning item-user relationships. While CCSD is based on balancing out multi-criteria ratings, it does so by assessing how the multi- criteria rating tracks together through simultaneous correlation and variability of the multi-criteria rating results of each of the multi-criteria evaluated by the user/item pair. In contrast, the ADRS model captures the nonlinear relationship between the user/item pairs through utilizing autoencoder approaches. The two baseline models were evaluated on the MovieLens ml-latest- small dataset. The two models (CCSD and ADRS) outperformed the latest techniques in both rating prediction and ranking accuracy, according to an evaluation of the trials carried out using them. Evaluation metrics of MAE and RMSE support both model designs as means of bettering the quality of recommendations made to users, as well as providing better personalization.

title: LAP-FNS: A Layered Adaptive Priority-Based Fuzzy Logic System for Intelligent and Energyefficient Autonomous Vacuum Navigation
authors: S. Gowtham, R. Sankarganesh, V. Ravi, K. Sridevi, K. Malathi, S. Divakar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: An intelligent and energy-efficient autonomous vacuum navigation system in dynamic indoor environments can be developed using the Layered Adaptive Priority-Based Fuzzy Logic System LAP-FNS which involves four-layer design consists of modules for perception, navigation, decision-making and energy management. A Mamdani-type fuzzy inference engine is included to enable adaptive responses in real time scenario. To create occupancy grids that make it simple to obstacle avoidance and design routes around dirt, it makes use of proximity sensors, IMU, and LIDAR-based mapping. Contextual elements such as obstacle proximity, battery level or dirt density cause the dynamic Layered Adaptive Priority (LAP) system to change control priority. Several simulations and realworld trials demonstrate that LAP-FNS outperforms PIDFuzzy and DQL-based models in terms of average power savings of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8. 4 \%}$</tex>, cleaning rate up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2. 5 \%}$</tex> and collision evasion rates up to level of 96%. The layered fuzzy control's strength was validated by the dynamic action response's average duration of less than 0.78 seconds. These results highlight the system's capacity based on navigation to strike a balance between environmental efficiency, autonomy and flexibility. The proposed LAP-FNS can be applied both at home and in offices as well as in semi-structured indoor areas. Further research in the future will involve the adoption of vision-based semantic mapping and reinforcement learning to augment the adaptability and human-conscious collaboration in shared living spaces further.

title: MS-CTAB-GAN+: A SHAP-Constrained Conditional Tabular GAN for Heart Disease Data Augmentation under Small-Sample Conditions
authors: Haoye Luo, Zhanfei Ma, Xu Wang, Wenjun Lv, Shuaiqi Ren
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Heart disease clinical datasets frequently suffer from small sample sizes and high missing value rates, which severely restrict predictive model training quality. This paper proposes MS-CTAB-GAN+, a novel conditional tabular generative adversarial network (CTGAN) that augments the CTAB-GAN+ framework with two targeted innovations. First, we integrate miceforest — a LightGBM-based multiple imputation method — to systematically handle missing values while preserving the statistical distribution of both continuous and categorical variables. Second, we introduce a SHAP-Constraint Module consisting of a pre-trained convolutional autoencoder (PreModule) and a fully connected mapping network (MappingModule), which encodes real-data feature importance patterns into the generator's training objective via a differentiable auxiliary loss. Experiments on the UCI Heart Disease dataset demonstrate that downstream classifiers trained on MS-CTAB-GAN+-generated synthetic data consistently outperform those trained on data from CTAB-GAN, CTAB-GAN+, and CTGAN across all tested models and evaluation metrics.

title: Efficient and Accurate Sales Forecasting for E-Retail: Evaluating LSTM, GRU, and Hybrid Approaches
authors: Tamoghna Mukherjee, Adarsh Shukla, Vasudha Vashisht
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Accuracy in sales forecasting is essential for efficient planning and optimal decision making; therefore, various techniques can be used for forecasting sales demand. The work aims to explore various methods and do a comparative analysis on a specific dataset to evaluate how they compare to each other. This evaluation considers simple machine learning techniques such as boosting methods and ensemble methods such as Random Forest. Statistical models like AutoRegressive(AR), MovingAverage(MA) and their variants are also compared including but not limited to ARIMA and SARIMAX. The widespread adoption of neural networks for forecasting poses a question on whether they perform better than traditional machine learning and statistical methods. The scope of this question can be enhanced to also include Hybrid models. This paper explores LSTM, GRU and TCN, and their bidirectional variants. Sequential Hybrid models, mainly LSTM, TCN and GRU-based are also explored, mainly in combination with Statistical methods. Specifically, it is found that the BiGRU-ARIMA demonstrates statistically significant improvements on the explored dataset, verified through a paired T-test with BiGRU as the base model. It is also to note that LSTM, BILSTM and TCN underperform against BiGRUARIMA on the specific dataset chosen

title: LiteSignalNet: Lightweight Hybrid Architecture with Multi-Scale Depthwise Separable Convolutions and Efficient Linear Attention for Real-Time Wireless Signal Classification
authors: Le Zhang, Xiang Zhang, Chunye Gong
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Automatic classification of heterogeneous wireless communication signals is essential for intelligent spectrum management in increasingly congested radio environments. While deep learning has demonstrated remarkable potential in this domain, existing approaches face three key limitations: (1) standard convolutional networks employ single-scale kernels that fail to capture the multi-granularity temporal structures inherent in diverse wireless protocols, from microsecond-level OFDM symbols to millisecond-level frame boundaries; (2) Transformer-based architectures incur quadratic computational complexity with respect to the input sequence length, hindering their deployment on resource-constrained edge devices for real-time spectrum monitoring; and (3) conventional models overlook the varying discriminative importance across feature channels, treating all learned spectral components equally regardless of their relevance to protocol identification. To address these challenges, we propose LiteSignalNet, a lightweight hybrid architecture that integrates three key innovations: (1) a multi-scale depthwise separable convolutional frontend with four parallel branches of kernel sizes 3, 7, 15, and 31 for hierarchical signal feature extraction at drastically reduced parameter cost; (2) an efficient linear attention encoder that replaces the quadratic softmax kernel with an ELU-based feature map, reducing complexity from $\mathcal{O}\left( {{L^2}d} \right)$ to $\mathcal{O}\left( {{L^2}d} \right)$ while preserving global temporal modeling capability; and (3) a squeeze-and-excitation channel attention module that adaptively recalibrates feature channel importance to emphasize protocol-discriminative spectral components. Extensive experiments on a real-world over-the-air dataset comprising Wi-Fi (IEEE 802.11ax), LTE, and 5G-NR signals demonstrate that LiteSignalNet achieves 99.83% classification accuracy with only 118K trainable parameters—approximately 7× fewer than standard Transformer architectures—while maintaining robust performance across varying signal-to-noise ratio conditions.

title: Machine Learning-Based Mixed-Flow Production Sequencing Demand Forecasting for H Semiconductor Company
authors: Libin Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a demand forecasting model based on machine learning for the mixed-flow production of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{H}$</tex> Semiconductor company. By constructing the LSTM-XGBoost hybrid model and combining the genetic algorithm to optimize the parameters, the dynamic sorting under multi-dimensional data fusion is realized. Experiments show that the proposed model is significantly superior to traditional methods in terms of prediction accuracy, sorting stability and computational efficiency. The research of this paper provides a reproducible intelligent decisionmaking framework for the semiconductor industry's mixed-flow production.

title: Delay-Optimal and Secure Digital-Twin-Assisted Federated Learning for Heterogeneous Industrial IoT
authors: Linjun Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The integration of Federated Learning (FL) and Digital Twin (DT) enables secure intelligence in heterogeneous Industrial IoT (IIoT). Conventional FL suffers from stragglers, and DT synchronization is vulnerable to eavesdropping. This paper proposes a cloud-assisted DT–FL framework that jointly optimizes computation, communication, and physical layer security. Strong General Local Devices (GLDs) perform local training and cooperative jamming, while resource-constrained Bad Local Devices (BLDs) offload tasks to cloud DTs. A delay minimization problem under secrecy and energy constraints is formulated and decomposed into tractable subproblems. The resulting scheme optimally allocates CPU rates, NOMA transmit powers, and jamming intensities, ensuring secure DT synchronization. Simulations show significant reduction in FL latency and improved secrecy performance over baseline methods.

title: Research on the Digital Translation Mechanism of College Chorus from the Perspective of Artificial Intelligence
authors: Xiao Liu, Lili Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This study focuses on the digital translation mechanism requirements in the field of choir singing in higher education institutions, and explores key technical issues such as multi voice audio analysis, real-time feedback, and intelligent model optimization. Innovatively adopt distributed collaborative processing architecture, edge computing efficiency improvement strategy and composite training corpus construction method to form a systematic solution. By establishing independent audio acquisition paths, configuring distributed edge computing units, and integrating multi-dimensional feature extraction and adaptive filtering technologies, the separation accuracy and processing efficiency of audio signals are significantly improved. At the same time, a training dataset containing environmental noise features was developed, effectively enhancing the recognition accuracy and adaptability of the algorithm in real singing environments. This research achievement provides a practical and feasible technical path and implementation framework for the intelligent transformation of the choir field.

title: Design of Laser Semi-Active Seeker with APD Quadrant Detector Based on STM32
authors: Boxuan Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: With the rapid development of science and technology, intelligence and precision have become the core development trends of modern weapon systems. Laser semi-active seekers achieve precise strikes through searching and tracking target laser echo signals, demonstrating significant tactical advantages in modern warfare. However, they have high requirements for detecting low-power laser energy. To address this technical challenge, this paper proposes a design scheme of Avalanche Photodiode (APD) quadrant detector-based laser semi-active seeker with ST Microelectronics 32-bit Microcontroller (STM32) microcontroller, aiming to realize efficient detection of low-power laser signals. The core of the design lies in the collaborative architecture of the APD quadrant detector, which enables the stable amplification of low-power laser signals and effectively improves the seeker's sensitivity. Experimental validation results demonstrate that, in contrast to conventional amplification circuits, the proposed design not only confines single-channel noise to below 200 m V but also enables the laser semi-active seeker to achieve a sensitivity threshold of less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex>. This dual achievement markedly enhances both the detection efficiency of low-power laser energy and the output stability of the laser semiactive seeker, thereby furnishing robust technical underpinnings for applications in long-range precision guidance.

title: Infrared Gas Leakage Target Detection Based on Improved YOLOv11n with RFAConv and P2 Detection Head
authors: Xuanjie Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Infrared gas leakage detection plays an important role in industrial safety monitoring. However, leakage targets in infrared images usually exhibit weak texture features, low contrast, blurred contours, and small target scales, which make accurate target detection difficult under complex backgrounds. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv11n-based method for infrared gas leakage target detection. The proposed method introduces an RFAConv module to enhance local feature extraction and improve the representation of weak leakage patterns, and adds a P2 detection head to retain higher-resolution shallow features for small-target perception. Experiments conducted on an infrared gas leakage dataset show that, compared with the baseline model, the proposed method improves Precision, Recall, mAP@0.5, and mAP@0.5:0.95 by 8.64, 3.90, 8.80, and 3.39 percentage points, respectively. The results demonstrate that the proposed improvements can effectively enhance the detection performance for weak and small gas leakage targets in infrared imagery, showing good potential for practical industrial inspection applications.

title: Research and Application of CSI-Based Fingerprint Localization Using Random Forest Regression for Indoor Mobile Robots
authors: Hong Li, Chicong Deng, Xiaoping Liang, Xin Liao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) and Channel State Information (CSI) are two key signal features in wireless communication. With advances in wireless technology, CSI is becoming more widespread and will serve as a critical enabler for future intelligent device positioning and communication. Compared with traditional RSSI, CSI offers richer channel characteristics, making it more suitable for indoor fingerprint positioning. However, the traditional weighted k nearest neighbor (WKNN) algorithm based on CSI suffers from high errors, struggling to meet the high-precision continuous positioning demands of indoor mobile robots. This paper proposes a CSI fingerprint localization algorithm integrating temporal windowing and Random Forest (RF) regression. The approach first preprocesses multi-antenna CSI raw data via phase unwrapping, Carrier Frequency Offset (CFO) correction, median filtering, and wavelet denoising. A temporal windowing strategy is then adopted, where the key parameters (window length and sliding step) segment CSI data into stable units, to extract window-level amplitude/phase features and enable multi-antenna fusion. Finally, an RF regression model is built for continuous coordinate-based localization. In indoor experiments using CSI signals, 5 million records were collected from 90 reference points to evaluate the algorithm. Results show a mean positioning error of 0.2346 m, with a 90th percentile error of 0.6475 m. The Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) outperforms the traditional machine learning algorithm, offering fast positioning, robustness, and accuracy approaching neural networks but with lower computational complexity. This algorithm effectively addresses the accuracy and continuity limitations of traditional fingerprinting, making it ideal for scenarios requiring precision and real-time performance.

title: New Media Non Heritage Tourism Data Clustering and Pattern Recognition Method for Rural Revitalization
authors: Chen Wei, Wei Pan, Xiaojing Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This article mainly focuses on the application of new media technology in non heritage tourism, while introducing technologies such as big data, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things to play a role in cultural digital transformation and dissemination. By analyzing tourist behavior data and cultural activity data, explore a reliable and effective identification method for a new cultural tourism data analysis model using deep learning and machine learning. Introducing the utilization of social media data and cross platform data fusion, advocating information dissemination strategies based on data, and promoting the public value and popularity of traditional cultural tourism. Finally, compare the effectiveness of several algorithms to provide a theoretical basis and reference direction for the development of intelligent tourism.

title: YOLO26 for Object Detection in Very High-Resolution Remote Sensing Imagery: A Systematic Evaluation on NWPU VHR-10
authors: Qi Cao, Yongqi Xia, Zenghui Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Object detection in Very High-Resolution (VHR) remote sensing imagery faces persistent challenges including dense distribution of small-scale objects, complex background interference, and severe class imbalance. While the recently proposed YOLO26 architecture has demonstrated competitive performance in general-purpose detection benchmarks, its applicability to remote sensing scenarios remains unexplored. In this paper, we present the first systematic empirical study of YOLO26 for VHR remote sensing object detection, using the NWPU VHR-10 dataset as a controlled evaluation environment to investigate whether YOLO26's architectural advantages transfer to small-scale, data-constrained remote sensing settings. Our experiments demonstrate that the lightweight YOLO26n (2.57M parameters) coupled with high-resolution input (1024×1024) achieves 95.64% mAP@50 and 69.05% mAP@50-95, with a 3.46 percentage point improvement over 640×640 input, where small objects such as vehicles and bridges benefit most (+14.71 and +10.64 points, respectively). Through a controlled comparison with YOLOv8n under identical training settings, we reveal a complementary characteristic between the two architectures: YOLO26 achieves superior recall (92.87% vs. 89.61%) and significantly outperforms YOLOv8 on small, densely distributed targets such as ships (+7.31), bridges (+5.39), and vehicles (+4.40), while YOLOv8 exhibits higher precision on large-scale structural targets. Furthermore, we conduct ablation experiments on the P2 detection head, Mixup augmentation, and Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) module, and find that these widely adopted strategies do not yield consistent improvements on the small-scale NWPU VHR-10 dataset, with category-specific performance fluctuations of up to 12 percentage points. These results suggest that enhancement techniques validated on large-scale general datasets cannot be directly transferred to data-constrained remote sensing environments, providing dataset-scale-aware practical guidance for model selection and configuration in data-constrained VHR detection scenarios, with all conclusions grounded in and bounded by the NWPU VHR-10 benchmark setting.

title: Firewall Log-Based Rule Extraction and Passive Anomaly Detection for Tobacco ICS Security
authors: Bingyu Yang, Zhongwei Li, Fei Wang, Weiming Tong, Qi Shan, Shiyu Liu, Shaoqing Shi, Bo Yang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Industrial control systems in tobacco manufacturing are increasingly exposed to cyber threats due to interconnected network architectures. To address the limitations of static firewall policies and the lack of interpretable anomaly detection solutions, this paper proposes a rule-mining-based anomaly detection method leveraging historical firewall logs. By applying the Apriori algorithm, frequent communication patterns are extracted to construct behavior models representing normal device interactions. A passive detection prototype is implemented in bypass mode, supporting real-time traffic analysis, rule-based matching, and topology-aware visualization without interfering with production processes. Compared with conventional machine learning approaches, the proposed method emphasizes interpretability, lightweight deployment, and adaptability to evolving industrial environments. Experimental results on real-world firewall logs from a tobacco manufacturing plant validated the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed approach in identifying anomalous communication behaviors.

title: Artificial and Generative Intelligence for Rare Neurodevelopmental Disorders: A Systematic Review and Gap-Driven HSAD Framework Proposal
authors: Ranjna Kumari, Deepti Malhotra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) with Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) comorbidity and Childhood Disintegrative Disorder (CDD) are rare sociobehavioral disorders that introduce specific problems with diagnosis and research since they have overlapping symptoms, heterogeneous development, and are not well studied. Despite the growing use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the diagnostics of neurodevelopmental disorders, its performance in rare diseases is limited due to data scarcity, imbalance in the classes, limited multimodal combination, and lack of practices related to validation. This paper is an analytical review of AI-based solutions in ASD+ADHD comorbidity and CDD that synthesises results in behavioral, electrophysiological, neuroimaging, genetic and sensory domains. The main methodological gaps highlighted through the systematic comparative analysis include the scarcity of use of generative modelling techniques, the lack of harmonized multimodal frameworks and the paucity of clinical translation mechanisms. According to the research gaps that have been established, a conceptual framework of the Harmonized Synthetic Augmentation and Diagnostic (HSAD) is presented. The architecture combines the multimodal harmonization, the learning of latent representation, diffusionbased synthetic augmentation, and structured diagnostic modeling into a single computational pipeline. Rather than presenting experimental validation, this work provides a methodological roadmap to advance generative AI applications for the diagnosis of rare neurodevelopmental disorders. The study contributes both a critical synthesis of current limitations and a structured direction for future research in AI-driven rare disorder modeling.

title: Hard-Negative-Aware Behavior Contrastive Learning for Unsupervised Skill Discovery
authors: Hanbin Tian, Liming Xin
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Unsupervised skill discovery learns diverse and reusable behaviors without task-specific rewards, providing transferable priors for downstream reinforcement learning. Existing behavior-contrastive methods improve skill diversity by enforcing same-skill consistency and cross-skill separation, but they usually treat all negative samples uniformly. This weakens discrimination when different skills induce highly similar states. To address this issue, we propose a hard-negative-aware behavior contrastive learning framework for unsupervised skill discovery. Our method estimates cross-skill similarity in the learned representation space and assigns larger weights to more confusing negative pairs, thereby strengthening inter-skill discrimination while preserving intra-skill consistency. We further introduce a progressive weighting strategy to stabilize optimization in early training. The resulting objective is integrated into a skill-conditioned reinforcement learning framework, where the contrastive score serves as intrinsic reward during reward-free pre-training. Experiments on state-based URLB tasks show that the proposed method learns more transferable skill representations and improves downstream adaptation over representative unsupervised reinforcement learning baselines.

title: An Improved Convolutional Neural Network with Variance Dynamic Adjustment for Brain Tumour Detection in MRI Images
authors: Yucheng Xie
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: Brain tumours are a severe form of illness that affects the brain and its spine. Identifying them early with an MRI is extremely helpful, as it enables individuals to recover more quickly and receive timely treatment. The traditional approach relies heavily on radiologists manually analysing images, which is slow, time-consuming, and prone to error. This paper presents an improved CNN by combining an EfficientNet-B0 as the base with a Variance Dynamic Block that dynamically changes weights based on a feature's mean and variance. The overall intent here is to achieve a higher degree of accuracy, robustness, and stability in classifying brain tumours across different categories. On the Kaggle brain MRI dataset, it is clearly observed that the proposed model performs remarkably well with higher values of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. AI could therefore utilise it widely for medical imaging, and people may see it being successfully implemented in hospitals and real-world clinical practices in the near future.

title: mcGAN based data reconstruction between heterogeneous phased arrays for 2D DoA estimation
authors: Song Liu, Bowei Hu, Li Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In order to facilitate Direction-of-arrival (DoA) estimation for irregular phased arrays, a multi-label conditional Generative Adversarial Network (mcGAN) is proposed to conduct data reconstruction between two heterogeneous arrays. First, system model for data reconstruction between heterogeneous phased arrays is established. A target array is designed with the same array aperture as the source array. Afterwards, data preprocessing is performed. The normalized real and imaginary data of the source array covariance matrix are set as input to the mcGAN generator. At the same time, the covariance matrix of the target array is specified as the generator’s output. The signal DoAs are used as multi-labels whose label dimension is twice the number of signals. To avoid information leakage, the multi-dimensional labels are only fed into the discriminator but not the generator. Finally, an appropriate DoA estimation algorithm is applied to yield the DoA estimates. An example of UCA to L-shaped array data transformation is demonstrated. Simulation results have verified the efficiency and accuracy of the irregular to regular, high-dimensional to low-dimensional data reconstruction carried out by the proposed mcGAN. It is revealed that mcGAN can accurately control the generation of expected patterns without pattern mismatch and shows good generalization abilities.

title: The Role of User Emotional Expression and Semantic Cues in Improving Content Recommendation Accuracy and Their Data Feature Analysis

title: Forest Phenology Retrieval from Gap-Filled MODIS EVI Time Series Using MTMGAN
authors: HuiMing Xu, Weitao Zou, Chao Li, Weipeng Jing
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The continuous Enhanced Vegetation Index (EVI) time series is fundamental for reliable forest dynamics monitoring and management. However, MODIS EVI products are frequently affected by cloud contamination and atmospheric disturbances, resulting in irregular and prolonged missing observations that severely compromise forest phenology monitoring. To mitigate these limitations, we propose a forest phenology-oriented Mask-Aware Temporal Multi-scale Generative Adversarial Network (MTMGAN) for reconstructing the MODIS EVI time series with irregular data gaps. The proposed framework explicitly incorporates observation reliability and temporal gap information through mask-aware temporal positional encoding, enabling robust modeling of long-range temporal dependencies that preserve seasonal vegetation dynamics. A temporal-gated deep mixing mechanism is further introduced to capture extended temporal context without introducing additional global temporal attention. Experiments conducted on artificial and observed missing-data scenarios show that MTMGAN achieves lower MAE and higher SSIM compared to selected baselines, while maintaining comparable performance in PSNR and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$R^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Phenology evaluation against PhenoCam GCC observations further demonstrates that the reconstructed EVI series yield seasonal trajectories highly consistent with near-surface observations, enabling reliable retrieval of SOS and EOS dates. These results highlight the effectiveness of MTMGAN as a robust approach for forest phenology-oriented MODIS EVI reconstruction and its potential for long-term vegetation dynamics monitoring.

title: Flexible Position Antennas Enhanced Covert Communications
authors: Qunshu Wang, Jiacheng Wang, Junsheng Mu, Chengwen Xing, Nan Zhao, Arumugam Nallanathan, Dusit Niyato
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: As a cutting-edge security technology, covert communication aims to hide the transmission behavior rather than the contents. However, its development is facing challenges due to the fact that the conventional fixed-position antennas cannot fully utilize the spatial degrees of freedom. Therefore, an effective way to overcome this challenge is to develop the more efficient flexible position antennas (FlexPAs), which can realize flexible beamforming and spatial multiplexing by dynamically regulating the position, shape or size, providing new solutions to covert communications. In this article, we first investigate three general architectures of FlexPAs and analyze their advantages and disadvantages, especially for covert communications. Then, we briefly review the evolution of covert communications, and illustrate the benefits that FlexPAs can bring. In addition, we present important specific methods to improve the covertness by exploiting FlexPAs with the emphasis on two perspectives, including the transmitter and receiver. Finally, we propose an FlexPAs-aided covert transmission scheme to verify its effectiveness, and future research directions and challenges are discussed.

title: Open-Slot Antenna Integrated in the Ceramic Head of a Ti-13Nb-13Zr Hip Implant
authors: Mehmet Emre Korkmaz, Wulf Wulff, Thomas-Martin Schuldt, Sema Dumanli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Wireless links between implants and external systems require carefully designed antennas due to strong electro-magnetic losses and detuning effects in biological tissues. This work presents the design and analysis of an open-slot antenna integrated into an instrumented orthopedic hip implant for real-time wireless telemetry. The antenna is located inside the modular ceramic head of a Ti-13Nb-13Zr alloy-based implant, establishing wireless communication while preserving the mechanical and antimicrobial properties of the titanium femoral stem. The proposed design eliminates the need for any surface modification of the metallic implant, maintaining its integrity and bio-compatibility. The antenna operates in the 2.4 GHz Industrial, Scientific, and Medical (ISM) band, supporting Bluetooth Low Energy connectivity. The wireless connection is validated through numerical analysis of the hip implant and surrounding tissues, as well as measurements in physical phantoms. The measurements show that the proposed antenna achieves a -10 dB bandwidth of 119 MHz. The transmission characteristics demonstrate a wireless communication range of up to 75 cm for a transmit power of -20 dBm and a receiver sensitivity of -95 dBm.

title: EEG-Based Speech Imagery Classification via Neural Architecture Search
authors: Taveena Lotey, Aman Verma, Rajkumar Saini, Partha Pratim Roy
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Speech imagery (SI) has emerged as a promising paradigm for brain–computer interfaces (BCIs) that enable hands-free communication without external stimuli. Classification of electroencephalography-based SI signals (SI-EEG) remains challenging due to limited data and high inter- and intra-subject variability. Most existing approaches rely on manually designed architectures, which is a time-consuming and labor-intensive process that requires significant domain expertise. To address these challenges, we propose a novel neural architecture search (NAS) framework for SI-EEG signal classification with bi-level optimization and path-level binarization to automatically and adaptively discover optimal model architectures. Furthermore, we propose Deformable ConvNeXt block, a novel convolutional neural network-based operation that integrates transformerinspired design principles and EEG-specific priors. This block enables each neuron to adaptively control the size of the EEG signal’s receptive field. It allows the model to more effectively capture temporal variations in mental activity in inter- and intra-subject classification settings. Evaluated across three SIEEG datasets, our approach achieves classification accuracies of 68.30%, 68.49%, and 72.32%, surpassing the state of the art. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and scalability of our NAS-based framework for next-generation real-time BCI systems.

title: Energy-Efficient Federated Edge Computing for Predictive Scheduling in Vehicular Networks
authors: Xiangjie Kong, Kaiyu Zhang, Zhenhui Xu, Junjie Zhou, Guojiang Shen, Amr Tolba, Osama Alfarraj
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Dynamic vehicular networks suffer from unstable connections, fluctuating bandwidth, and high mobility, leading to energy-intensive and redundant federated learning (FL) updates. Conventional reactive scheduling methods fail to predict short lived connections or sudden traffic surges, resulting in inefficient resource utilization. To address these challenges, we propose EcoFed, a novel energy-efficient framework that integrates predictive scheduling with adaptive model compression in a closed loop architecture. EcoFed employs a communication-aware re inforcement learning scheduler to optimize client selection by anticipating connectivity issues and low-contribution clients. It incorporates a dynamic FL strategy with a knowledge transfer mechanism to compress model updates based on real-time net work conditions. Additionally, a lightweight Transformer-based prediction module forecasts client connectivity and workload patterns to enable proactive scheduling. Experimental results demonstrate that EcoFed significantly reduces energy consumption and achieves competitive latency by intelligently trading off latency for energy savings under heavy loads. The framework accelerates model convergence across diverse vehicular scenarios, including multi-modal tasks, while maintaining high prediction accuracy. It also ensures robust performance under network disruptions and scales effectively. The framework supports privacy-preserving updates for 6G-driven vehicle-to-everything applications, outperforming state-of-the-art methods in intelligent transportation systems.

title: Bio-inspired Optofluidic Molecular Communication with Photothermally Actuated Microrobot Swarms_supp5-3698265.mp4
authors: Tingting Yu
publish: N/A
abstract: Molecular communication (MC) offers a bio-inspired paradigm for information transfer in environments inaccessible to conventional electromagnetic waves. However, translating MC concepts to the microscale has been hampered by a lack of integrated, biocompatible testbeds. Inspired by biological spectral-dependent photothermal transduction of specific light wavelengths into thermal energy, we present the first fully integrated microscopic MC platform utilizing photothermally responsive microrobot swarms. Our platform employs core-shell microrobots that exhibit a strong photothermal response, enabling precise and non-invasive navigation within microfluidic channels via near-infrared (NIR) light. This optofluidic architecture facilitates a symbiotic dual-bit encoding scheme, which concurrently modulates information onto both microrobot arrival and the optical control states. We demonstrate a complete communication workflow, from microrobot emission and laser-guided modulation to real-time optical detection and signal demodulation. The system achieves a data rate of 0.63 bits · min−1 with a low bit error rate of 4%, validated by a multi-sampling detection algorithm and the transmission of the ASCII string “HELLO WORLD”. This work provides a robust testbed for validating MC theories in biologically relevant microenvironments and serves as a step toward applications in the Internet of Bio-Nano Things.

title: Dual Band, Dual Plane, Monopulse Comparator with Vivaldi Antenna Array using Compact Dual Band Power Dividers for Aircraft Tracking
authors: Hrijith K, Sukomal Dey
publish: IEEE Journal on Miniaturization for Air and Space Systems
abstract: This paper presents a dual-band, dual-plane planar monopulse antipodal Vivaldi antenna system integrating a dual-band, dual-plane monopulse comparator with an antenna array operating at 2.4 GHz and 5.8 GHz. A compact dual-band power divider and dielectric-loaded antenna are used to achieve high gain and improved null depth. The main contribution is the system-level integration of the comparator and antenna array using a single-circuit approach, along with experimental validation. To the authors’ knowledge, this is the first planar dual-band, dual-plane monopulse antipodal Vivaldi antenna system using a single topology with demonstrated angular error measurement for accurate target estimation. Most existing monopulse systems operate only at a single frequency, while some dual-band systems require separate feeding networks for different frequencies, resulting in larger size, multilayer complexity, and fabrication misalignment. In addition, these systems cannot simultaneously track targets at multiple frequencies in both azimuth and elevation planes. The proposed system overcomes these limitations through a single-circuit approach, enabling simultaneous dual-band operation with improved null depth and tracking capability in both planes. Furthermore, the dual-band operation allows simultaneous tracking of near-range and far-range targets, making the system suitable for real-world tracking applications. The final design is fabricated, tested and compared with the simulated results. The measured impedance fractional bandwidth for matching below -6 dB is 15.83% at 2.4 GHz and 5.68% at 5.8 GHz. The measured highest gain at the sum channel is 13.9 dBi at 2.4 GHz and 18.5 dBi at 5.8 GHz. The measured E-plane side lobe values are -20 dB and -13.2 dB at 2.4 GHz and 5.8 GHz, respectively, with a null depth of below -40 dB at both frequencies.

title: CFEM-Net: Cross-Scale Feature Enhancement and Edge-Aware Learning for Geological Fracture Segmentation
authors: Siyao Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of rock fractures is fundamental to geological hazard assessment. However, geological fractures exhibit extreme scale variations, and complex surface features such as lichen, mineral veins, and weathering textures cause serious visual interference. This paper proposes CFEM-Net for geological fracture segmentation. The network incorporates a Multi-Scale Strip Attention (MLSA) module that decomposes large kernels into orthogonal strip convolutions to model fracture directionality. An Enhanced Dilated Module (EDM) employs parallel atrous convolutions with multiple dilation rates to enlarge the receptive field. An Advanced Feature Fusion (AFF) module utilizes channel-spatial dual-path attention for cross-scale feature alignment. Additionally, an edge-aware loss function integrating the Sobel gradient operator is designed to enhance boundary accuracy. On the GeoCrack dataset, CFEM-Net achieves a sample-wise average Dice coefficient of 77.3%, increasing to 79.6% with test-time augmentation, demonstrating the effectiveness of our task-specific design tailored to fracture anisotropy and scale variation.

title: Attribute-Based Keyword Search Scheme for Multi-Data Owner Scenarios
authors: Jianxin Zhu, Liangliang Wang, Weiwei Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the rapid development of the digital economy and the widespread deployment of IoT technology, data has become a critical factor of production. However, the limited resources of IoT devices pose significant challenges to data security. An increasing number of individuals and organizations outsource data to cloud servers for storage and sharing. While encrypting data before storage ensures confidentiality, it makes standard keyword searches on ciphertext difficult, thereby affecting data availability. Existing searchable encryption schemes still face issues such as insufficient fine-grained access control, privacy leakage in access policies, and complex key management in multi-owner scenarios. To address these challenges, we propose an attribute-based keyword search scheme K-ABKS for multi-owners for typical multi-owner data sharing scenarios.. This scheme designs a master key generation and private key aggregation mechanism, enabling multiple data owners to collaboratively generate a single aggregated private key for data users.

title: Wavelet and Artificial Neural Network Based Forearm Movements Classification: A Paradigm for Prosthesis Design
authors: Parbhat Gupta, Amit Kukker
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: In this paper, Wavelet transform and Artificial Neural Network is used for forearm movements classification. The six forearm movements are selected as: Hand Pronation, Flexion, Open, Close, Extension and Supination. In first step, EMG dataset is collected using Biopac 100c System for 20 healthy subjects aged between 16-30 years. Afterwards, Wavelet transform is used to decompose signals and features are extracted (i.e. 10 features in our case). Afterwards, these features are used as ANN input and based on six movements target matrix is designed. The purpose of Wavelet is to deal with non-stationary behavior of the signal which is difficult to handle with Fourier transform and different wavelet functions make it possible to deal with non-stationary behaviour of varying EMG signals. ANN is supervised learning having features to classify objects with good accuracy and fast response. The proposed approach shows accuracy 97.6. This will help medical practitioners for early diagnosis and prosthesis design for amputation.

title: Intrusion Detection Method Based on Autoencoder Conditional Generative Adversarial Network
authors: Huakang Hu, Yanjiao Wu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address existing Network Intrusion Detection Systems’ (NIDS) pain points in feature extraction for high-dimensional, heterogeneous traffic, and classifier long-tail distribution bias from extremely scarce minority attack samples, this paper proposes an intrusion detection method based on the deep fusion of a 1D Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN) and an Autoencoder-based Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (AE-CGAN). First, a 1D-CNN is used as a lightweight feature aggregator to perform feature mapping on preprocessed, high-dimensional network traffic data, obtaining precise conditional guidance labels. Secondly, an AE-CGAN architecture is constructed, featuring a symmetric autoencoder as the feedback core, abandoning traditional probabilistic discrimination and relying on Wasserstein distance to measure the reconstruction error distribution between real and synthetic samples. Boundary equilibrium mechanisms and conditional label embedding are also introduced, effectively overcoming training bottlenecks like gradient vanishing and mode collapse, enabling high-fidelity, directional generation of specified scarce attack types (e.g., U2R, R2L). Finally, the augmented, balanced dataset is fed back to the 1D-CNN classifier for end-to-end parameter fine-tuning. Comparative experiments on the CICIDS 2017 baseline dataset demonstrate that, without significantly increasing inference overhead, this method substantially enhances the identification sensitivity of intrusion detection systems to rare network threats.

title: High-frequency small-signal excitation-based precision measurement of voltage divider resistor ratios and error analysis
authors: Zhaoxing Xu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the inaccuracy of mains voltage measurement in traditional resistive voltage dividers caused by component precision and temperature drift, this paper proposes an online self-calibration method for measuring the resistor divider ratio based on high-frequency signal injection. A precise 2 Vpp, 10 MHz small-signal voltage source, phase-locked to the mains voltage, is coupled into the measurement circuit via a series transformer. At the divider output, the mixed signal containing 50 Hz and 10 MHz components is separated. The real-time divider ratio is accurately calculated using the amplitude of the extracted 10 MHz component, and the actual mains voltage is then derived from the separated 50 Hz component. This paper focuses on two key technologies: first, a high-stability 10 MHz signal source, synchronized in phase with the mains, implemented based on an OCXO+DDS architecture with phase detection and amplitude feedback control; second, a dual-channel parallel processing architecture employing digital band-pass filtering, direct down-conversion, and digital phase-locked loops to achieve high-fidelity separation of mixed signals with a large frequency span. A system model was built on the Simulink platform for simulation verification. The results demonstrate that the method can accurately generate the required excitation signal and successfully separate the two frequency components. The measured divider ratio closely matches the set value, validating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: The Implementation Method of Facial Expression Reconstruction and Driving Algorithm for 3D Characters in Digital Media Art
authors: Mi Jing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: 3D character facial expression reconstruction and driving algorithms are important key technologies in the field of digital media art. This study mainly describes facial expression reconstruction methods based on technologies such as geometric modeling and deep learning, and discusses the implementation of different driving algorithms, including algorithms based on muscle motion models, key point tracking, audio driving, and physical simulation. A detailed discussion and analysis were conducted on the characteristics and execution processes of various technologies, thereby proposing an optimization scheme for facial expression reconstruction and driving algorithms. Experimental results prove that this method can improve the authenticity and vividness of facial expressions, providing technical support for the character expressions, movements, and interactive experiences in digital media art.

title: Bio-Inspired Adaptive Optimal Control for Stable and Efficient Motor Learning
authors: Fei Li, Qi Yang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This study introduces a bio-inspired adaptive optimal control framework that integrates Lyapunov stability, Fisher Information geometry, and Bayesian inference to model robust motor adaptation. Drawing inspiration from biological fast–slow learning processes, the model guarantees global convergence under uncertainty through explicit stability and energy criteria. Lyapunov and Monte Carlo analyses demonstrate stable adaptation across stochastic environments, while Fisher Information and hierarchical Bayesian inference establish high identifiability and noise-resilience of key control gains. Extending to nonlinear and triple-state architectures, we show that biological adaptation trajectories approximate LQR-optimal solutions, implying that the nervous system performs constrained optimal feedback regulation. Principal component and spectral analyses uncover a low-dimensional manifold coordinating fast corrective and slow integrative dynamics, mirroring cortical theta–beta coupling. The proposed framework establishes a principled bridge between neural motor control, adaptive systems, and robust machine intelligence.

title: UAV Frequency-Hopping Signal Recognition: A Survey on Collaborative Methods of Feature Extraction and Deep Models
authors: Xiang Ni, Huixian Sun, Yang Zhou, Zhaorui Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Focused on specific emitter identification (SEI) for UAS frequency-hopping (FH) command-and-control signals, this study systematically reviews the synergistic mechanisms between feature extraction and classification models. We critically evaluate the evolutionary trajectory of signal representations, transitioning from 1D temporal sequences to 2D time-frequency spectrograms. The synthesis reveals that prevailing data-driven architectures encounter significant bottlenecks, particularly regarding the lack of physical interpretability and the superficial coupling between hand-crafted features and deep learning classifiers. Future research must prioritize the transformation of classical signal processing algorithms into embedded neural modules. By incorporating physical priors into customized radiofrequency (RF) networks, a seamless integration of physical feature extraction and classification optimization can be achieved.

title: Research on the Improvement of LSTM Architecture for Speaker Recognition Performance
authors: Wensheng Gu, Zhoulin Chang, Rong Huang, Zhanhuang Lu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To enhance the efficiency and performance of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks in speaker recognition tasks, this paper investigates two architectural improvement strategies. The first strategy focuses on reducing internal parameters by introducing a Coupled Input and Forget Gate (CIFG) mechanism into both LSTM and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) cells, aiming to accelerate training while evaluating recognition accuracy. The second strategy involves optimizing the hierarchical combination of LSTM/GRU networks with auxiliary layers (e.g., fully connected layers). Experimental evaluations are conducted on the AISHELL dataset, where speech features (MFCC) are extracted and fed into various proposed models. Results demonstrate that the CIFG-LSTM model achieves a significant improvement, reaching a test accuracy of 88.5%, which outperforms the standard LSTM (74.7%) and other variants. This study confirms that parameter reduction via CIFG is particularly effective for LSTM architectures in speaker recognition, while architectural adjustments show limited and model-dependent effects.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis of Intrusion Detection Systems Across Cloud, IoT, and Cyber-Physical Environments
authors: Shelley Gupta, Mohit Jain, Tanya Chaudhary, Harikesh Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Today, the concept of an Intrusion Detection System (IDS) is vital when it comes to safeguarding the information infrastructures of modern computer systems from complex cyberattack methods. The rise of cloud computing, the internet of things, and cyber-physical systems has produced more different types of attack surfaces than ever before. This leads to an IDS that scale up and down, adapt inexpensively or even for free, and can work within heterogeneous environments. The paper evaluates an emerging type of IDS, multi-level intrusion detection systems, being specifically developed for Cloud-based and distributed systems. Each of the four techniques used to identify fraud in a client-initiated transaction will be applied in this project in terms of their effectiveness and efficiency (Signature, Anomaly, Behavior, Hybrid) while also noting how machine learning, deep learning, and AI techniques apply to these methods. Comparative performances on the most widely used benchmark datasets show that by implementing Hybrid or Multi-Level IDS architectures, companies can obtain better detection accuracy while achieving lower false positives and less operational overhead compared to traditional IDS architectures. The paper reviews existing research, identifies remaining challenges in the field of IDS, and outlines a framework for future IDS research and development through an overview of challenges faced by IDS such as encrypted traffic, scalability, detection in real time, and energy constrained environments.

title: Explainable AI for Sleep Health: Phenotype Discovery and Risk Prediction
authors: Priyanka Suyal, Deeksha Joshi, Suraj Mishra, Vineet Kumar, Kamal Kumar Gola, Mohit Suyal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Poor sleep quality and undiagnosed sleep disorders are common and are linked to serious health risks, yet simple and transparent prediction tools are still limited. In this work, we study the publicly available Sleep Health and Lifestyle dataset to answer two questions: (i) can routinely collected lifestyle and vital-sign variables be used to predict sleep quality and disorder status, and (ii) how can these predictions be explained in a way that is useful for clinicians and individuals? We first clean and preprocess the data, then discover sleep phenotypes using principal component analysis and k-means clustering on standardized numerical features. In addition to these phenotypes, we train machine learning models (logistic regression, random forests, XGBoost) for sleep disorder classification and quality-of-sleep regression, evaluated using stratified cross-validation. To make the models interpretable, we use SHAP values to quantify global and individual feature contributions and derive simple counterfactual-style recommendations that show how changes in modifiable factors (e.g., sleep duration or stress level) are associated with better predicted sleep outcomes. The study shows that a compact, explainable model based on easily measured variables can achieve competitive performance while still providing clear, phenotype-aware insights rather than black-box predictions.

title: Efficient Pathology Simulation in Chest X-Rays Using Conditional CycleGAN with ROI Guidance
authors: Shubhangi Sankhyadhar, Mohit Pandey, Madhuri Gupta, Deepika Pantola
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Timely and accurate diagnosis of thoracic diseases from chest X-rays (CXR) is crucial for effective clinical decisionmaking. However, the visual similarities among various thoracic pathologies and the lack of annotated data pose significant challenges for automated multi-disease classification. In this study, we propose an efficient deep learning-based framework for multi- disease detection in CXR images, leveraging a lightweight CNN architecture combined with a Conditional CycleGAN for robust synthetic data generation. The CycleGAN is conditioned using anatomical region-of-interest (ROI) masks to enhance disease representation and generalization, especially under limited or imbalanced data scenarios. Feature extraction is performed using a pre-trained EfficientNet-B0 model, followed by classification into multiple disease categories. Experimental evaluations are conducted on the NIH ChestXray14 dataset, demonstrating that our approach outperforms several existing models in terms of accuracy, precision, and F1score. The proposed framework achieves a balance between computational efficiency and classification performance, making it suitable for deployment in low- resource clinical settings. Qualitative results and visualization maps further validate the interpretability and clinical relevance of the predicted outcomes.

title: Multi-Level Particle Swarm Clustering Approach Grounded in Three-Way Decision Theory
authors: Yongpeng Ren, Yang Ma, Yanfeng Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the issues of swarm intelligence clustering algorithms tending to fall into local optima when processing high-dimensional data and possessing weak capacity in handling data uncertainty, this paper puts forward a multilevel particle swarm clustering algorithm APSO-TWKM grounded in three-way decision theory. In the first step, a multilayer stacked encoder is applied to carry out hierarchical feature extraction as well as dimensionality reduction on high-dimensional datasets. secondly, a multilevel particle swarm optimization framework with an adaptive learning strategy is constructed, enhancing global exploration and local exploitation capabilities via dynamic grouping and differentiated learning mechanisms; finally, three-way decision theory is incorporated, paired with a hemispherical neighborhood information granulation approach, to realize quantitative characterization and redistribution of uncertain samples. Relative to existing clustering approaches, the proposed algorithm is able to effectively elevate clustering accuracy and stability, substantially lowering the decision risk triggered by data uncertainty.

title: High-Precision Trajectory Tracking of Humanoid Robot Legs Using PSO-Optimized Fuzzy PID Control
authors: Minrui Liang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Humanoid robots are widely applied in complex service and industrial scenarios, where high-precision trajectory tracking of multi-degree-of-freedom (DOF) legs is essential for stable locomotion. The strong coupling and nonlinear dynamics of humanoid joints bring severe challenges to conventional control methods. This paper presents a dual-layer adaptive control scheme for the leg of the Unitree H1 humanoid robot. By combining precise kinematic and dynamic modeling, a hybrid PSO-Fuzzy PID (Proportional-Integral-Derivative) controller is designed. The baseline PID gains are optimized offline via particle swarm optimization (PSO) to avoid the subjectivity of manual tuning. Then, a Mamdani fuzzy inference system adjusts the gains online in real time to suppress dynamic tracking errors. Co-simulation results show that the proposed method evidently reduces dynamic delay and phase lag relative to traditional PID. The maximum tracking error and root-mean-square error are reduced by 92.9% and 95.1%, respectively. Meanwhile, the controller improves trajectory smoothness and robustness to parameter perturbations. The proposed scheme offers a reliable and high-performance control solution for precise motion control of advanced humanoid robots.

title: ACO-UNet: Ant Colony Optimisation-Guided U-Net with Distance-Aware Weighted Loss for 2D Occupancy Grid Path Planning
authors: Qiao Li, Hongyu Zhou
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Autonomous path planning on occupancy grid maps is a core capability for mobile robots and intelligent logistics systems. Despite recent advances, existing approaches suffer from three persistent limitations: (1) classical search-based methods such as A* and D* Lite incur super-linear computational cost that prevents real-time deployment on dense maps; (2) pure deep reinforcement learning planners require thousands of environment interactions and lack explicit safety-margin enforcement near obstacle boundaries; (3) standard encoder-decoder networks trained with binary cross-entropy ignore the severe class imbalance between path pixels and background, yielding fragmented or collision-prone predictions. To address these limitations, we propose ACO-UNet, a supervised end-to-end framework that fuses a U-Net multi-scale encoder-decoder backbone with a differentiable ant-colony optimisation (ACO) pheromone guidance module. Our key innovations are threefold: (1) a pheromone-weighted heatmap prediction mechanism that biases feature learning toward globally coherent trajectories; (2) a distance-aware weighted loss function that amplifies penalties in obstacle-boundary regions via max-pooling dilation, directly encoding safety constraints into training; (3) a top-K path extraction strategy that adaptively selects path-length-consistent cells from the predicted heatmap without threshold tuning. Trained on 172,192 D* Lite-labelled occupancy maps and evaluated on 51,103 test instances across three difficulty levels, ACO-UNet achieves 76.3% path IoU, 1.8% collision rate, and 3.42 ms inference time, outperforming A* and vanilla U-Net on all four evaluation metrics.
