title: A Cross-Layer Deep Reinforcement Learning–Based Framework for STAR-RIS-Assisted MISO Video Communications
authors: Yu-Chun Huang, Shu-Ming Tseng
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: The rapid proliferation of multimedia services has placed stringent requirements on wireless networks to simultaneously support high data rates and consistent perceptual video quality. To address coverage limitations and unfavorable propagation conditions in beyond-5G and emerging 6G systems, Simultaneously Transmitting and Reflecting Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (STAR-RIS) have been introduced as a flexible solution capable of reshaping wireless channels over the entire spatial domain. Despite their potential, most existing STAR-RIS designs remain confined to physical-layer performance objectives and adopt complex deep reinforcement learning (DRL) algorithms, leaving the interaction between wireless resource allocation and application-layer video characteristics largely unexplored. In this work, we develop a cross-layer optimization framework for STAR-RIS-assisted multi-user MISO video communication that explicitly targets end-to-end video quality. A Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO)-based learning strategy is employed to jointly optimize base station beamforming, STAR-RIS transmission and reflection coefficients, and video encoding control, while maintaining a lightweight and stable training structure. By embedding video rate–distortion parameters into the learning state and directly maximizing Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), the proposed approach enables content-aware STAR-RIS configuration with improved robustness. In addition, the proposed framework is extended to a STAR-RIS-assisted mobile edge computing (MEC) environment using a two-stage learning architecture that decouples wireless configuration from computation offloading decisions. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed cross-layer scheme achieves an average PSNR gain of approximately 3-4 dB in downlink video transmission, and an additional improvement of 1-3 dB in MEC offloading scenarios, confirming its effectiveness for multimedia-oriented wireless systems.

title:
High-Performance In-Sn-Zn-O Vertical Thin-Film Transistors With Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Tunneling Layer
authors: Xuyang Li, Congyang Wen, Shuo Zhang, Xianwen Liu, Yidan Jin, Jin Zhang, Haifeng Liang, Jianshe Xue, Guangcai Yuan, Zhinong Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: To address performance degradation caused by the drain-induced barrier lowering (DIBL) effect in short-channel vertical thin-film transistors (VTFTs), this work proposes an ultrathin tunneling layer (TL) modification strategy. A 2 nm Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> TL with low roughness, narrow bandgap, and low barrier height is fabricated at the drain-spacer sidewall-source interface, forming a TL-VTFT based on an In-Sn-Zn-O active layer (AL). This design overcomes the trade-off between DIBL suppression and performance enhancement. The TL increases the contact barrier, enabling space-charge-limited conduction (SCLC) at low electric fields and Fowler–Nordheim tunneling at medium-high fields, achieving a DIBL of only 0.045 V/V. Additionally, it optimizes the back-channel interface, reducing charge scattering and enhancing carrier transport. For short-channel devices (width 2000 nm and length 200 nm), a saturation mobility of 30.22 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s, subthreshold swing of 67.3 mV/dec, and current drivability of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16.58~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>A/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {ds}} =0.5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> V) are achieved. This work offers new insights for designing high-performance, low-DIBL short-channel VTFTs.

title: 3D Single-Tooth Reconstruction via Curriculum Learning and Topology-aware Generation_supp2-3696646.pdf
authors: Lin Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Automated single-tooth reconstruction is a pivotal task in modern digital dentistry, yet existing point cloud completion methods often lack the specialized anatomical priors and spatial constraints necessary for clinical integration. In this paper, we propose Tooth-STR, a hierarchical generative framework designed to synthesize anatomically high-fidelity and collision-free tooth models from partial intraoral scans. Our Tooth-STR consists of a Topology-Aware Coarse Generation (TACG) module that utilizes differential edge convolutions to reason about the missing dental manifold based on the topological trends of the surrounding dentition, and a Curriculum Anatomical Constraint (C<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) strategy that implements a phased, differentiable boundary penalty to balance unconstrained geometric exploration with strict spatial regularization. Extensive experiments on the constructed Tooth-STR dataset demonstrate that our method significantly improves reconstruction performance across various tooth categories.

title: Resonant Tunneling Diodes as Tunable Low-Noise mmWave Reflection Amplifiers: Experimental Investigations Using InGaAs RTDs
authors: Mahmoud Wagih, Afesomeh Ofiare, Elliott Brown, Jue Wang, Edward Wasige, Abdullah Al-Khalidi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Reflection amplifiers have recently attracted interest in new applications such as active antenna arrays and long-range backscattering. While resonant tunnelling diodes (RTDs) are a potential candidate for reflection amplifiers, their potential as millimeter-wave (mmWave) reflection amplifiers was not explored experimentally. Here, we report the first investigation of one-port reflection amplifiers with a tunable operation between 20 and 32 GHz based on InGaAs RTDs on an indium phosphide (InP) substrate, in both stable and unstable (in-band oscillation) operation conditions. The single-tunnel diode-based amplifier occupies <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$177{\,}\times {\,}280~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m active area and can be tuned to different frequencies between 22 and 33 GHz through the device’s capacitance, with up to 25-dB gain, not affected by oscillations, and up to 32-dB gain when locked to an external signal source. The IV properties are exploited to achieve switchable operation modes, allowing stable and low-noise operation with up to 13-dB gain. Detailed noise figure (NF) measurements are presented showing an NF between 4 and 12 dB up to 40 GHz. These results show that InP reflection amplifiers could serve as high-gain and low-noise amplifiers in future mmWave networks and in active reflectarrays and intelligent surfaces.

title: Full-Acoustic Assessment of Digital MEMS Microphones for Traffic Noise Monitoring
authors: María Campo-Valera, Fabio Saba, Mario Corallo, Paolo Guidorzi, Andrea Osele, Carlo Costa
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Traffic noise is a major environmental pollutant affecting public health and quality of life. Although sound level meters (SLMs) compliant with IEC 61672 and IEC 61260 provide reliable measurements, their high cost limits large-scale deployment. Digital MEMS microphones offer a compact and cost-effective alternative for distributed monitoring networks; however, their integrated digital architecture prevents conventional electrical testing, challenging metrological traceability and regulatory compliance. This work presents a full-acoustic verification methodology for digital MEMS microphones intended for sound level metering, inspired by IEC 61672-3. The method uses controlled playback of standardized test tones and representative traffic noise signals in an anechoic test box. Unlike established approaches for conventional SLMs verification based on both electrical and acoustic tests, the proposed methodology introduces a full-acoustic, SI-traceable verification framework tailored to digital MEMS architectures. By leveraging easily controllable pressure-field comparison techniques against a reference microphone, this method guarantees measurement accuracy and reliability while facilitating repeatability. It allows for the consistent characterization of sensitivity, frequency response, and dynamic performance under realistic conditions, resulting in a scalable, metrologically robust, and cost-effective solution for advanced traffic noise monitoring applications.

title: Chain-of-Thought for Large Language Model-Empowered Wireless Communications
authors: Xudong Wang, Jian Zhu, Ruichen Zhang, Lei Feng, Dusit Niyato, Jiacheng Wang, Hongyang Du, Shiwen Mao, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have opened new possibilities for automated reasoning and decision-making in wireless networks. However, applying LLMs to wireless communications presents challenges such as limited capability in handling complex logic, generalization, and reasoning. Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting, which guides LLMs to generate explicit intermediate reasoning steps, has been shown to significantly improve LLM performance on complex tasks. Inspired by this, this article explores the application potential of CoT-enhanced LLMs in wireless communications. Specifically, we first review the fundamental theory of CoT and summarize various types of CoT. We then survey key CoT and LLM techniques relevant to wireless communication and networking. Moreover, we introduce a multi-layer intent-driven CoT framework that bridges high-level user intent expressed in natural language with concrete wireless control actions. Our proposed framework sequentially parses and clusters intent, selects appropriate CoT reasoning modules via reinforcement learning, then generates interpretable control policies for system configuration. Using the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) network as a case study, we demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly outperforms a non-CoT baseline in both communication performance and quality of generated reasoning.

title: Decoupling Governance from Consensus: An Anchor-Only Architecture for EHDS Based on eIDAS 2.0
authors: Elder Bruno Evaristo Correa, João C. A. Ferreira, Antonio J. G. Abelém
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The European Health Data Space (EHDS) requires interoperable and secure infrastructures capable of supporting cross-border health data exchange while remaining aligned with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). Existing Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT)-based approaches remain predominantly ledger-centric, coupling governance logic with consensus execution and introducing non-deterministic scalability, high computational overhead, and structural conflicts with GDPR principles such as data minimization and the Right to be Forgotten. This paper proposes the Machine-Readable Governance (MRG) Anchor-Only architecture, a federated and infrastructure-agnostic model that decouples governance semantics from blockchain execution. Leveraging eIDAS 2.0 components—Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) and Verifiable Credentials (VCs)—the architecture externalizes identity verification, policy evaluation, and purpose-bound access control to decentralized off-chain agents operating at the network edge. The blockchain is reduced to a passive semantic anchoring layer recording only fixed-size 32-byte cryptographic commitments derived from deterministic canonicalization (RFC 8785). A Proof-of-Concept (PoC) was implemented on an 8-node Hyperledger Besu consortium and evaluated under cross-border healthcare conditions. The complete smart contract OrganicGovernanceRegistry.sol, ABI, deployment scripts, workload generator, raw benchmark data, and Besu configuration are publicly available at https://github.com/evaristocpqd/ehds-mrg.git. Experimental results demonstrate a three-order-of- magnitude reduction in cryptographic processing latency relative to a worst-case pure-Solidity on-chain verification baseline (from ∼2500 ms on-chain to ∼2.5 ms off-chain; the choice of baseline and its limitations are discussed explicitly in Section V-A), sub-100 ms DID resolution latency at 2,000 requests/hour, and constant gas consumption of 64,960 units per anchoring transaction independent of governance policy complexity. As a structural consequence of the anchor-only design, the on-chain footprint is fixed at 192 bytes per record (versus >2,560 bytes for full OIDC4VP payloads in ledger-centric baselines), and no Protected Health Information (PHI) or Personally Identifiable Information (PII) is ever recorded on the ledger.We emphasize that all GDPR-related guarantees claimed in this work apply strictly to the blockchain anchoring layer only. No Protected Health Information (PHI) or Personally Identifiable Information (PII) is ever recorded on the ledger. Off-chain agents, the Data Sharing Repository (DSR), and the Verifiable Data Registry (VDR) process personal data and remain fully subject to standard GDPR obligations under Articles 5, 6, 25, and 32, which must be addressed by complementary technical and organizational measures.Within this scoped interpretation, the architecture achieves purpose-bound governance (P1), deterministic on-chain anchoring (P2), and ledger-level data minimization with zero on-chain exposure of PHI/PII (P3).

title: Cooperative Optimal Guidance With Inter-Interceptor Avoidance and Angle Constraints
authors: Ce Chen, Shashi Ranjan Kumar, Shaoming He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a cooperative optimal guidance law that accounts for terminal angle constraints and collision avoidance. By constructing a linearized engagement model and applying the zero-effort transformation, the problem is formulated as a linear quadratic optimal control problem with the energy consumption being the cost function. The analytic guidance command is derived by utilizing the general dead-zone function. The proposed guidance law extends conventional optimal impact angle control guidance law to the multi-interceptor scenario, i.e., it enables <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math></inline-formula> interceptors to intercept <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$n$</tex-math></inline-formula> corresponding targets at specified terminal angles, while generating appropriate control commands based on the interceptors' maneuvering capabilities to avoid mutual collisions. Two engagement scenarios involving two and three interceptor-target pairs are analyzed to illustrate the applications under varying levels of complexity. The three-pair case is simulated to validate the effectiveness and performance of the proposed optimal cooperative guidance law.

title: Speech-Driven Gesture Generation Via Conditional Flow Matching With Masked Training and Clamped Sampling
authors: Bowen Wu, Carlos Toshinori Ishi, Takashi Minato, Hiroshi Ishiguro
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: The ability to perform co-speech gestures is essential for robots with human-like appearances. In this paper, we present a novel conditional flow matching-based generative model to generate co-speech gestures from speech input. Moreover, we propose masked training and the corresponding masked generation to ensure smooth transitions in sequential gesture movements when processing segmented, long speech inputs. Furthermore, masked generation inherently supports general purpose partial gesture completion. While objective evaluation and user study confirm that our method outperforms existing baselines in gesture naturalness, it shows weak speech appropriateness. In addition, to enable direct deployment on humanoid robots, we introduce clamped sampling which enforces physical limits during generation, thereby avoiding hard post-processing (e.g., clamping) that may distort the intended gestures. We conducted a proof-of-concept experiment to demonstrate the efficacy of clamped sampling.

title: IEEE Feedback filler
authors: N/A

title: Research on a Blockchain Secure Data Sharing Mechanism Based on Proxy Re-Encryption
authors: Silong Huang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of blockchain applications in data-sharing scenarios such as healthcare and government, how to achieve flexible authorization and trusted auditing while protecting privacy and key security has become an urgent problem to solve. Based on the existing proxy re-encryption data-sharing model, this paper proposes a blockchain secure data-sharing mechanism based on proxy re-encryption. By combining symmetric encryption, conditional proxy re-encryption, and smart contracts, it achieves off-chain storage of data ciphertext and on-chain management of authorization information. At the same time, it introduces time constraints, attribute tags, and permission levels to build a fine-grained access control model, and designs a revocable authorization and on-chain access log mechanism to enhance the security, traceability, and tamper-resistance of the sharing process. Experimental results show that this mechanism ensures privacy protection and fine-grained access control while maintaining low computational and communication overhead, with overall performance superior to traditional solutions.

title: From Complex Spectrum Estimation to Magnitude-Phase Decomposed Modeling for Speech Enhancement
authors: Wei Qiu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Complex spectrum estimation is widely used in speech enhancement, but its unified formulation tends to treat magnitude and phase as homogeneous prediction targets despite their different restoration characteristics. We revisit this paradigm and propose a magnitude-phase decomposed modeling framework for speech enhancement. The model first learns a shared time-frequency representation from complex spectral inputs, and then performs asymmetric restoration through two task-specific branches. The magnitude branch estimates a spectral mask for speech recovery, while the phase branch refines the noisy phase by residual prediction conditioned on the enhanced magnitude representation, allowing phase estimation to exploit restored spectral structure instead of relying only on shared latent features. To further improve temporal-frequency modeling under this decomposed formulation, we introduce a gated residual adaptation strategy that injects GridNet blocks into the shared representation in a stable and controllable manner. In this way, the proposed method preserves the representation strength of complex spectrum modeling while better matching the heterogeneity of magnitude and phase restoration. Experiments on the VoiceBank+DEMAND dataset demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently improves speech quality and intelligibility, validating the effectiveness of decomposed magnitude-phase modeling for speech enhancement.

title: Soft-Guided Balanced Query Clustering for Efficient Local Attention in 3D Point Cloud Classification
authors: Isam K. Alshanawi, Wei Xia
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Recent studies have explored Transformer architectures for 3D point cloud classification; however, most approaches primarily adopt local self-attention to alleviate the computational burden, which consequently constrains their ability to extract semantically meaningful relationships among spatially distant yet related points. To overcome this limitation, we propose an efficient Transformer model, termed SBQC-Former for short, which performs soft-guided balanced clustering followed by local self-attention within each group, thereby enabling efficient modeling of long-range dependencies while substantially reducing computational cost. In addition, high- and low-frequency signals are captured using a parallel structure. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed network achieves state-of-the-art performance on three widely used benchmarks, including the synthetic ModelNet40 and two real-world datasets, namely, the Sydney Urban Objects and ScanObjectNN datasets, with accuracies of 93.80%, 95.40%, and 88.40%, respectively.

title: U-BDF: A Unified Bidirectional Diffusion Framework for High-Fidelity Scalable Virtual Try-On
authors: Jitendra Nath Singh, Anshika Sharma, Rajat Upadhyay
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Virtual try-on (VTO) technology has emerged as an essential ingredient of the fashion e-commerce world by mitigating uncertainties pertaining to the fitting and appearance of the garments. In this article, an in-depth examination of image-based virtual try-on techniques is presented. This encompasses adversarial learning-based approaches, as well as the state-of-the-art diffusion-based generative approaches. This article discusses the development process of these techniques from the fixed single pose overlay to the more flexible multi-pose and identity-keeping synthesis chain. Based on the developments in the area of learning excellent representations in the latent space using conditional generation, the article encompasses the difficulties entangled with shape, pose, and occlusions. Additionally, it offers a bidirectional diffusion approach that can address these difficulties.

title: Smart Farming Assistant Using ML
authors: Rajani Singh, Prabhanjan Maurya, Shashwat Kumar, Prachi Srivastava, Prateek Shakya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: In several nations, such as India, agriculture is a crucial economic sector that sustains billions of jobs and addresses future challenges like climate change and climate change related pest and disease outbreaks. Based on continuous research, a web based system has been created that uses real-time data to recommend specific crops to grow, taking into consideration the state of soil nutrient availability, weather, and moisture. With the advancement of technology in both machine learning and artificial intelligence, we can now be more precise with our agriculturerelated data analysis and provide farmers with accurate, data driven predictive outcomes (or predictions) regarding their crop yields and the timing of crop diseases. All of the above mentioned technologies are revolutionising the agriculture sector, as they allow for greater accuracy and efficiency in predicting crop production and identifying pests or diseases. We applied machine learning models to train and assess seven different machine learning models (Decision Trees, Naive Bayes, SVM, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XG Boost, and KNN). The best performing model for predicting the yield of crops is Random Forest, as it achieved the highest level of accuracy. The web application also includes a Plant Disease Identification Application that uses Convolutional Neural Networks to detect plant diseases. CNN detects and accurately classifies plant diseases by analysing leaf images, helping farmers to take early action to mitigate crop loss. The purpose of this research is to provide farmers with easy to use technology so they can make informed choices regarding what crop to plant, and how to treat their plants when diseased. The combination of disease detection and crop recommendation will lead to the implementation of smarter crop recommendation systems that incorporate plant disease detection, these will aid in creating sustainable agricultural systems, promoting economic stability, and ensuring food security in India and other countries across the World.

title: FedSGA: A Collaborative Semantic Rectification and Graph Augmentation Framework for Imbalanced Federated Graph Learning
authors: Yaokui He, Xin Yan, Xiangyu Fan
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Federated graph learning preserves graph data privacy but suffers from client heterogeneity and class imbalance, which make minority-class recognition particularly challenging. On the one hand, minority nodes are more vulnerable to interference from majority neighbors during neighborhood aggregation, leading to semantic drift in their representations. On the other hand, minority samples are scattered across clients and cannot be directly pooled for re-balancing, resulting in insufficient supervision. Existing methods usually address these issues from either representation optimization or data augmentation, but lack a unified treatment. To this end, we propose FedSGA, a collaborative framework for imbalanced federated graph learning. FedSGA performs minority semantic rectification with a global MLP teacher and a local EMA teacher, and further builds personalized semantic prototypes as shared anchors for both minority representation regularization and synthetic node calibration. Based on this, it introduces a teacher-guided two-stage graph augmentation strategy to jointly improve minority representation learning and sample supplementation. Experimental results show that FedSGA consistently improves minority recognition performance and overall classification effectiveness in imbalanced federated graph learning.

title: A Pattern Search-Driven Particle Swarm Optimization with Adaptive Initialization for 3D Path Planning in Dense Urban Environments
authors: Ruochen Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In dense urban environments, UAV path planning faces challenges from complex obstacles and strict safety constraints. This paper presents Pattern Search-Driven Particle Swarm Optimization (PSD-PSO), a hybrid algorithm integrating adaptive initialization, PSO-based global search, and pattern search local refinement. The objective function balances path length and obstacle avoidance, with linear interpolation ensuring smoothness. Experimental results demonstrate that PSD-PSO achieves faster convergence, higher path quality, and improved stability compared with traditional PSO and other algorithms.

title: IFS-SAM: An Intuitionistic Fuzzy Set Enhanced Framework for Pore Structure Segmentation in Carbonate Casting Thin-Section Images
authors: Xin Chen, Bowei Chen, Liumei Zhang, Zhilin Cheng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Pore-structure segmentation in plane-polarized light carbonate casting thin-section images is pivotal for linking microscale pore morphology to reservoir evaluation, yet it remains challenging by intrinsically uncertain boundaries induced by low porosity, micro-fractures, complex diagenesis, and dye–mineral interference. Existing approaches often rely on manually tuned heuristics or treat all annotation errors uniformly, which amplifies label noise around diffuse pore margins and reduces robustness across lithologies and imaging conditions. Here, we propose an intuitionistic fuzzy set enhanced segmentation framework that explicitly decomposes each pixel into membership, non-membership, and hesitancy to model uncertainty, and injects these descriptors into adaptive prompt selection, hesitancy-aware attention modulation, and an uncertainty-weighted optimization objective. We evaluated the framework on a carbonate thin-section dataset comprising 2835 color images acquired under plane-polarized light with strict specimen-level splitting. The framework outperforms strong segmentation baselines by 7.2 percent to 15.6 percent on the primary overlap metric. These results demonstrate that principled uncertainty modeling can systematically enhance generalizable pore segmentation, providing a reliable foundation for downstream quantitative pore characterization and decision support in geological appraisal workflows.

title: Cross-Modal Alignment and Fusion Network for Remote Sensing Image Segmentation
authors: Ziwen Fu, Huajun Shi, Zhan Guo
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the challenge of cross-modal feature alignment caused by information heterogeneity between RGB and Digital Surface Model (DSM) in multi-modal remote sensing image semantic segmentation, this paper proposes a novel Cross-Modal Alignment and Fusion Network (CAF-Net). This paper innovatively designs an asymmetric bidirectional prior-guided fusion module that decouples the feature interaction process across both channel and spatial dimensions. Specifically, it utilizes the channel-level semantic priors from the RGB modality to enhance the feature representation of the DSM, while leveraging the spatial-level geometric priors from the DSM to guide the boundary constraints of objects in the RGB modality. Furthermore, the network integrates a multi-level cross-modal Transformer at the bottleneck layer to synergistically extract the global context. Experimental results on the ISPRS Potsdam dataset demonstrate that CAF-Net can efficiently fuse complementary features, achieving state-of-the-art segmentation accuracy and significantly improving the edge sharpness of the segmented objects.

title: Multimodal Small Object Detection for UAV Aerial Images Based on Improved YOLOv12
authors: Wenjie Chen, Yin Liang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of tiny scale, weak features, and complex backgrounds in UAV aerial images, as well as the limited capability of the original attention and C3k2 module in capturing small object features and insufficient multimodal fusion in YOLOv12, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv12-based multimodal small object detection model. The original attention is replaced by LiteAttBlock to build the A2C2f_Lite module. The C3k2_Lite module is designed to replace the original C3k2 module for enhancing small object feature extraction. The MFA_Fuse module is introduced to achieve adaptive alignment and fusion of visible and infrared features. Experimental results show that the proposed model significantly improves small object detection accuracy and multimodal robustness with reasonable model complexity, which is suitable for real-world UAV aerial scenarios.

title: Intelligent Predictive Policing System Using AI for Crime Prevention Strategies
authors: Pandit Muskan Sharma, Sarika Kumari, Aasatha Kumari, Raju Ranjan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The present paper discusses the application of artificial intelligence to assist in predictive policing through the evaluation of crime data and the determination of meaningful patterns. The study analyses the crime trends with a dataset of 40,160 crime records gathered in a few cities of India (2020-2024), conducts basic statistical analysis, and tests various machinelearning models. Among all the models employed, the Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, SVM, and Random Forest models, Random Forest achieved the highest accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\approx 95 \%$</tex>). Other findings of the study include the apparent hot spots in major cities and the apparent variation in time of violent and non-violent crimes. Meanwhile, the work brings into the fore such critical issues as inequality of classes, bias in historical data, and the problem of equity and openness. The results imply that AI may assist in policing, although it will have to be applied in a responsible and ethically sound manner.

title: Research on Intelligent Calculation Method of Asset Impairment Loss Provision Based on Machine Learning
authors: Zhihan Ouyang, Yuanyue Zeng, Binrui Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This paper introduces machine learning as the model dynamic mechanism and builds an intelligent computing system specifically for real-time risk calculation under the strong support of deep trust features and expression. With the help of the time-varying attention mechanism that integrates deep learning and time series modeling, It can extract the irregular variation characteristics of risk indicators and achieve precise prediction and real-time adjustment of the adaptive adjustment amount of loan losses. In a rich and diverse dataset environment, it can demonstrate strong flexibility and universality, thereby enhancing the accuracy and sensitivity of risk measurement.

title: High-Precision and Efficient Robotic Assembly Strategy via Curriculum Reinforcement Learning
authors: Minghui Cheng, Jingyi Ou, Wenbo Zhu, Liang Cao, Haowen Tang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: High-precision robotic peg-in-hole assembly under tightened tolerances typically suffers from the curse of dimensionality and extreme sparse rewards, causing severe exploration stagnation in conventional reinforcement learning. To address this challenge without artificially decoupling the continuous assembly process, this paper proposes a progressive Curriculum Reinforcement Learning (CRL) framework based on dynamic tolerance constraints. The framework guides the agent through five progressive stages by smoothly shrinking the target hole size and tolerance threshold down to an extreme 0.8 cm. Furthermore, a Cross-stage Knowledge Preservation Mechanism (C-KPM) is introduced to enable continuous network weight inheritance, effectively eradicating catastrophic forgetting during difficulty leaps. Extensive simulation experiments demonstrate that our CRL-based strategy overwhelmingly outperforms standard baselines in both convergence speed and final success rate. This approach fully preserves end-to-end policy coherence, providing a highly efficient and robust paradigm for flexible manufacturing.

title: Deep Learning for Oral Cancer Detection: A Comparative Review
authors: Pravesh, Richa Srivastava, Ajeet Pratap Singh, Barkha Rohtagi, Narinder Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Oral cancer screening with deep learning (DL) techniques that make use of medical images i.e clinical camera images and histopathology slides has gaining interest for early detection of OPML. Oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC) remains a predominant malignancy with approx. 377,713 new cases worldwide in 2020, that also leads to poor survival rate in late-stage detection. Early intervention to diagnose the malignancy is therefore critical. This paper provides recent peer-reviewed studies from 2018 to 2024 that are focused on convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and advanced architectures such as hybrid models, attention mechanisms, and Vision Transformers (ViT) for OPML detection. This paper compares results of two primary modalities i.e clinical images and histopathological slides. Histology-based models provide relatively high performance, achieving approximately 99 % sensitivity and 97 % specificity, while models trained on clinical images illustrate slightly lower accuracy of around <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8}-\mathbf{9 9 \%}$</tex>. Table I summarizes various studies that includes detailing architecture, data, and performance metrics. The results show that CNN models like ResNet, DenseNet, and EfficientNet, as well as hybrid CNN-ML and Vision Transformer (ViT) models, perform very well. Recently, transformer models have started to match or even do better than CNNs. Challenges include limited data diversity, poor generalizability, and the need for explainable AI tools such as Grad-CAM. Whereas, DL shows high potential as a diagnostic tool, clinical validation across heterogeneous datasets remains essential for real-world deployment.

title: Research on Domain Adaptation Methods of Interpretable Machine Learning in Cross-Project Software Defect Prediction
authors: Haoxuan Zou, Laichao Qu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Cross-Project Defect Prediction (CPDP) is a key technology to address the "cold start" challenge in new projects, but it faces industrial implementation bottlenecks such as feature distribution drift between source and target domains and lack of model interpretability. This paper proposes a Graph Regularized Dictionary Learning framework (G-RDL) to tackle these challenges through a two-stage strategy of "alignment-screening" : first, achieving domain distribution alignment using a hybrid dictionary group, and then screening key features through an improved Weighted PageRank algorithm (WPRFS) to restore the physical interpretability of the model. Validated on five Java open-source projects from the PROMISE dataset, G-RDL achieves an average AUC of 0.796, representing an 18.5% improvement over traditional models such as SVM and maintaining a 7.7% advantage over the CSDP algorithm, particularly performing well in projects with extremely imbalanced class distributions. After WPRFS removes 60% of redundant features, the F1 score increases from 0.615 to 0.702, and core features such as Coupling Between Objects (CBO) screened by WPRFS provide clear evidence for defect diagnosis.

title: Exploring Influential Features in Graduate Outcomes Prediction Through Interpretable Modeling
authors: Suhail Shakeel, Pawan Kumar, Anik Acharjee
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: This study uses explainable artificial intelligence, no- tably LIME (Local Interpretable Modelagnostic Explanations), with machine learning to construct an integrated method for predicting whether a graduate student would find employment. The crucial problem of precisely predicting college graduates' job placement outcomes is addressed in this study. This subject remains partially addressed due to the complex and stochastic character of different impacting elements. We compiled a com- prehensive dataset encompassing academic performance metrics like marks and attendance and non academic indicators of professional development. Despite many attempts in the field, prior work has frequently lacked dataset comprehensiveness and rigorous application of advanced interpretability tools like explainable evaluation of different machine learning algorithms.

title: Research on Optimization and Reconstruction Methods of AI Empowered Innovation System Model Driven by Interdisciplinary Approach
authors: Liu Juanjuan, Pan Hongli
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: In response to the common problems of weak structural coupling, inefficient resource scheduling, and response delay in multi-component systems, this study innovatively integrates interdisciplinary AI empowerment optimization methods using graph neural networks (GNN), deep reinforcement learning (DRL), and multi-objective scheduling algorithms. By establishing a task topology network for knowledge graphs, the collaborative efficiency between components has been significantly improved; Adopting an adaptive allocation mechanism to implement a task node relationship graph for multi-dimensional tasks, enhancing information accessibility between modules; Form an iterative evolutionary loop of “perception decision optimization”. The experimental results show that this scheme outperforms traditional architectures in key indicators such as task execution success rate, resource utilization rate, and system collaboration accuracy, fully demonstrating its technical advantages and practical significance in intelligent system integration and dynamic environment adaptation.

title: Implementation of an Intelligent Identification and Tracking System for Urban Environmental Pollution Sources Based on Deep Learning
authors: Xin Jin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: In the process of modernization of urban environmental pollution control, the identification and tracking of pollution sources play a crucial role. To address the challenges of complex distribution and rapid dynamic changes of pollution sources, the system has constructed a technical framework for identification and tracking based on deep learning. By integrating multi-source data to establish a pollutant traceability and tracking model, convolutional neural networks are adopted to achieve pollutant localization, and then deep neural networks are used to realize pollutant path tracking and diffusion prediction. In the deployment strategy, sensor devices and computing hardware are built into an integrated system to complete the online, continuous source tracing and tracking tasks of environmental pollutants.

title: Research on Trajectory Tracking Algorithm for Multi-Joint Arm Coordination
authors: Cai Dianhan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: Aiming at the cooperative trajectory tracking problem of multi-joint arm system, this research proposes an integrated algorithm framework integrating adaptive control, anti-interference communication and energy-saving optimization, aiming at achieving high precision, strong robustness and low energy consumption.

title: Research on College English Teaching for Hearing-Impaired Students and Sign Language Translation Recognition Methods
authors: Dandan Sun, Yang Yu, Jinhu Lü, Chunfeng Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Based on a systematic analysis of current teaching methodologies and challenges in college English instruction for hearing-impaired students, this study proposes an innovative approach that deeply integrates English instruction with artificial intelligence technology. A smart auxiliary system for sign language translation and recognition is developed, leveraging the YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once version 8) model. The system facilitates communication between teachers and students by enabling bidirectional conversion between classroom speech and English sign language animations, as well as between students' sign language expressions and English texts. Training conducted on an experimental platform yielded an F1 score exceeding 0.90, with the Precision-Confidence Curve (PCC) and Recall-Confidence Curve (RCC) demonstrating high accuracy and recall rates even under low-confidence conditions. The Precision-Recall Curve (PR Curve) consistently resides near the upper-right quadrant, indicating stable performance across both metrics. Experimental results confirm that the YOLOv8-based sign language recognition method significantly enhances information absorption in English instruction for hearing-impaired students and improves teacher-student interaction quality, providing a practical technical solution and support for intelligent English teaching in higher education settings.

title: Anti-Saturation Flexible Prescribed Performance Control (AFPC) for 6-DOF Microgravity Vibration Isolation Systems
authors: Jian Liang, Shuquan Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: This paper presents a proposed Anti-saturation flexible prescribed-performance control (AFPC) scheme for six-degree-of-freedom (6-DOF) micro-gravity vibration-isolation systems. The scheme integrates three key modules: an adaptive-bandwidth extended state observer, a cascaded auxiliary dynamic system, and a coordinated anti-saturation mechanism. It enables saturation-driven dynamic boundary adjustment, resolving the rigidity of conventional PPC, balancing observation noise against accuracy, and eliminating observer drift. The proposed approach is integrated into the back-stepping framework for constrained error transformation and Lyapunov stability proof to ensure that all closed-loop signals are semi-globally uniformly ultimately bounded (SGUUB). Relaxed and tight constraint comparative simulations show that AFPC is a more effective solution to improve the problem of dynamic response in mechanical systems. Quantitative results show that AFPC achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0. 6 \%}$</tex> faster convergence, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0 \%}$</tex> higher steady-state accuracy, and 92% lower ITAE versus static PPC under strict constraints, while maintaining zero boundary violation under actuator saturation. This work advances constrained nonlinear control under actuator saturation and provides a practical paradigm for future space station scientific experiments requiring ultra-quiet microgravity environments.

title: PWMamba: Physics-Driven Wavelet State Space Model for Underwater Image Enhancement
authors: Baoshi Wei, Ao Yang, Yuanfa Hu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Underwater image enhancement is crucial for marine exploration but suffers from severe color distortion and blurring due to complex light absorption and scattering. While existing methods improve image quality, they struggle to balance global context modeling with computational efficiency and lack physical interpretability. We propose PWMamba, a novel physics-driven wavelet state space model. Specifically, our WaveMamba block uses the wavelet transform to decouple high-frequency textures and low-frequency structures. To preserve spatial integrity, we introduce a 2D Wavelet-Tree Scanning mechanism that enables linear-complexity, frequency-aware selective scanning while maintaining structural correlations. Furthermore, a Physical Model-Guided Block (PMGB) explicitly estimates transmission maps and background light to spatially modulate deep features using underwater optical priors. Crucially, we incorporate a physical constraint loss during training to ensure the learned parameters strictly conform to underwater optical laws. Extensive evaluations demonstrate our method effectively restores clear details and authentic colors in severely degraded scenes.

title: POSTER: SMAUG-SCA: Machine Learning Based Power Side-Channel Attack on SMAUG-T

title: A Hybrid Non-Resonant Node Filter Design Method Based on Knowledge-guided Machine Learning-assisted Global Optimization Techniques
authors: Zhengyu Sun, Bo Liu, Yi Wang, Abolfazl Mostaani, Muhammad Imran, Yang Yu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Electromagnetics, Antennas and Propagation
abstract: For microwave filters with non-resonant nodes, while routine network synthesis-based methods often require significant design expertise and a complicated design process, existing machine learning-assisted optimization methods often cannot handle filters with non-resonant nodes. Hence, there is a demand for an effective and easy-to-use design approach for filters with non-resonant nodes. To address this challenge, a hybrid approach utilizing filter design knowledge and a machine learning-assisted evolutionary algorithm is proposed. The key innovation is a systematic design procedure combining the advantages of both kinds of methods. Its effectiveness and efficiency are verified by a 3-2 and 6-2 waveguide filters with a single dangling node, and a third-order waveguide filter with multiple dangling nodes. Moreover, due to the avoidance of equivalent circuit models, the proposed hybrid approach is fully programmable.

title: User-Centric Stream Sensing for Grant-Free Access: Deep Learning with Covariance Differencing_supp1-3699766.pdf
authors: Hyun Jong Yang
publish: N/A
abstract: Grant-free (GF) access is essential for massive connectivity but faces collision risks due to uncoordinated transmissions. While user-side sensing can mitigate these collisions by enabling autonomous transmission decisions, conventional methods become ineffective in overloaded scenarios where active streams exceed receive antennas. To address this problem, we propose a differential stream sensing framework that reframes the problem from estimating the total stream count to isolating stream variations via covariance differencing. We analyze the covariance deviation induced by channel variations to establish a theoretical bound based on channel correlation for determining the sensing window size. To mitigate residual interference from finite sampling, a deep learning classifier is integrated. Simulations across both independent and identically distributed flat Rayleigh fading and standardized channel environments demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms conventional baselines and remains robust in overloaded scenarios.

title: High Step-Up Coupled-Inductor DC-DC Converter Using Switched-Capacitor and Coupled-Inductor Techniques
authors: Shuangming Duan, Jianwei Zhang, Zhichao Hu, Yizhen Qu, Ye Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a high-voltage-gain DC–DC converter featuring low switch voltage stress and high efficiency, which integrates a two-stage boost structure, a switched-capacitor network, and a coupled-inductor technique. The switched-capacitor network cascaded with the two-stage boost converter significantly enhances the voltage gain. Meanwhile, the coupled inductor further increases the voltage gain, reduces the voltage stress on semiconductor devices, enables zero-current switching (ZCS), and recycles the leakage energy, thereby improving overall efficiency. The proposed converter also exhibits continuous input current and a common ground between the input and output. The operating principle and steady-state characteristics of the converter are analyzed in detail, and a comprehensive mathematical analysis and performance comparison with recently reported high-gain converters are presented. In addition, a 150 W experimental prototype is implemented, based on which the dynamic behavior of the proposed converter is experimentally evaluated under input voltage step changes and load transients, demonstrating stable and well-damped transient responses.

title: Efficient Lyapunov Stability-Based Model Predictive Control of a Grid-Tied Inverter With an LCL Filter
authors: M. Nauman, W. Shireen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: A grid-tied inverter with an LCL filter is widely used for high-quality power injection into the grid. However, the LCL filter introduces resonant dynamics that complicate controller design, particularly in the presence of system constraints. Finite control set model predictive control (FCS-MPC) methods commonly used for such systems suffer from variable switching frequency and high computational burden. This brief proposes a computationally efficient continuous control set MPC (CCS-MPC) strategy. The proposed method incorporates constraints on the inverter-side filter current and duty cycle to ensure safe operation. A single-step prediction horizon together with a tailored active-set optimization algorithm reduces computational complexity. Asymptotic stability is established using a Lyapunov-based cost function and the passive structure of the system. Controller hardware-in-the-loop (C-HIL) results demonstrate improved dynamic performance, robustness to model mismatches, and computational efficiency compared with conventional FCS-MPC and CCS-MPC approaches.

title: Adaptive High-Order Supercritical Sliding Mode Control for Position Tracking of PMLSM Based on Control Barrier Function
authors: Zekun Wang, Chun He, Yong Li, Haohong Wu, Yifei Zhang, Fangrui Wei, Chenxiao Jiu, Qian Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Sliding mode control (SMC) is widely used in permanent magnet linear synchronous motor (PMLSM) servo systems. However, improving the antidisturbance performance of SMC requires increasing the control gain, and excessive gain may induce severe sliding mode chattering. To resolve this trade-off, this manuscript proposes an adaptive high-order supercritical sliding mode control (AHOSSMC) method for PMLSM servo systems. By integrating high-order sliding mode control (HOSMC) with an adaptive gain strategy based on control barrier function (CBF), the conflict between disturbance rejection and chattering suppression is effectively mitigated, and the friction force in the PMLSM servo system is better compensated. Both the high-order sliding mode algorithm and the adaptive law are improved to enhance the performance of the AHOSSMC. Moreover, the switching behavior of the adaptive law is explicitly addressed to ensure continuous variation of the adaptive gain. Finally, comprehensive experimental validation is conducted to demonstrate the superior position tracking performance of the proposed AHOSSMC.

title: Tube-Based Robust Smooth Transfer for Switched Systems
authors: Shengao Lu, Bo Li, Tong Wu, Lixian Zhang, Lin Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This note proposes a tube-based robust smooth transfer (TRST) control framework for a class of discrete-time switched systems under exogenous <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell _\infty$</tex-math></inline-formula> additive disturbances. TRST applies a set of smooth transfer (ST) controllers and stabilizing controllers alternately to guarantee the robust stability of system. Particularly, it ensures that the system state, control input, and their bumps during switches are all bounded within the respectively characterized tubes that depend on the disturbance amplitude. We derive control synthesis and tube-based analysis methods for generic asynchronously switched systems, which include the synchronous case as a special case. Furthermore, we propose a new criterion named absolute ST performance (ASTP) to evaluate the bump of the system induced by the disturbances, independent of system states. Finally, we employ an aero-engine control example to illustrate the application of TRST and show the advantage of ASTP over the commonly used state-dependent relative ST performance index.

title: Learning Feature Distributions via Wasserstein Measure Embeddings for Few-Shot Chest X-Ray Disease Classification
authors: Ayan Sar, Tanupriya Choudhury, Vartika Jain, Minakshi, Shilpi Sharma, Shilpa Gite
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Few-shot recognition in medical imaging remains challenging due to the long-tailed distribution of rare pathologies and the loss of spatial information in conventional feature pooling. We propose a novel Wasserstein Measure Embedding Network (WME-Net) that models convolutional feature maps as discrete probability measures rather than vectors. A learnable WME layer embeds images by computing Sliced Wasserstein distances to reference measures, preserving spatial and textural distributions critical for diagnosis. Integrated within an episodic few-shot framework, the proposed approach enables geometry aware comparison without external pre-training. Experiments on three benchmarks achieve up to +2.26% accuracy and +2.57% AUC gains with competitive efficiency. The results highlight the effectiveness of distribution of distribution-based representations for robust pattern recognition in data-scarce regimes.

title: Implementation of AI in Classroom and its effects on OBE Implementation
authors: Yazhinian S
