title: An Explainable XGBoost and Deep Learning Framework for Next-Day Rainfall Prediction
authors: Chandrakanta Rajput, Rajendra Bahadur Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The prediction of next-day rainfall with high reliability, faces difficulties because atmospheric systems operate through complex nonlinear dynamics. The research introduces an explainable data-driven system for predicting next-day rainfall in Aligarh, India by using daily meteorological records from the NASA POWER database between 2013 and 2022. The framework combines an ensemble learning model (XGBoost) with SHAP-based explainability and advanced deep learning architectures which include a hybrid GRU-LSTM recurrent neural network and a one-dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN). The method combines time-series data splitting with lag-based feature engineering to identify brief temporal patterns in weather data. The experimental results show that the XGBoost model surpasses deep learning models with an accuracy of 89.25%, precision of 87.89%, recall of 86.53% and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 7. 2 1 \%}$</tex>. SHAP analysis shows that precipitation together with humidityrelated variables, wind direction, dew point, and surface pressure, determines the occurrence of rainfall. The research findings demonstrate that ensemble learning with deep sequential models and explainability produces precise and understandable rainfall predictions that work for operational meteorological applications.

title: Hierarchical Nonlinear Flow-based Causal Representation Learning for Diffusion-based Counterfactual Generation
authors: Jian Zheng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Causal representation learning aims to recover latent factors that support intervention and counterfactual reasoning. Existing VAE-based causal models are limited in modeling nonlinear causal mechanisms, while diffusion models produce realistic samples but typically edit semantic latents without exact individual-level causal attribution. In this work, we propose HCNF-DiffAE, a causal framework that combines hierarchical nonlinear flow-based causal representation learning with diffusion-based counterfactual generation. Our model uses a causal normalizing flow to map exogenous variables to a structured latent causal space and conditions a diffusion decoder on these causal latents for high-fidelity reconstruction and counterfactual synthesis. We further introduce flow-based exact abduction, which exploits invertibility to perform individual-level inference and intervention in latent causal space rather than directly manipulating semantic codes. By combining structurally grounded causal encoding with visually faithful diffusion generation, the proposed framework provides a unified approach for precise and high-quality counterfactual generation.

title: Adaptive Vital-Sign Extraction Using Crested Porcupine Optimizer for VMD and ESPRIT in UWB Radar Sensing
authors: Weixin Zhang, Chengpei Tang, Sophie Li, Ji Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the increasing demand for daily health management, non-contact monitoring of vital signs such as heart rate (HR) and respiratory rate (RR) has attracted growing attention. Ultra-wide-band (UWB) radar, with its high range resolution and strong penetration capability, has emerged as an ideal platform for this purpose. However, accurate estimation of HR and RR remains challenging due to the weak amplitude of heartbeat signals, spectral leakage, and interference from respiratory harmonics.To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel method named CPO-VMD-ESPRIT, which integrates Crested Porcupine Optimizer (CPO)-optimized Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) with signal parameter estimation based on Estimation of Signal Parameters via Rotational Invariance Techniques (ESPRIT). Experiments using the Xethru X4M03 radar module on eight subjects under varying distances and angles demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior accuracy and stability compared with traditional VMD-FFT and ESPRIT-only approaches, with average RR and HR estimation accuracies exceeding 93% and 95%, respectively. These results indicate that CPO-VMD-ESPRIT is a reliable and effective solution for non-contact daily health monitoring.

title: Evo-Multi-SLM: Evolutionary Multi-Agent Logic Alignment for Small Language Models under Resource Constraints
authors: Lisheng Ni, Xinpeng Xu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Alignment of large language models usually relies on gradient-based methods such as RLHF and DPO. However, when resources are limited, there are computational, memory and data efficiency bottlenecks for small language models (SLM). This paper proposes an evolution-based multi-agent logical alignment framework, Evo-Multi-SLM. The framework is evaluated on mathematical reasoning tasks, demonstrating that evolutionary strategies achieve gradient-free alignment with competitive efficiency. Experiments show that its multiple metrics significantly outperform the baseline and LoRA fine-tuning, establishing the potential of evolutionary strategies in the efficient resource alignment of SLMs.

title: Tandem Spreading Multiple Access With Cascaded LT-RS Codes for mMTC in 6G IoT
authors: Kailin Wang, Guoyu Ma, Bo Ai, Guoyu Wang, Yiyan Ma, Jingya Yang, Mi Yang, Yunlong Lu, Guowei Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Next-generation multiple access (NGMA) technologies enable non-orthogonal transmissions to improve spectral efficiency, but at the cost of increased data collisions. In sixth-generation (6G) massive machine-type communications (mMTC), ultra-massive connectivity and stringent reliability requirements render conventional error control mechanisms insufficient, as fixed-rate forward error correction (FEC) lacks adaptability and automatic repeat request (ARQ) incurs prohibitive latency due to feedback dependence. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a tandem spreading multiple access (TSMA) scheme integrated with cascaded Luby transform–Reed–Solomon (LT–RS) codes for short-packet mMTC transmissions. The proposed cascaded LT–RS coding framework leverages the rateless property of LT codes and the strong burst-error correction capability of RS codes to dynamically generate redundancy and enhance transmission reliability under collision-prone access. By combining with TSMA, the proposed scheme effectively mitigates collision-induced impairments and supports efficient short-packet transmission in ultra-dense connectivity scenarios. A two-stage LT decoding strategy is further employed to improve decoding robustness under segment-level collision patterns. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed cascaded LT-RS codes-TSMA scheme significantly outperforms existing methods in terms of decoding reliability and collision recovery performance, highlighting its effectiveness for 6G mMTC applications.

title: Pretrained Mobility Transformer: A Foundation Model for Human Mobility
authors: Xinhua Wu, Haoyu He, Yanchao Wang, Qi R. Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Ubiquitous mobile devices are generating vast amounts of location-based service data that reveal how individuals navigate and utilize urban spaces in detail. In this study, we utilize these extensive, unlabeled sequences of user trajectories to develop a foundation model for understanding urban space and human mobility. We introduce the pretrained mobility transformer (PMT), which leverages the transformer architecture to process user trajectories in an autoregressive manner, converting geographical areas into tokens and embedding spatial and temporal information within these representations. Experiments conducted in three U.S. metropolitan areas over a two-month period demonstrate PMT’s ability to capture underlying geographic and socio-demographic characteristics of regions. The proposed PMT excels across various downstream tasks, including next-location prediction, trajectory imputation, and trajectory generation. These results support PMT’s capability and effectiveness in decoding complex patterns of human mobility, offering new insights into urban spatial functionality and individual mobility preferences.

title: Deep Learning with Predictive Uncertainty Estimation for Extreme Rainfall Event Detection in the Indian Himalayas
authors: Tejinder Kaur, M. Samba Siva Reddy, Shiwani, Anjali Thakur, Chetna Dahiya, Asha Bhatt
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The frequency of the emergence of a cloudburst has increased significantly and the rate of clouds is measured up to 100 millimeters per hour which has made these phenomena the dominant and apex destructive hydrometeorological hazards in the hilly regions of India. In 2025 alone, the region in the upper northern mountainous zone, i.e., Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, and Jammu & Kashmir, experienced around 765 incidents of extreme heavy rainfall, which is the highest that was recorded in the last five years. Uttarakhand recorded about 25 direct instances of cloud burst occurrences from July to August 2025, causing flash flooding and landslides that made cause discomfort to hydropower infrastructure/transportation networks. Between midmountain June and September 2025 Himachal Pradesh alone reported 266 deaths and economic losses to the tune of Rs 4080 crore of which at least 17 are proven to have died because of the cloudburst incidences. In order to overcome the imbalance, we hereby propose a hybrid CNN-BiLSTM-Attention architecture with Bayesian probabilistic output layer. The model makes use of multimodal inputs, based on INSAT-3D/3DR satellite imagery, Doppler radar mosaics and topographic covariance and ERA5 reanalysis data. Over time and with input BiLSTM component learning temporal dynamics of storm evolution and CNN, spatial convection structures are unraveled and attention mechanism highlights the high intensity cores of prevailing rainfall. Using Monte Carlo dropout ensembles improves the interpretability using SHAP and Grad-CAM analysis.

title: End-to-End Tri-Modal Deep Learning for Cognitive Load: CNN-BiLSTM EEG, CNN-LSTM Physiology, and Dense Gaze Fusion on CLARE
authors: Jyothika Rajesh, Gaurav Agarwal, Tanish Dwivedi, Shweta Tiwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Managing cognitive load is central to designing adaptive human-computer interaction - particularly in contexts where personalized learning must respond to individual user needs, or where system reliability is nonnegotiable in safety-critical environment. This paper presents a real-time multimodal deep-learn configuration that attracts EEG, peripheral physiological signals and gaze information to determine the mental workload of someone. Much of the work in the past only focuses on one modality or merges features naively and this is simply all that makes the models weak and fails to take advantage of cross-modality. In the combination of modelling the brain activity, autonomic arousal and visual attention, there is a far better signal of cognitive-load in our system. The architecture can be thought of as dividing the data types into distinct deep subnetworks, namely, an encoder consisting of CNN-BiLSTM (when applied to EEG) and a CNNLSTM combo with a pair of plain fully-connected layers (when applied to the gaze features). These are then combined with cross-modal attention which balances out each modality on the fly rather than simply overlaying them. This will enable the model to concentrate on what is important at a specific time. We tested and trained the network with a publicly available multimodal dataset in which the protocol is subject independent and real-time sliding-window labelling. The experiments demonstrate that trimodal fusion including attention is significant in comparison to unimodal and bi-modal baseline, emphasizing the effectiveness of the offered approach to adaptive interactive systems in reality.

title: An Empirical Analysis of Multi-Objective Scheduling Mechanisms for Scheduling Workflows in Cloud Environment
authors: Surya Vikram Singh, Pooja Khanna, Puneet Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Cloud is an eminent technique for convoluted business, research, industry, and computing applications used to provide on-demand utilities using workflows. Workflow management is considered as an eminent concern in the cloud environment because of its extensive demand in various sectors aimed at absolute implementation of workflows by focusing on their QoS constraints. For the scheduling of workflows efficiently, it is important in cloud domain to equipping the resources virtually to the workflows, functionally by minimizing execution time and increasing resource employment in the cloud computing domain. Assignment for web resources in the cloud domain using workflow scheduling is complicated because of the computationally rigorous essence of the workflows, the mutual dependency of jobs, and the multifarious cloud-based resources. In assigning resources, the execution time and execution outlay are examined as a crucial problem while executing jobs in the cloud domain, which can perchance decay the standard of service delivered to end users. This study analyzes the application of heuristic methods, meta-heuristic methods and hybrid methods in cloud workflow assignment and resource allocation problems and did an empirical survey of various methods and algorithms to identify the scope of research in cloud workflow scheduling and resource allocation.

title: Study on spatial transmission characteristics of Blue-green laser in seawater channel
authors: Lu Ding, Chensheng Wang, Ming Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In underwater wireless laser communication systems, the attenuation characteristics of seawater channels serve as critical constraints on the effective propagation distance and received optical power of optical signals. Based on measured water quality parameters of typical offshore seawater, this paper conducts a theoretical analysis on the attenuation characteristics of 532nm blue-green laser in natural seawater, and simulates its entire propagation process in seawater channels using the Monte Carlo (MC) method. The received optical power is evaluated under different water conditions and propagation distances, and the spatial power distribution at different receiver positions is quantitatively characterized. The radiative transfer model is then employed to fit the simulation results. When the relative power error is below 2.17%, the agreement between the spatial power distributions predicted by the two models reaches up to 90%, indicating that the proposed model can efficiently estimate the received power at arbitrary receiver positions under short-range and small-divergence conditions while significantly reducing the computational cost of full MC simulations. In addition, a spot expansion model is established to study the influence of seawater properties on the spatial characteristics of Gaussian beams. The results show that the 1/e spot radius of Gaussian beams, defined by the light intensity ratio, exhibits a strict linear correlation with propagation distance, and its linear growth coefficient is consistent with the divergence half-angle of the laser source. These results provide a useful basis for rapid link evaluation and preliminary design of underwater wireless optical communication systems.

title: Dual-Modal Vehicle Detection Algorithm from UAV Perspective Based on YOLOv11
authors: Yujie Zhou, Shengnan Lu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address low accuracy and high missed/false detection rates of single-modal UAV small-target detection, this paper proposes HMF-YOLOv11, a dual-modal (RGB+IR) vehicle detection algorithm for UAVs. The model adopts a heterogeneous dual-branch backbone: a lightweight MobileNet-based branch for shallow detailed features and a YOLOv11-based deep branch for deep semantic features. A CAAM-FM module is designed for heterogeneous feature fusion via dynamic channel alignment and adaptive attention. Experiments on DroneVehicle show it improves precision, recall, and mAP@0.5 by 7.8, 4.4, and 11.6 percentage points vs. the single-modal IR baseline, and outperforms the dual-modal baseline by 6.3% in mAP@0.5 with fewer parameters and faster inference. Ablation experiments confirm the effectiveness of the lightweight backbone and fusion module, and generalization tests on LLVIP show excellent low-light robustness.

title: A Dynamic Cryptographic Obfuscation Scheme for Power Grid Communication Security
authors: Gang Zhao, Yide Zhang, Yijie Xun, Gang Liang, Bomin Mao, Xuerui Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the emergence of new attacks like new side-channel attacks and smart traffic analysis, traditional static encryption and fixed obfuscation can not meet the high-security needs of power grid communication. Therefore, this paper proposes a dynamic cryptographic obfuscation scheme featuring two integrated layers: encryption and obfuscation. At the encryption layer, the Rivest-Shamir-Adleman (RSA) algorithm is used to package dynamically generated Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) session keys for each packet. This effectively mitigates historical data leakage when keys are leaked. At the obfuscation layer, a ring buffer and modular arithmetic spread encrypted payloads evenly. This uniform byte distribution minimizes statistical edge effects, significantly degrading payload-focused machine learning steganalysis.Simulations show the scheme maintains deterministic low latency without severe jitter, providing a resilient and stealthy confidential communication mechanism for power grids.

title: Research on the identification and processing method of AIS trajectory anomaly data based on time distribution
authors: Peiliang Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In order to eliminate the abnormal data of the Automatic Identification System (AIS) caused by environmental interference, equipment failure and human operation error, etc., and improve the effectiveness of data analysis, a novel method for identifying and processing AIS anomalies of vessels in navigation based on time distribution is proposed. Firstly, the AIS update interval is analyzed statistically to determine the distribution rule and identify data anomalies. Then, a Generalized Regression Neural Network (GRNN) model is used for predictive interpolation of the abnormal data. The feasibility and effectiveness of the method are verified using a specific water area in Zhoushan, China, as the study region. The experimental results demonstrate that abnormal data in AIS locus can be identified effectively through the statistical distribution of update interval, and the interpolation effect of GRNN algorithm is significantly better than that of back propagation (BP) neural network algorithm and cubic spline interpolation algorithm. The research results can provide references for AIS data mining and analysis, ship behavior analysis and supervision and other related fields.

title: Machine Learning Approach in LncRNA and Diseases Applications
authors: Yasir Khan, Ayush Kumar Sharma, Raheel Hassan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Long non-coding RNAs (IncRNAs) have stated their role in the disease pathogenesis of human beings, especially in cancer and neurology conditions. The given analysis covers the development of machine learning methods of IncRNA functional prediction including classic and the most modern techniques presented in Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). We analytically compare computational systems to solve fundamental challenges such as scarcity of data, constraints of sequence conservation, and imbalance of types of classes. We provide the analysis showing that GNN-based models, especially, HGC-GAN performs extremely well in terms of AUC above 0.95 because they employ the intricate biological networks. The clinical implication is shown by the effective discovery of the FDA approved biomarkers such as PCA3 and new therapeutic targets. This piece of work denotes strategic considerations regarding multiple modal data unification and clarifying AI tactics necessary to convert computational findings to clinical use.

title: A Transformer-Enabled Approach for Diabetic Retinopathy Classification from OCT Imaging
authors: Ojas Shrivastava, Manoj Diwakar, Neeraj Kumar Pandey, Aditya Joshi, Sanjay Roka, Prabhishek Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: It is well established that diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a leading cause of blindness; therefore, there is an urgent need for automated and scalable mechanisms for screening DR patients. In this study, we propose a framework to predict DR from optical coherence tomography (OCT) images using a Vision Transformer (ViT) based architecture. This method is unique in that it does not require the traditional method of manually extracting retinal features from OCT images or manual segmentation of OCT images into different layers for the purposes of building machine learning models. Instead, the proposed framework builds a feature set directly from the OCT scans by means of a proprietary patch embedding and transformer-based self-attention model that can learn to identify discriminative patterns from the OCT scans. The proposed framework consists of a preprocessing step, as well as a training/testing partitioning of the datasets using a stratified partitioning approach and a transformer-based training process with an additional dense decision final layer utilizing transfer learning techniques (AdamW and cross-entropy loss) and utilizing backpropagation. In trials conducted on the OLIVES dataset, the proposed method converged very quickly and exhibited excellent generalization capabilities, achieving 98.35 % validation accuracy after eighty epochs. The results indicate that the ViT has the capability to interpret the OCT biomarkers and provides a solid basis for DR screening in a clinical setting. Furthermore, the proposed approach highlights the advantages of using transformer-based self-attention for the automated diagnosis of retinal diseases and the future potential for computer-aided healthcare for detecting retinal diseases.

title: Short-Term Traffic Flow Prediction in Place Charles de Gaulle, France: A Study Based on Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Kanghao Guo, Yishi Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This study focuses on short-term traffic flow prediction in Place Charles de Gaulle, France, aiming to explore the temporal patterns of traffic flow and provide support for optimizing urban traffic management strategies. Using 60-day traffic data collected by Telraam devices at Charles de Gaulle Square in Paris (from June 11th to August 10th, 2020), the research centers on hourly traffic flow as the core indicator and constructs a prediction model combining Long Short-Term Memory, Dropout, and Dense layers. In handling time - series data, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) serves to capture prolonged temporal dependencies, a core requirement for sequential pattern learning. To safeguard against overfitting—a common pitfall in neural network training—Dropout is deployed, which works by randomly nullifying neuron activations during the training process. Parallelly, the Dense layer augments the model's proficiency in distilling sophisticated feature correlations, enabling more nuanced exploitation of inter - feature relationships. After 300 epochs of training, the losses of both the training set and the validation set stabilized at approximately 0.06, indicating good convergence and generalization ability. This research substantiates the applicability of machine learning methodologies in short - term traffic flow prediction, while concurrently establishing a theoretical and empirical underpinning. Such a foundation enables the advancement of intelligent traffic regulation initiatives and scientific route planning endeavors, facilitating the translation of algorithmic insights into tangible improvements in traffic system efficiency.

title: A Machine Learning Study for Fake News Detection
authors: Laxmi Saraswat, Simar Preet Singh, Sidharth Quamara
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: We have witnessed a change from our old traditional method of print media to social media platforms in this new age of technology and computing. Everybody has utilized various social media platforms to stay updated about events occurring globally. However, the news and sources on these social networking sites are frequently unreliable. As a result of misinformation spreading, this causes information pollution. Recognizing bogus material is the first step in stopping its dissemination on social media. This study provides a road map of previous research efforts aimed at developing a reliable system for automatic identification. Several supervised machine learning system models that are intended to identify false news can be used to do this. Fake news is categorized using various machine learning models which analyze the patterns and features to distinguish the information. Using various tools such as Python and NLP, BERT securing 97% accuracy and precision 95%, outperform on traditional machine learning and earlier Deep Learning Models.

title: Critical-State Guided Multi-Dimensional Feature Fusion Attack for Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based UAV Navigation
authors: Yirui Wang, Zhenxiang Huo, Chun Liu, Shiwei Fu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is prone to adversarial attacks, posing potential risks to its application in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) navigation. Research on adversarial attacks will reveal vulnerabilities in the algorithms and lay the foundation for enhancing their robustness. To address the inability of existing attack methods to accurately identify critical decision states, this paper proposes a critical-state guided multi-dimensional feature fusion (MDFF) attack method. Specifically, it combines the environmental risk and action confidence with action entropy and Q-value difference of the policy network—four complementary metrics—to derive an indicator of system vulnerability. Vulnerable navigation moments are identified across four dimensions: environmental risk level, policy stability, decision uncertainty and value estimation consistency. To validate the effectiveness of this attack method, various experiments were conducted on the AirSim simulation platform. The results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a greater average reward reduction than uniform attacks at a 60% attack rate, with a reward reduction approximately three times that achieved by traditional random-time attacks. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly reduces resource consumption while maintaining high attack effectiveness, thereby providing a novel framework for the security evaluation of DRL-based UAV navigation systems.

title: A Study on Tombstone Technology for Multi-Priority Biometric Matching Tasks
authors: Rongxin Qian
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the issues of high response latency for high-priority tasks and reduced matching efficiency due to low-priority tasks seizing core resources in biometric matching scenarios, this paper proposes a multi-priority biometric matching task scheduling scheme based on tombstone technology. It models multi-priority biometric matching tasks, then adapts register-level tombstone technology, combined with resource isolation for multiple priority tasks within a single instance and flexible suspension techniques, the mechanism achieves rapid response and efficient matching for high-priority tasks. This mechanism deploys multiple processes/threads corresponding to different priority matching tasks on each computing node, marking resource occupancy status through register-level tombstones. When a high-priority task is initiated, it quickly suspends or reduces the resource allocation of low-priority tasks, releases register resources, and loads the memory data required for the high-priority task, ensuring immediate startup of high-priority tasks. Experimental results show that, compared to traditional scheduling methods, this scheme reduces the response time of high-priority biometric matching tasks by 32.7%~45.2%, lowers the starvation rate of low-priority tasks to below 1.3%, and improves system resource utilization by 28.5%~36.1%. It effectively resolves the scheduling bottleneck of multi-priority biometric matching tasks, enhancing the overall performance and stability of the system.

title: A Unified Execution Semantics Layer for Structured and Sparse Kernels
authors: Chaochao Yang, Hu Ren, Chengpeng Zhao, Fei Gao, Zengran Ge, Xiuxi Zhai
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Structured kernels and sparse kernels are often described differently at the frontend. However, on scratchpad- or LDM-based backends, they must ultimately address the same execution questions: how to tile, which objects should reside locally, when to move data, and when to write back. This paper proposes a unified execution semantics layer. It represents a kernel using its iteration domain, object roles, set-valued access mappings, target-position mapping, update mode, and pure computation body. Based on this interface, we divide the execution semantics into three layers: description, legality, and lowering. In the SpMV case, we clarify that the semantic iteration unit is an output row, not a nonzero element. Thus, accumulation within a row is part of the body, while the target-position update remains assignment. Experiments demonstrate that this interface allows reuse of the same frontend description across multiple executors. This showcases its feasibility on the Sunway platform. It achieves an order of magnitude end-to-end speedup on structured stencil workloads and delivers performance comparable to specialized parallel implementations on selected SpMV workloads. The current method mainly applies to single-step, gather-dominant computation patterns whose output locations can be determined statically.

title: Early Academic Performance Warning in Online Education via Dual-Stream Heterogeneous Graph and Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks
authors: Hong Wang, Ziyi Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Early academic performance warning in online education Data Mining (EDM). Timely identification of at-risk students during course progression can provide effective grounds for personalized intervention for both teachers and students. Existing methods, however, still face significant limitations in mining heterogeneous relationships, capturing temporal dynamic features, and fitting non-linear patterns, which constrain predictive performance. This paper proposes a hybrid predictive model, the Dynamic Heterogeneous Graph-KAN (DyHG-KAN). The model integrates heterogeneous graphs, time-series modelling, and the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) to predict academic performance across multiple time windows in online education. DyHG-KAN employs a dual-stream Heterogeneous Graph Transformer (HGT) module to extract global static heterogeneous association features and cross-time-slice dynamic evolution features from educational data. These features are then adaptively fused through a gating mechanism and fed into the KAN prediction layer to generate academic performance warning results. Experiments on the Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD) use seven evenly spaced time points within the first 50% of the course as prediction nodes. Results show that DyHG-KAN outperforms baseline models in accuracy and F1 scores across all time windows. The improvements are particularly significant during the early stage. Ablation studies further confirm the synergistic contributions of heterogeneous graph modelling, time-series modelling, and the KAN module to the overall performance. The proposed method can effectively support practical applications of early academic performance warning systems in online education and provides a methodological reference for early intervention in teaching.

title: Protein Phosphorylation Site Prediction Based on Gated Fusion of CNN-BiLSTM and Transformer
authors: Huaizhou Yang, Xinyang Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Protein phosphorylation is a critical post-translational modification (PTM). Accurate prediction of phosphorylation sites is of great significance for understanding cell signaling and disease mechanisms. Existing deep learning prediction models often struggle to simultaneously capture both local conserved motifs and long-range global dependencies when extracting sequence features; moreover, simple concatenation strategies in multi-feature fusion are prone to introducing noise and causing the curse of dimensionality. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel CNN-BiLSTM and Transformer architecture equipped with a learnable Gated Attention Fusion mechanism for adaptive feature-level fusion. Extensive experiments are conducted on two benchmark datasets of serine/threonine (S/T) and tyrosine (Y) sites. Results show that the model achieves strong performance on S/T sites (ACC=0.8513, AUC=0.9286), and demonstrates excellent generalization on the small-sample Y sites (ACC=0.9048, AUC=0.9184). The proposed dynamic fusion strategy effectively overcomes feature redundancy in traditional methods, providing an efficient and general network architecture for intelligent biological sequence analysis.

title: Nuclear Graph-Guided Multiple Instance Learning for Weakly Supervised Tumor Region Discovery
authors: Jiachen Yang, Andi Duan, Guipeng Lan, Xiaohua Yin, Shuai Xiao, Qinggang Meng, Baihua Li
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Weakly supervised tumor localization in whole-slide images (WSIs) remains challenging due to the absence of region level annotations and the difficulty of capturing diagnostically relevant cellular organization under gigapixel resolution. Most existing multiple instance learning (MIL) methods rely primarily on patch-level appearance features, overlooking the nuclear structural patterns that underlie pathological interpretation. We propose Nuclear Graph-Guided Multiple Instance Learning (NGG-MIL), a weakly supervised framework that explicitly incorporates nuclear-level structural information into WSI analysis. Within each patch, nuclei are represented as a Direction Weighted Nuclear Graph, where edges encode both spatial proximity and nuclear orientation consistency. Graph Neural Network (GNN) is employed to learn structure-aware patch representations, which are subsequently aggregated through Attention Based MIL for slide-level prediction. The learned patch attention scores are spatially reprojected to generate interpretable tumor probability maps without requiring region-level supervision. Extensive experiments on three public breast cancer WSI datasets (CAMELYON16, TCGA-BRCA, and BRACS) demonstrate that NGG-MIL consistently surpasses strong MIL baselines in both slide-level classification performance and interpretability of localization maps, achieving consistent AUC improvements and competitive F1 scores across datasets.

title: A Deep Neural Network-Based Adaptive Gamification Approach for Virtual Learning
authors: Yi Lin, Jingjing Chen, Feng Chen, Weiquan Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Learning Technologies
abstract: Gamification learning in virtual reality (VR) environments is extensively utilized to stimulate learner motivation and enhance engagement. However, most existing gamification approaches rely on manually predefined rules or static matching, limiting their adaptability to dynamic changes in learning motivation and behaviors throughout the learning process. Accordingly, we propose a Deep Neural Network-based Adaptive Gamification for Virtual Learning (DAG-VL) method and integrate it into an instructional objective-oriented virtual learning pedagogical framework to facilitate adaptive gamification. Within this framework, structured instructional activities are designed according to predefined instructional objectives, and learner feedback data drive the adaptive gamification mechanism, which invokes DAG-VL to dynamically optimize gamification element configurations for subsequent activities. By integrating individualized incentives into the learning process, the mechanism strengthens learner autonomy and competence, thereby encouraging active interaction and improving learning outcomes. DAG-VL incorporates Hexad user types (a learner profiling model categorizing learners into six motivational orientations), alongside learning behavioral characteristics and task contextual information as input to train an end-to-end neural network model, enabling the automatic capture of nonlinear relationships between learner characteristics and learning tasks, facilitating dynamic recommendation of gamification elements. Experimental results indicate that DAG VL exhibits significant advantages in improving academic performance, promoting the practical application of knowledge, and providing a positive user experience. This study offers a reusable framework and implementation pathway for adaptive gamification instructional design in virtual learning.

title: A 6.3–18.4 GHz I/Q Receiver With RF and LO Path Reconfigurability for 6G FR3 Applications in 22-nm FD-SOI
authors: Haisu Ju, Yingtao Zou, Gabriel M. Rebeiz
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a 6.3–18.4GHz in-phase and quadrature (I/Q) direct down-conversion receiver featuring reconfigurability in both the radio frequency (RF) and local oscillator (LO) paths. The receiver comprises a multi-band reconfigurable RF front-end, double-balanced passive I/Q mixers, an I/Q LO generation network with a tunable I/Q vector correction circuit, and wideband baseband amplifiers. The integrated reconfigurable RF front end enables optimized performance across different bands. The LO drivers and the polyphase filter (PPF) are co-designed to maximize the overall LO chain voltage gain with low power consumption. Also, the tunable LO I/Q vector correction circuit is implemented at the LO output drivers to minimize I/Q phase error over a wide frequency range while introducing minimal additional voltage loss. Fabricated in 22-nm CMOS fully depleted silicon-on-insulator (FD-SOI) technology, the receiver achieves a measured 3-dB conversion gain (CG) of 20dB at 6.3–18.4GHz, a noise figure (NF) of 2.4–5.3dB, an image-rejection ratio (IRR) exceeding 40dB, and an input 1-dB compression point (IP1dB) greater than –26dBm, with an average power consumption of 79 mW. The receiver targets high-data-rate 6G frequency range 3 (FR3) wireless communication applications.

title: State-Aware Attention Refinement Transformer: Progressive Purification Tower and Historical State Retrieval for Industrial Process Modeling
authors: Hongrui Liu, Yalin Wang, Diju Liu, Cao Song, Yitao Chen, Chenliang Liu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Online prediction of key quality indicators is a fundamental requirement in the process control of complex industrial systems. High prediction accuracy hinges on the effective representation of typical operating states and the modeling of long-range temporal dependencies, as typical states capture dominant process dynamics over finite intervals, while long-term dependencies reflect gradual evolution and cumulative effects that influence future quality outcomes. However, industrial process data are often highly redundant and subject to significant temporal delays, making it challenging for predictive models to extract representative conditions and characterize long-horizon behavioral patterns. These limitations ultimately constrain both predictive accuracy and generalization capability. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a state aware attention refinement Transformer network (SAAR-Former) for industrial quality indicator prediction. In the first stage, a progressive refined attention mechanism is introduced to suppress redundant information and enhance the representation of typical operating states. To mitigate potential information loss during this refinement process, a pre-training reconstruction strategy is employed to preserve the integrity and fidelity of the learned features. In the second stage, a state neighbor-assisted prediction mechanism is developed to identify and fuse historically similar operating states, with the aim of overcoming significant time delay effects and strengthening long-range temporal dependency modeling. Experimental evaluations on two real industrial process datasets demonstrate that SAAR-Former improves prediction performance by 0.078 in the coefficient of determination (R2), and reduces the root mean square error (RMSE) and mean absolute error (MAE) by 8.19% and 9.44%, respectively, compared with the standard Transformer baseline.

title: Self-adaptive energy-aware power management for CubeSat electrical power systems under dynamic orbital conditions
authors: Youssef Akarne, Imad Boulafrouh, Abderrahman Aberda, Mohammed Amlieh, Abdelouahad El Fatimy
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The operational longevity of CubeSat missions is frequently compromised by the tight coupling between limited energy harvesting, battery degradation, and thermal instability. To address these conflicting constraints, this study proposes a robust Energy Management System (EMS) that synergizes Model Predictive Control (MPC) with a bio-inspired Sparrow Search Algorithm (SSA). Unlike conventional static schedulers, the proposed SSA-MPC framework dynamically optimizes control parameters to navigate the non-convex landscape of orbital power flows, explicitly integrating non-linear battery aging models and radiative thermal dynamics into the decision process. Comparative simulations under realistic LEO conditions demonstrate that the SSA-MPC strategy significantly outperforms baseline architectures. Specifically, the proposed method increases critical load availability from 90.5% to 99.9% while virtually eliminating load shedding events. Furthermore, the strategy enhances system sustainability by reducing residual energy waste by 17%, lowering peak internal temperatures by 2◦C, and improving end-of-mission battery State-of-Health. Validated through extensive Monte Carlo robustness analyses, this framework offers a resilient, computationally efficient solution for autonomous energy regulation in next-generation nanosatellites, ensuring reliable performance even under stochastic environmental uncertainties.

title: A Comparative Study of Chaos Theory and Deep Learning Techniques for Nifty 50 Price Forecasting
authors: Nidhi Sharma, Manisha Jailia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The Nifty 50 index prediction process presents significant challenges to researchers. The prediction difficulty arises from three core characteristics which include the system's nonpredictable behavior and its capacity to forecast certain parts of its movement patterns. The system exhibits these characteristics through the combined effects of various elements which include trader actions and institutional trading patterns and overall market conditions. The research study we present conducts a comparative evaluation between two widely used techniques which are chaos theory and deep learning to forecast short-term price movements. The research study uses Nifty 50 market data which researchers collected daily for more than two decades. The market operates as a system which exhibits nonlinear behavior because market conditions depend on initial market conditions. The research study evaluates the performance of deep learning models through a comparison with other systems. The assessment shows that chaos theory creates market structure and regime transition insights which people can understand while deep learning systems provide better performance and more precise prediction results during times of market unrest. The research study introduces a hybrid forecasting system which uses two strengths to identify different market conditions. The model uses chaos diagnostics to determine market conditions which enable the system to switch between chaos-based and deep learning systems for improved forecasting results under various market conditions.

title: Intelligent Network Anomaly Detection Using LSTM Autoencoders and Transformer Attention
authors: Vansh Pundir, Km Meenakshi, Ajay Rana
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Network security has seen a sea change in its concept with the development of sophisticated and complex cyber-attacks every day. The conventional methods for anomaly detection based on rules are unable to handle these zero-day and novel kinds of attacks, as the detection is done by the help of predefined signatures of the attack. This paper proposes an artificial intelligence-driven novel anomaly detection system in networks, which combines LSTM Autoencoder and Transformer attention mechanism for enhanced learning of temporal features and context-awareness. Extensive experiments conducted on three benchmark datasets namely CIC-IDS2017, UNSW-NB15, and In-SDN resulted in 96.8 percent detection accuracy, with better performance than several contemporary approaches, and also reduced the false positive rate. It can detect different types of attacks such as DDoS, brute force, and Advanced Persistent Threat (APTs).

title: Evidential Cross-Modal Alignment and Fine-Grained Enhancement for Robust Multimodal Medical Diagnosis
authors: Bangyi Zeng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Addressing the need for auxiliary diagnosis in scenarios with limited medical resources, this paper investigates a multimodal medical diagnosis method jointly driven by chest X-ray images and radiological text to enhance the automation of medical information interpretation and the efficiency of diagnostic support. Multimodal medical diagnosis faces several key challenges. First, the evidence provided by different modalities regarding the same disease label often varies in terms of supporting strength, representational form, and semantic focus, which can easily compromise the reliability and interpretability of diagnostic conclusions. Second, lesions in chest X-ray images typically exhibit small scale, low salience, and scattered distribution, making it difficult to fully characterize disease-related local anomaly patterns. Third, medical images and clinical texts inherently differ in information organization, descriptive levels, and semantic granularity, further complicating cross-modal semantic association and information integration. To address these issues, this paper proposes PatientEviNet, a multimodal diagnostic framework for joint modeling of dual-view chest X-rays and text. It introduces an evidence-opinion-uncertainty learning mechanism at the label level and combines it with Dempster-Shafer rules to achieve robust fusion of image, text, and dual-view information. To enhance fine-grained lesion representation capabilities, this paper designs an attention enhancement module in the image encoding stage to strengthen the ability to distinguish key regions and local abnormal features within a controlled computational cost.

title: Historical Aware Visual Place Recognition with Gated Grouped Attention and Degradation Embedding Adaptation
authors: Xiaohan Gao, Zhinong Zhong, Qingren Jia, Anran Yang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Visual Place Recognition (VPR) matching historical query images against modern street view databases presents significant challenges due to temporal disparities and severe domain shifts. In this paper, we propose HA-VPR, a robust framework for historical urban images. Built upon the CricaVPR architecture, HA-VPR integrates the Local-Global Attention Guidance (LGAG) module and introduces a Historical Head coupled with a HistoricalDegrade module. This design simulates historical image degradations to enable effective lightweight fine-tuning using exclusively modern street view data. Experimental results demonstrate that compared to the original model, HA-VPR improves R@1 by 1.3% and R@5 by 1.7% on the historical AmsterTime dataset. While on datasets with extreme lighting and weather conditions (e.g., SVOX-Night/Sun/Snow), R@1 increases by 1.8% to 2.2%. Besides, performance on standard modern benchmarks such as Pitts250k remains stable, with fluctuations within ±1%.

title: AWCT-DRG: Implicit Space Regularized GAN with Adaptive Window Convolution for Text Generation
authors: Shupu Wu, Zhilin Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Text generation models combining Transformers with latent space adversarial training face two key challenges: training instability and attention redundancy. To tackle these challenges, we introduce a model integrating adaptive dynamic convolution with stable adversarial training. It embeds an adaptive dynamic convolution module and grouped feedforward network into the Transformer encoder to explicitly capture local dependencies and reduce parameter redundancy. To stabilize adversarial training, we adopt a relative average generative adversarial network with zero-center gradient penalty, ensuring negative eigenvalues of the Jacobian matrix at equilibrium and theoretically guaranteeing local convergence. A semantic mutual information loss further enforces decoder compatibility with the latent generator, improving semantic consistency and text diversity. Experiments on MSCOCO and WMTNews reveal that our approach surpasses existing baselines across both quality and diversity.

title: Facial Emotion Recognition for Distress and Depression Prediction: A Comprehensive Literature Review
authors: Aarav Govil, Garima Bohra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Recently, the further development of the Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) technologies has turned it into a simple way of classifying into a strong tool that can be used to evaluate the psychological condition of fear, chronic stress and depression. The paper presents the review of the literature published in 2023-2025 to describe the present state of FER research. We emphasize the fact that the field has shifted focus of analyzing non-dynamic images to dynamic, video-based systems and multimodal learning to create temporally conscious systems by explainable AI. Although we considered many datasets and models with accuracy, our assessment displays a well-defined approach to such methods, which take an almost temporal orientation. Nevertheless, there are numerous issues even with such progress. There are significant challenges to the growth of FER into an effective tool in early mental distress detection, which we outline as data bias, poor external validity to real life conditions, and challenges in clinical interpretation. It is on the basis of this review that we recommend the creation of non intrusive, robust and clinically validated systems that offer useful information regarding clinically relevant events.

title: Deep unfolding Based Joint Beamforming, Position and Rotation Optimization of 6DMA system
authors: Zhijie Fang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In order to solve the defect that fixed position antenna can not make full use of the spatial degree of freedom, six dimensional movable antenna (6DMA) is proposed to enhance system performance by optimizing spatial position and rotation. In this work, we proposed a downlink communication scenario where each user is equipped with a 6DMA surface. We aim to maximize the sum rate of users by optimizing the beamforming vectors, the position and rotation of the 6DMA surfaces. To solve this problem, We first transform the original problem into equivalent weighted minimum mean square error (WMMSE) problem. Through derivation, closed-form expressions for all optimization variables are obtained. To reduce the computational complexity and accelerate convergence, we design a deep unfolding network to map the iterative process into a neural network. Simulation results confirm the superior performance of the 6DMA system.

title: Active Jitter Suppression in VLA-Driven Robotics via Cascaded Tracking Differentiators
authors: Haowen Tang, Weilin Chen, Wenbo Zhu, Zihan Chen, Jin Pang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Although the production of Computer, Communication, and Consumer Electronics (3C) components is highly automated, the assembly process is still subject to errors caused by manual handling. The Vision-Language-Action (VLA) model offers a new approach to assembling such components; its demonstrated generalization capabilities enable it to identify various assembly parts based on instructions and perform assembly with consistent force, thereby improving task execution. However, during peg-in-hole assembly operations, the clearance in the action chunks generated by the model can cause highfrequency oscillations in the robot arm end-effector, resulting in imprecise assembly force and angle, and ultimately leading to assembly failure. This paper proposes a VLA framework based on a cascaded filtering control architecture, designed to reduce jitter during the assembly process. This study abandoned the simulated environment and instead conducted physical assembly tests using a 6-Degree of Freedom (6-DOF) robotic arm. The decision layer uses the Action Chunking with Transformer (ACT) policy to generate sequences of action chunks, while the execution layer employs a post-processing filter system that combines an Exponential Moving Average (EMA) with a Tracking Differentiator (TD). Through physical cascading, the system effectively combines jitter with trajectory replanning. Experiments have shown that, supported by 40 expert datasets, this combination of parameters achieves an engineering compromise that effectively eliminates high-frequency jitter while maintaining smooth transition trajectories. This architecture effectively eliminates control discrepancies between policy output and electromechanical hardware, increasing the end-to-end physical assembly task success rate from 72.7% to 92.5%, providing a robust framework for high-precision peg-in-hole assembly task in the intelligence manufacturing paradigm.

title: Polar Lights Optimizer-Based Feature Selection for Optimal Brain Tumor Classification
authors: Anirban Datta Baruah, Gaurav Pandey, Amit Kukker, Aditya Sharma, Sweta Gupta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Brain tumor is caused by unregulated, rapid proliferation of aberrant cells and pose a severe global health concern, resulting in a substantial number of fatalities each year. Early cancer detection is very important for improving patient survival rate and treatment outcomes. The main aim of proposed method is to assess the efficacy of several machine learning (ML) approaches combined with the different feature extractions and feature selection methods for accurately detection and classifying brain tumors. In this study, total 38 features are obtained from four classes preprocessed input human brain MRI images using GLCM, GLRLM and Hu moments algorithms. The extracted image features are normalized using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Z$</tex> score normalization and top 25 features are selected using Polar lights optimizer (PLO) based feature selection algorithm. For classification three different ML based classification algorithms- XGBoost, Random Forest, and SVM are applied. The experimental results show that the XGBoost based classifier performed better as compared to other classifiers in both cases, before and after feature selection. After feature selection, XGBoost classifier achieves an average accuracy of 96.20 %.

title: Design and Implementation of an Intelligent Message Broadcasting and Content Processing Mobile Application Based on Multimodal AI
authors: Dequan Lin
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the widespread use of instant messaging, safe and efficient message access in hands-occupied or silent scenarios is crucial. Traditional word-for-word broadcasting suffers from information redundancy, limited scenario adaptability, and an inability to process non-text content, while existing research lacks systematic engineering implementations. To address these challenges, we propose a context-aware adaptive message scheduling framework utilizing Android native services and UniApp. The core methodological contributions include: 1) a state-aware dual-mechanism conflict resolution algorithm to resolve missed-message issues across diverse Android versions; 2) an algorithm-optimized three-threshold AI summarization rule engine for the adaptive processing of both long texts and batch group chats; 3) a deterministic-AI hybrid scheduling edge-cloud architecture that quantifies and distributes computational tasks to balance local response efficiency with cloud-based semantic accuracy. Experiments show the system achieves a 98.7% capture success rate, 96.3% core text retention, and 93.6% image description accuracy, reducing average broadcasting duration by 62.4% compared to traditional methods. Further cross-app compatibility tests and high-load stress evaluations validate the system's robustness and scalability.

title: CLIF: Self-Supervised Representation Learning with Isolation Forest for Tabular Anomaly Detection
authors: Jiacheng Zhang, Sheng Wang, Zhenyu Xu, Chenyi Si, Ke Wang, Chaoxu Mu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Unsupervised anomaly detection on tabular data is challenging due to heterogeneous feature scales, complex feature dependencies, and the lack of label information. Isolation Forest (iForest) is simple and efficient, but it relies on axis-parallel partitions in the original feature space. This limits its ability to detect anomalies caused by nonlinear feature interactions. To address this problem, we propose CLIF, a self-supervised framework for tabular anomaly detection. CLIF learns latent representations from Gaussian-noise-perturbed views of normalized tabular samples with the VICReg objective, and performs anomaly scoring by applying an Isolation Forest ensemble in the learned latent space. Experiments on six public tabular anomaly detection datasets show that CLIF achieves competitive overall performance against representative unsupervised baselines, with average AUC-ROC, AP, and F1 scores of 0.7817, 0.5973, and 0.5827, respectively.

title: Green Building Tech in Construction: Handling and Innovating via Multi-Objective Optimization with Deep Learning Algorithms
authors: Ji Zhang, Xiaona Li, Paul Anthony Mariadas, Ho Ming Kang, Lin Wang, Chunchun Yu, Diwei Wang, Bei Xu, Luning Qian
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The building sector faces critical challenges in balancing energy efficiency, environmental impact, and construction costs, a multi-objective dilemma often inadequately addressed by traditional static analysis. To address this, the present paper introduces a hybrid framework integrating deep learning with multi-objective optimization (DL-NSGA-II) for sustainable green building design. A deep multi-layer perceptron (MLP) acts as a surrogate model to accurately estimate heating and cooling loads, which are subsequently mapped to carbon emissions and construction costs. The surrogate is trained on the standard Building Energy Efficiency (BEE) dataset comprising 768 building designs and 8 features. The NSGA-II algorithm is then employed to derive Pareto optimal solutions, evaluated against linear regression and random forest benchmarks. The optimized neural network achieves an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value exceeding 0.96 for load predictions. Furthermore, in an extended airport-lounge simulation scenario, the framework successfully demonstrates a 15% reduction in cooling loads, alongside a 22.4% decrease in carbon footprint and an 18.7% reduction in construction costs. These findings validate the model’s capacity to support LEED-compliant practices and advance the broader application of computational intelligence in the digital construction transformation.

title: Radiology Report Generation via Cross-Modal Memory and Supervised Contrastive Learning
authors: Chuan Mei, Shengnan Lu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Radiology reports are essential to clinical diagnosis, yet manual reporting is time-consuming and highly dependent on reader experience. To narrow the semantic gap between chest X-ray images and medical language, and to better exploit clinical supervision, we develop a chest X-ray report generation framework that introduces a cross-modal memory module into a Transformer encoder-decoder and jointly optimizes report generation, multi-label disease classification, and supervised contrastive learning. The cross-modal memory uses learnable memory slots with attention-based retrieval to enrich visual and textual representations, while supervised contrastive learning leverages disease labels to cluster same-class samples and strengthen semantic alignment. On IU X-Ray, replacing a ResNet101 encoder with a CheXNet-pretrained encoder consistently improves all reported metrics, with CIDEr increasing from 0.3361 to 0.4375. The framework does not rely on manually constructed knowledge graphs and is easy to transfer and reproduce on similar medical imaging datasets.

title: Query flow based 3d object temporal detection model
authors: Sen Gao, Yanfeng Li, Meihong Su, Han Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the challenges of cross-frame geometric alignment and insufficient utilization of query-level temporal information in multi-frame point cloud 3D object detection, a query-flow-based temporal fusion model, QFlow-Net, is proposed. This model centers on sparse object queries, establishes cross-frame associations through motion compensation and the Hungarian algorithm, and utilizes GRU to jointly encode temporal fusion features and dynamic difference features. Experiments show that on the nuScenes dataset, QFlow-Net improves the mAP and NDS by 1.7% and 1.1% respectively compared to the TransFusion-L baseline, effectively enhancing the temporal consistency of detection.

title: Safety - Critical Control of Switched Linear System Under Safety Domain with an Obstacle
authors: Liang Zhu, Zhendong Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: The safety-critical problem of switched systems in the occasion where there exists an obstacle in the safety domain is investigated in this article. The definition of divergent switching path is first given. Then, a destabilizing state feedback path-wise switching law for obstacle avoidance is described with the help of an auxiliary coordinate system. The safety initial domain is further formulated under the given definitions of undershoot and overshoot. Finally, an example is given to verify the obtained results.

title: FSBA: Feature-Space Backdoor Attacks in Federated Learning
authors: Yewei Yin, Qifu Zhang, Mengyuan Fan, Haoyu Wang, Tianqi Liu, Dailin Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables collaborative model training while preserving data privacy, but its decentralized nature introduces security risks, particularly from backdoor attacks. Existing methods still face limitations in effectiveness and persistence. In this paper, we propose a feature-space backdoor attack framework for FL, termed FSBA. The method first generates triggers using feature alignment, aligning poisoned samples with the target class in the feature space, and then injects the backdoor by selectively poisoning task-insensitive and infrequently updated neurons. Experimental results on MNIST and CIFAR-10 show that FSBA achieves higher attack success rates than baseline methods while maintaining strong persistence.

title: SMGAN: A Multi-Path Generative Adversarial Network for Remote Sensing Image Super-Resolution Reconstruction
authors: Wei Huo, Xiaodan Zhang, Qiyuan Zhang, Naihao Hu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Remote sensing image super-resolution (SR) aids in the restoration of details and structures, but traditional methods struggle to balance local texture and global structure. To address this, this paper proposes the multi-path generative adversarial network (SMGAN), which integrates deep residual CNN, improved Mamba, and constant-scale Swin Transformer for hierarchical feature modelling. Additionally, it introduces a two-stage residual augmentation module (RAM) and image domain/gradient domain discriminators to enhance structural restoration and texture representation. Experiments show that SMGAN outperforms existing methods in terms of PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS, and other metrics on both the self-built RS-SR19 dataset and the publicly available AID dataset, with LPIPS reaching 0.344 and 0.357, respectively. In the complex Three Rivers Source dataset, PSNR reaches 36 dB, demonstrating excellent robustness and generalisation capabilities. Our data is available at https://hw-star.github.io/SMGAN/.

title: Data-Driven Joint Optimization of Array Design and Imaging
authors: Ziyuan Song, Jianping Wang, Zehua Dong
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Sparse arrays provide an effective approach for high-resolution imaging by synthesizing a larger effective aperture with fewer antenna elements. However, the large inter-element spacing often introduces severe sidelobes and grating-lobe artifacts, thereby degrading imaging quality. In conventional methods, sparse array design is usually separated from the imaging process, making it difficult to fully exploit the synergy between two-dimensional (2D) array topology and nonlinear reconstruction. Moreover, existing studies on joint optimization are mostly limited to one-dimensional (1D) arrays. To address these issues, this paper proposes a data-driven joint optimization framework for 2D sparse array design and imaging, in which the array design problem is formulated as a differentiable probabilistic sampling task, and a learnable antenna selection module, a deterministic linear imaging layer, and a residual-enhanced U-Net imaging network are integrated into a unified end-to-end framework. Numerical simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively suppress sidelobe interference and grating-lobe artifacts under highly sparse sampling conditions. Compared with random arrays, uniform arrays, and genetic algorithm optimized arrays, the proposed method achieves superior imaging performance with the same number of array elements.

title: Forest Fire Detection and Localization via Swin Transformer and Multi-Source Data Fusion
authors: Yong Yu, Shenglan Qi, Qian Huang, Jiahao Chen, Wenjun Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In China’s forest regions, fire monitoring commonly relies on communication-tower video surveillance. However, in complex outdoor environments it suffers from high false alarms, inaccurate fire-point localization, and failure under occlusion. This study proposes an integrated fire recognition and localization framework. A Swin-Transformer-based micro-target feature capture and a SimAM attention–fused background suppression module enable accurate detection of flames and smoke. A fusion-attention smoke-shape classifier distinguishes two occlusion types—severe occlusion (terrain/mountains) and slight occlusion (trees/buildings)—to provide prior information for localization. Localization leverages real-time camera parameters (azimuth and pitch), camera geolocation, sensor altitude, and Digital Elevation Model(DEM) data: ray–surface intersection is used for slight occlusion, and a peak-point–based back-projection strategy estimates the fire-location range for severe occlusion. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves a fire/smoke recognition accuracy of 86.5% and a smoke classification accuracy of 85.1%. For localization, in slight occlusion conditions, multi-source fusion reduces average localization error versus geometric triangulation and single-image-plus-DEM by 369.41m and 27.88m, respectively. In large-occlusion scenarios, the method outputs a location range, reducing the difficulty of identifying fire origins and supporting more automated, intelligent forest fire prevention. The proposed approach enables effective early-warning and spatial localization of forest fires utilizing tower-mounted monitoring frameworks.

title: Integrated ANN-XGBoost Framework for Groundwater Depletion Risk Prediction in Semi-Arid Regions of India
authors: Vikas Sharma, Divya Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Ground water degradation is a very serious environmental and agricultural problem in the semi-arid areas of India. Traditional surveillance is based on the regular surveys and the use of manual observations that inhibit the opportunity to conduct risk assessment in time. The research paper introduces a risk forecasting model on the level of district to predict the risk of groundwater depletion, combining formal data of Stage of Groundwater Extraction with satellite-based environmental factors, such as the state of vegetation, land surface temperature, and the level of nighttime lighting as an indicator of anthropogenic stress. It is a hybrid ensemble of Artificial Neural Network and XGBoost models that classifies districts as safe, semi-critical, and critical. The suggested method had a test accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 1. 1 5 \%}$</tex> and also shows stable classification results over the prevailing groundwater stress classes. Explainability analysis reveals that the influence of illumination at night and land surface temperature has a strong role on the critical conditions of groundwater, but healthier vegetation patterns are related to safer areas. Spatial risk mapping indicates high-stress clusters of much importance in the northwest and western regions of India which are consistent with nationwide groundwater evaluations. The results indicate that combination of hydro-climatic and anthropogenic satellite indicators with public hydrological data can bring scalable, interpretable, and low-cost risk assessments of groundwater of early-warning systems used in the district level.

title: Cross-Modal Deep Emotion Fusion for Video Background Music Generation
authors: Cailing Wang, Liyu Cheng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Generating background music highly aligned with video content and emotional tone remains a core challenge in cross-modal multimedia computing. Existing methods often rely on shallow feature concatenation, failing to capture the non-linear mappings and long-range temporal dynamics between visual semantics and musical emotions. To address this challenge of deep emotional alignment, we propose VES-BGM (Video-Emotion Synergy Background Music), a cross-modal deep fusion framework driven by emotional trajectories. We establish a synergistic mechanism for audiovisual synchronization through three novel components: (1) an emotion entropy enhancement strategy that reconstructs discrete probability distributions into high-density semantic embeddings for fine-grained modeling; (2) a bidirectional temporal emotion encoder that leverages Bi-LSTM to explicitly model the continuous evolution of video emotions, resolving logical discontinuities caused by abrupt emotional shifts; and (3) an emotion-guided cross-modal modulation mechanism via multi-layer cross-attention, which forces visual features to dynamically focus on relevant semantic cues, achieving a paradigm shift from simple content matching to deep emotional resonance. Experiments on the MuVi-Sync dataset demonstrate that VES-BGM significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines such as Music Transformer and CMT in both chord prediction accuracy and subjective audiovisual coherence. Our results validate that an emotion-trajectory-driven modulation architecture effectively bridges the semantic gap in cross-modal generation, providing a robust paradigm for intelligent music scoring.

title: Parametric Design and Modification of RV Cycloidal Wheel Profile Based on NX Software
authors: Chao Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: As a precision transmission mechanism featuring a large transmission ratio and high load-carrying capacity, the cycloidal-pin wheel reducer relies heavily on the profile design of its core component—the cycloidal gear—which directly determines its transmission performance and meshing quality. Conventional design methods depend on formula derivation and two-dimensional drafting, which are cumbersome and unable to respond rapidly to parametric variations during design. This paper presents a rapid and accurate three-dimensional parametric modeling approach for RV cycloidal gears based on the NX platform, leveraging its robust parametric modeling capabilities. By establishing the correlation between basic cycloidal gear parameters (including tooth number, eccentricity, pitch circle radius of pin teeth, pin radius, etc.) and the profile equation, a fully parametrically driven cycloidal gear model is constructed in NX. Modifications such as equidistant modification, shift modification, and rotational angle modification are introduced to optimize the meshing behavior between the cycloidal gear and pin, enabling an oil film lubrication thickness of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, thereby reducing friction and improving transmission efficiency. The proposed method supports real-time parameter modification and automatic model regeneration, significantly enhancing design efficiency and accuracy, and laying a solid foundation for subsequent kinematic simulation, numerical control machining, and parameter optimization. Parametric design of the cycloid gear profile can effectively reduce transmission backlash and vibration to improve the positioning accuracy and trajectory smoothness of industrial robots, while the quantitative correlation between profile parameters and meshing characteristics provides a reliable physical foundation for sensor-based error compensation and force feedback control, thereby optimizing dynamic response and enhancing the stability and robustness of the automatic control system.

title: Stabilizing Design for Language Constrained Discrete-Time Switched Linear Systems
authors: Qiang Zhang, Zhendong Sun, Qinghua Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: This paper studies the feedback stabilization problem for discrete-time switched linear systems whose switching sequences are constrained by formal languages, generated by a nondeterministic finite automaton. Traditional stabilization methods, particularly those relying on stepwise Lyapunov decay, often exhibit high conservatism and fail to handle systems containing unstable or uncontrollable sub-modes that cause transient energy growth. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel stabilization design based on a transition-dependent Lyapunov framework integrated with a lifted automaton formulation. A transition-dependent Lyapunov framework is first developed, where Lyapunov matrices are associated with automaton transitions. To further reduce conservatism, an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M$</tex>-step lifted automaton formulation is introduced, which relaxes the requirement of stepwise Lyapunov decrease by enforcing contraction over a finite switching step horizon. Finally, a numerical example is presented to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.
