title: Online Junction Temperature Monitoring Method for Paralleled SiC MOSFETs with the Discrete Gate Drive Control
authors: Lixian Huang, Yujia Song, Xiaochen Liu, Peng Sun, Zhibin Zhao, Yumeng Cai, Xuebao Li, Bing Ji
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: To meet higher power demands, multiple SiC MOSFET chips are often connected in parallel. However, non-uniform junction temperature distribution among these paralleled devices has emerged as a critical challenge, significantly impacting system reliability and lifetime. Despite various thermal management efforts, existing techniques lack an accurate, real-time method to monitor individual chip junction temperatures in paralleled configurations. In this article, a junction temperature monitoring method for paralleled SiC MOSFETs with discrete gate drive (DGD) control is proposed. And a PCB Rogowski coil is settled on the confluence of paralleled branches to capture dynamic current characteristics. By actively adjusting the turn-on delay among SiC MOSFETs, the junction temperature can be extracted by leveraging the temperature dependence of quasi-threshold voltage (Vth-qua) during a gate delay interval without intrusive sensing. Comprehensive analysis is conducted to evaluate the impact of device temperature imbalance and varying operating conditions on measurement accuracy. The proposed method is finally applied and verified online in a buck experiment platform to sensing junction temperature of paralleled SiC MOSFETs. Under three distinct uneven cooling scenarios, the maximum monitoring error is limited to just 2.9 °C, confirming the method's high accuracy and robustness. This work offers a practical and scalable approach for junction temperature monitoring in paralleled SiC MOSFETs, paving the way for more reliable thermal design and intelligent health monitoring in high-power, safety-critical applications.

title: From Policy to Practice: A Systematic Literature Review of AI-Driven Knowledge Base Construction for Cybersecurity Compliance
authors: Luis-Carlos Herrera, Justinas Janulevičius, Andrea Pinto, Yezid Donoso
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Managing the lifecycle of cybersecurity policies from high-level governance documents to low-level technical implementation remains prone to human error and operational inefficiency. With the rapid evolution of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and the increasing volume of regulatory standards, there is a critical need for automated methods to bridge the gap between "what is written" (Policy) and "what is executed" (Practice). This systematic literature review examines the state of the art in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques for automating cybersecurity knowledge management. This study was conducted in accordance with the PRISMA 2020 statement for reporting systematic reviews. We systematically identified and analyzed 55 peer-reviewed studies published between 2020 and 2025, selected from an initial corpus of 230 papers sourced from IEEE, ACM Digital Library, Scopus, ScienceDirect, and Springer Journals. Our analysis reveals a transition from discriminative information extraction to hybrid architectures integrating deep learning-driven extraction with symbolic logic formalisms for verified knowledge graph construction. We categorize existing approaches into four key themes: (1) Automated Policy Extraction, (2) Knowledge Graph Construction, (3) Policy-as-Code Compliance, and (4) Dynamic Threat Translation. The review identifies a significant gap in "neuro-symbolic" verification methods capable of mitigating stochastic failure modes and hallucination risks inherent in probabilistic LLM-driven compliance pipelines, outlining a roadmap for future research in automated security governance. Beyond a descriptive summary, this review provides a critical comparative analysis of extraction accuracy, reasoning scalability, and the risks inherent in Large Language Model (LLM) approaches.

title: A Single-Stage Cuk-Based Partial Power Conversion Topology With Intra-Cycle Control for Wireless Power Transfer System
authors: Muxing Wu, Io-Wa Iam, Pui-In Mak, Rui P. Martins, Chi-Seng Lam
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article proposes a single-stage Cuk-based partial power conversion (PPC) topology for wireless power transfer systems, integrating the advantageous features of semi-active rectifier (SAR) and Cuk-based bridgeless rectifier to achieve high system efficiency, low voltage stress, and enhanced voltage regulation. In conventional PPC structures with receiver-side control, the regulating power follows the sequence of “separating first, regulating later,” and the overall structure adopts a quasi-single-stage configuration. Our approach introduces an innovative intra-cycle control scheme achieving true single-stage operation. Herein, it utilizes the secondary current zero-crossing as a reference to perform simultaneous power separation and regulation within one switching cycle. In the positive half-cycles, power flows directly to the load, while in the negative half-cycles power is dynamically split with part reaching the load directly and the remainder undergoing regulation. Moreover. the SAR-Cuk integration expands the output regulation range and enables zero-voltage switching at the receiver-side without additional components, eliminating the extra conversion stage through this “simultaneous separation and regulation” approach. A 1.2 kW prototype achieved 95.17% peak efficiency and 264–402 V output range, validating the practicality of the system.

title: Cascaded Semantic-Guided Network for Surface Anomaly Localization in Steel-Cased Batteries
authors: Ying Xu, Dongyan Lin, Tianlei Wang, Yingwen Chen, Yikui Zhai, Jianhong Zhou, Pasquale Coscia, Angelo Genovese, C. L. Philip Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Localization of surface anomalies in steel-cased batteries remains challenging due to subtle defect textures, nonuniform illumination, and complex surface reflections. Existing methods often struggle to separate fine-grained anomalies from normal patterns, leading to incomplete or inaccurate localization. To address these challenges, this paper presents the cascaded semantic-guided network (CSG-Net) framework that performs coarse-to-fine anomaly localization through cross-stage semantic refinement. Within the cascaded architecture, each stage refines anomaly localization using semantic priors from the preceding stage. In the coarse stage, a reconstruction–discrimination subnetwork models normal appearance distributions to generate preliminary anomaly cues. In the fine stage, a semantic-guided enhancement module (SGEM) transfers semantic priors from the coarse stage to adaptively modulate encoder features, thereby strengthening semantic consistency and enhancing discriminative capability. Furthermore, a deep supervision mechanism with weighted multi-scale loss is introduced to improve sensitivity to small defects and stabilize convergence. A complete steel-cased battery surface anomaly detection system is also developed and deployed on an industrial production line to validate engineering feasibility. Experiments on the steel-cased battery surface anomaly dataset (SCBSA), together with evaluations on MVTec AD and VisA, show that CSG-Net delivers strong localization performance across industrial inspection settings. Our code and dataset are available at https://github.com/yikuizhai/CSG-Net.

title: An S-matrix Formalism for the Nonclassical Optical Response of Plasmonic Sphere Aggregates
authors: Xin Zheng, Christos Mystilidis, Christos Tserkezis, Guy A. E. Vandenbosch, Xuezhi Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A computational method for the scattering of light by multiple nonclassical plasmonic nanospheres, each of which has multiple (non-)concentric dielectric or metallic layers, is presented. The electromagnetic (EM) response of the free electrons in the metals is described by three popular mesoscopic models: the nonlocal hydrodynamic Drude model (NLHDM) and its diffusive variant, namely the generalized nonlocal optical response (GNOR) model, as well as the surface response model (SRM). The main equation behind the method is set up by detailing the evaluation of the S-matrix for each individual spherical interface and the interactions amongst the interfaces. The algorithm is numerically validated by comparing with an in-house boundary element method (BEM) solver for a spherical nanoparticle with two smaller embedded spheres, and physically checked on a trimer configuration, where the responses from the NLHDM and SRM are contrasted with the local response model (LRM). In both cases a very good agreement is seen regarding frequency shifts and field enhancements.

title: Impact of Interface Properties on Direct Tunneling in Al/ALD-Al₂O₃/Al Capacitors for Josephson Junction Applications
authors: Daniel Rocha-Aguilera, Gabriela Méndez-Jerónimo, Joel Molina-Reyes
publish: IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering
abstract: The Al/AlO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub>/Al system is widely used for the fabrication of Josephson junctions, which constitute the central element of superconducting qubits. The process parameters for growing ultra-thin AlOx barriers by thermal oxidation in-between physical vapor deposition (PVD) runs of Al films are well understood, but the resulting barriers present several drawbacks that limit performance such as thickness inhomogeneity and oxygen deficiency that leads to high defect densities in its bulk and interfaces. In this work, we present advances in the development of an alternative fabrication process for Al/Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/Al structures by using thermal atomic-layer deposition (ALD) able to produce high-quality Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> films with thickness close to 2 nm. We focus on reducing the low-quality interfacial oxide between Al and Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> by using well-controlled wet chemical etching to eliminate the surface oxide layer that forms on the bottom PVD-deposited Al film. Additionally, the ALD deposition process for Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> is also optimized by adjusting the length of the precursor pulses and purges to promote the nucleation of the Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> first cycles on the Al surface. The effect of the processing conditions was observed through spectroscopic ellipsometry, electrical measurements at room temperature, and modeling of charge transport mechanisms on Al/Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/Al structures. The electrical characterization revealed nonlinear J–V characteristics consistent with tunneling dominated transport, while the transport modeling enabled estimation of effective barrier thickness, interfacial layer thickness, barrier normal resistance, and critical current. The results show that interface engineering and ALD process optimization can effectively reduce the interfacial oxide contribution and improve barrier uniformity, providing useful insights for the development of Al/Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/Al tunnel junctions for superconducting quantum circuit applications.

title: Shape-Shifting Malicious Code in Software Backdoors via Language Models
authors: Mohammad Ebrahimi Fard, Felix Weissberg, Erik Imgrund, Thorsten Eisenhofer, Konrad Rieck
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Asia Conference on Computer and Communications Security
abstract: None

title: A Linear Operator Framework for Polynomial Divisions in Cryptography
authors: Varun Madathil, Arnab Roy, Konstantinos Chalkias, Charanjit Jutla, Jonas Lindstrom
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Asia Conference on Computer and Communications Security
abstract: None

title: Hybrid RDH Method for JPEG Images Based on Quantization-Table-Modification and STC
authors: Jiuchao Ban, Mengyao Xiao, Xiaolong Li, Bin Ma, Yao Zhao
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Quantization-table-modification (QTM) is widely utilized in current studies of reversible data hiding (RDH) for JPEG images. However, the performance of existing QTM-based methods is far from optimal due to the uniform quantization step division and imperfect distortion modeling. In this letter, by incorporating Syndrome-Trellis-Code (STC) into QTM, a novel hybrid JPEG images RDH method is proposed. Firstly, instead of the uniformly dividing strategy conducted in previous works, by adaptively dividing the quantization steps, a hybrid embedding mechanism combining binary and ternary embedding is proposed. Then, the corresponding capacity-distortion model is established, by which the spatial domain distortion is estimated. Finally, based on the derived capacity-distortion model, for performance optimization, STC is utilized to minimize the cover modification. In this way, JPEG images RDH can be effectively conducted so that the visual quality of the marked image is well maintained. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms some state-of-the-art works in terms of visual quality.

title: Resource-Efficient Neural Network for Crop Damage Classification in Precision Agriculture
authors: Md Tanvir Islam, Shehzad Ali, Abdul Khader Jilani Saudagar, Mohammad Hijji, Yazeed Masaud Alkhrijah, Khan Muhammad, Victor Hugo C. de Albuquerque
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Timely and accurate crop damage classification (CDC) is vital for informed decision-making in the industry of precision agriculture. Traditional manual methods are slow and unreliable, whereas recent deep learning models, although accurate, are often too computationally intensive for resource-constrained environments. In this study, we present LNetCDC, a lightweight attention-based convolutional neural network tailored for CDC. The architecture integrates an EchoBlock for efficient feature extraction, combined with residual pathways enhanced by “Channelwise Refine” and “Dual Gate Attention” modules to emphasize critical spatial and channelwise features. Also, dilated convolutions are incorporated into deeper layers to capture multiscale contextual patterns. We evaluated our LNetCDC on a benchmark crop damage dataset, where it outperformed existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) models in terms of both accuracy and efficiency. Notably, it achieves around 2.3% gain in accuracy with only 0.86 million parameters compared with 1.13 million in the prior SOTA model for CDC. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and suitability of LNetCDC for real-time deployment on industrial edge devices.

title: Simulation and Structural Optimization of a MEMS Piezoelectric Pressure Sensor Based on COMSOL Multiphysics
authors: Kai Sheng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the growing need for high-precision, self-powered Micro-Electro-Mechanical Systems (MEMS), this study utilizes COMSOL Multiphysics to develop a high-fidelity, 2D axisymmetric finite element model of a cylindrical piezoelectric pressure sensor. We systematically evaluate the electromechanical performance of six piezoelectric materials—Quartz RH, Zinc Oxide, PZT-4, PZT-7A, Lithium Niobate, and Lithium Tantalate—under pressures ranging from 5 to 25 MPa. Results demonstrate that all tested materials exhibit excellent voltage-pressure linearity, with Quartz RH achieving the highest sensitivity of 20.12 V/MPa at 25 MPa. Further geometric analysis reveals that wall thickness is the primary factor influencing performance; reducing the outer diameter significantly amplifies von Mises stress and subsequent voltage output, whereas varying tube height has a negligible effect. These findings provide critical design guidelines, emphasizing wall-thinning strategies for optimizing electromechanical conversion efficiency in future MEMS sensor fabrication.

title: UNIFY: A Reconfigurable Processing Unit for Orthogonal Dataflows in CNN Accelerators
authors: Liang Yang, Yicheng Yan, Xinwei Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Planar pooling and channel-wise normalization follow orthogonal memory-access patterns, often forcing CNN accelerators to adopt separate post-processing units and thereby introducing redundant logic, extra buffering, and unnecessary off-chip round-trips. To address this inefficiency, this paper proposes UNIFY, a unified and reconfigurable post-processing unit that supports both planar (W-H) and channel-wise (C) dataflows via a flexible DMA controller for efficient data movement. UNIFY improves hardware utilization and reduces latency by sharing arithmetic resources and local buffers across operators, while also enabling operator fusion without intermediate DRAM accesses. Implemented in SystemVerilog and synthesized with a generic technology library, UNIFY achieves 44.0% lower logic area and 43.0% lower total area than a conventional segregated PDP+CDP design. For a fused pooling-softmax workload at 500MHz, it further reduces end-to-end latency by 5.8% when data resides in on-chip SRAM and by 12.3% when data is stored in off-chip DDR. These results indicate that UNIFY provides a flexible and area-efficient post-processing solution for resource-constrained edge AI accelerators.

title: A Dual-Contrastive Multi-View Clustering Framework with Cross-View Alignment and Heterogeneity Awareness
authors: Xiaolei Lv, Jun Ma, Wenjing Jia, Yuquan Shao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Multi-view clustering aims to uncover consistent clustering structures from multiple complementary views. However, real-world multi-view data often suffer from strong view heterogeneity, inconsistent noise distributions, and difficulties in cross-view representation alignment, which severely degrade clustering performance. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a dual-contrastive multi-view clustering framework with cross-view alignment and heterogeneity awareness. Specifically, under a unified deep autoencoder architecture, the proposed method introduces dual-level contrastive learning in both feature space and label space. By incorporating instance-level and cluster-level contrastive mechanisms, cross-view consistency is explicitly enhanced. Furthermore, a cross-view augmentation module is designed to explicitly model similarity propagation across views, while a heterogeneity-aware adaptive weighting strategy is proposed to mitigate the impact of varying view quality on clustering performance. In addition, by integrating diversity constraints and alignment regularization, the proposed method effectively preserves discriminative representations while preventing representation collapse. Extensive experiments on multiple public multi-view datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in terms of ACC, NMI, and PUR.

title: An Intelligent Domain-Adaptive System for Robust Hyperspectral Classification via Attention-Based Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Ritu Pathak, Badiea Abdulkarem Mohammed, Lalit Kumar, Varun Shukla, Manoj Kumar Misra, Mehul Kumar Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: For various remote sensing tasks, hyperspectral images (HSI) need to be classified. However, robust model generalization does not come easily due to large domain shifts from different sensors, different atmospheres, and different scene dynamics. To address these challenges, we propose the RDAHSI, a flexible and Intelligent Domain Adaptive System (IDA-S). The IDA-S uniquely combines Self-Supervised Learning (SSL), which yields good representations needed in the framework, and Local Texture Extraction (LTE) which captures the local details in the dataset. Spectral-Spatial Transformers model complex long-range dependencies and we ensure Adversarial Robustness and embed Uncertainty Awareness to mitigate the challenges posed by ambiguous data. This Intelligent System to retain Engineering Adaptability for real-world, large-scale, and complex deployments.

title: Energy-Efficient Beamforming and Power Allocation in Satellite-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Junwei Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Satellite-terrestrial integrated networks (STINs) have emerged as a promising architecture for providing seamless global coverage. However, the resource management in STINs is further complicated by the inherent asymmetry between the high-speed terrestrial access and the capacity-constrained satellite backhaul. The stringent power limitations of satellite payloads and the backhaul-induced rate ceilings pose severe challenges. In this paper, We identify the backhaul-capacity bottleneck as the fundamental limit of STIN energy efficiency and investigate the joint EE maximization problem in a broadband STIN, where a multi-satellite constellation serves multiple ground users via a multi-antenna terrestrial transceiver TST. Unlike conventional terrestrial networks, the performance of STINs is strictly governed by the limited satellite-terrestrial backhaul capacity. To address this unique bottleneck, we formulate a joint beamforming and power allocation problem that explicitly accounts for the backhaul-rate coupling. To tackle the resulting non-convex fractional programming problem, we propose an efficient algorithm based on SCA. Specifically, we exploit the maximum ratio transmission (MRT) scheme to effectively reduce the dimensionality of the satellite-to-TST backhaul link, transforming the intractable capacity constraint into a mathematically tractable scalar form. Furthermore, by introducing first-order Taylor expansion and variable slack techniques, the non-convex signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) constraints are rigorously recast into standard second-order cone programming (SOCP) formulations. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves rapid convergence and significantly enhances the system EE. Moreover, the results reveal the existence of an optimal transmit power threshold and the performance saturation effect caused by the capacity bottleneck of the space-segment backhaul link.

title: StatGraph: A Statistics-First, Graph-Based Framework for Auditable and Integrated Biomarker Discovery
authors: Chuanliang Chen, Zhimo Han
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Artificial intelligence holds transformative promise for biomedical discovery, yet prevailing pipelines conflate linguistic fluency with scientific validity, producing outputs that lack the statistical rigor and audit trails demanded by high-stakes clinical research. We introduce StatGraph, a Statistics-First, Graph-Based framework for Auditable and Integrated Biomarker Discovery, predicated on a single organizing principle: statistical evidence is the primary and non-negotiable arbiter of clinical relevance. StatGraph implements this principle through a four-stage pipeline—AI-enhanced data quality assurance, dual-stream analysis, knowledge-aware evidence synthesis, and auditable structured reporting—in which each stage is strictly gated by the preceding stage’s statistical outputs. The architectural centerpiece is the Integrated Evidence Score (IES), a formally grounded fusion metric with proven boundedness IES<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf> ∈ (0, 1], strict monotonicity in both statistical strength and predictive importance, and closed-form limiting behaviors that transparently encode the statistics-first philosophy. Unlike heuristic fusion baselines (rank aggregation, Borda count, weighted sums), IES admits a principled α-calibration procedure with a data-driven selection protocol. Evaluated on a clinical infection-biomarker cohort with cross-disease generalizability analysis spanning oncology (TCGA-BRCA, n=1,098) and metabolic disease (UK Biobank, n=2,341) contexts, StatGraph achieves state-of-the-art topic adherence (59.1%, GPT-4o), faithfulness (0.86), and expert-validated clinical consistency (78.1%), surpassing TriAgent by 26.8 percentage points on clinical alignment at only 5–7% additional runtime. Ablation studies provide component-level causal attribution for each performance gain, establishing StatGraph as a rigorous, deployable infrastructure for AI-assisted clinical biomarker prioritization.

title: Optimization Method for Small Object Detection in ViT-DETR Based on Deformable Cross-Attention and Multi-Scale Feature Alignment
authors: Yi Ren
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To solve the ViT-DETR detection algorithm small target detection performance is limited for small target detection in complex scenarios by the undemocratic environmental position of they are small target, weak texture, insufficient cross scale semantics interaction, the paper puts forward ViT-DETR small target detection optimization method based on deformable cross-attention and multi-scale feature alignment, based on the original ViT-DETR. First constructs a multi-scale feature extraction branch, jointly preserves the high resolution detail information from its shallow layers and the semantic information from its deep layers, and then designs a multi-scale feature alignment module to perform spatial semantic calibration on features of different levels to mitigate the cross layer feature distribution differences and enhance their sensitivity for small target localization. Then, a deformable cross-attention mechanism is devised in the decoding stage, allowing the object queries to dynamically attend to the sparse key locations in the candidate regions, thus suppressing background noises while enhancing the fine-grained representation capability, whereas a scale-aware query refinement combining utilizes the fine grained representation to enable the small target recognition in dense occlusions. The experimental results demonstrate consistent detection accuracy and robustness improvements over the baseline model.

title: Research on Speech Privacy Protection Method Based on Data Splitting
authors: Ruixiong Huang, Haojun Lu, Rui Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the risk of biometric privacy leakage arising from the widespread adoption of intelligent speech technologies, this paper proposes the first speech privacy protection method based on split storage and reconstruction (WSSR). Conventional Traditional encryption and anonymization methods usually suffer from high computational costs or the threat of de-anonymization, and lack mechanisms for preventing tampering and traceability supervision of speech data flow. WSSR enables privacy isolation at the source by employing a data splitting strategy that decomposes the speech signal into semantic content and identity features, which are then stored in a distributed fashion. Upon legal authorization, the system leverages large language model-based synthesis techniques to reconstruct the speech, while incorporating a dual watermarking scheme—combining robust and semi-fragile watermarks—to jointly enable source tracing and tampering detection. Experiments conducted on the LibriTTS dataset demonstrate the efficacy of WSSR: the average processing time is on the order of seconds, the word error rate is substantially reduced to 3.64%, and the watermark extraction accuracy exceeds 90% under various attack scenarios. Compared with TraceableSpeech, which provides only traceability, WSSR achieves an effective balance among privacy protection, data utility, and security assurance.

title: An Intelligent Surveillance Through Real-Time License Plate Detection and Recognition
authors: Chitresh Malhan, Kirandeep Kaur, Neetu Rani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: This study presents a real-time license plate detection and recognition system using computer vision and deep learning techniques. The proposed pipeline integrates image preprocessing, plate localization using YOLOv5, feature extraction, and optical character recognition (OCR). The system was evaluated on four diverse datasets consisting of 28,000 images captured under varying conditions such as daylight, low-light, occlusion, and fast motion. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves an overall detection accuracy of 94.5 %, with a precision of 92.8 %, recall of 93.6 %, and an average processing speed of 34 FPS, making it suitable for real-time surveillance applications. The findings indicate that the combination of deep learning-based localization and OCR significantly improves recognition robustness compared to traditional image-processing methods. This work contributes to the development of accurate, scalable, and efficient intelligent surveillance systems

title: Wind Turbine Target Recognition Method Based on Improved YOLOv11
authors: YiWen Zhang, Xuefeng Jiang, Min Peng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Most oil field wind farms are built in areas with open terrain and abundant wind resources. The fan images captured by UAV during inspection are vulnerable to the interference of light and shadow transformation, shooting Angle, similar surrounding structures, and scale imbalance, resulting in low accuracy of fan target recognition. Therefore, this paper proposes a fan target recognition method based on improved YOLOv11. Firstly, in order to enhance the extraction ability of the underlying texture and edge features of the fan, the Windmill convolution (APConv) was introduced to replace the original C3k2 module. Secondly, the multi-scale adaptive Spatial attention Gate mechanism (MASAG) was integrated into the neck network, so that the model could adaptively focus on the key regions of fan targets at different scales. Thus, the model has higher detection accuracy while maintaining lightweight. The results show that the average detection accuracy of the improved model reaches 95.4%, which has higher accuracy and better detection effect in fan target recognition. It is practical and robust, suitable for deployment in UAV inspection platform, and provides a basis for oilfield fan intelligent inspection.

title: Dynamic Trim-Guided Residual Reinforcement Learning Control for Tiltrotor Transition
authors: Zhenhao Su, Jie Wu, He Yu, Jiandong Duan
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: During the tilt transition of a tiltrotor aircraft, the trim point continuously evolves with varying tilt angle and inflow, while coupled torques from rotor wake and configuration changes cause rapid disturbances, leading to trim offset and attitude instability. This paper proposes an aerodynamic stability control strategy based on residual reinforcement learning to address these challenges. First, a dynamic trim model is established by coupling aerodynamics with kinematic equations, numerically solving for the trim point trajectory throughout the tilt envelope to provide a control benchmark. Then, a residual policy structure integrated with the TD3 algorithm learns compensation terms that jointly adjust rotor collective pitch, rotor speed and tilt angle, maintaining aerodynamic symmetry and torque balance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively suppresses attitude oscillations and trim drift under strong nonlinear and unsteady disturbances, significantly enhancing control robustness during transition.

title: Ship Target Detection Model Based on Improved YOLOv11
authors: Yang Liu, Chaofeng Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Ship detection in UAV aerial imagery is complicated by small, densely clustered targets, large scale variation, and cluttered ocean backgrounds. This paper presents MDW-YOLO, a YOLOv11-based detector addressing these challenges through three modifications. The MBFC module replaces standard Conv blocks in the neck, using parallel heterogeneous branches to jointly enrich spatial texture and channel semantics for small targets. DA- Attention combines coordinate channel and spatial attention in parallel, improving small-target recognition under complex backgrounds by capturing long-range dependencies without sacrificing spatial precision. Finally, CIoU loss is replaced by WIoU, whose dynamic focusing mechanism down-weights degenerate anchors and sharpens sensitivity to blurred or partially occluded boundaries. On SeaDronesSee, MDW-YOLO gains 3.7 pp on mAP50 and 6.3 pp on mAP50:95 over YOLOv11n, with only 0.2 M additional parameters. Comparisons against RTDETR, YOLOv8s, and other detectors confirm the approach's effectiveness.

title: The Design and Research of a Triple-Transformation Based Land-Air Amphibious Reconnaissance Robot
authors: Haisheng Liu, Yaokai Liu, Jiahui Lu, Wenshen Chen, Yuhan Liu, Mengxi Xia
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: The conventional disaster response robots have encountered limitations of efficiency because of the size limitation and the monotony of locomotion. To overcome these challenges, the paper introduces a land-air amphibious reconnaissance robot with innovative triple transformation mechanism. The main kinematic mechanism uses a three-link structure, i.e., two servomotor-activated crank, an L -shaped coupler, and an electrical telescopic rod, which can be locked into a rigid three-bar triangle structure by means of geometric singularity to stabilize it. This mechanism allows the smooth switching between a coaxial embedded track-rotor arrangement to provide ground traction and an open arrangement to fly in air. Hierarchical cascaded control is used to balance nonlinear dynamics when switching modes. Experimental simulations show that the robot performs very well in multi-modal motion making a dependable hardware system to undertake complex rescue operations in unstructured settings.

title: Memory-Decoupled Event-Driven SNN Deployment on Edge Microcontrollers
authors: Canyang Yang, Jingping Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Deploying Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) on edge microcontrollers (MCUs) offers a highly promising pathway for ultra-low-power artificial intelligence. However, mapping highly biomimetic SNNs onto standard von Neumann architectures introduces severe memory walls and computing mismatches. Specifically, the conventional spatial unrolling of time steps induces an unsustainable expansion of dynamic activation buffers, frequently triggering Out-of-Memory (OOM) failures on resource-constrained devices. To bridge this gap, we propose a hardware-software co-optimization architecture tailored for ARM Cortex-M7 processors. First, we introduce a double-depth rolling array mechanism that deeply decouples sequence length from memory footprint, reducing the spatial complexity of dynamic hidden states from O(N×T) to a constant O(N). Second, an event-driven sparse execution restructuring based on the Coordinate List (COO) format is implemented to bypass resting neurons, transforming power-intensive dense Multiply-Accumulate (MAC) operations into single-cycle conditional additions. Supported by an asymmetric mixed-precision mapping strategy, our architecture strictly preserves complex neural dynamics. Crucially, rigorous white-box testing confirms a bounded state error of 2.38×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-6</sup> with zero spike-timing deviation, guaranteeing a mathematically lossless accuracy migration. Physical benchmarks on the STM32H7 MCU across both static MNIST and continuous Speech wake-word tasks demonstrate that the proposed framework slashes peak dynamic SRAM overhead by nearly 74%, completely eliminating OOM vulnerabilities. Evaluated on the MNIST task using a recurrent SNN (784-256-10) with partial readout, the system achieves an inference latency of 197.69 ms and an energy consumption of 76.98 mJ, significantly outperforming iso-parametric recurrent neural networks in both temporal and spatial dimensions.

title: Comparative Benchmarking of TD3 for Multi-Stage Indoor Navigation
authors: Shuofang Sun, Jinshu Han, Xiaomin Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: For autonomous systems operating in environments lacking Global Positioning System (GPS) signals, indoor Mapless navigation via Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) has emerged as a promising end-to-end solution. However, existing work often overlooks the unique demands of “multi-stage indoor navigation. While ordinary indoor navigation typically focuses on point-to-point movement within consistent environment types, multi-stage navigation requires an agent to adapt to a sequence of tasks characterized by abrupt topological shifts-such as transitioning from expansive hallways into dense, cluttered rooms. The core challenge in this multi-stage setting is the policy instability caused by environmental distribution shifts, which frequently leads to catastrophic mode collapse or severe performance degradation in traditional DRL algorithms. To address this, we evaluate the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) framework against the traditional Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) baseline and the State-of-the-Art (SOTA) Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm. Utilizing an escalating training setup-progressing from empty spaces to complex multi-obstacle rooms-we analyze how TD3's Clipped Double Q-learning and delayed policy updates mitigate value estimation bias under shifting constraints. Experimental results demonstrate that TD3 achieves a stable average reward of 0.62 in the final stages, whereas DDPG and PPO plateau near 0.5. Quantitative analysis indicates that TD3 yields a 22 % higher mission success rate and significantly faster recovery during environment transitions compared to the baselines. These findings prove that structural constraints in value estimation are essential for managing distribution shifts in multi-stage navigation. Ultimately, this work provides a scalable and resilient framework for high-fidelity autonomous systems, offering critical technical support for the deployment of industrial robots in unpredictable and GPS-denied operating conditions.

title: Difficulty Assessment System of Chinese Vocabulary Acquisition Based on Artificial Intelligence
authors: Shiyifan Cheng, Qiaohong Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence technology, the traditional evaluation method of vocabulary acquisition difficulty, which relies on teachers' experience and intuition, has gradually failed to meet the needs of modern education. By integrating in-depth learning and natural language processing technology, the system realizes multi-dimensional comprehensive evaluation of Chinese vocabulary complexity, frequency of use and language environment. The system consists of five modules: user interface, vocabulary database management, intelligent assessment, personalized learning recommendation and learning progress tracking and feedback, which can provide customized learning suggestions and real-time feedback according to the individual differences of learners. Through the test of 100 different levels of Chinese learners, the results show that the accuracy of the system evaluation is more than 85%, and the user satisfaction is as high as 90%, which significantly improves the learning efficiency and motivation.

title: InfraGas-YOLO: A Lightweight Improved YOLOv8 for Infrared Micro-Gas Leakage Detection
authors: Xingyu Yang, Penglong Zhao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Infrared micro-gas leakage detection suffers from intractable challenges including small target scale, weak contrast, diffuse morphologies, and high background noise, while practical scenarios demand a good trade-off between detection accuracy and lightweight deployment, which generic models fail to achieve well. This paper proposes InfraGas-YOLO, a tailored lightweight model based on YOLOv8 for single-frame infrared micro-gas leakage detection. Retaining the classic backbone-head topology and SPPF module of YOLOv8, the model integrates four targeted innovative modules: ConvDS for reducing redundant computation in backbone downsampling, ConvECA for enhancing weak-contrast gas features in PAN, C2fD for expanding receptive field on the deepest P5 layer, and LRS for improving training stability of C2f bottlenecks. Results show that InfraGas-YOLO achieves an excellent balance between detection accuracy and lightweight deployment. Despite limitations including the inability to utilize video temporal dependencies and insufficient optimization for ultra resource-constrained edge devices, InfraGas-YOLO stably and accurately detects infrared micro-gas leakage targets, and is well-suited for real-time industrial monitoring and edge deployment.

title: GCNShield: Advanced Fraud Detection in Payment Systems Using Graph Convolutional Networks
authors: Ajit Patel, Ayush Kumar, Simar Preet Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: As digital payment systems continue to evolve and grow in scale, fraudsters take advantage and exploit the hidden patterns, making traditional methods insufficient. The study proposes GCNShied, an advanced Graph Convolutional Network(GCN) driven model. It helps enhance fraud detection by apprehending complex relations present alongside transactions taking place financially. This is done by integrating tabular data with topological graph features in order to improve precision and accuracy, helping outperform baseline models. Through research and experimentation, GCNShield had achieved 93.4% accuracy as well as 96.7% precision, which helped effectively reduce false positives. The research also explored possible future directions elaborating upon heterogeneous and temporal graph structures, educational awareness tools, and explainable AI. Overall, the findings underscore the need for having graph-based approaches, in order to build scalable and reliable fraud pattern detection systems and strengthen resilience and financial security.

title: PAD-Stream: A Parallel Stream Clustering Framework with Adaptive Load Balancing
authors: Xuehu Zuo, Ning Shi, Zhongguo Yang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The online phase of grid-density-based stream data clustering methods can achieve constant-time single-point updates. However, in the offline phase, density decay and cluster structure adjustment scale linearly with the number of active grids, making it difficult for serial processing to meet the real-time throughput requirements of high-speed data streams. Simple data-parallel strategies struggle to ensure clustering quality due to the inherent contradiction that density information possesses both spatial locality and global correlation. To address these issues, this paper proposes PAD-Stream, a parallel framework for stream data clustering based on spatial adaptive partitioning and dynamic load balancing. This framework employs variance-first dimension selection and equal-sample bisection to construct a KD-Tree partition tree, ensuring strict alignment between partition boundaries and grid boundaries. A Master-Worker collaborative architecture is designed, where each Worker independently maintains the grid density and local clusters within its partition, and the Master restores the global cluster structure integrity through a cross-partition boundary cluster merging algorithm based on the Union-Find disjoint set. Furthermore, to handle load skew caused by data drift, a dynamic load balancing mechanism based on sliding window monitoring and partition tree rebalancing is designed. The framework is implemented on Apache Flink. Experiments on multiple real-world and synthetic datasets demonstrate that PAD-Stream achieves significant parallel speedup while maintaining clustering quality, with acceptable inference latency and resource footprint on commodity hardware, verifying the effectiveness and engineering applicability of the proposed framework.

title: Multimodal Diagnosis of Parkinson's Disease Using Voice and Facial Movement Analysis
authors: Taksharya Gupta, Garima Aggarwal, Atharva Singh, Aman Sharma, Sanyam Bhardwaj, Seema Rawat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Parkinson's disease is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder in which early diagnosis is challenging due to the gradual and subtle onset of symptoms and its reliance on subjective clinical evaluation. Studies have established that vocal deterioration and diminished facial expressiveness serve as distinguishing and measurable biomarkers of Parkinson's disease. However, existing automated screening methods predominantly depend on a single data modality, which constrains their reliability and clinical applicability. This work presents a multimodal screening framework that integrates voice-based acoustic analysis with video-based facial movement assessment. Acoustic biomarkers including jitter, shimmer, harmonic to noise ratio, and spectral features are extracted from patient speech recordings, while facial asymmetry and motion features are derived from frontal video data. XG Boost and SVM classifiers are trained independently for each modality, and their outputs are combined through decision-level fusion to generate a final screening prediction. Experimental results demonstrate that the multimodal framework consistently outperforms each unimodal baseline, achieving higher accuracy and sensitivity in Parkinson's disease detection. The system is further deployed as an asynchronous web application, supporting its use as a scalable, non-invasive decision-support tool in both clinical and remote healthcare settings.

title: A Lightweight Construction Waste Detection Method Based on Improved YOLOv13n
authors: Xinqiang Zhong, Li Lou
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the challenges of target occlusion, diverse morphologies, and complex lighting in construction waste sorting, this study proposes a lightweight detection model named OE-YOLOv13. The method integrates Online Convolutional Re-parameterization (OREPA) and Ghost Convolution (GhostConv) to construct an efficient backbone, reducing computational redundancy through structural re-parameterization and feature hallucination. The Efficient Multi-scale Attention (EMA) mechanism is introduced to enhance interference resistance. An enhanced Feature Pyramid Network (FPN) that integrates OREPA, GhostConv, and EMA is designed for adaptive multi-scale fusion. Experiments show that this model maintains high accuracy while significantly improving inference efficiency and edge-device adaptability, achieving an optimal balance between precision and efficiency.

title: Prediction and Analysis of Infectious Disease Spread Based on Bi-GRU and SVEIQR Model
authors: Si Lu, Shange Huang, Jingmeng Zhang, Kaiqin Wu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: During the spread and development of infectious diseases, some people prevent viral infection through vaccination or home isolation. This paper defines these two groups as vaccinated and quarantined, respectively. Parameters in traditional infectious disease models are constant, which cannot fully reflect the real transmission patterns. Therefore, this paper sets key parameters as time-dependent functions of t, and proposes a time-varying parameter SVEIQR (Susceptible-Vaccinated-Exposed-Infected-Quarantined-Recovered) model to explore the diffusion regularity of infectious diseases. To accurately validate the model effectiveness, official real epidemic datasets of Australia are selected for simulation. The Bi-GRU (Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit) is used for data training. Experimental results show that the coefficient of determination R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> between the predicted trend of infected individuals by the model and real data is 0.9988. This indicates that SVEIQR model can effectively predict the transmission trend of infectious diseases and provide technical support for governments to formulate and select appropriate prevention and control measures.

title: Hybrid Multi-Feature Framework for Robust Digital Image Forgery Detection Using CNN Models
authors: Raja Muthiah, P. Nagaraj, U. V. S. Karthik Reddy, K. Sai Krishna, G. Hussain Nagaraju, S. Sanjeev Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The proliferation of sophisticated editing software has ensured that digital image manipulation has become a key issue in the media, journalism, and forensic arena. The manipulated images may mislead the opinion of the people, distort the evidence, and diminish the trust towards the visual information. Since manual inspection frequently cannot see finer changes, automated systems that are able to scan images in more than one view are urgently required. The study provides an all-inclusive forgery detection scheme of images by combining various analysis techniques. The system uses copymove forgery detection to detect duplicated regions, noise pattern analysis to detect irregularities in the noise distribution among sensors, JPEG compression artifact detection to demonstrate irregularities in frequency components, lighting consistency evaluation to discover irregular light patterns, and edge artifact detection to reveal irregular contours. All the detection modules generate a confidence score, which is combined in an overall evaluation of the probability and degree of manipulation. In contrast to single methods of investigation that in many cases yield unreliable results, this hybrid design takes advantage of synergistic strengths both spatially, statistically, and with respect to frequency. Visualizations of the suspicious areas are also included in the framework, and help the investigators and end-users interpret the results. As experimental assessment shows, the multi-feature analysis method offers an adequate trade-off between accuracy and interpretability, which is why it is applicable to the real-world forensic tasks. In this work, it is highlighted that automated decision support should be combined with the traditional image analysis to achieve reliable systems of digital content validation. Advancements in the future can incorporate machine learning designs to enable the refinement of accuracy in detection processes and to deal with new threats like AI generated synthetic imagery.
