title: Energy Consumption Prediction for Green Buildings Based on LSTM Model
authors: Wenjie Zhou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Green building energy efficiency governance is the core spatial carrier for implementing the “dual carbon” strategy. However, the building energy consumption system is highly coupled with meteorological sudden changes, behavioral dynamics, and equipment inertia, and traditional models often fall into the predicament of “perception failure”. In response to the demands of the intelligent perception era, this paper proposes an intelligent monitoring architecture based on Long Short-Term Memory Network (LSTM). Empirical evidence shows that its root mean square error (RMSE) is significantly reduced by 47.2 % compared to the integrated benchmark model, and the coefficient of determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>) is as high as 0.96. Moreover, through an extreme pressure test using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> Monte Carlo simulations, it is confirmed that the model has excellent anti-noise robustness in extreme conditions. This research provides a highly engineeringvaluable algorithm base for the zero-carbon evolution of smart buildings.

title: Adaptive Feature Decoupling for Text-Video Retrieval: A Dynamic Feature Selection and Semantic Clustering Approach
authors: Wenbin Meng, Ming Xu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Cross-modal retrieval between textual and video modalities remains a formidable challenge due to the inherent semantic gap and the heterogeneity of feature distributions. Existing approaches typically align features in a unified global embedding space, often neglecting modality-specific redundancies and lacking fine-grained semantic grounding. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Adaptive Feature Decoupling (AFD) framework. AFD integrates a dynamic feature selection mechanism that intelligently filters non-discriminative background noise and a semantic clustering module that decomposes holistic embeddings into hierarchical concept-level prototypes. By adaptively adjusting contrastive granularity, our model ensures robust alignment even under noisy inputs. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets—MSR-VTT, ActivityNet Captions, and DiDeMo—demonstrate that AFD consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Specifically, our approach achieves significant gains in Recall@1 and retrieval efficiency, validating the effectiveness of decoupling feature selection from semantic alignment.

title: A CLIP-Guided Generator Domain Adaptation Method Based on Structural Enhancement and Joint Optimization
authors: Xudong Bu, Lianqiang Yang, Bowen Lu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In recent years, generator domain adaptation methods based on vision-language alignment have demonstrated strong cross-domain transfer capability under the zero-shot setting, enabling generative models to perform semantically driven style or domain transformation without requiring target-domain data. Existing generator domain adaptation methods based on CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training) semantic supervision can achieve text-driven zero-shot generation and provide an initialization foundation for subsequent few-shot transfer. However, such methods mainly focus on high-level semantic alignment and remain insufficient in constraining local structural consistency, fine-grained texture quality, and style diversity, which in turn limits the generation quality and its ability to support subsequent few-shot transfer. To address these issues, this paper proposes an enhancement strategy built upon a CLIP-guided generator domain adaptation framework, improving zero-shot generation from both the generator architecture and the optimization objective. By strengthening multi-scale feature interaction and combining joint loss optimization, the proposed method improves local detail quality and style diversity while preserving semantic consistency. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves better generation performance than the baseline model in zero-shot generation tasks and obtains lower Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) values in subsequent few-shot transfer experiments. These results indicate that improving zero-shot generation quality helps enhance subsequent few-shot transfer performance, thereby providing a feasible direction for improving generative model adaptation under low-data conditions.

title: Marathon Bib Detection Algorithm Based on Improved YOLOv11-OBB
authors: Yang Xu, Li Lou
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The detection of marathon runners' bibs is of great significance for the intelligent management of events and for addressing the time-consuming and labor-intensive issues associated with the traditional manual method of retrieving race photos. Aiming at the challenges of scarce datasets, small bib sizes, and missed detections and localization deviations caused by tilt and distortion in the detection process, this paper proposes a rotated object detection algorithm based on the improved YOLOv11-OBB (You Only Look Once version 11-Oriented Bounding Box). First, the advanced Deformable Convolutional Network v3 (DCNv3) is introduced to construct the C3K2-DCNv3 network module, which improves the detection accuracy and adaptability by enhancing the model's spatial modeling capability for geometric deformations. Second, the LSKAM attention mechanism is incorporated to strengthen the feature representation capability for small and distant bibs, while suppressing noise from complex backgrounds. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method enables high-precision and high-recall detection of bibs with low latency on the self-constructed Marathon dataset, facilitating the intelligent management of marathon events.

title: Identification of Hemispheric Lateralization in Lower Limb Motor Imagery Brain Networks Based on RMT Analysis and GCN
authors: Lingyun Gu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Decoding unilateral lower limb motor imagery (MI) from EEG is challenging due to significant individual variability. This study integrates Random Matrix Theory (RMT) with a Graph Convolutional Network (R-GCN) to classify left versus right foot MI at the group level. EEG data from 65 subjects were analyzed using five functional connectivity methods (e.g., PCC, COH, iCOH, PLV, and PLI). RMT was first employed to investigate the spectral properties of these networks, revealing that while most statistics showed universal behavior, the largest eigenvalue differed significantly between tasks, particularly for iCOH networks. The trial-wise connectivity matrices were then vectorized and used as node features, whereas the graph structure was constructed by combining subject-level trial relationships with correlations between the full eigenvalue spectra of trial-level connectivity matrices. Accordingly, the largest eigenvalue was used only as an RMT descriptor for statistical interpretation and was not used as a node feature or as a direct edge weight. Using this design, R-GCN achieved superior classification performance. When significant matrices were used to control spurious connections, performance exceeded 90% for most methods, with iCOH-alpha reaching 93.7% and PLI-theta 95.3%. In contrast, traditional classifiers such as SVM and KNN performed near chance levels. These results demonstrate that an RMT-informed GCN effectively decodes unilateral lower limb MI by mitigating individual variability, offering a robust and accurate approach for EEG-based BCI systems.

title: Trajectory Tracking of Quadrotor UAVs Using Backstepping Control
authors: Kuiming Jiang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: This paper addresses the trajectory tracking control problem of quadrotor unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) with nonlinear and coupled dynamics. A disturbed dynamic model is first established in the inertial frame, and the system is decomposed into position and attitude subsystems. A hierarchical backstepping control framework is proposed, in which an outerloop position controller generates desired roll, pitch, and yaw angles, while an inner-loop attitude controller recursively stabilizes the rotational subsystem. Intermediate control signals are transformed into actual control inputs through a coordinate transformation module. Closed-loop stability is rigorously analyzed using Lyapunov theory, and simulation results show that the proposed controller drives the position errors to within ± 0.02 m, suppresses yaw deviations to nearly zero, and maintains smooth thrust and torque responses with only small oscillations. These findings confirm that the backstepping-based strategy achieves rapid convergence, minimal steady-state errors, and strong robustness against disturbances. Overall, the work demonstrates both the theoretical rigor and the practical effectiveness of backstepping control for precise trajectory tracking and stable attitude regulation in quadrotor UAVs.

title: Smart Healthcare Logistics Integrating Blockchain for Transparent Supply Chain Management System
authors: Karuna Sharma, Ravindra Chauhan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The healthcare supply chain is a sensitive infrastructure that is usually characterized by fragmentation, absence of transparency, and counterfeits of pharmaceuticals. The conventional centralized monitoring systems do not offer real-time insight and immutability of provenance, which pose serious safety hazards and operational inefficiency. This paper suggests a Smart Healthcare Logistics model that incorporates the idea of blockchain technology to create a decentralized, indisputable environment for managing supply chains. The proposed system will generate end-to-end visibility of the medical product trace from manufacturers to patients by utilizing a distributed ledger. The technology based on smart contracts helps automate compliance processes and logistics updates, and thus decreases the administrative burden and avoids intermediaries. Moreover, blockchain integration ensures that all data on transactions, such as monitoring the temperature and transfer of custody, cannot be changed and can be reviewed by all parties authorized to access them. As demonstrated by the results of the experiments, the suggested framework can dramatically improve the level of data integrity, decrease tracking latency, and reaffirm confidence in the pharmaceutical supply chain through verifiable transparency.

title: Wavelet and Attention Enhanced Network for Urban UAV Small Object Detection
authors: Xinyang Zhu, Xitai Na, Jinshuo Shi, Yuheng Ding, Yue Shen, Keyan Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Complex scenes cause severe missed and false detections in UAV-based small object detection, due to blurred tiny target features and intensive background interference. Most YOLO-based improvements focus on single-module tuning, lacking collaborative optimization of feature extraction, background suppression, and high-precision positioning. We propose WLS-YOLO, an enhanced YOLOv11 for UAV small object detection, with three collaborative optimizations: wavelet-based feature enhancement via a C3k2-WTConv module, background suppression via a Local Selective Kernel Attention (LSKA) mechanism, and localization optimization via SIoU loss. Experiments on VisDrone2019 show WLS-YOLO reaches 33.4% mAP@50, 1.3 percentage points above the YOLOv11 baseline, with only 2.69 M parameters, balancing accuracy and real-time efficiency for UAV detection.

title: Impact of Regulatory Announcements and Sentiments on Market Volatility
authors: Varun Saini, Aryan Datla, Ravindra Koranga
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Emotions greatly influence the decisions of humans. We do not feel the emotions because it is something we can control, we feel emotions because we are members of the human race. The sentiments are the main influencing factor when it comes to make decision, either in personal life or in financial terms. Stock market reacting to changes in government policies, economic conditions, and the overall mood of investors, are the rigid example that how the sentiments can shake the trends in stock markets. The numeric data, past prices, OHLC data are the parameters which traditional stock prediction models use. These models left behind an important factor which is also a key parameter, sentiments. Sentiments may change the curve of the stock after a sudden policy change, or after financial news. These models are incapable of measuring these sentiments or how these news impact the sentiments of investors and traders, and how this impact can affect the stock market. In this study, we introduce a new Regulatory-Aware Multimodal Stock Prediction Framework. This new framework combines the sentiment value of regulatory announcements, public opinions, financial news with past market data like OHLC data, past prices, etc., providing more informative predictions during high-impact events. To understand the sentiment values of the financial news, policy changes we use NLP (Natural Language Processing) model such as FinBERT. At the same time, time-series models like LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) help capture the changes and patterns in stock prices over time. At the final step, through an attention based fusion process the model merges both the sentiment and numeric information providing a framework that can analyse the impact of sentiments on market volatility. Adani Enterprise Ltd. was tested using the proposed framework over the Adani-Hindenburg event. The experiment shows that market volatility caused by regulatory and news-driven events are more effectively captured by the model which has integrated sentiments with numeric data for prediction while the only numeric model produces comparatively stable predictions with lower short-term error. This experiment showcases the role of human sentiments in market trends.

title: A Low-Cost VLA-RL Framework for Dexterous Multi-Category Fruit Harvesting in Orchards
authors: Wei Tao, Lufeng Luo, Mingyou Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: In recent years, The Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have shown significant promise for applications in embodied agricultural tasks. To handle the complexities of deploying harvesting robots in orchards with a variety of crop types, we aim to address several key challenges, such as the one-fruit-one-strategy development dilemma, poor generalization capability, high hardware costs, and insufficient operational stability under complex unstructured disturbances. We propose a compact and easily deployable general dexterous harvesting framework based on the integration of VLA model and Reinforcement Learning. The framework takes the lightweight SmolVLA model as its backbone and introduces the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm to perform online finetuning on the initial imitation learning policy. We systematically explores the transfer value of pre-trained vision-language knowledge to agricultural manipulation tasks. With a focus on the pick-and-place harvesting tasks, the model's generalization capabilities are assessed across three dimensions: Vision, Semantic, Execution. We conducted experiments under various challenging conditions, including new backgrounds, dynamic lighting, unseen fruit types, multi-object scenes, and variations in initial poses. Result show that the framework improves the adaptability and stability of harvesting robots under the above conditions. In real-world trials, the model achieves a task success rate over 80%, validating its practical applicability and in dexterous fruit picking. This method in this paper effectively reduces the hardware requirements and deployment costs of harvesting robots. It provides a practical high-generalization and low-cost solution for resource-limited small-scale orchards.

title: Virtual Interactive Visualization Modeling Method for ACL Injury Rehabilitation Process Driven by MR
authors: Chaohong Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: With the rapid development of hybrid display technology (MR), the field of sports injury rehabilitation has also ushered in new development opportunities. Among them, anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injury is one of the most common and difficult types of injuries, while the traditional injury rehabilitation process often has problems such as insufficient individualized rehabilitation and low patient participation. This paper proposes a virtual interactive visualization modeling method for the rehabilitation process of ACL injuries based on MR Technology. By integrating MR, motion acquisition and physiological signal monitoring, an interactive design and realtime response are carried out for the virtual rehabilitation environment. This method can provide patients with an immersive rehabilitation experience and assist doctors in accurately adjusting treatment plans, thereby enhancing treatment efficacy and work efficiency.

title: Performance-Aware Zero-Trust Architecture for Secure and Scalable Multi-Cloud Computing
authors: Nassir Ali Nassir El-Anqoudy, Ajay Shriram Kushwaha, Dinesh Chacko, Lalit Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Cloud computing has grown so quickly that businesses have had to change how they protect themselves. Traditional perimeter-based defences are no longer useful. This paper shows a full zero-trust architecture that was made just for multi-cloud environments. It has adaptive threat response, micro-segmentation, and continuous verification. We have created a single security model that does away with implicit trust assumptions by combining Experimental validation using the CSE-CIC-IDS2018 benchmark dataset (1,048,575 network traffic records) across Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform which we simulated on the real dataset, this method found unauthorised access 96.3 % of the time and avoided false positives 50 % of the time better than traditional security models. The system takes an average of 3.2 milliseconds to authenticate users, and it stops 96.2 % of lateral movement attempts and 100.0 % of privilege escalation attacks. Analysis of performance shows that linear scalability can reach <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0, 0 0 0}$</tex> concurrent sessions without affecting the user experience. These results show that you can use zero-trust principles in real-world cloud environments without making them less secure or less efficient.

title: A Review of Interferometer Direction-Finding and Threat Assessment for LSS UAVs
authors: Min Huang, Zhaorui Li, Huixian Sun
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Low-altitude, slow-speed, small-size (LSS) unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) have become a major threat to low-altitude security due to their strong concealment, high maneuverability, and low cost, posing significant challenges to traditional detection and assessment methods. Interferometer direction-finding technology, with its advantages of high precision, strong real-time performance, and good concealment, occupies a central role in passive radio frequency detection. Threat assessment technology, in turn, serves as the critical link for transforming detection information into actionable command decisions. This paper systematically reviews the current state of research on interferometer direction-finding localization and threat assessment technologies, analyzes the challenges faced by existing approaches in complex electromagnetic environments and multi-target saturation attack scenarios, and explores the technical integration from precise sensing to intelligent decision-making. The study indicates that developing an integrated system combining high-precision direction-finding with intelligent threat assessment represents a crucial direction for enhancing the operational effectiveness of anti-UAV systems.

title: A Comprehensive Review on Machine Learning Approaches for Thermochemical Conversion of Biomass to Biofuels
authors: Ravneet Kaur, Ishan Budhiraja, Rajesh Kumar Shrivastava
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The global and renewable nature of biofuels produced from biomass makes them an attractive alternative source of energy. Converting biomass to biofuels and valueadded products usually occurs via Biochemical and Thermochemical processes. However, the complicated and nonlinear dynamics of Thermochemical processes impede accurate prediction of product quality and process optimisation. Machine Learning (ML) provides an excellent solution for addressing the challenges associated with the data required for performance prediction and product yield and conversion forecasting, as well as optimising Thermochemical Processes. This manuscript investigates the use of various ML practices for the Thermochemical Conversion of Biomass to Biofuels, including ML algorithm families, algorithm performance metrics. The major challenges identified to date include a lack of data, standards for the form and structure of data and model generalization. The review identified gaps in current research and proposed possible avenues for future study in this area, as well as community efforts to enhance the development of efficient, sustainable technologies that can convert biomass into biofuels and value-added products.

title: Development and Implementation of a Virtual Reality Interactive Training Platform for Physical Education Teaching
authors: Yang Fei, Jiying Wei, Dianli Ji
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: The rapid development of Virtual Reality technology enables the introduction of this technology in the process of physical education teaching to achieve better learning effects and interactive experiences. This paper elaborates on the development and implementation of a virtual reality interactive training platform in physical education teaching, and proposes a design scheme of an interactive training platform based on VR technology. It meets the needs of students' physical exercise by establishing virtual scenes, action recognition and real-time response mechanisms. The design concept of the system, key technologies, and the implementation of each functional module were elaborated in detail, and the application of the system in physical education teaching was verified through experiments. This system can enhance students' interest and enthusiasm in exercise and accelerate the learning effect of skills.

title: Learning a Semantic Calibration Network for Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation
authors: Yang Sun, Tao Wang, Anastasia Ioannou, Ge Xu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Semantic image segmentation assigns a predefined category label to each pixel, has achieved significant progress lately. Open-Vocabulary Segmentation (OVS) extends the segmentation task from a fixed set to an open set, enabling the identification and segmentation of novel concepts based on arbitrary text inputs, such as category names or descriptions. In this paper, we propose a novel Semantic Calibration Network (SCN) for open-vocabulary semantic segmentation. Different from prior approaches that focus on feature aggregation or simple fine-tuning of pre-trained models, SCN refines the mask classification process by explicitly modeling the semantic correlations between classes, aiming to enhance the model’s discriminative power while effectively preserving the generalization abilities of the pre-trained CLIP model. Specifically, SCN comprises two core components: Class Disambiguation (CD) and Logits Fusion (LF). First, a cross-attention mechanism is utilized to transform the text embeddings into visually aware pseudo-text embeddings, in order to derive an enhanced similarity score that complements the original mask-text similarity score. Subsequently, the Class Disambiguation module captures implicit inter-class dependencies through a residual architecture to effectively resolve semantic ambiguities. Finally, the Logits Fusion module dynamically integrates multifaceted semantic evidence to ensure that the model achieves a robust semantic consensus while maintaining CLIP’s inherent generalization capability. Comprehensive experimental results on mainstream benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed method achieves significant performance improvements compared to state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: CHART: Clustering and Hardness-Aware Data Selection for Table Understanding
authors: Dong Wang, Xiyue Wang, Haoqi Zheng, Yunpeng Qi
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Table understanding with large language models has recently moved from prompt-based inference to supervised fine-tuning. However, current fine-tuning pipelines often rely on large volumes of distilled data without sufficiently controlling data quality, structural diversity, or sample difficulty. This weakens data efficiency and often hurts out-of-distribution generalization on complex table reasoning tasks. This paper presents CHART, a clustering and hardness-aware data selection framework for table understanding. CHART constructs a compact but informative training set through four stages. It first purifies distilled chain-of-thought data with intra-rationale redundancy detection. Then it builds representative diversity seeds by semantic embedding, whitening, nonlinear manifold projection, and density-based clustering. Next, it trains a probe model on the seeds and mines hard examples from the remaining pool by combining explicit errors with high-perplexity correct samples. Finally, it performs global joint fine-tuning on the union of seed samples and hard samples. Experiments on WikiTableQuestions and TabFact, together with four out-of-domain benchmarks, show that CHART consistently outperforms full distilled-data training while using substantially fewer samples and less training time. The results indicate that, for table understanding, data structure and information density matter more than raw scale.

title: Structure-Aware 3D Gaussian Splatting for Weak-Texture Furniture Reconstruction
authors: Fengbo Bao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) often lacks explicit structural constraints, leading to spatial inconsistency and position drift among Gaussian primitives, which limits its capacity for accurate surface geometry reconstruction. This issue is particularly pronounced in weak-texture furniture scenes, where large planar regions and sparse visual features make reliable reconstruction difficult. To address these challenges, this paper presents a structure-aware 3D reconstruction framework that introduces geometric constraints into the 3DGS pipeline. The proposed method utilizes a visual hull constructed from multi-view foreground masks to constrain Gaussian initialization within object boundaries, reducing the reliance on noise-prone sparse point clouds. During the optimization phase, boundary-aware constraints are applied to suppress drifting Gaussians, alongside planar regularization and unbiased depth-guided optimization to enforce physical surface consistency and continuity. Extensive evaluations on the MVImgNet 2.0, ABO, and uCO3D datasets demonstrate that this approach achieves competitive rendering performance while substantially improving geometric reconstruction quality. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each structural component in textureless object reconstruction.

title: Biocompatible Building-Block-Inspired Brick-Shaped Triboelectric Nanogenerator (TENG) for Energy Harvesting
authors: Muhammad Umaid Bukhari, Alimohammad Haji Adineh, Valliammai Palaniappan, Anthony J Hanson, Abdulrahman Olalekan Yusuf, Pranava Avuthu, Simin Masihi, Dinesh Maddipatla, Bradley J Bazuin, Massood Z. Atashbar
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: The rapid growth of wearable electronics and sensors has driven a substantial increase in the use of portable batteries which in turn leads to regular battery replacement and substantial electronic waste. Improper disposal of these batteries poses serious environmental and health risks by releasing toxic chemicals into soil and waterways. Recently, energy harvesters have emerged as sustainable energy generators due to their compact size, low cost, and ability to utilize ambient mechanical energy. Among these energy harvesters, triboelectric nanogenerators (TENGs) have attracted particular attention due to their high output voltage, flexible design, and efficient operation at low frequencies. Although TENGs have exhibited promising performance throughout various applications, their adoption is often limited by complex fabrication processes and the use of expensive materials. In this work, an economical, easily fabricated building-block-inspired triboelectric nanogenerator (BBI-TENG) is presented using biocompatible and readily available materials such as polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) and copper tape. The proposed TENG generates an open-circuit voltage (Voc) of 43.15 V, a short-circuit current (Isc) of 2.66 μA, and a maximum output power of 10.66 μW/cm2. BBI-TENG converts simple hand tapping into electrical energy capable of operating low-power electronics, and its novel modular brick-shaped design enables scalable stacking to increase electrical energy output. Furthermore, visually engaging colored BBI-TENG can help promote the basic concepts of renewable energy to children, making it well-suited for hands on early STEM education.

title: Efficient Image Dehazing via Prior-Guided Hierarchical Harmonization Network
authors: Chen Wu, Xiongfei Su, Yuning Cui, Mingyue Cui, Wenqi Ren, Jianhou Gan, Xin Yuan, Yulun Zhang, Jingyuan Xia
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: This paper proposes an efficient image dehazing method based on networked physical priors and coordinated fusion, aiming to resolve the long-standing trade-off between performance and computational complexity in deep learning-based dehazing. Existing approaches typically treat physical priors as fixed constraints or introduce them only in shallow layers, failing to achieve hierarchical integration with deep features, which forces networks to stack more modules for better performance at the cost of heavy computation. To address this, our work introduces two key innovations. First, we design three learnable prior operators that transform the Dark Channel Prior, Bright Channel Prior, and Histogram Equalization prior into adaptive modules embedded within the network, enabling dynamic modeling of haze distribution, structural details, and feature statistics. Second, we construct an asymmetric UNet-based fusion architecture, which employs gated attention and a dual-bottleneck design to achieve organic multi-level integration of prior information and deep features throughout the encoding–decoding pipeline. Since the physical priors are structurally encoded into the network, the model does not require a large number of parameters to implicitly learn haze-related patterns. Combined with redundancy suppression through the asymmetric design, this naturally yields a lightweight yet high-performance dehazing network. Extensive experiments on 11 benchmark datasets, including SOTS and Dense-Haze, demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art dehazing performance while maintaining only 1.69M parameters and 17G FLOPs, significantly surpassing existing methods. This provides an effective solution for high-quality image dehazing under resource-constrained scenarios.

title: Generative AI and Digital Ecosystem Resilience: A Proactive Lifecycle-Based Survey
authors: Jonghyun Chung, Rishabh Chaddha, Amanpreet Kaur, Debanshu Das, Sanket Badhe, Nathan Huang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The proliferation of adversarial synthetic content, accelerated by Generative AI (GenAI), is making traditional reactive detection methods increasingly insufficient. This survey synthesizes emerging research on the shift toward proactive detection of adversarial synthetic campaigns. We adopt a unified, lifecycle-based taxonomy that combines socio-technical lifecycle models of coordinated synthetic campaigns with computational methodologies for synthetic content cluster detection. By structuring the analysis around the C5 Interaction Model (Context, Causes, Content, Cycle of Amplification, Consequences), we integrate research streams from machine learning, network science, multimodal forensics, and social science. To differentiate synthetic amplification from authentic baseline traffic, this paper surveys techniques for modeling the creation, seeding, and propagation of new attack vectors, including Coordinated Inauthentic Behavior (CIB), epidemiological modeling, Hawkes processes, graph-based learning, multimodal/deepfake detection, and benchmark datasets. The survey further compares proactive detection methods at different C5 stages using operational criteria such as signal granularity, data requirements, detection lead time, scalability, erroneous-alert risk, and deployment maturity. Finally, we address GenAI-driven challenges, including moving-target synthetic swarms, multimodal drift, benchmark limitations, and the need for standards-based provenance, and we outline a measurable research roadmap for anticipatory and resilient information ecosystems.

title: A Single-Stage Wireless Pulse Charger With Robust Built-in Resistance Compensation and Charging Time Reduction
authors: Junhyuck Lee, Yemin Kim, Dongrim Kim, Byunghun Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article presents a single-stage wireless pulse charger (WPC) for biomedical and wearable devices that integrates AC–DC conversion and battery charging profile into a single power stage. The WPC utilizes a pulse current charging profile to effectively mitigate lithium plating, thereby ensuring extended battery longevity. By utilizing the zero-current intervals, the WPC eliminates voltage drops caused by the battery’s built-in resistance (BIR), enabling direct and accurate sensing of the cell voltage without complex computational circuitry. This robust BIR compensation eliminates the need for the inefficient constant-voltage (CV) mode, significantly reducing charging time while preventing overcharging through precise end-of-charging (EOC) detection. In the fully charged state, the WPC enters a freewheeling state to automatically minimize power consumption and enable the seamless management of charging and recharging modes. Fabricated in a 180-nm BCD process, the proposed WPC achieves a peak receiver efficiency of 92.6%.

title: SJD++: Improved Speculative Jacobi Decoding for Training-free Acceleration of Discrete Auto-regressive Text-to-Image Generation
authors: Yao Teng, Zhihuan Jiang, Han Shi, Xian Liu, Xuefei Ning, Guohao DaiMember, Yu Wang, Zhenguo Li, Xihui Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Large autoregressive models can generate high-quality, high-resolution images but suffer from slow generation speed, because these models require hundreds to thousands of sequential forward passes for next-token prediction during inference. To accelerate autoregressive text-to-image generation, we propose Speculative Jacobi Decoding++ (SJD++), a training-free probabilistic parallel decoding algorithm. Unlike traditional next-token prediction, SJD++ performs multi-token prediction in each forward pass, drastically reducing generation steps. Specifically, it integrates the iterative multi-token prediction mechanism from Jacobi decoding, with the probabilistic drafting-and-verification mechanism from speculative sampling. More importantly, for further acceleration, SJD++ reuses high-confidence draft tokens after each verification phase instead of resampling them all. We conduct extensive experiments on several representative autoregressive text-to-image generation models and demonstrate that SJD++ achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> inference latency reduction and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> step compression, while preserving visual quality with no observable degradation.

title: Embodied Spatial Affordance: Spatial-Aware Affordance Learning for Embodied Navigation and Manipulation
authors: Xiaoshuai Hao, Yingbo Tang, Lingfeng Zhang, Long Chen, Wei Zhou, Jungong Han, Wenbo Ding, Xiao-Ping Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Embodied navigation and manipulation are fundamental capabilities for embodied agents operating in physical environments. A key challenge in this process is understanding the spatial context and the affordances of the environment, which involves recognizing how objects can be interacted with (object affordance) and identifying suitable locations for movement and object placement (free space affordance). While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have shown promise in high-level task planning, their ability to translate reasoning into precise executable actions remains limited, particularly in image-based spatial understanding and precise affordance localization—a critical gap in image processing for robotics. To bridge this gap, we propose EspA, a novel image-to-keypoint model that leverages spatial-aware affordance learning to predict actionable affordances directly from 2D image inputs. Built on a hierarchical vision-language architecture, EspA jointly reasons about object affordances and free space affordances, enabling pixel-level localization of both types of interactions. Crucially, EspA translates language instructions into precise 2D affordance keypoints from observed images, which are then projected into 3D actionable coordinates using depth information. To support this unified affordance reasoning, we introduce the Embodied Spatial Affordance (ESA) dataset, which captures both object-centric interactions and free space contexts. By jointly modeling these affordances in a shared representation space, EspA overcomes the limitations of prior works that treat them independently. The dataset’s fine-grained annotations enable our model to learn the intricate relationship between object functionality and spatial feasibility, significantly enhancing the spatial understanding in embodied tasks. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that EspA outperforms existing state-of-the-art Vision-Language Models (VLMs), both open-source and closed-source, in object and free space affordance prediction. Furthermore, it exhibits superior performance in real-world embodied navigation and manipulation experiments. Our work advances the field of image-based spatial reasoning by providing a scalable solution for translating high-level instructions into low-level actionable affordances. We believe this work paves the way for more robust and versatile embodied agents capable of effectively interacting with complex environments. The dataset, benchmark, and evaluation code will be publicly available to facilitate future research. Project website: https://embodied-spatial-affordance.github.io/.

title: An Interpretable Fault Diagnosis Method for Hydrogen-Related Equipment in Hydrogen Refueling Stations via TSK Fuzzy System
authors: Bing Zhou, Hui Zhang, Haoyu Ming, Xiao Fan, Cong Cui, Bo Sun
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Equipment such as dispensers and compressors in hydrogen refueling stations operates under prolonged high-pressure and high-frequency conditions. Traditional fault diagnosis methods for hydrogen-related equipment typically rely on data-driven deep learning models.Although these models can achieve high diagnostic accuracy, their “black-box” nature hinders fault cause tracing. Moreover, they cannot verify the reliability of the inference process, which limits their interpretability. To address these issues, this study proposes an interpretable equipment-level fault diagnosis method based on sensor data. First, a fuzzy parameter optimization algorithm is developed to jointly optimize the antecedent and consequent parameters of a multi-rule-based Takagi–Sugeno–Kang (TSK) fuzzy system. The diagnostic accuracy reaches 96.54%. Second, an interpretability matrix is constructed to characterize the relationships between sensor data and fuzzy rules, facilitating fault cause tracing. In addition, an inference-based interpretability method is proposed to reveal the internal logical chain from multi-sensor inputs to the final diagnostic results, thereby verifying the accuracy of the inference process. Finally, a case study is conducted using real multi-sensor monitoring data collected from an operating hydrogen refueling station. The results demonstrate that the proposed method maintains high diagnostic accuracy, while providing clear interpretability in decision-making and inference processes. Thus, the proposed method can support the reliable operation and maintenance of hydrogen energy equipment.

title: 3D Beamforming in Multi-cell mmWave Beamspace MIMO Systems
authors: Mengxin Zhou, Zitian Zhang, Xiaoli Chu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Beamspace techniques that reduce radio-frequency (RF) chain complexity in millimeter-wave (mmWave) massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems offer a promising solution to meet the growing data demands of mobile networks. However, they have mainly been modeled and analyzed in a single-cell network without considering inter-cell interference. In this paper, we investigate the downlink transmission in a dense three-dimensional (3D) beamspace MIMO-enabled mmWave multi-cell network with complex interference dynamics. To reduce RF chain complexity, an exclusive multi-beam selection (EMBS) scheme is proposed to establish the effective beamspace channels. Based on this, a power allocation optimization problem is formulated to maximize the cell sum rate, which is then efficiently solved by an iterative water-filling (IWF) algorithm. The cell sum rate is evaluated under both minimum mean square error (MMSE) and zero-forcing (ZF) precoding. Simulation results show that the proposed EMBS scheme combined with IWF achieves an effective trade-off between cell sum rate performance and RF hardware complexity.

title: Near-field Optical Imaging of Ranunculus Pollen Grain Nanoscale Architecture
authors: Denis E. Tranca, Stefan G. Stanciu, Radu Hristu, George A. Stanciu
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: Plant pollen grains exhibit complex nano-architectures on their outer exine surface, which play key critical roles in adhesion, pollination efficiency, and environmental resistance. In this work, we investigate the nanoscale morphology and optical properties of Ranunculus pollen grains using scattering-type scanning near-field optical microscopy (s-SNOM) operating in the near-infrared region (1550 nm). The proposed method builds on s-SNOM's capabilities to provide simultaneous topographical and quantitative optical contrast with nano-resolution, opening new opportunities for detailed characterization of pollenkitt residues and sporopollenin exine structures. To this end, a two-step refractive-index reconstruction procedure is implemented. First, pollenkitt fragments are first analyzed on a silicon reference substrate; subsequently, the extracted refractive index is used to compute the optical constants of exine features. High-resolution nanoscale maps of the refractive index (n) and extinction coefficient (k) reveal compositional heterogeneity and distinct morphological signatures. These results highlight the potential of near-field optical techniques for non-destructive biophotonics investigations of pollen grains and open new potential directions for studying environmental and stress-induced modifications.

title: Benchmarking Event-Driven Spike Encoders on Resting EEG: A Comparative Study
authors: Claudio Frongia, Riccardo Collu, Luigi Raffo, Massimo Barbaro
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: Event-driven spike encoders translate analog input signals into sparse spike trains, enabling ultra-low-power neuromorphic analytics. We compare five spike encoders applied to electroencephalogram (EEG) traces: fixed-threshold asynchronous Delta modulation (ADM), adaptive-threshold ADM (ADM-A), first- and second-order asynchronous Sigma-Delta modulation (ASDM-1, ASDM-2) and a multi-level Level-Crossing scheme (LCS). The comparative study aims to identify the most suitable candidate(s) for downstream Spiking Neural Network (SNN) processing. The test dataset comprises eyes-closed (EC) and eyes-open (EO) resting-state 64-channels EEG from 109 subjects, segmented into 4 s, 6 s, and 8 s epochs (ten windows for each subject). Metrics reported are SNR, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, spike rate, RMSE, band-power error, spike train entropy and power consumption estimation of theoretical circuits associated with the methods. The encoders exhibit clear trade-offs between fidelity, sparsity, and energy. ASDM-2 achieves the best overall fidelity (about 21 dB SNR) at a moderate spike rate (about 360 spikes s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>), whereas ADM-A provides the second-highest fidelity (about 19 dB SNR) at a higher spike rate (about 590 spikes s<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>). Behavioral power estimates in a 45nm CMOS setting indicate that ASDM-2 has lower dynamic power and lower energy per spike than ADM-A, while requiring about 40% higher static power because of its additional integration stages. Overall, the proposed benchmarking framework provides a principled basis for selecting spike encoders in mixed-signal neuromorphic EEG front-ends and motivates future studies in which different encoders are coupled with SNNs to evaluate task-level accuracy and energy per inference.

title: Enabling Automatic Coin Grading Through Late-Fusion of Vision-Language and Hierarchical Transformers
authors: Melaku N. Getahun, Andrey Somov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Measuring coin condition is a procedure performed by specialist in the area of numismatics and museum technologies. Although, the Sheldon scale formalizes the coin conditions within a 70 point system, the grading remains highly subjective process as it is based on the human assessment of the coin strike, luster, wear, and overall preservation. Existing approaches addressing the problem of automatic coin condition measurement are limited in scope, focusing on a small number of coin types or simplifying the grading task into a few broad categories, which restricts their real-world applicability. In this work, we propose automatic mint state coin grading using computer vision methods. We introduce SwinCLIP, a two-stream transformer framework that integrates the Swin Transformer and CLIP models through a late-fusion mechanism to leverage both fine-grained spatial features and high-level semantic information available during the coin image processing. Prior to image processing, we collected a diverse, fully balanced Mint State (MS) coin dataset across eleven distinct MS grades. Experimental results show that SwinCLIP achieves a Top-1 accuracy of 0.591, outperforming strong CNN and Transformer baselines, as well as existing coin grading studies. The model also achieves a Top-5 accuracy of 0.960, indicating reliable ranking of plausible grades. In addition, ±1 and ±2 grade tolerance accuracies of 0.785 and 0.922, respectively, further demonstrate the model’s consistency in making predictions close to the true grade. An ablation study confirms that the fusion of Swin and CLIP provides complementary strengths in texture sensitivity and semantic discrimination.

title: Performance and Sensitivity of the BICEP Array 150 GHz Receiver
authors: S. Zhang, P. A. R. Ade, Z. Ahmed, M. Amiri, D. Barkats, R. Basu Thakur, C. A. Bischoff, D. Beck, J. J. Bock, H. Boenish, V. Buza, B. Cantrall, J. R. Cheshire IV, J. Connors, J. Cornelison, M. Crumrine, A. J. Cukierman, E. Denison, L. Duband, M. Echter, M. Eiben, B. D. Elwood, S. Fatigoni, J. P. Filippini, A. Fortes, M. Gao, C. Giannakopoulos, N. Goeckner-Wald, D. C. Goldfinger, S. Gratton, J. A. Grayson, A. Greathouse, P. K. Grimes, G. Hall, G. Halal, M. Halpern, E. Hand, S. A. Harrison, S. Henderson, T. D. Hoang, J. Hubmayr, H. Hui, K. D. Irwin, J. H. Kang, K. S. Karkare, S. Kefeli, J. M. Kovac, C. Kuo, K. Lasko, K. Lau, M. Lautzenhiser, A. Lennox, T. Liu, S. Mackey, N. Maher, K. G. Megerian, L. Minutolo, L. Moncelsi, Y. Nakato, H. T. Nguyen, R. O'Brient, S. N. Paine, A. Patel, M. A. Petroff, A. R. Polish, T. Prouve, C. Pryke, C. D. Reintsema, S. Richter, T. Romand, M. Salatino, A. Schillaci, B. Schmitt, R. Schwarz, B. Singari, A. Soliman, T. St Germaine, A. Steiger, B. Steinbach, R. Sudiwala, G. P. Teply, K. L. Thompson, C. Tucker, A. D. Turner, C. Vergés, A. G. Vieregg, A. Wandui, A. C. Weber, J. Willmert, C. L. Wong, W. L. K. Wu, H. Yang, C. Yu, L. Zeng, C. Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The BICEP/Keck collaboration searches for B-mode polarization in the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) as evidence of inflationary gravitational waves, with current best constraints placing <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$r_{0.05} < 0.035$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Building even deeper and larger maps requires scaling detector count at 150 GHz, where B-mode power is expected to peak at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell \sim 80$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The BICEP Array 150 GHz receiver (BA150) fields 7776 superconducting transition edge sensor (TES) detectors, over 15× the 512 deployed in the previous BICEP/Keck 150 GHz receiver, making it the densest time-division multiplexed readout receiver in the collaboration's history. We present the complete detector characterization of all 12 detector modules as deployed in BA150. We report thermal properties (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$T_{c}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$G_{c}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$P_\rm{sat}$</tex-math></inline-formula>), end-to-end optical efficiencies of 20–48%, and fractional bandwidths of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim$</tex-math></inline-formula>28% across all modules. We additionally report the identification and mitigation of an out-of-band high-frequency photon leak spanning 180–265 GHz, and present preliminary investigations of elevated on-sky noise attributed to aliased high-frequency detector noise.

title: A Tractable Gamma Approximation for Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface-Assisted mmWave Channel Analysis
authors: Olzhas Yesken, Leila Tlebaldiyeva, Galymzhan Nauryzbayev, Durvudkhan Suragan, Sultangali Arzykulov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) are emerging as a key enabler of 6 G wireless systems, offering the potential to significantly enhance signal propagation and network performance. This paper presents a novel approach for modeling RIS-assisted millimeter-wave communication channels using a Gamma-distributed approximation. By modeling the channel gains between the base station, RIS, and user as Gamma random variables, the proposed method captures both line-of-sight conditions and shadowing effects effectively. We derive closed-form expressions for two critical performance metrics, such as outage probability (OP) and ergodic capacity, along with their corresponding upper and lower bounds. The analytical results are validated through extensive Monte Carlo simulations, demonstrating close agreement between the approximation and simulation data. Furthermore, we observe that the accuracy of the Gamma approximation improves as the number of RIS elements increases, and the OP bounds tighten under less severe channel conditions.

title: FillFusion-GS: From Sparse Scans to Dense Scenes Via Visual-Structure-Guided 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Yunda Sun, Zhong Wang, Lin Zhang, Ying Shen, Shengjie Zhao
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Achieving high-fidelity dense 3D reconstruction remains a critical challenge in autonomous robotics. While 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has shown promise, its application with LiDAR is severely hindered by the mismatch between the dense initialization required by 3DGS and the inherent sparsity and limited field of view of LiDAR. This data deficiency results in inter-scan voids and unobserved structural voids, which force Gaussian primitives to undergo disordered deformation and splitting during optimization, ultimately degrading reconstruction quality. To bridge this gap, we propose FillFusion-GS, a novel geometry-aware hybrid explicit-implicit LiDAR-Camera fusion reconstruction framework. Unlike existing methods, our method prioritizes establishing a structurally complete and dense spatial distribution of primitives before optimization. Specifically, implicit neural representations are employed to extract continuous scene surfaces from sparse LiDAR scans to resolve inter-scan voids. Building upon this, a Visual Planar-Guided Structure Completion (VP-GSC) module is introduced to leverage 2D visual cues for completing unobserved structural voids. With this complete primitive spatial distribution established, a tightly-coupled optimization scheme is further incorporated to ensure cross-modal consistency, enabling both high-fidelity rendering and accurate geometry. Additionally, a multi-sensor indoor dataset named the Jishi dataset is collected using a custom handheld device, allowing for flexible trajectories. Extensive experiments on public benchmarks and the Jishi dataset demonstrate that FillFusion-GS effectively fills geometric voids, outperforming state-of-the-art methods in terms of both geometric accuracy and visual rendering quality.
