title: LoRA-Based Benchmarking of Multilingual BERT Models for Hindi-English Code-Mixed Text
authors: Shashank Vishwakarma, Rakesh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Hindi-English code-mixed text (“Hinglish”) is ubiquitous in social media, but token-level sequence labeling remains difficult due to transliteration, non-standard spelling, and frequent code-switching. We benchmark parameterefficient fine-tuning via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) on three pre-trained transformer encoders (mBERT, MuRIL, and IndicBERT) using the COMI- LINGUA benchmark. In this paper, we found that MuRIL performs best in the Language Identification (LID) task, as it was specifically trained to understand Hindi written in English characters, whereas mBERT is strongest in Part-of-speech (POS) tagging and Named Entity Recognition (NER). We also showed that LoRA, which updates only a small fraction of parameters, is able to achieve a good enough level of performance for the resource constrained code mixed Natural Language Processing (NLP)

title: Smart Carbon Emission Analytics for Household Loads: A Gradient Boosting-Based Predictive and Real-Time Framework
authors: Naman Sharma, Rinchen Angmo, Divya Asija, R. K. Viral
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Decarbonization efforts must now be extended be-yond industrial sectors into urban residential settings due to the rapid rise in atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO2). Despite the fact that the real estate industry is responsible for about 40% of global carbon emissions, household mitigation solutions now primarily rely on passive monitoring or delayed feedback loops due to a lack of active remediation techniques. This paper presents the design, construction, and validation of a novel Cyber-Physical System (CPS) that links digital prediction and physical chemical conversion. The system can predict short term appliance level emission spikes with Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.0260 kg using the Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) which is trained on REFIT Smart Home dataset (917756 data points). Internet of Things (IOT) based wet scrubber is powered by the proposed predictive engine. It uses feedforward control logic in AI predictions pre trigger 1.7 MHz ultrasonic atomization unit which creates a huge calcium hydroxide mist, unlike traditional reactive systems having detection latency. By eliminating the dead time, “Predict and Mineralize” technique ensures zero latency capture. The ESP32 microcontroller-driven hardware prototype gives a scalable path for active urban decarbonization and shows the viability of decentralized, small-scale carbon collection.

title: Frequency-Aware Shared Mixture-of-Experts for Efficient Network Traffic Anomaly Detection
authors: Shenglin Teng, Cheng Fang, Hongjiu Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Modern network traffic anomaly detection is hindered by ubiquitous encryption and label scarcity. To tackle this, we propose a frequency-aware two-stage framework. During self-supervised pre-training, Haar Wavelet decomposition isolates trend structures from high-frequency noise, while a Tri-Band Circular Cross-Attention (TB-CCA) mechanism and a shared Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) head capture deep cross-frequency dependencies to establish robust generic representations. These features are then fused with raw inputs during supervised fine-tuning for accurate classification. Evaluated on the CICIDS2017 dataset, our model achieves 99.25% accuracy and a 98.92% F1-score. Notably, it maintains a 96.51% F1-score using only 3% of the labeled data, demonstrating exceptional robustness against sparse supervision.

title: Federated DDQN Driven Latency Optimization for VRCS Using Star RIS in 6G Networks
authors: Shivam Chaudhary, Ishan Budhiraja, Rajat Chaudhary
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Ensuring ultra-reliable, low-latency communication in 6G-enabled VRCS is still difficult because vehicles move quickly, channels change rapidly, and devices have varying computing capacities. The conventional Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) approaches, including Deep QNetwork (DQN) and DDQN, facilitate adaptive decisionmaking; however, their reliance on centralized training leads to substantial communication overhead and potential privacy concerns. This paper presents a Federated Double Deep QNetwork (Fed-DDQN) framework to minimize latency in simultaneous transmitting and reflecting reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (STAR-RIS) assisted Vehicular Road Cooperative Systems (VRCS). The proposed framework performs joint optimization of spectrum allocation, transmit power, and RIS phase configuration in a distributed learning environment. Through the fusion of federated learning and double Q-value estimation, it reduces overestimation bias, maintains privacy, and achieves faster convergence. Simulations conducted using a 3GPP-based vehicular model indicate that the proposed Fed-DDQN framework achieves up to 35% lower latency than existing DRL and Fed-DRL approaches, confirming its potential for efficient and scalable vehicular communication in 6G networks.

title: A Study on a Trusted Storage System for Cybersecurity Data Based on Blockchain Technology
authors: Simeng Ma, Xing Fan
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Cybersecurity data storage faces challenges regarding integrity and trustworthiness. This paper designs a blockchain-based trustworthy cybersecurity data storage system that innovatively adopts a hybrid on-chain/off-chain storage architecture and a customized consensus mechanism, thereby ensuring the tamper-proof nature and full traceability of security data. The research establishes a comprehensive technical framework encompassing data collection, verification, storage, and analysis. It overcomes the security limitations of traditional centralized storage and significantly enhances the system’s tamper-resistance and audit efficiency. The system implements fine-grained access control and efficient query capabilities, providing a trusted infrastructure for cybersecurity incident analysis and threat intelligence sharing.

title: Smart IoT-Based Pest Detection System Using Computer Vision
authors: Prince Raj, Nidhi Kumari, Chhoti Kumari, Kajal Yadav, Kalpana Sagar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Due to small datasets, complex visual conditions, and the demands of deep learning models, automated pest detection is still a difficult task in real agricultural settings. The lightweight computer vision pest detection system presented in this paper is intended for practical implementation in agricultural settings with limited resources. The suggested method uses a YOLOv8n object detection model that was only trained on real-world pest photos taken in the field by our team. To minimize model complexity and ensure a reliable early-stage pest presence monitoring dashboard, a single-class detection strategy is used. To reflect practical edge deployment constraints, the entire process of training and evaluation pipeline is run on a CPU setup only. The suggested system achieves a precision of 0.829, a recall of 0.631, a mAP @ 50 of 0.707, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 50-95$</tex> of 0.411, according to the experimental results. On a typical consumer-grade processor, the model achieves an average inference time of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4 4 m s}$</tex> per image, suggesting that it is suitable for near real-time edge deployment. These findings support the viability and efficacy of the suggested strategy for IoT-enabled smart agriculture applications.

title: Polarization image restoration method based on channel attenuation differences in casing of oil wells
authors: Yifan Wang, Jinchuan Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the imaging degradation caused by suspended particles and oily contaminants in oil well drilling water, this study proposes a polarization image restoration method based on the prior of channel attenuation differences. By analyzing the entropy distribution of the three color channels, it is observed that the red channel exhibits enhanced information while the blue channel is suppressed by scattering. Based on this finding, a strategy for extracting the target light and backscattered light is developed. Within the framework of active polarization imaging, frequency domain filtering is employed to estimate the degrees of polarization of the two types of light, and an enhancement metric is introduced to adaptively optimize the cutoff frequency, thereby achieving effective separation between the target and the background. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches across various image quality metrics and can significantly improve the imaging quality of the inner wall of oil well casings.

title: MCDDIM: A Fast Multi-Conditional Diffusion Model for 3D Porous Microstructure Reconstruction
authors: Boyu Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The stochastic reconstruction of 3D porous microstructures is crucial for understanding and designing advanced building materials. While denoising diffusion models have shown promise in generating high-quality reconstructions, their reverse sampling process is notoriously time-consuming. To address this, we propose a multi-conditional denoising diffusion implicit model (MCDDIM). MCDDIM builds upon the denoising diffusion implicit model (DDIM) framework to significantly accelerate generation by employing a non-Markovian reverse process with a reduced number of sampling steps. More importantly, it integrates four key physical property conditions—porosity, pore radius, throat radius, and coordination number—as constraints to guide and control the reconstruction, ensuring accuracy despite the acceleration. We validate MCDDIM on a porous material dataset (FinneyPack) and compare it against state-of-the-art methods. Evaluations across pore space, morphology, connectivity, and permeability demonstrate that MCDDIM achieves reconstruction quality comparable to the much slower MCDDPM, while drastically reducing the generation time. This makes MCDDIM an efficient and effective tool for 3D porous microstructure reconstruction.

title: Swin-AttU-Net: A Hybrid Swin Transformer and Attention-Gated Network for Brain MRI Tumor Segmentation
authors: Yunfei Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of low-grade gliomas in FLAIR MRI remains challenging due to ambiguous tumor boundaries, peritumoral edema, and imaging artifacts. This paper proposes Swin-AttU-Net, a hybrid architecture that integrates a hierarchical Swin Transformer encoder for global context modeling with a dual attention-gated decoder incorporating channel and spatial attention, further enhanced by residual dense connections for improved feature reuse. Evaluated on the LGG MRI Segmentation dataset, the model achieves a Dice score of 92.3% and superior boundary precision compared to U-Net and recent transformer baselines. Extensive ablation studies and visualizations confirm the effectiveness of each component. The approach bridges local convolutional inductive bias with long-range transformer dependencies, offering a computationally efficient solution for clinical neuro-oncology applications. Future extensions to multimodal and 3D data are discussed.

title: Urban Spatial Evolution Prediction Based on the Spider Monkey Optimization Algorithm
authors: Yan Yan, Xiaoyan Tang, Funan Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: For cities in arid regions characterized by complex climates and varied topography, the use of cellular automata (CA) to construct spatial simulation and prediction models is a key approach to advancing sustainable urban development. To this end, this paper proposes a novel SMO-CA model based on the Spider Monkey Optimization (SMO) algorithm, and employs Urumqi as a case study to simulate the urban evolution patterns for the years 2012 and 2022. The results indicate that between 2002 and 2022, the urban evolution of Urumqi primarily centered on the outskirts of the city center, exhibiting a marked trend of agglomeration. In terms of model accuracy, the overall accuracy of the SMO-CA model for 2012 reached 98.39%, with a FOM of 42.27%; for 2022, the overall accuracy was 98.42% and the FOM was 36.81%, fully validating the model’s effectiveness. Finally, the constructed SMO-CA model was employed to forecast urban development in Urumqi under two different scenarios for 2032, with the aim of providing a scientific basis for optimizing urban development planning.

title: IoT-Based Adaptive Solar Street Lighting System with Dynamic Dimming and Fault Detection
authors: Paranay Gupta, Pooja Anand, Akanksha Singh, Lipsa Das, Durgesh Kumar Jha, Amit Kumar Saroj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The infrastructure in urban and the rural environment includes street lighting that is crucial in road safety, security of the people, and night visibility. Nevertheless, the traditional street lighting systems are unsuitable as they do not take into account the nocturnal real-life features, including the traffic congestion and the natural lighting, during the night with full brightness. This will lead to a high energy usage, higher operation expenses, and higher environmental impact. Moreover, traditional systems fault detection is performed by hand, this method becomes time consuming, inefficient and causes late maintenance. To resolve the above problems, this paper will present an IoT-based adaptive solar street lighting system that dynamically dims and detects faults. To offer an energy-efficient and intelligent street lighting solution, the system will require renewable solar energy, sensor-based automation, and IoT. Solar panels are used to produce electrical power during the day that is used on rechargeable batteries and used at night to power LED street lights. This makes it less reliant on grid electricity and allows using the system in remote and rural regions and cities. The system uses a Light Dependent Resistor that deciphers the ambient light levels and then automatically governs the ON and OFF action of street lights. There is a Passive Infrared sensor installed which detects the presence of pedestrians or vehicles at night and allows dynamic dimming of the LED lights. In case of no movement, the lights will turn on with lower brightness in order to save on energy. The moment the movement is detected, the brightness improves instantly to maintain the safety and the adequate visibility. Through this adaptive lighting system, a lot of unnecessary power gets saved and at the same time, effective light is produced. The main characteristic of the proposed system is that it is able to detect and monitor faults through the use of the IoT technology.

title: Research on the Influence of Rotor Spacing on Lift in Coaxial Dual-Rotor Drones
authors: Zipeng Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: Coaxial dual-rotor drones have broad application prospects in both civil and military fields due to their compact structure, strong maneuverability, and portability. However, the aerodynamic interference between the upper and lower rotors of coaxial dual-rotors can significantly affect their aerodynamic performance and lift efficiency. As a key design parameter for regulating the aerodynamic interference of dual rotors, the optimization of rotor spacing is of great importance for improving overall performance. This paper establishes an improved partitioned inflow model based on the blade element momentum theory, taking into account the rotor spacing parameter, and systematically analyzes the influence of different rotor spacings on the lift characteristics and efficiency of coaxial dual-rotors in a hovering state. Using the blade element momentum theory, the disturbed flow field of the lower rotor is divided into two independent uniform flow fields to determine the optimal rotor spacing. Under the experimental conditions of this study, it is found that when the ratio of rotor spacing to rotor radius is between 0.775 and 1, the dual-rotor system operates with high efficiency. In addition, it was observed that when the ratio exceeds 0.55, the rate of increase in total thrust diminishes significantly, and the rate of increase in figure of merit also diminishes significantly.

title: Efficient AI-Enabled Disaster Identification Using Drone Imagery and Transfer Learning
authors: Chinmay Lakra, Misbah Anjum, Partha Sarathi Chakraborty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Natural disasters are very frightening, and it is crucial to identify the type of destruction they cause. Traditional ground-based assessment methods are slow and can be hazardous when responding to a disaster. The sooner their true nature (disaster types) is understood, the faster the relief agency can provide the necessary relief. This paper presents an efficient AI-enabled disaster identification system that rapidly determines the disaster type by analyzing aerial images captured with quadcopter drones using deep learning-based image classification. The model employs a MobileNetV2-based Convolutional Neural Network that takes flight data images as input. The proposed model is trained on a 2,676-labelled drone image dataset and uses transfer learning to predict four disaster categories: earthquakes, fires, land disasters, and water disasters. Further, the proposed model is implemented using TensorFlow and Keras, then evaluated through experiments conducted on Google Colab, and inference is demonstrated on a local system. The experimental evidence and empirical analysis show that the proposed model performs well, high accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 90 \%$</tex>, and does so in a speedy, energy-efficient manner, making it ideal for real-time disaster monitoring.

title: Dual-Guided Dense Video Captioning with Event Saliency and Semantic Memory
authors: Siming Yang, Hongxi Chen, Jiangyuan Zhao, Zhiguo Du
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Writing coherent descriptions for unedited, untrimmed videos presents a unique challenge. Models must identify exactly when different events occur. Recent end-to-end systems treat this task as a unified sequence problem. However, because they assign equal weight to every frame, these models face two major difficulties. First, background noise produces serious temporal redundancy. Second, fine-grained actions often become semantically ambiguous. To address these structural limitations, we develop the Memory-Enhanced Saliency Architecture (MESA). As an end-to-end pipeline, MESA filters visual inputs while expanding their semantic context. To remove non-event background frames, the Saliency-Enhanced Sequence Network applies a dynamic reweighting mechanism. At the same time, a Contextual Recall Unit retrieves high-level semantic prototypes from an external memory bank, which helps reduce visual ambiguity. Experiments on the ActivityNet Captions and YouCook2 benchmarks show that MESA effectively suppresses noise and improves the clarity of complex actions. As a result, it localizes and describes events more precisely than standard sequence-to-sequence baselines.

title: An Improved SVMD Based Method for Underwater Passive Range Estimation
authors: Zheng Xiong, WangSheng Lin
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Successive variational mode decomposition (SVMD) can automatically extract signal modes with rigorous mathematical proof. However, in different scenarios, it tends to misinterpret non-modal components, such as noise, as modes when processing signals, affecting the accuracy of the result in the next process negatively. To address this issue, the paper proposes a sparsity-improved SVMD method based on the dispersive property of underwater acoustic signals, which uses Sparse Fourier transform (SFT) to locate mode in frequncy domain approximately after warping transform and then uses SVMD to decompose various mode so as to avoid non-modal component extraction. Moreover, this method bases on the prior information and accelerates the computation by reducing iterative counts.

title: Semi-Supervised Domain Adaptation for Specific Emitter Identification via Spherical Space Mapping
authors: Xinping Du, Wei Xia, Mingchen Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Specific Emitter Identification (SEI) based on Radio Frequency Fingerprints (RFFs) is an effective approach for mitigating spoofing attacks in wireless communication networks. However, deep learning-based SEI suffers from severe performance degradation when training and testing signals are collected under different channel conditions, and acquiring sufficient labeled samples is often impractical in non-cooperative scenarios. To address these issues, we propose a semi-supervised domain adaptation SEI method via Spherical Space Mapping (SEI-SSM) with few labeled target-domain samples. By embedding features into a spherical space, the extractor could extract domain-invariant features. In addition, we propose a pseudo-label weighting strategy to fully leverage unlabeled target-domain samples for domain adaptation. Experimental results on the public WiFi dataset demonstrate that SEI-SSM outperforms state-of-the-art domain adaptation SEI methods.

title: A Study of Friction Accuracy and Trajectory Error in Robots
authors: Kunpeng Wang, Qilin Shu, Haoyang Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: An eStribeck friction model is proposed, and a more comprehensive friction dynamics framework is constructed by incorporating nonlinear stiffness terms and adaptive temperaturedependent parameters. The model combines the dynamic characteristics of the LuGre model, which significantly improves the simulation accuracy of mixed lubrication and boundary friction phenomena. Based on this model, a friction-compensated controller is designed for the trajectory tracking task of a six-degree-of-freedom industrial robot arm. In order to update the parameters of the friction model in real time, a genetic algorithm is used for parameter identification, and the parameters are dynamically updated under an online learning framework. Through simulation experiments and physical verification, the results show that after the friction compensation, the robot trajectory tracking error is reduced by 35%, and the energy consumption is reduced by 18%, which effectively improves the robot's localization accuracy and dynamic response capability. In addition, the friction model proposed in this paper not only improves the theoretical depth of friction modeling, but also provides a reliable engineering tool for precision assembly and medical robotics in intelligent manufacturing, which has important theoretical significance and practical application value.

title: A Method for Visual Communication Image Style Recognition and Creative Reconstruction Based on Deep Learning
authors: Guicai Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This study designs a method for visual communication image style recognition and creative reconstruction based on deep learning, aiming to enhance the accuracy of image style recognition and the innovation ability of reconstruction. For image features, a convolutional neural network (CNN) is selected for style recognition. Through style encoding and decoding, as well as depth feature matching, the image style is accurately discriminated and analyzed. At the same time, by means of pixel super-resolution, self-attention generation methods and image texture synthesis, etc., the creative reconstruction of images is achieved. The research results show that the proposed method has achieved good effects in both style discrimination and image creative reconstruction, and can be applied to practical work, demonstrating innovation.

title: A Portable C-V Characterization System for MEMS Sensors
authors: Zhou Zheng, Haoxiang Li, Wang Meng, Chao Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Capacitance-voltage (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{V}$</tex>) measurement is widely used to study the electromechanical behavior of microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) devices. Most existing measurements rely on laboratory impedance analyzers, which limits portability and prevents on site testing. This work develops a compact platform for portable C-V characterization of MEMS devices. The system integrates a symmetric high voltage bias generator with a relaxation oscillator based capacitance sensing circuit. The driver stage produces stable bipolar excitation up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 90 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex>, allowing electrostatic actuation of the device under test. Capacitance variations are translated into oscillation frequency and processed by a microcontroller to reconstruct the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{V}$</tex> response. Experimental evaluation confirms stable high voltage operation and femtofarad level measurement capability. Measurements on a capacitive micromachined ultrasonic transducer (CMUT) array reveal the nonlinear capacitance behavior associated with membrane deflection and clearly identify the collapse and recovery voltages. The results demonstrate that the proposed platform enables practical portable characterization of MEMS devices. This compact system enables picofarad-level on-site <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C}-\mathbf{V}$</tex> characterization outside the laboratory and offers a practical and scalable solution for high-precision MEMS device evaluation in real-world environments.

title: Dynamic Credit Scoring Model Enhanced by Machine Learning Algorithms: A Case Study of Sesame Credit
authors: Ruitong Bian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This paper uses Sesame Credit as a case study to explore dynamic credit scoring models based on digital footprints and their application in cross-border credit expansion. The research employs machine learning algorithms to optimize Sesame Credit's dynamic credit scoring model, enhancing the real-time processing capability and predictive accuracy of digital footprint data. Quantitative results show that the enhanced model improves accuracy by 6.20%, F1-score by 6.23%, and AUC value by 2.83% compared to the traditional model. The study also analyzes computational efficiency challenges in cross-border expansion. Simultaneously, its overseas expansion faces multiple challenges, including data compliance, adaptation to religious cultures, and geopolitical risks. The research reveals the innovative value of machine learning-enhanced digital credit scoring and holds significant importance for improving credit systems and promoting the development of inclusive finance.

title: Intelligent Traffic Signal Control Using Fuzzy Logic, Multi-Agent Systems, and Optimization Techniques: A Comprehensive Study
authors: Shalini Aggarwal, Ayan Rajput
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The problems caused by growing numbers of vehicles and rapid urban development in first-tier Indian cities are now commonplace. Managing traffic lights in the traditional way is inefficient and continues to worsen the problem by increasing congestion and wait times, wasting fuel, and polluting the environment. Integrated traffic control systems can utilize IoT and Intelligent Transport Systems (ITS) to overcome these challenges. This paper focuses on the design and performance analysis of an easily scalable and cost effective IoT sensor architecture for smart traffic light control within the budget and infrastructural constraints of Indian metropolitan cities. The system utilizes economical sensors, edge controllers, efficient embedded systems, value engineering in communications, and adaptive signal processing for traffic management based on the actual density of vehicles. For modularity and positive engineering in the extension and the utilization of artificial intelligence optimization techniques, the system employs a scalable, fault-tolerant modular IoT stack. The results show an average reduction in intersection throughput and emission proxies, as well as average waiting times and queue lengths. This confirms the proposed method, evaluated against SUMO microscopic traffic simulations, is effective compared to the conventional fixed-time signal method. The results support the adaptive, IoT-driven traffic control approach as a viable and effective smart city solution for emerging urban areas.

title: Bi-Directional DFE-Based Maximum Likelihood Sequence Cross-Detection in a 112-Gbps PAM4 Wireline Transceiver
authors: Yafeng Zhang, Junpeng Zhou, Hui Wang, Haonan Wang, Xiang Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: As wireline data rates continue to scale, pre-cursor inter-symbol interferences (ISIs) become increasingly significant due to channel dispersion and bandwidth limitations. Conventional decision feedback equalizers (DFEs) are inherently limited to cancel post-cursor ISIs only, while feedforward equalizers (FFEs) suffer from fundamental trade-offs, either reducing signal swing at the transmitter or amplifying noise at the receiver. To address these problems, a bi-directional DFE (BiDi-DFE) architecture is introduced to equalize both pre-cursor and post-cursor ISIs without noise amplification. Leveraging the opposite decision-propagation directions of the two DFE paths, a maximum likelihood sequence cross-detection (MLSCD) scheme is further proposed to detect error events and selectively enable maximum likelihood sequence detection (MLSD), improving energy efficiency. A 112-Gbps DSP-based wireline transceiver deployed over a 45 dB insertion-loss channel achieves a bit error rate (BER) reduction from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.5\times 10^{-6}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.6\times 10^{-7}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> using BiDi-DFE, and further to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7.5\times 10^{-9}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> with MLSCD, incurring only an estimated 20-mW power increase in post-layout compared with a 1-tap DFE while achieving an estimated 40-mW power saving in post-layout relative to a full-state MLSD (FS-MLSD). These results indicate that the proposed BiDi-DFE and MLSCD framework offers a scalable solution for future high-loss, high-speed wireline transceivers.

title: FMamba: Bi-Mamba based on Fast Gated-attention for Multivariate Time-series Forecasting
authors: Shusen Ma, Yun-Bo Zhao, Yu Kang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: In multivariate time-series forecasting (MTSF), extracting both temporal features and inter-variable correlations is crucial. While Transformer-based models excel at capturing variable correlations, their quadratic computational complexity creates significant overhead, particularly for inputs with numerous variables. Recently, Mamba, a selective state space model, has shown promise due to its linear complexity and strong temporal extraction capabilities. However, Mamba lacks the explicit capability to model inter-variable dependencies found in Transformers, and its unidirectional nature limits its receptive field. To address these limitations while maintaining efficiency, we propose FMamba, a novel framework with global selective capabilities. FMamba integrates a bidirectional Mamba (Bi-Mamba) structure with Gated-Performers—an improved linear Transformer with a content-aware gating mechanism. Technically, Gated-Performers explicitly compute dependencies among input variables, while Bi-Mamba selectively models temporal dependencies. Experimental results on seven public datasets demonstrate that FMamba outperforms Mamba-based and Transformer-based baselines, reducing the Mean Squared Error (MSE) by an average of 15.5% and 15.2%, respectively.

title: Toward Globally Connected Vehicles: A Technical Analysis of NTN-V2X Integration, Security, and Standardization
authors: Faisal Rasheed Lone, Azra Nazir, Prabal Verma, Huaming Wu, Sukhpal Singh Gill
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: The realization of globally connected and Automated Vehicles (CAVs) demands ubiquitous, reliable and secure Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication. While 5G NR-V2X establishes a robust terrestrial foundation, critical coverage gaps persist in remote, rural, maritime, and disaster-affected areas. This paper presents a comprehensive technical and standardization-focused framework for integrating Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTNs), satellite constellations and High-Altitude Platform Stations (HAPS), into a Space-Air-Ground Integrated Network (SAGIN) for global V2X services. The paper does a comparative analysis of the evolution of V2X technology from IEEE 802.11p to 5G NR-V2X with explicit mapping of normative capabilities against unresolved NTN-V2X A technical analysis of the SAGIN architecture aligned with 3GPP TS 38.821 and O-RAN Alliance specifications, including standardised interface definitions and NTN handover signalling flows, while also highlighting a comprehensive security threat paradigm for Open RAN-based NTN-V2X systems, with countermeasures linked to 3GPP TS 33.501/33.536, ETSI TS 102 941, O-RAN WG11, and NIST FIPS 203–205 post-quantum standards. a concrete 3GPP Release 20 and 6G standardization roadmap with actionable work items addressing identified gaps is proposed.

title: Anomaly Detection for Stealthy FDIAs in Distributed Cyber-Physical Electric Vehicle Scheduling Systems
authors: Saba Marandi, Danial Jafarigiv, Ribal Atallah, Mohsen Ghafouri, Chadi Assi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: This paper presents a cyber-physical anomaly detection framework to safeguard distributed EV scheduling systems against stealthy training-time False Data Injection Attacks (FDIAs). First, a hybrid CNN–LSTM model that captures both spatial and temporal patterns in EV charging behavior is employed to forecast aggregated EV loads accurately. In the subsequent stage, the forecasted values are incorporated into a distributed convex optimization framework. The proposed ADMM-based formulation is designed at the aggregator level and explicitly integrates voltage regulation and power-loss constraints to ensure scalable and feasible scheduling across the grid. Then, systematic training-time FDIA scenarios are generated and injected into the historical data to analyze how corrupted forecasts propagate through the scheduling process and cause voltage deviations and local constraint violations. To mitigate these threats, an image-based anomaly detection model is proposed using a modified Residual Network (ResNet50) architecture tailored to structured EV scheduling data. These design enhancements improve the model's sensitivity to subtle spatio-temporal anomalies that conventional CNN, LSTM, and standard ResNet50 models fail to capture. The framework is validated using real-world EV charging data mapped to IEEE benchmarks, showing that stealthy FDIAs corrupt scheduling and cause voltage deviations, while the proposed detection model effectively identifies these malicious patterns with high accuracy

title: A Reset-Less PCB Rogowski Sensor with High- and Low-Frequency Signal Superposition Circuit for Accurate Switch-Current Measurement
authors: Peng Sun, Guangyao Wu, Zelong Qu, Zhibin Zhao, Yumeng Cai, Xuebao Li, Bing Ji
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Fast power electronics systems, especially those based on SiC devices, are vulnerable to cyber intrusions. Physical signals such as switch current enable cyber-security detection. Hence, there is a need for high bandwidth, compact, and accurate current measurement solution to serve the fast power electronics systems. Embedded PCB Rogowski sensor is among the favorites. However, existing PCB Rogowski sensors struggle to simultaneously achieve high steady-state and transient accuracy in long-term switch-current detection, which limits their application in security-aware sensing. To address this issue, this paper proposes a reset-less PCB Rogowski sensor with high- and low frequency signal superposition (HLSS) circuit. Without reset, the proposed sensor enables long-term accurate measurement of high speed switch current, with significantly enhanced transient oscillation capture capability. Experimental results show that the proposed HLSS circuit achieves a maximum transient current measurement error of only 5.1%, whereas the PCB Rogowski sensor with precision half-wave rectifier (PHR) circuit exhibits a maximum error of up to 55.77%. The results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method in capturing dynamic characteristics under high-frequency switching conditions, providing critical support for state awareness and intrusion detection in fast power electronics systems.

title: Cyber-Resilient Grid-Tied PEC9 Rectifier for Smart EV Charging : A Neural-Watermarking Framework for Stealth Attack Mitigation
authors: Meysam Gheisarnejad, Soroush Oshnoei, Mohammad Sharifzadeh, Razieh Abbasi, Eric Laurendeau, Kamal Al-Haddad
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: While the incorporation of communication networks into smart grids brings significant benefits in terms of regulation, remote monitoring, and grid resilience, the deployment of such technologies introduces risks of cyber-attacks. With the rise of more intelligent and stealthier cyberattacks (e.g., Denial of Service (DoS), and replay attacks), Multilevel Voltage Source Rectifiers (MVSRs) as critical units of smart grids are faced with serious security and stability challenges which necessitate advanced defense schemes to address such threats. This work develops an event-triggered defense mechanism based on Multiplicative Watermarking to identify and mitigate the effect of mentioned cyber-attacks in the grid-tied Nine-Level Packed E-Cell (PEC9) rectifier as a smart EV charger system. In the proposed scheme, a Watermarking producer filter is utilized to insert hidden watermarks into measurement signals before data transmission while a Watermarking remover filter eliminates the effect of watermarks after signal transmission. The training capability of neural network-based fault detection (NNFD) is utilized to estimate measurement signals during the MVSR operation. By calculating a residual signal which is obtained from the difference between the NNFD estimation and actual measurement, the presence of anomaly is recognized. Comprehensive experimental tests on a prototype of the grid-tied PEC9 rectifier are done to demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed defense scheme (designed by Multiplicative Watermarking) to tackle sophisticated cyber threats in the EV battery charger applications.

title: A 5-V Input to 0.7–1.8-V Output Always-Constant-Current Hybrid DC–DC Converter With 2.5-mV Output Voltage Ripple and 1.3-μs Settling Time
authors: Mingi Jeong, Hyunjin Kim, Taehyeong Park, Chulwoo Kim
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a 5-V input to 0.7–1.8-V output always-constant-current (ACC) hybrid DC–DC converter for high-performance computing (HPC) device applications. The introduced converter has a capacitor current path that compensates the AC component of the inductor current path to maintain the ACC condition. Thus, while reducing the DC inductor current, the proposed converter reduces the output voltage ripple and improves the transient response performance compared to the conventional hybrid DC–DC converters, due to the compensating operation of the ripple sensing amplifier (RSA) feedback network. This work was fabricated in a 180-nm CMOS process. The measurement result shows a minimum output voltage ripple of 2.5 mV and a fast load transient settling time of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.3~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s. Moreover, the converter achieves 93.4% peak power conversion efficiency with an inductor of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>H that has a DC resistance (DCR) of 80 m<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula> for a small converter volume.

title: A Hybrid LSTM-GARCH Model Integrating Volatility Factors from the US Financial Markets
authors: Xiaoliang Zhang
