title: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Physically Constrained Scheduling of Accelerated Weathering Tests
authors: Xiaozheng Wang, Zheng Wang, Meijun Sun
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Accelerated weathering tests are crucial for cultural heritage conservation. However, scheduling these tests currently relies on empirical rules, which are time-consuming and poorly adaptable to site-specific climatic conditions. Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a promising approach for climate-aware and automated schedule generation. However, purely LLM-based methods may produce physically inconsistent or infeasible schedules due to the lack of explicit constraint handling. To address these issues, we propose a neuro-symbolic agent that combines LLM-based planning with a deterministic constraint-based scheduling module. The agent generates accelerated weathering schedules that balance environmental fidelity and physical feasibility. We evaluate the generated schedules using a dual-metric framework. Specifically, the Normalized Energy Trajectory Similarity (NETS) measures environmental fidelity, while total duration reflects efficiency. We validate the proposed framework using 10-year continuous meteorological data from four distinct climatic zones. Compared to traditional empirical baselines, the proposed agent significantly reduces the experimental duration while maintaining comparable physical fidelity. In addition, the use of deterministic constraints reduces the risk of physically infeasible schedules and supports safe execution within practical limits.

title: An Improved Clustering Framework for RGB-D Image Segmentation Using the Crayfish Optimization Algorithm
authors: Nand Kishor Yadav, Amit Kumar Sharma, Mukesh Saraswat, Himanshu Mittal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Accurate image segmentation in complicated situations is obstructed by obstacles, color variability, and light changes. The integrating depth information gives strong geometric clues to improve image perception and overcomes these difficulties. To effectively identify areas of interest in difficult environments, this research inquiry introduces a new unsupervised clustering framework called Depth Aware Crayfish Optimization Clustering. The suggested technique uses color, normalized depth, and surface normal based geometric features of image and generates superpixels by improving region consistency and reducing noise. Moreover, the Crayfish Optimization Algorithm adaptively used to refine the final segmentation results. The research was carried out on the NYU RGB-D database and examined both visual and quantitative criteria. The suggested technique has been contrasted to other algorithms, including the Chaotic k-best Gravitational Search algorithm. The comparative analysis indicates that the suggested technique substantially enhances overall segmentation results.

title: Hybrid ResNet-Based Deep Learning Framework with Classical Machine Learning Algorithms for Plant Disease Detection
authors: Sachin Kr. Gupta, Avneesh Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Plant diseases have a significant effect on the agricultural output and food security, and thus they require accurate and automated solutions to diagnose them. This paper suggests a Hybrid ResNet-Based Deep Learning Framework using Classical Machine Learning Algorithms to detect plant diseases using leaf images. The framework makes use of a prepared ResNet-50 model (deep feature extractor) and Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and k-Nearest Neighbors <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(k$</tex>-NN) to perform final classification. The plantvillage dataset (Multi crop, multi disease plant leaf) was used in experiments with multi crop and multi disease plant leaf images. The preprocessing of the images and data augmentation were done to enhance generalization. It has been experimentally shown that the ResNet-50 + SVM hybrid model achieves the highest accuracy (97.84), precision (97.92), recall (97.81), and F1-score (97.86), with a training time almost 40 percent shorter than that of an end-to-end ResNet- 50 model. The presented hybrid structure provides a precise, computationally effective and scalable model to the real-world application to plant disease detection.

title: Lightweight and Explainable Deep Learning Models for Personalized Recommendation Systems
authors: Twinkle Kumari, Archana Kumari, Sarika Nirala, Sanjeev Kumar Prasad
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Personalized recommendation systems further increase user interest because they show users items according to their choices. They are used in many areas such as job portals, social media, news app and online learning etc. Traditional deep learning-based recommendation models generally provide good and highly accurate results, but they also come with several challenges. Their major limitations include managing the system at a large scale and clearly understanding how the model works (interpretability). Considering all these challenges, this study presents a simple and easy-to-understand deep learning framework that can be effectively used for personalized recommendation systems. A hybrid deep architecture, combining convolutional and sequential learning components, is used in the proposed model. It has enhanced with model pruning and knowledge extraction, which can reduces complexity while maintaining correctness. At the same time, explainability is also included in the model, which uses attention mechanisms and SHAP-based feature attribution to make recommendations transparent and easily interpretable. Tests on benchmark recommendation datasets show that the proposed approach greatly lowers the model size and inference time without affecting its overall performance.

title: Physical-layer Security via Distributed Directional Modulation
authors: Shuo Feng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: As an emerging secure transmission technology, directional modulation (DM) based on antenna arrays enables physical-layer secure communication toward desired directions in wireless systems. This paper proposes a novel artificial noise (AN)-aided randomized symbol-splitting decomposed and distributed directional modulation (RS-D3M) scheme that achieves enhanced security performance in both angle and range dimensions. Specifically, each Quadrature Phase Shift Keying (QPSK) modulated symbol is randomly split into two components and transmitted by two distributed transmitters separately to enhance physical-layer security. Simulation results demonstrate that compared with conventional D3M methods, the proposed approach exhibits narrower bit error rate (BER) beamwidth and smaller low-BER regions, while providing higher BER at positions within the mainlobe but away from the legitimate user (LU), thereby offering superior anti-eavesdropping performance.

title: Emotune: a Multimodal Emotion and Gesture-Aware Music Recommendation System Using Deep Learning
authors: Divya Bajla, Sudhanshu Tripathi, Anil Saroliya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Emotion aware recommendation systems play a very important role in improving user experience by suggesting content according to the user's mood. Due to this capacity these systems have gained significant attention in the previous years. Most of the traditional music recommendation systems and approaches like listening history or collaborative filtering techniques which in turn fail to detect the user's real time emotional state. Additionally the music recommendation systems that are available use old datasets like the FER-2013 dataset. Also, current facial emotion recognition methods based on deep learning often rely on image-based convolutional neural networks. This leads to high computational complexity and limits their use in real-time situations. In order to address these limitations this paper proposes an efficient multimodal emotionbased music recommendation system. It integrates facial expression and hand gesture analysis by using MediaPipe's holistic landmark extraction framework. Instead of providing raw images directly to the model, we provide normalized facial and hand landmark features that are classified used a very lightweight deep neural network which in turn reduces the computational complexity while ensuring high accuracy. The system which is proposed detects the user's emotions in real time and then dynamically generates custom and personalized music recommendations based on the mood detected. The system provides an overall classification accuracy of 87% which is better than several existing landmark-based and unimodal techniques. Including gesture information further improve the emotion detection reliability while highlighting the effectiveness of multimodal affective computing for personalized recommendation systems. The current model is constrained as we are using a limited dataset size and emotion categories which are predefined, the future work will target on increasing the size of the dataset, training data and incorporating temporal emotion modelling. Also leveraging advanced multimodal learning techniques to further improve accuracy and adaptability, making the proposed framework suitable for nextgeneration intelligent media recommendation applications.

title: Mitigating Class Imbalance in Medical Imaging: A Data-Centric Attention U-Net Framework for Automated Liver Segmentation
authors: Parangat Thakur, Rahul Bhandari, Raman Chadha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Standard deep learning metrics can obscure important failures in medical image segmentation especially if target organs are in a small proportion of the scan volume. Previous case studies have shown that for standard U-Net models, it is possible to achieve misleadingly high accuracy by effectively predicting the background, where we do not want to segment the liver. To overcome this “phantom segmentation” problem, the current paper offers a solid framework that reduces class imbalance with a three-pillar scheme. First, we implement the data-centric smart filtering protocol which removes non-informative background slices in the training set, thus forcing the model to learn the liverspecific features. Second, we incorporate Attention Gates in the U-Net architecture, in an effort to make the network focus on the salient liver parts by explicitly suppressing the irrelevant abdominal tissues. Finally we make use of a Hybrid Focal-Dice loss function to dynamically give weight to tricky to classify pixels, creating a heavy penalty to the model for examples on boundaries. Experimental results show that this framework avoids converging to trivial background predictions and produces stable both quantitatively and visually reliable segmentation masks for surgical planning.

title: A Spatio-temporal Graph Signal Processing Framework for Multi-source Anomaly Propagation in Infrastructure Networks
authors: Xiaojuan Fu, Hongkun Wu, Zongtao Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This paper proposes a physics-informed graph signal processing framework for analyzing multi-source anomaly propagation in networked infrastructure systems. The infrastructure network is modeled as a graph, with anomalies treated as time-varying graph signals propagating according to domain-specific physical laws. Under multi-source excitation, signal superposition occurs at graph junction nodes, which is quantified using a superposition coefficient. The proposed method is validated on two real-world graph datasets. Results SHOW that about 4% of graph edges exhibit superposition coefficients higher than 1.2, with maximum enhancement reaching 88.3%. The framework successfully captures spatio-temporal signal evolution without large-scale retraining, demonstrating its potential for real-time infrastructure monitoring. This work bridges the gap between physics-based modeling and graph signal processing for anomaly detection in networked systems.

title: Multi-Level Ensemble Anomaly Detection for Microservices: Combining Statistical Analysis, Isolation Forest, and Adaptive Thresholding
authors: Ramit Gangwar, Vaibhav Srivastava, Prashant Srivastava, Ashik Yadav, Gaurav Parashar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The use of microservices systems complicates the detection of anomalies since it is distributed and has dynamic workloads, and exhibits complicated interactions among services. Within this paper, we introduce a multi-level multi-service based ensemble anomaly detector system. It gathers finely grained Prometheus metrics and streams them through a layers pipeline which uses statistical profiling, Isolation forest and adaptive threshold rules. These layers each detect the various kinds of anomaly: temporal changes, high-dimensional outliers, and rulebased violations and then this is synthesised into a single score of anomaly by the system. An informational dashboard interface gives alerts and real-time information. The system was tested with the help of an ERP-like setting including services like API gateway, user service, file service, paste service, and notification service. The findings indicate that the multi-level ensemble is a stable and handy way to detect various abnormal behavioral patterns, such as latency, the increase of traffic surge, raising of error rate, memory issues and service degradation, among others, revealing the feasibility of the concept to be implemented in multiple situations.

title: Research on Proactive Data Leakage Prevention in Cloud Environments Based on AI-Driven Behavioral Analysis
authors: Jingchuan Feng, Han Yang, Minjia Yan, Qiyao Li, Yong Liu, Kai Tao, Jiangwei Tan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: An AI-driven behavioral analysis framework is proposed for proactive data leakage prevention in cloud environments characterized by distributed architecture and multi-tenant complexity. The framework systematically aggregates heterogeneous cloud event logs, applies Z-Score normalization and temporal feature engineering, and constructs a high-dimensional feature tensor to capture both short-term anomalies and long-term behavioral drift. Detection is achieved thru a hybrid ensemble of unsupervised isolation forest anomaly scoring and supervised gradient boosting classification, with outputs ensemble thru a weighted meta-learner to achieve robust threat identification. Online concept drift detection allows continuous model adaptation under dynamic workloads, while a real-time streaming pipeline ensures sub-second processing latency. Experimental evaluation on a simulated enterprise-grade cloud testbed, including over 25 million labeled events and adversarial tracking, showed a 15% improvement in early detection rate and a significant reduction in false positives compared to traditional static DLP baselines. The modular pipeline design allows seamless integration with existing cloud security infrastructure and supports operational scalability and transparent incident response. The results show that AI-driven behavior modeling can effectively prevent data leakage in complex cloud computing environments.

title: 3D-CRDM: A Spatially Conditioned Residual Diffusion Model for 3D Binary Sandstone Reconstruction
authors: Jie Xiang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The three-dimensional pore–grain structure of sandy quartzite critically affects porous media behavior, but high-fidelity reconstruction under sparse data is challenging. Here, sandy quartzite is modeled as a 3D binary indicator function on a voxel grid. A three-dimensional conditional residual diffusion model (3D-CRDM) with spatial coordinate conditions is proposed to address structural blurring, connectivity distortion, and limited diversity in high-dimensional discrete reconstruction. The binary structure is embedded into a continuous latent space during diffusion and mapped back via thresholding. Experiments show that 3D-CRDM preserves topological connectivity and structural features while maintaining binary consistency, achieving competitive reconstruction using only L1 loss with batch size 1.

title: A Survey of IoT-Assisted Healthcare Systems: Machine Learning Techniques, Security Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Fayeza Zobair, Md Ozaif, Suhel Mustajab, Mahfooz Alam, Ilma Khan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The integration of machine learning (ML) and the Internet of Things (IoT) has enabled intelligent real-time healthcare services, but it also introduces significant security, privacy, and implementation challenges. This review summarizes existing research on ML-based IoT healthcare systems, including a classification of architecture types, ML paradigms (supervised, unsupervised, deep, federated), common datasets, and evaluation metrics. We examine the most critical threats, such as data confidentiality attacks, integrity breaches, DoS attacks, malware, and discuss mitigation techniques like lightweight cryptography, differential privacy, secure multiparty computation, and blockchain. Analysis of accuracy, resource consumption, and deployment methods reveals key gaps, including the lack of realistic datasets, vulnerabilities to adversarial attacks, and privacy-utility tradeoffs. Finally, we propose practical research directions focused on privacy-preserving ML at the edge, federated models, explainable AI, and lightweight ledger integrations to develop secure, scalable, and patient-centric IoT healthcare.

title: Design and Optimization of a Dual-Loop Fuzzy Control System for an Inverted Pendulum Based on an Adaptive Genetic Algorithm
authors: Qingsong Yin, Yizhe Wang, Wei Zhang, Lei Xu, Zheng Guo, Jincan Ding
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: The inverted pendulum represents a classic benchmark system characterized by pronounced nonlinearity, strong coupling, and inherent instability. Conventional fuzzy control approaches encounter two primary obstacles: the curse of dimensionality, which leads to an exponential explosion in the rule base, and the heavy reliance on expert knowledge for defining membership function parameters. This paper proposes a dual-loop fuzzy control strategy augmented by an adaptive genetic algorithm to address these challenges. Initially, a parallel and series dual-loop architecture is employed to decouple the four-dimensional state space, thereby partitioning the complex control task into separate angle and position regulation loops. This decomposition effectively compresses the size of the fuzzy rule repository. Subsequently, a novel two-tier nested composite gene encoding scheme is devised. The first tier combines heterogeneous genes representing membership function parameters, rule consequents, and output scaling factors within a single fuzzy inference engine. The second tier concatenates these composite genomes across multiple fuzzy engines. This structure enables simultaneous and autonomous optimization of all controller parameters, eliminating subjective manual calibration. At the implementation level, the physical model of the inverted pendulum is exported as a C++ class using MATLAB/Simulink code generation tools, thereby establishing a high-fidelity simulation environment. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared with a conventional Linear Quadratic Regulator, the genetically optimized parallel dual-loop fuzzy controller achieves comprehensively superior performance compared with LQR, fuzzy PID, and sliding-mode–LQR composite control strategies, exhibiting markedly improved dynamic response and robustness. This research enriches the design methodology that integrates adaptive fuzzy control with evolutionary optimization, and it offers a new engineering pathway for the robust stabilization of strongly nonlinear systems.

title: Non-Uniform Diffusion Model for LDCT Denoising
authors: Yuhang Wang, Bin Liu, Xueying Cui, Hong Shangguan
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) denoising remains challenging due to complex noise distributions. Existing diffusion models perform denoising based on Gaussian noise. This paper presents a non-uniform diffusion model for LDCT denoising, which introduces a physically consistent non-uniform noise injection strategy in the forward process. In the reverse denoising step, a U-Net architecture is enhanced with a noise-level aware and a temporal feature-aware Mamba block to capture noise-level and long-range spatial dependencies efficiently. Experiments on the Mayo dataset demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods in denoising performance.

title: Cross-Attention Based Deep Learning Model for Enhanced Crop Classification Using SAR and Optical Satellite Data Fusion
authors: Vikas Kumar Tiwari, Raman Chadha, Rajeev Tiwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Precise satellite monitoring of crops is essential in agricultural monitoring and assessment of food security. In this paper, a new deep learning architecture has been introduced, which uses cross-attention mechanisms to successfully combine the Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and the optical satellite images to achieve better crop classification. In contrast to earlier or later fusion methods, our cross-attention model allows bidirectional flow of information between modalities, the different sensors focusing on the information of the other. Our results are based on the PASTIS-R dataset, which has time-series observations of Sentinel-1 (SAR) and Sentinel-2 (optical) satellites of 20 types of crops. Our model has 78.2% overall accuracy and 72.8% macro F1-score, both 3.4 and 3.4 points higher than late fusion baselines and single-modality models, respectively. Experiments on ablation show that cross-attention, self-attention, and the residual connections are important. The suggested architecture is an effective structure to multi-model satellite data fusion in agricultural applications.

title: Cross-Modal Fusion Architecture Based on Efficient Attention Mechanism in Autonomous Driving Systems
authors: Tao Fang, Na Xia, Jiahao Zhang, Yimeng Niu, Wenxin Fei, Mengyao Zhao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This paper addresses the real-time and robustness challenges in multimodal perception of vision and point clouds for autonomous driving by proposing an efficient deep perception temporal fusion framework. Existing methods often suffer from high feature noise and inefficiency due to projection conflicts, occlusion issues, and high computational complexity. To address this, we design a triple-innovation architecture: First, we construct a lightweight visual feature extraction module that combines depth-separable convolutions with channel reshuffling to extract rich spatial context at minimal computational cost. Second, we propose a temporally consistent point cloud feature extraction pipeline that generates geometrically aligned spatio-temporal point features through soft correspondence matching and online BiLSTM modeling. Finally, we innovatively propose an Extended Convolutional Attention (ECA) mechanism that integrates deep perception. By explicitly addressing occlusion issues through forward deep sorting, and employing a linear-complexity attention mechanism, we achieve bidirectional interaction and gated fusion between visual features and point cloud features. This approach significantly enhances perception robustness and accuracy in dynamically complex scenes while maintaining real-time efficiency.

title: Alternate Traffic Path Optimization Algorithm by Multi Metrics
authors: Tianyu Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This technical paper describes two factors involved in selection and optimization of alternate routes for its working route. The first is from the point of view of topology survivability to make the alternate routes generated are node-dis-jointed to each other, and are all nodes dis-jointed to their working route. The feature of node dis-joint of alternate routes helps to get better availability of communication path to improve the reliability of traffic transferring when working route gets into malfunction. Another is the reliability evaluation of alternate routes, which considers the reliability of all nodes and links along the route. Evaluating alternate route's reliability is necessary to optimize them to acquire the best alternate route for standby. Based on the two factors above, two metrics are defined in the paper, one is Maximum Node-Disjoint Path (MNDP) which is used to compute most available alternate routes which are node dis-jointed, and another is Most Reliable Replacement Node-disjoint Path (MRRNP) which is utilized to optimize alternate routes generated to find the best alternate route by reason of path reliability. Algorithms are put forward to search for node dis-jointed working route and its alternate routes, and to compute reliability of all alternate routes as well as that of their working route. Finally, the paper gives numerical results computed on the example topology of a network, The results demonstrate usage and functions of multiple metrics to decide alternate routes to be taken, and to optimize them further to select the best one of them.

title: Research on Multi-source Knowledge Fusion Technology Based on Cross-media Semantic Association Mining in Knowledge Discovery
authors: Liping He, Yabang Lin
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of semantic barriers in multi-source heterogeneous knowledge fusion, this paper proposes a multi-source knowledge fusion technology based on cross-media semantic association mining. This technology realizes the effective integration of multi-source cross-media data by building a multi-source knowledge fusion framework covering the data access layer, semantic association mining layer, knowledge fusion layer, and knowledge output layer.The four layers adopt a pipeline-driven collaborative mechanism with intermediate result caching: the data access layer outputs standardized data and feature statistics; the semantic association mining layer returns association pairs and confidence scores; the knowledge fusion layer feeds back fusion quality for adaptive learning; the knowledge output layer encapsulates results and logs.Cooperating with the cross-media data preprocessing module, using the semantic association mining engine with clear dynamic selection implementation, and combining the knowledge fusion and conflict resolution modules, the system achieves robust fusion. Experiments show that this technology performs excellently in terms of fusion accuracy and completeness. When processing 100,000-level data volume, the fusion accuracy reaches 89.7%, which is significantly improved compared with traditional methods and advanced methods that do not adopt cross-media semantic association mining. The number of knowledge entities that can be fused increases by an average of 37.6% compared with the comparison methods.Ablation experiments verify the independent contribution of each sub-module, parameter sensitivity tests confirm model stability, and interpretable evaluation shows cross-media semantic alignment consistency rate reaches 91.2%.The processing efficiency can also meet the needs of practical applications.

title: Robust Drone Identification via RF Spectrogram Analysis Using Robustspecnet
authors: Arti JainTejas Jain, Simran, Annu Chhaperwal, Vishal Kumar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The growing use of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) has raised security concerns in restricted and sensitive areas. This creates a need for reliable and hidden methods to detect and identify drones. Passive radio frequency (RF) sensing is an effective solution because it uses the communication signals that drones naturally transmit, without sending any signal back. In this paper, we propose RobustSpecNet, a deep learning based system for identifying commercial DJI drone models using RF spectrograms. RF signals are collected using software defined radios and converted into spectrograms using short time Fourier transform. A preprocessing step is applied to reduce noise and hardware related effects. Our proposed model include a Gated Unit (GUnit) that focuses on repeated signal patterns while reducing environmental interference. The Experimental results show that our RobustSpecNet achieves 99.94 % accuracy with only 20 training epochs and its performance is well in noisy conditions, making it suitable for real world drone monitoring systems.

title: Dynamic Adaptive Prompt Optimization (DAPO): A Smarter Approach to Prompt Engineering
authors: Krish Singhal, Amrita Kaul
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Language models are a fundamental pillar of NLP that drive various applications such as sentiment analysis, question-answering, and adaptive learning. However, their per formance is highly dependent on the input quality of the prompts. Classic prompt engineering, based on static prompts and hard rules is inflexible and struggles to respond in real time situations. To address this problem, we introduce the novel approach of Dynamic Adaptive Prompt Optimization (DAPO), which optimizes prompts dynamically through evolutionary search, selective gradient fine-tuning, and self-regulation mechanisms. DAPO is a recursive process that starts with an initial set of prompts, assesses their performance, searches for variations using evolutionary strategies, and optimizes the best alternatives possible. Our tests reveal that DAPO signifi cantly outperforms traditional methods. In sentiment analysis, it achieves precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2 - 9 4 \%}$</tex> compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 5 \%}$</tex> with fine-tuning. For the SQuAD data set, it achieves 93-95% precision compared to 88% with Bayesian optimization. In adaptive learning, it achieves 94 % precision outperforming evolutionary algorithms, achieving 90%. Demonstrating efficient, near-logarithmic scal ability, DAPO is best suited for real-time applications because of its dynamic adaptation to different tasks and optimization of computational resources. Thereby, it works as a great breakthrough in the art of prompt engineering.

title: RESILIENT-SAT: Sky-Ground Collaborative Framework for GEO-MEO-LEO Satellite Communication Integration Toward 6G
authors: Wenting Li, Quan Wang, Yue Wang, Yue Xiong, Yuanjie Li, Guangjian Ye
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The rapid expansion of global satellite internet demands high-throughput, low-latency, and ubiquitous coverage that no single orbital layer can provide alone. Existing satellite communication architectures face three critical limitations: (1) severe rain attenuation at Q/V/E bands (33–86GHz) causes frequent link outages, undermining the capacity gains of high-frequency operation; (2) conventional mechanical antennas and static waveform terminals cannot support the millisecond-level beam switching and heterogeneous protocol adaptation required by multi-orbit hybrid networking; and (3) satellite networks remain loosely coupled with terrestrial 6G systems, lacking unified air interface design, elastic resource deployment, and intelligent cross-domain management. To address these challenges, we propose RESILIENT-SAT, a comprehensive technical framework for GEO-MEO-LEO satellite communication integration built upon three tightly coupled innovations: (1) a three-layer sky-ground collaborative resilient transmission system that combines link-level ACM/ULPC, space-based ISL rerouting, and ground station diversity to sustain ≥99.5% availability at Q/V bands under tropical rainfall; (2) a dual-path phased array architecture paired with an RFSoC-based multi-mode waveform dynamic reconfiguration engine that achieves 48× faster switching than full-device reprogramming; and (3) a 6G-native integration architecture featuring unified variable-numerology air interface, cloud-native distributed UPF deployment, and digital-twin-driven intelligent resource management. Extensive analytical evaluation grounded in ITU-R propagation models, 3GPP NTN specifications, and DVB-S2X standards validates the effectiveness of the proposed framework across all key performance dimensions.

title: Graph Convolutional Network and Transformer-Based Algorithm for Multi-Channel Spectrum Prediction
authors: Fan Ding
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the increasing scarcity of spectrum resources, dynamic spectrum access based on spectrum prediction technology has gained widespread recognition. Spectrum prediction plays a crucial role in cognitive radio processes, aiding in spectrum management and improving spectrum utilization. This paper constructs spectrum monitoring data into a graph-structured data model based on the inherent correlations within the spectrum itself and proposes a graph network model combining Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) and Transformer for multi-channel spectrum prediction. The method utilizes GCN to extract frequency-domain correlations from the preprocessed data, followed by iterative learning in the temporal dimension through Transformer. The experiments are conducted using a real-world spectrum measurement dataset. The results demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms existing methods in terms of prediction performance.

title: Development of Intelligent Control and Optimization System for Coal Chemical and New Energy Coupled Systems
authors: Yannan Zheng, Erlun Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: This paper develops an intelligent control system to resolve the contradiction between stable coal chemical demands and fluctuating new energy output. First, a multi-scale collaborative framework of “data perception-model prediction-real-time optimization-feedback control” is constructed. Next, an innovative deep reinforcement learning optimization strategy that integrates a hybrid prediction model of LSTM-ARIMA (Long Short-Term Memory-Autoregressive Integrated Moving Average) with the fusion of process mechanisms is proposed. Finally, a distributed architecture of cloud-edge collaboration is designed to decouple decision-making and execution. In full-process simulation, the system raises renewable energy consumption to 94.9% and cuts unit energy consumption by 12.9%. Field pilot tests achieve 92.3% power tracking accuracy and 3.1% energy savings. This study provides an effective solution for the dynamic synergistic optimization of coal chemical industry and new energy through multi-technology integration and architectural innovation, which is of significant importance to industrial intelligent control, low-carbon energy systems, and China's “dual carbon” goals.

title: Collaborative Optimization of Domain Adaptation and Confidence-Regularized Self-Training for Vehicle Classification
authors: Yueqiao Sun
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Vehicle classification is a fundamental task in intelligent transportation and autonomous driving, yet existing models suffer from significant performance degradation when applied to new target domains due to cross-domain distribution shift. To address this challenge, we investigate two technical approaches: (1) domain adaptation methods (DANN and MCD) to align cross-domain feature distributions, and (2) self-training strategies including threshold filtering, global ratio selection, Class-Balanced Self-Training (CBST), and Confidence-Weighted Self-Training (CRST) to enhance pseudo-label utilization efficiency. Using ResNet-18 and MobileNetV2 as backbone networks, comparative experiments are conducted on two vehicle datasets with consistent categories but significant distribution discrepancies. This study provides a feasible solution for cross-domain vehicle classification and offers empirical evidence for backbone selection and strategy design in related tasks.

title: Research on Global Path Planning Algorithms for Mobile Robots in Non-Flat Terrain
authors: Jiayue He, Zhenyu Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: To improve terrain adaptability and safety in mobile robot path planning on uneven terrain, an artificial potential field-jump point search (APF-JPS) algorithm based on a 2.5 dimensional (2.5D) grid map is proposed. A dual-layer grid map preserves elevation information while reducing storage compared with full 3D maps. An elevation-cost potential field is introduced to guide jump-point expansion and incorporate slope constraints. Simulation results show that the method significantly reduces elevation variation and improves terrain adaptability for efficient path planning.

title: Router Buffer and Membrane Potential Memory Sharing Mechanism for Spiking Neural Network Hardware Systems
authors: Rongping Jiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: In traditional spiking neural network (SNN) hardware systems, the use of independent membrane potential memory in neural computing units imposes significant hardware overhead on the system. To address this issue, this paper attempts to store membrane potential information in the internal buffers of routers, thereby realizing the multiplexing of router buffers and membrane potential memory. Compared with traditional SNN hardware systems, this paper avoids the use of independent membrane potential memory inside neural computing units through the optimization of the router architecture, thus reducing the hardware overhead of the entire system.

title: Cardinal-Ordinal Consistency Adjustment Driven Group Consensus Decision Making With Distributed Preference Relations and Application in Remanufacturing Quality Management
authors: Wan-Lin Liu, Mi Zhou, Ba-Yi Cheng, Jian Wu, Xin-Bao Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: The consistency of expert’s assessment and consensus reaching process (CRP) is a hot topic in group decision making (GDM). When an expert provides the comparison between different alternative pairs, how to measure the consistency degree and adjust inconsistent judgments is important. In this article, the construction of complete cardinal consistent distributed preference relations (DPRs) from multiple routes is first proposed. The advantage lies in that all possible results could be captured instead of just depending on adjacent alternative pairs. Then, the ordinal consistency measurement of DPRs is provided based on the ranking order dissimilarity derived from possibility degree. Second, the concepts of soft and strict consistent DPRs are given, which deduce four consistency types, i.e., perfect consistent, partial consistent, partial inconsistent, and completely inconsistent. We also discuss the modeling of consistent DPR matrix when adding new alternatives. Third, several optimization models are established to adjust inconsistent DPRs under different consistency types. These models all minimize the modification extent of individual’s original judgment. Ordinal consensus index is then defined, followed by the optimization model to make experts reach ordinal consensus while maintaining cardinal and ordinal consistency. A case study about the selection of a dismantling scheme in remanufacturing quality management process is finally provided to illustrate the effectiveness and validity of the proposed method.

title: Error Detection Schemes for Barrett Modular Multiplication of CT-BU on FPGA in PQC and FHE
authors: Paresh Baidya, Rourab Paul, Vikas Srivastava, Sumit Kumar Debnath
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computing
abstract: Unintentional or intentionally faults into hardware accelerators of Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) or Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) algorithms may lead to the leakage of sensitive information. If appropriate fault protection mechanisms are not employed, recent PQC schemes such as Kyber, Dilithium, Falcon, and NTRU, as well as FHE schemes such as Brakerski-Fan-Vercauteren (BFV) and Brakerski Gentry Vaikuntanathan (BGV), may leak sensitive information at the hardware implementation level. This work proposes three effi cient recomputation-based fault detection methods for Barrett Modular Multiplication (BMM) in the Cooley–Tukey Butterfly Unit (CT-BU) of Number Theoretic Transform (NTT), imple mented on a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) for PQC and FHE algorithms. The CT-BU and BMM are fundamental components in lattice-based PQC and FHE schemes. This pa per introduces a new algorithm, Recomputation with Swapped Operand (RESWO), for fault detection. While Recomputation with Negated Operand (RENO) and Recomputation with Shifted Operand (RESO) are existing methods used in other PQC hardware algorithms. To the best of our knowledge, RENO and RESO have never been used in wordwise BMM before. The proposed RESWO method consumes a comparable number of slices to RENO and RESO. However, for most PQC and FHE variants, RESWO achieves lower energy consumption and reduced slice utilization compared to both RENO and RESO. The fault detection efficiency of RESWO, RENO, and RESO is nearly ∼100%.

title: Digital Twin-assisted Topology Control for Unmanned Robotic Networks under Uncertainty
authors: Bowen Wang, Yanjing Sun, Yu Huo, Berk Canberk, Trung Q. Duong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Digital twin (DT) technology can assist the predictive topology control through real-time predictions and physical-virtual interactions for unmanned robotic collaboration in hazardous and unknown environments. Nevertheless, the uncertainty of topology structures caused by prediction deviations under small-sample conditions inherently complicates the topology control process and potentially result in collaboration failures. To address this challenge, we first introduce the framework of uncertain graph to model the uncertain topology and deduce the theoretical formula of global k-connectivity index that provides resilience guarantees beyond single connectivity. Considering the availability of local and uncertain topology information in real scenarios, we explore the correlation between local and global connectivity, and then formulate the local topology optimization problem under uncertainty as an uncertain graph game, in which each node tends to create or break edges with neighbor nodes based on multilateral consensus rules. By utilizing the collaborative relationships in virtual layer, we propose a DT-assisted topology control (DTATC) algorithm to construct the local multilateral stable topology in a distributed manner. Furthermore, we prove that our proposed algorithm can converge to the global stability as long as the joint physical-virtual interaction graph is connected for each node, which reveals the positive interplay between connectivity and stability. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed algorithm in adapting to uncertain and dynamic environments.

title: Track-centric Iterative Learning for Global Trajectory Optimization in Autonomous Racing
authors: Youngim Nam, Jungbin Kim, Kyungtae Kang, Cheolhyeon Kwon
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper presents a global trajectory optimization framework for minimizing lap time in autonomous racing under uncertain vehicle dynamics. Optimizing the trajectory over the full racing horizon is computationally expensive, and following such a trajectory in the real world hardly assures global optimality due to uncertain dynamics. Yet, existing work mostly focuses on dynamics learning at the control level, without updating the trajectory itself to account for the learned dynamics. To address these challenges, we propose a track-centric approach that directly learns and optimizes the full-horizon trajectory. We first represent trajectories through a track-agnostic parametric space in light of the wavelet transform. This space is then efficiently explored using Bayesian optimization, where the lap time of each candidate is evaluated by running simulations with the learned dynamics. This optimization is embedded in an iterative learning framework, where the optimized trajectory is deployed to collect real-world data for updating the dynamics, progressively refining the trajectory over the iterations. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated through simulations and real-world experiments, demonstrating lap time improvement of up to 8.9% over a nominal baseline and consistently outperforming state-of-the-art methods.

title: Bio-inspired Optofluidic Molecular Communication with Photothermally Actuated Microrobot Swarms_supp8-3698265.pdf
authors: Tingting Yu
publish: N/A
abstract: Molecular communication (MC) offers a bio-inspired paradigm for information transfer in environments inaccessible to conventional electromagnetic waves. However, translating MC concepts to the microscale has been hampered by a lack of integrated, biocompatible testbeds. Inspired by biological spectral-dependent photothermal transduction of specific light wavelengths into thermal energy, we present the first fully integrated microscopic MC platform utilizing photothermally responsive microrobot swarms. Our platform employs core-shell microrobots that exhibit a strong photothermal response, enabling precise and non-invasive navigation within microfluidic channels via near-infrared (NIR) light. This optofluidic architecture facilitates a symbiotic dual-bit encoding scheme, which concurrently modulates information onto both microrobot arrival and the optical control states. We demonstrate a complete communication workflow, from microrobot emission and laser-guided modulation to real-time optical detection and signal demodulation. The system achieves a data rate of 0.63 bits · min−1 with a low bit error rate of 4%, validated by a multi-sampling detection algorithm and the transmission of the ASCII string “HELLO WORLD”. This work provides a robust testbed for validating MC theories in biologically relevant microenvironments and serves as a step toward applications in the Internet of Bio-Nano Things.

title: A Memory-Optimized Constant Geometry NTT-Based Polynomial Multiplier With a Conflict-Free Bit-Reverse Reordering Method
authors: Jinyang Hu, Yongkui Yang, Enyi Yao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: The number theoretic transform (NTT) and its inverse (INTT) are frequently employed to accelerate polynomial multiplication, which represents both the core computational paradigm and the performance bottleneck in lattice-based cryptography (LBC). As an important variant in the NTT algorithm family, the constant geometry (CG) NTT has received significant attention from many researchers due to its simple and consistent memory access pattern. However, it suffers from two inherent and unsatisfactory limitations: its storage capacity requirement for <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {N}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> polynomial coefficients will exceed <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {N}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> to ensure conflict-free read and write; and it inevitably requires data relocation in memory between NTT and INTT. To address these challenges, this work proposes a modified ping-pong memory structure with reduced size and a novel conflict-free bit-reverse reordering method. For the modified ping-pong memory structure, each PE is allocated only two memory banks, and the overall storage requirement is reduced to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.5\boldsymbol {N}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. The proposed bit-reverse reordering algorithm, through simple logic and low-resource overhead, effectively avoids read–write conflicts and achieves seamless data relocation across the memory without stalling or additional space. Furthermore, we also introduce a lightweight twiddle factor memory access scheme, which, combined with the above techniques, drives down the resource consumption of both control logic and memory to a compact level. Finally, the FPGA implementation results of the proposed architecture demonstrate significant performance advantages over existing works in terms of area efficiency.

title: Twining Stems Inspired Soft Actuators with High Aspect Ratio: Design, Characterization, and Application_supp1-3697813.avi
authors: Jingjing Feng
publish: N/A
abstract: Inspired by the helical growth strategy of natural twining stems, this study introduces a pneumatically driven Soft Twining-stem-inspired Actuator (STA) with high aspect ratio (> 20:1) and thin-walled structures. Fabricated via an integrated dip-molding and fiber-reinforced process, the STA achieves 3D spatial curling deformation with only a single pneumatic chamber, eliminating the need for multi-chamber coordination. The establishment of mechanical model identified the key parameters governing its deformation, which was then validated through performance testing and analysis. This revealed that fiber thread density regulation enable up to 67.1 mm radial contraction (33.6% length) and 1021° rotation at 80 kPa. Practical applications demonstrate STA’s versatility across multiple domains. The actuators function effectively as artificial muscles to drive a robotic elbow, and demonstrate a snap-through mechanism which facilitates rapid bidirectional motion switching. Beyond actuation applications, soft universal grippers composed of 6 STAs achieve non-destructive grasping of both common objects and irregularly shaped targets, highlighting the system’s multi-functional versatility. Furthermore, a pipe-crawling robot was constructed utilizing STAs, showcasing the ability to generate effective anisotropic friction for locomotion within confined pipelines. Overall, this study aims to establish STAs as a versatile actuator design, offering insights for simplified and high - performance soft robotics.

title: High-Speed and High-Brightness 850 and 1060 nm VCSEL Arrays for Free-space Optical Communication
authors: Jian-Wei Tung, Po-Jui Lai, Zhe-Wei Hsu, Jia-Liang Yen, Jin-Wei Shi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: Vertical-cavity surface-emitting laser (VCSEL) arrays featuring high-speed and exceptional brightness are critical for minimizing diffraction loss on the receiver side of optical wireless communication (OWC) channels, especially in 6G and satellite communications. Compared with their edge emitting counterpart, such as DFB lasers, VCSELs typically offer stronger radiation hardness. However, the traditional high-power, high-brightness, and (quasi-) single-mode (SM) VCSELs frequently experience pronounced relative intensity noise (RIN) and spatial hole burning effects, both of which limit their effectiveness for high-speed data transmission. This paper demonstrates Zn-diffusion VCSEL arrays with a novel ring shaped aperture and a compact dual-pad layout at both 850 and 1060 nm wavelengths, fundamentally resolving the trade-offs among the SM output power, brightness, RIN, and modulation speed. Compared to the traditional 7 7 VCSEL arrays employing circular Zn-diffusion apertures at the 850 nm wavelength, the new ring-shaped aperture exhibits higher (quasi-) SM output power (160 vs. 145 mW), higher brightness (73 and 66 KWcm-2sr-1), and a wider 3-dB E-O bandwidth (22 vs. 17 GHz). Clear and error-free 32 Gbit/sec eye-opening over free-space transmission can be measured. The remarkable static/dynamic performance of our array can be attributed to the suppression of spatial hole burning effect in the ring-shaped aperture. Moreover, the application of a similar device structure and dual-pad layout to 1060 nm VCSELs results in comparable high-power, high-brightness, and high-speed error-free 26 Gbit/sec free space transmission performance.

authors: N/A
