title: Vibration of Soft, Twisted Beams for Underactuated Quadrupedal Locomotion
authors: Yuhao Jiang, Fuchen Chen, Jamie Paik, Daniel M. Aukes
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: Underactuated compliant robotic systems offer a promising approach to mitigating actuation and control challenges by harnessing predesigned, embodied dynamic behaviors. This article presents Flix-Walker, a novel, untethered, centimeter-scale quadrupedal robot inspired by compliant underactuated mechanisms. Flix-Walker employs flexible, helix-shaped beams as legs, which are actuated by vibrations from just two motors to achieve three distinct mobility modes. We analyze the actuation parameters required to generate various locomotion modes through both simulation and prototype experiments. The effects of system and environmental variations on locomotion performance are examined, and we propose a generic metric for selecting control parameters that produce robust and functional motions. Experiments validate the effectiveness and robustness of these actuation parameters within a closed-loop control framework, demonstrating reliable trajectory-tracking and self-navigation capabilities.

title: Latent Variable Inversion and Causal Quantification Framework Based on EA-EPF and Dual-Channel Bayesian Hierarchical Model
authors: Yaochen Dong, Mingzhi Huang, Hongwei Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This study proposes an Entity-Level Aggregated Evolutionary Particle Filter (EA-EPF) framework for latent variable inversion from discrete observations. By integrating genetic crossover and mutation operators into the resampling process, the proposed method effectively alleviates particle degeneracy and enhances inference robustness. To further support quantitative analysis, a Monte Carlo counterfactual simulation framework is developed to evaluate the impacts of different evaluation systems. In addition, a hybrid modeling approach combining XGBoost and a dual-channel Bayesian hierarchical structural equation model is employed to identify key influencing factors and quantify causal effects. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves stable effective sample size (ESS), maintains strong consistency with observed data, and provides reliable inference under different scenarios. These findings highlight the effectiveness and general applicability of the proposed framework for latent variable analysis.

title: Fuzzy-Based Optimal Control for Stochastic Game Systems With Time-Varying State Constraints via Event-Triggering
authors: Chenyi Si, Junnan Zhang, Chaoxu Mu, Zhong Ji
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: In this article, the event-triggered optimal problem is investigated for stochastic game systems subjected to time-varying state constraints and input constraint. First, by introducing a time-varying transition function, the stochastic game systems subject to time-varying state constraints are transformed into an unconstrained form. Then, by employing the fuzzy logic systems to approximate the optimal value functions and the optimal control inputs, an actor–critic (AC) framework is designed that achieves the Nash equilibrium. Moreover, by introducing additional dynamic variables, a dynamic event-triggered mechanism (DETM) is developed. In contrast to traditional static event-triggered mechanism, the DETM mechanism embeds the trigger information directly into the design process. Theoretical analysis mathematically proves the stability of the closed-loop system, and the supersonic transport aircraft system is utilized to illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Two-Stage Fine Estimation for MIMO-OFDM Integrated Sensing and Communication Based on Private Subcarriers
authors: Hangyu Lin, Biao Tian
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: With the evolution of the sixth-generation (6G) mobile communications, Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) has emerged as a key technology to address spectrum congestion. However, achieving high-precision detection of dense targets in resource-constrained MIMO-OFDM ISAC systems remains a significant challenge. To address this issue, this paper proposes a two-stage fine target parameter estimation scheme based on private subcarriers and sparse reconstruction. First, a private and shared subcarrier mechanism is introduced in the waveform design to ensure the orthogonality of radar signals and the parallel transmission of communication data. On this basis, a "coarse-to-fine" cascaded estimation strategy is designed. Specifically, a spatial Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) is first employed to rapidly obtain coarse angle-range-velocity information of the targets. Then the channel response of the private subcarriers is extracted to construct a local dictionary, and the LASSO algorithm is applied to perform sparse signal recovery within the coarsely estimated angular region. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm effectively breaks through the limitation of the physical aperture of the receiving array on the angular resolution. The proposed algorithm effectively distinguishes dense targets with small angle intervals under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions, and significantly improves the accuracy and robustness of angle estimation while ensuring low computational complexity.

title: Dynamic Fusion and Lightweight Multi-Task Network for Traffic Flow Perception in Complex Environments
authors: Juqing Yang, Jiaoyue Liu, Lei Tang, Shiyu He
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: Accurate and real-time traffic flow parameter detection (volume, speed, queue length, vehicle type) is crucial for intelligent transportation systems (ITS), yet it remains challenging in complex environments (e.g., rain, fog, backlight) and on edge devices. This paper proposes a two-stage solution combining dynamic multi-sensor fusion and a lightweight multi-task network. First, a dynamic weighting fusion method adaptively integrates Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) and camera data based on environmental context and sensor confidence, generating robust multimodal features. Second, a lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN) with multi-scale feature enhancement and taskdecoupled branches extracts four traffic parameters simultaneously. Knowledge distillation and physical correlation constraints further boost accuracy without increasing inference latency. Experiments on a real-world dataset show that our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance compared to four representative baselines (PureVision, EarlyFusion, LateFusion, and MMFusion): traffic volume Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 2.7 veh/5 min, speed Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 2.5 km/h, queue length error of 2.8 m, and vehicle classification accuracy of 94.3%, while running at 78 ms per frame on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano, meeting edge real-time requirements. This work provides a practical and deployable solution for edge-based intelligent traffic perception, which is of significant importance to the advancement of real-time ITS applications such as adaptive signal control and traffic monitoring.

title: Research on the Construction of State Owned Enterprise Talent Supply Chain Management System and Intelligent Scheduling Mechanism Integrating Big Data Analysis
authors: Lin Xie, Baoxing Liang, Minglei Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: This article combines the demand for data-driven and intelligent construction in talent management work of Chinese enterprises, and constructs an intelligent scheduling method for a unified big data talent supply system based on data analysis. Utilizing various data to support talent prediction models, intelligent job matching models, and employee mobility monitoring models, achieving precise allocation and dynamic response of human resource management. Adopting real-time data calculation, internal and external collaborative scheduling, and other technologies to achieve maximum resource utilization and efficient system operation. This model has high scheduling efficiency, high prediction rate, and high data fusion, providing effective support for promoting the modernization of talent training and management in state-owned enterprises.

title: Silicon integrated balanced-photonic general matrix-computing system
authors: Lijun Yang, Xiaoyan Hu, Zhiqiang Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Over the past decade, the leap in deep learning has relied on an increasingly unsustainable demand for computing power, with its growth far exceeding the pace of energy efficiency improvements in electronic hardware. Photonic computing is expected to address many challenges in the field of computing; however, integrated photonic processors are often limited by the physical size of processing units and the energy consumption caused by high-speed analog-to-digital conversion. We propose a hybrid optoelectronic computing architecture that utilizes a modular dot product unit (DPU) composed of photonic crossbars and balanced detection to achieve on-chip general matrix-matrix multiplication. We modeled a 4×4 matrix computing system using INTERCONNECT, and simulated and analyzed the 4×4 matrix-matrix multiplication process. The maximum error between the simulated values and the theoretical calculation values is 0.5%, thus verifying the effectiveness and accuracy of the computing system. This computing system bypasses the bottlenecks of high-speed electronic readout and frequent reprogramming of photonic weights, which can significantly reduce energy consumption and latency when dealing with large-scale matrices. Therefore, our research lays the foundation for realizing efficient and scalable on-chip optical computing for various AI applications.

title: YOLOv11-ByteTrack-ReID: Trajectory-Level Occlusion-Aware Multi-Object Tracking Algorithm
authors: Junqing Bai, Chenchen Lv
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: We propose an integrated YOLOv11-ByteTrackReID framework to mitigate identity switches under frequent occlusion in multi-object tracking (MOT). By embedding discriminative ReID features into a lightweight YOLOv11-ByteTrack pipeline, our system optimizes the balance between localization precision and computational efficiency. Core innovations include trajectory-level temporal feature aggregation to stabilize appearance representations against single-frame degradation, and an occlusion-aware dynamic matching strategy that adaptively weights motion and appearance cues using real-time visibility scores. On the MOT17 benchmark, our method achieves 43.9% HOTA, 50.5% IDF1, and 57 identity switches at ~25 FPS.

title: A Lightweight Digital Twin System for Real-Time Monitoring of Cementing Operations
authors: Mingxin He, Jiaxin Han, Caili Song
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the invisibility of downhole conditions during cementing operations and the inability of traditional numerical simulations to meet real-time monitoring requirements, this paper proposes a lightweight digital twin approach for cementing under a Web environment. Centered on the principle of "real-time priority," a data-driven mapping mechanism across physical–data–visual spaces is established. A fluid displacement model based on volume conservation is designed, transforming complex fluid dynamics into a one-dimensional algebraic volume propagation problem, thereby significantly reducing computational overhead. The system adopts a browser/server (B/S) architecture with an HTML5 Canvas-based 2D rendering engine to achieve low-latency dynamic reconstruction of downhole fluid evolution. Experimental results demonstrate that the displacement error is less than 3.5%, the computation time per step is under 5 ms, and the end-to-end latency is within 2 s, satisfying real-time monitoring requirements. This work provides a lightweight implementation pathway for industrial digital twin systems under resource-constrained conditions.

title: LS-YOLO: a lightweight detector for ship detection in SAR images
authors: Li Lou, Zhipeng Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) plays an irreplaceable role in marine surveillance. However, ships in SAR images typically manifest as tiny, densely distributed speckles, and edge computing platforms impose stringent constraints on the parameter size of detection models. Existing general-purpose one-stage detectors employ symmetric feature pyramid structures, which not only lose crucial spatial details of small targets in deep neural layers but also cause severe parameter redundancy due to the independent detection heads assigned to multi-scale features. To address this architectural mismatch, we propose LS-YOLO, a lightweight detector tailor-made for small SAR targets. First, we design an asymmetric feature pyramid neck, which decisively discards the deep features that are unhelpful for small targets and introduces high-resolution shallow features, structurally tilting computational resources towards small targets. Second, we propose a lightweight shared convolutional detection head. By sharing network weights across different scales, it drastically compresses the model size. Furthermore, the introduction of group normalization and a learnable scale layer effectively resolves the scale-feature conflicts caused by weight sharing. Extensive experiments on the challenging high-resolution SAR images dataset demonstrate that, compared to the baseline, LS-YOLO achieves a 3.2% increase in mean average precision while slashing the parameter count by 32%, requiring only 1.80 million parameters. This achieves an optimal accuracy-efficiency balance, providing a new paradigm for the efficient edge deployment of SAR ship detectors.

title: Performance Comparison of Hard-Switching and Interactive Multiple Model Algorithms in Maneuvering Target Tracking
authors: Jaming Cheng, Zhengmin Zhang, Meili Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Maneuvering target tracking is a key technology in fields such as military early warning and autonomous driving. Its core challenge lies in the uncertainty of target motion patterns. Multiple Model (MM) methods are the dominant strategy to address this challenge, with the threshold-discrimination-based hard-switching strategy and the probability-fusion-based Interactive Multiple Model (IMM) strategy representing two fundamentally different design paradigms. Existing research lacks a systematic comparison and revelation of their respective advantages in smoothness and underlying mechanisms under complex maneuvers. To this end, this paper designs a comprehensive trajectory encompassing Constant Velocity (CV), asymmetric turn, Constant Acceleration (CA), and Constant Deceleration (CD) motions. A systematic simulation comparison and theoretical analysis between the hard-switching MM algorithm and the IMM algorithm are conducted from three dimensions: estimation error, model evolution, and trajectory tracking. Experimental results show that during model-matching phases, the IMM algorithm, leveraging its probabilistic weighted fusion mechanism, reduces estimation error by approximately 26% on average compared to the hard-switching algorithm. In the final deceleration phase where the motion model is mismatched, the IMM algorithm maintains stable tracking by dynamically fusing the CA and CV models, achieving an 86.8% reduction in error compared to the hard-switching algorithm. In terms of overall performance, the IMM algorithm achieves an average Root Mean Square Error (ARMSE) of 6.44 meters, representing a 77.5% performance improvement over the 28.59 meters of the hard-switching algorithm. Mechanistic analysis reveals that the IMM algorithm fundamentally overcomes the inherent limitations of the hard-switching algorithm—such as estimation jumps, sensitivity to noise, and vulnerability to model mismatch due to its threshold-based decision-making—through the smooth evolution of model probabilities and the soft fusion of outputs enabled by Bayesian inference. This study systematically demonstrates the core advantages of the probabilistic soft fusion mechanism in achieving smooth, stable, and adaptive tracking, providing theoretical and experimental foundations for high-maneuverability target tracking.

title: Sequential Recommendation with Diffusion Model and Frequency-enhanced Guidance
authors: Wen Zeng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To solve the problem that the generation process lacks structural prior constraints in existing diffusion-based sequential recommendation models, this paper proposes CDFRec(Conditional Diffusion with Frequency-enhanced Sequential Recommendation) a diffusion sequential recommendation model with frequency-domain conditions. First, the low-frequency components obtained by frequency-domain decomposition are introduced as condition information into the denoising process of the diffusion model. In this way, global structural constraints are directly applied in the generation stage.At the same time, CDFRec only performs diffusion modeling on target item embeddings and uses the weighted MSE loss function. It greatly improves training efficiency and convergence stability while keeping good modeling ability. Experimental results on several public datasets show that, compared with the best baseline methods, the proposed CDFRec improves Recall@20 and NDCG@20 by an average of 4.08 and 2.95 percentage points, respectively.

title: Bayesian Priors Meet Attention: A Tri-Branch Network for Lung Nodule Segmentation
authors: Qianglong Kuang, Lijia Zhi, Shaomin Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of lung nodules with ambiguous boundaries is crucial for early lung cancer diagnosis and surgical planning. However, existing deep learning models often struggle to precisely delineate true nodule boundaries due to complex morphological variations and low contrast in Computed Tomography (CT) scans. To address this challenge, we propose a novel Tri-Branch Bayesian Prior Network (TB-BPN). The proposed architecture features three parallel streams: a primary intensity branch extracting raw regional texture, a Sobel branch computing spatial gradients to capture high-frequency geometric discontinuities, and a Bayesian Shape Generator (BSG) acting as a probabilistic regularizer to provide low-frequency shape priors. To overcome the semantic misalignment and feature redundancy caused by the direct concatenation of heterogeneous features, we introduce a Multi-Scale Attention Fusion (MSAF) module. This module adaptively allocates attention weights to precisely amplify pathological edge signals. Extensive experiments on the LUNA16 and LIDC-IDRI datasets demonstrate that the TB-BPN achieves state-of-the-art performance. On the LUNA16 benchmark, the model yields a Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) of 89.47% and a 95% Hausdorff Distance (HD95) of 3.85 mm, significantly outperforming mainstream frameworks such as nnU-Net and TransUNet. By integrating explicit Bayesian geometric priors with multi-scale attention, the TB-BPN provides a robust and highly accurate solution for complex pulmonary nodule segmentation.

title: Resource Scheduling Method Based on Neural Network Dynamic Network Coding
authors: Guanghui Shao, Yanbo Yang, Jiawei Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the limitation of MDS-coded offloading schemes in mobile edge computing networks, which rely on fixed topology and ideal link assumptions, this paper proposes an intelligent offloading scheme based on dynamic stochastic linear network coding. A three-stage neural optimization architecture is designed: first, a graph convolutional network (GCN) is used to extract time-slice topological features and generate network representations; second, an LSTM-based generative network is constructed to fuse spatio-temporal features and predict network state evolution; furthermore, a Wasserstein GAN framework is introduced to optimize prediction errors through adversarial training between the generator and discriminator. Based on this, a hybrid LSTM-WGAN-GCN model is developed to establish a dynamic probabilistic mapping between coding parameters and network states, combined with a real-time feedback mechanism to enable adaptive redundancy adjustment. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly improves resource scheduling efficiency compared with traditional methods in dynamic environments.

title: Counterfactual Multimodal Deep Learning for Personalized Breast Cancer Risk Intervention
authors: Simran Chugwani, Ajay Shriram Kushwaha
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The multimodal method of Deep Learning despite having promising results in predicting the risk of breast cancer is primarily just predictive and does not necessarily enable decisionmaking and intervention. Consequently, this renders them less useful for clinically-based decisions. In preventive medicine, understanding how to deal with modifiable factors that can change an individual's risk profile is essential. In this respect, we propose an innovative model called Counterfactual Multimodal Deep Learning (CMDL), which uses mammograms, clinical factors, and biomarkers collectively to assess breast cancer risk. The CMDL model not only provides a baseline assessment of risk but also includes a counterfactual reasoning model that assesses different risk profiles based on manipulated (i.e., controlled) factors. By using the CMDL model, researchers can calculate how much one's risk decreases or increases based on different treatment and lifestyle regimens. Through the validation of counterfactual reasoning techniques, the proposed CMDL methodology can maintain a high level of accuracy in classifications while providing analytically-stable, individualized risk estimates that enable researchers to develop personalized risk estimate-based breast cancer decision support systems that extend beyond standard classification methodologies.

title: Privacy at Risk: A Deep Learning Framework for Identifying Data Leaks on Social Networks
authors: Ganesh Narayan Prajapat, Sunita Sharma, Anjula Gupta, Suraj Singh, Aman, Pooja Sharma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The deep learning-based framework has been proposed for data leak detection in online social networks for different data modalities such as text, image, audio, video, and behavioral data. BERT has been utilized for text data, autoencoders and CNNs for image and video data, and LSTMs for behavioral data. The BERT model achieves the highest accuracy of 98.0%, while the ensemble model, which utilizes all the modalities, performs much better in terms of accuracy, between the range of 96.3%, precision between the range of 95.7%, and a recall, between the range of 96.8% to fight false negatives against false positives. In each parameter, the ensemble performed higher than single models, confirming the reality that the ensemble's existence could provide a much stronger and stable solution in detecting privacy threats in different kinds of data in social networks. Employing data from more than a single source, it is ensured that the framework will identify these leaks in a comprehensive and delicate manner. The team proves that deep learning models can excellently protect privacy and filter out leaks, and offer a scalable technique for social networking sites that are more susceptible to privacy attacks. The results also highlight that multi-modal approaches are required for improving accuracy and reducing false positives, thus making this framework an exciting tool in practical applications for the ongoing efforts in ensuring online privacy.

title: Research on Process Operation Recognition in Industrial Production Lines Based on Multimodal Visual Information Fusion
authors: Zijian Zhu, Jiajie Liu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This paper addresses the issues of heavy reliance on high-cost sensors and poor environmental robustness in the recognition of process movements on industrial production lines by proposing a vision-based multimodal recognition method. By integrating human skeleton poses extracted via MediaPipe, workpiece information detected by YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once version 8), and tracking data from ByteTrack, we have constructed a multidimensional quantitative metric system that incorporates movement amplitude, joint angles, duration, and human interaction. Based on this, a dual-attention LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) recognition model (SE-TA-LSTM) was designed by integrating channel attention (Squeeze-and-Excitation, SE) and temporal attention (Temporal Attention, TA) to enhance feature extraction capabilities for key joints and key frames. Experiments on a self-built simulated industrial process motion dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a recognition accuracy of 100%, representing improvements of 12.46% and 5.64% over the baseline LSTM and single-attention LSTM models, respectively. Furthermore, it converges more rapidly, reaching peak accuracy within just 90 training cycles. The contribution of this study lies in providing a low-cost, high-precision, and easily deployable process motion recognition solution that requires only a standard camera. It holds practical application value for monitoring production efficiency, evaluating operational compliance, and issuing safety alerts in industrial settings.

title: A CRT-based Certificateless Signcryption Scheme with Dynamic Key Agreement for V2G Network
authors: Wentao Tang, Liangliang Wang, Weiwei Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology enables electric vehicles to feed power back to the grid. The core of V2G is to use the energy stored in numerous electric vehicles as a buffer between the grid and intermittent renewable energy sources. However, the communication process between electric vehicles and charging stations involves a significant amount of sensitive information. If compromised, the user’s private data could lead to serious consequences for vehicle owners. To address this issue, we propose a signcryption scheme with dynamic key agreement based on the Chinese Remainder Theorem (CRT), allowing electric vehicles to dynamically update their pseudonyms, in which the public key within the communication unit can be updated without costly computations when vehicles entering or exiting a charging station. We implement our scheme on a Raspberry Pi to evaluate both computation and communication overheads, comparing it with recent related schemes. Experimental results show that the proposed scheme achieves low computational overhead on resource-constrained devices, with the total time for signcryption generation and a single verification phase being less than 8 ms. Although some lightweight baseline schemes achieve lower raw transmission latency, they sacrifice important security properties, such as resistance to replay attacks or public key replacement attacks. Our scheme accepts a moderate communication cost in exchange for stronger security guarantees, including EUF-CMA security, IND-CCA security, message authentication, integrity, anonymity, and unlinkability. Therefore, it provides a more balanced and practical solution for secure V2G communications.

title: Brake Component Recognition of Railway Freight Trains Based on Deep Learning
authors: Zilong Peng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To improve brake component detection accuracy and reduce missed detection on railway freight trains, an enhanced YOLOv11n-FPBD algorithm is proposed. It introduces a P2 small-object detection head, incorporates the parameter efficient C3K2_FDConv module for robust feature extraction in complex scenes, replaces PANet with BiFPN for adaptive cross-scale fusion, and adopts a dynamic upsampling mechanism to enhance detail recovery. Experiments show that, compared to YOLOv11n, the proposed method improves accuracy, recall, mAP@50, and mAP@50:95 by 2.7%, 3.1%, 1.5%, and 3.9%, respectively, reduces model parameters by 14.7%.

title: A Deep Classification Method for Alzheimer’s Disease Staging Based on Joint Modeling of Neuroimaging and Clinical Information
authors: Zhou Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease staging is crucial for early diagnosis, disease progression assessment, and decision support in cognitive impairment. However, due to the complex coupling between brain pathological changes and clinical manifestations, traditional methods relying on a single information source often fail to adequately depict the true state of patients at different stages. To address this issue, a deep classification method for Alzheimer's disease staging based on joint modeling of neuroimaging and clinical information was developed. This method uses neuroimaging data and clinical descriptive information as dual-source inputs, extracting imaging and clinical representations separately through modality-specific encoders. A gating fusion mechanism is then introduced to adaptively integrate heterogeneous features, enhancing the synergistic expression ability between multimodal information. Finally, a deep classification network is used to determine the disease stage. This framework can consider both brain structural abnormalities and patient state information during unified modeling, thereby improving the feature expression quality and class discrimination ability in Alzheimer's disease staging tasks. The proposed method was systematically validated on a staging identification task under open-source data conditions. The results show that the method performs well in terms of comprehensive classification performance and can effectively enhance the identification ability of normal cognition, mild cognitive impairment, and different stages of Alzheimer's disease. Research shows that joint modeling strategies based on neuroimaging and clinical information can provide more stable and discriminative deep representations for Alzheimer's disease staging, and also provide a feasible methodological basis for intelligent auxiliary analysis of cognitive impairment-related diseases.

title: Sparse Bayesian Learning with Robust Covariance Fitting for Conformal Array DOA Estimation
authors: Qian Gao, Jialin Huang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Small underwater platforms like unmanned underwater vehicles face severe aperture constraints, and element position errors in conformal arrays further complicate the resolution of closely spaced sources. This paper proposes a robust sparse Bayesian learning framework for conformal array direction-of-arrival estimation. The method combines three elements: adaptive diagonal loading to stabilize covariance inversion; two-stage grid refinement with noise variance re-estimation; and a second-order Taylor expansion of the steering vector for accurate off-grid modeling. Simulations on a nine-element U-shaped conformal array show that for two sources separated by 4.6°, the proposed algorithm achieves a root-mean-square error of about 3.0° under element position errors, outperforming conventional MVDR, diagonal loading MVDR, and existing sparse Bayesian learning methods. The results confirm that the approach enhances both estimation accuracy and robustness for small-platform applications.

title: Parameter Fine-tuning Method for Qwen-VL-2B-Instruct Based on Autonomous Driving Multimodal Dataset
authors: Zhixian Chen, Canyue Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the challenges of multimodal question answering in autonomous driving scenarios under complex weather conditions, including limited contextual understanding, insufficient response standardization, and the poor adaptability of general-purpose vision-language models, this paper presents a multi-image question-answering fine-tuning approach based on Qwen-VL-2B-Instruct. The constructed dataset covers sunny, rainy, and foggy conditions and contains approximately 160,000 samples. It is organized in a structured form at consisting of image paths, instruction texts, and structure d answers, enabling environmental information, risk factors, and control parameters to be transformed into learnable question-answering samples. To improve training efficiency, LoRA is adopted for parameter-efficient fine-tuning. The propose d method is evaluated using four metrics, namely exact match, XML match, inclusion rate, and average text similarity. Experimental results on 3,252 test samples show that the fine-tuned model achieves 14.82 percent on exact match, XML match, and inclusion rate, while the average text similarity improves from 27.74 percent to 28.76 percent compared with the baseline model.

title: A Geometric Method to Complete Closed-Form Solutions for UAV-assisted 3D Coverage
authors: Xuan Wen, Peng Wu, Yunfei Gao, Xiaopeng Yuan, Yao Zhu, Yulin Hu
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: This letter investigates a UAV-assisted wireless communication coverage network, where a UAV equipped with a directional antenna is dispatched to provide communication coverage service to two ground nodes. To improve communication quality under a fairness consideration, we aim to minimize the maximum UAV-to-node distance (i.e., a min–max fairness objective) while ensuring both nodes lie within the antenna beamwidth cone through jointly optimizing the 3D position and the antenna orientation of UAV. To tackle the formulated highly complicated non-convex problem, we propose a novel geometric approach which rigorously maps the original 3D position design problem to an equivalent 2D position design problem through leveraging the axis-symmetric structure of the problem and applying a bijection-based transformation. Then, via characterizing the monotonicity of the objective function, the closed form expression for the optimal solution is derived which can be equivalently covered to the optimal UAV 3D position of the original problem by inverse mapping. Afterwards, the corresponding optimal antenna orientation is obtained by analyzing the coverage constraints of the original 3D problem. Finally, simulation results demonstrate that our method provides the complete set of globally optimal closed-form solutions compared with benchmark.

title: High-Precision Hybrid CMOS/MTJ BGR With Improved Temperature Coefficient and Reduced Area
authors: Ravi S. Siddanath, Mohit Gupta, Deepak Joshi, Souvik Kumar Das, Prasanna Kumar Misra, Manish Goswami, Kavindra Kandpal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: This article introduces a hybrid bandgap reference (HBGR) with improved temperature coefficient (TC) and reduced area. Conventional curvature-compensated BGR (CBGR) achieved a 0.647-V reference with a TC of 17.69ppm/°C across –<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$40~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$125~^{\circ }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>C temperature range, consuming <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$38.62~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W with a 0.082-mm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{\mathbf {2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> area. In HBGR, selected resistors are replaced with magnetic tunnel junction (MTJ) devices to simultaneously improve temperature stability and silicon area efficiency. MTJs provide tunable temperature-dependent resistance, enabling additional nonlinear compensation, reducing the simulated TC to approximately 1ppm/°C over the same range. Vertical integration of compact MTJ devices on MOS drain regions achieves a reduced layout area of 0.016mm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{\mathbf {2}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. MTJ-based resistor replacement provides cutting-edge accuracy and area efficiency, as confirmed by comparison with current voltage reference techniques. These findings show that MTJ integration is a viable technique for next-generation and high-order curvature-compensated BGRs in cutting-edge CMOS technologies, making the proposed architecture suitable for area-constrained mixed-signal and precision analog systems.

title: SegPainter: User-Controllable Face Inpainting via Mask-Aware Semantic Segmentation-Guided Mamba
authors: Rongji Ke, Degang Chen, Hengyou Wang, Debby D. Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: With the growing demand for face editing applications, faceinpainting has become an increasingly important subfield within image inpainting research. While many existing methods use semantic segmentation guidance, they apply uniform weighting across all regions of the map. Since the missing areas of the image lack meaningful features, this uniform treatment provides insufficient guidance in missing areas, leading to unrealistic or structurally incoherent results. Moreover, these methods generally lack the capacity to adapt inpainting results to individual user preferences, thereby limiting their effectiveness in personalized face editing. To address these limitations, we propose SegPainter, a Mamba-based architecture for user-controllable face inpainting that enables customized restoration guided by user-defined semantic segmentation maps generated using the Image Segmentation Annotation Tool (ISAT) integrated with Meta's Segment Anything Model (SAM). Specifically, we propose Hard Mask Soft One-Hot Encoding (HMSOE) to adaptively weight regions in the segmentation map based on whether they correspond to known or missing areas of the masked image. This strategy amplifies semantic guidance in missing regions while attenuating it in known regions to avoid over-constraining existing content. We further introduce Semantic-Guided State Space Model (SG SSM) to dynamically modulate the Mamba layer with semantic features, adapting guidance to the masked image. To enhance the quality of inpainting results, we also propose Tri-Scan Inspection (TSI), a scanning mechanism designed to capture both global and local dependencies while preserving spatial continuity and facial structure. Extensive experiments on the CelebAMask-HQ and FFHQ datasets demonstrate that our framework outperforms state-of-the-art methods, producing sharper and more semantically consistent face inpainting results. Codes are available at the link: https://github.com/langka9/segpainter.git.

title: Rethinking Token-wise Feature Caching: Accelerating Diffusion Transformers with Dual Feature Caching
authors: Chang Zou, Shikang Zheng, Evelyn Zhang, Runlin Guo, Haohang Xu, Zhengyi Shi, Conghui He, Xuming Hu, Linfeng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Diffusion Transformers (DiT) have become the dominant methods in image and video generation yet still suffer substantial computational costs. As an effective approach for DiT acceleration, feature caching methods are designed to cache the features of DiT in previous timesteps and reuse them in the next timesteps, allowing us to skip the computation in the next timesteps. Among them, token-wise feature caching has been introduced to perform different caching ratios for different tokens in DiTs, aiming to skip the computation for unimportant tokens while still computing the important ones. In this paper, we propose to carefully check the effectiveness in token-wise feature caching with the following two questions: (1) Is it really necessary to compute the so-called ”important” tokens in each step? (2) Are so-called important tokens really important? Surprisingly, this paper gives some counterintuition answers, demonstrating that consistently computing the selected “important tokens” in all steps is not necessary. The selection of the so-called “important tokens” is often ineffective, and even sometimes shows inferior performance than random selection. Based on these observations, this paper introduces dual feature caching referred to as DuCa, which performs aggressive caching strategy and conservative caching strategy iteratively and selects the tokens for computing randomly. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in DiT, PixArt, FLUX, and OpenSora, demonstrating significant improvements than the previous token-wise feature caching.

title: Reimagining Wireless Connectivity: The FAS-RIS Synergy for 6G Smart Cities
authors: Tuo Wu, Kai-Kit Wong, Jie Tang, Junteng Yao, Baiyang Liu, Kin-Fai Tong, Chan-Byoung Chae, Matthew C. Valenti, Kwai-Man Luk
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Fluid antenna system (FAS) represents the concept of treating antenna as a reconfigurable physical- layer resource to broaden system design and network optimization and inspire next-generation reconfigurable antennas. FAS can unleash new degree of freedom (DoF) via antenna reconfigurations for novel spatial diversity. Reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) on the other hand can reshape wireless propagation environments but often face limitations from double path-loss and minimal signal processing capability when operating independently. This article envisions a transformative FAS-RIS integrated architecture for future smart city networks, uniting the adaptability of FAS with the environmental control of RIS. The proposed framework has five key applications: FAS-enabled base stations (BSs) for large-scale beamforming, FAS-equipped user devices with finest spatial diversity, and three novel RIS paradigms -fluid RIS (FRIS) with reconfigurable elements, FAS-embedded RIS as active relays, and enormous FAS (E-FAS) exploiting surface waves on facades to re-establish line-of-sight (LoS) communication. A two-timescale control mechanism coordinates network-level beamforming with rapid, device-level adaptation. Applications spanning from simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) to integrated sensing and communications (ISAC), with challenges in co-design, channel modeling, and optimization, are discussed. This article concludes with simulation results demonstrating the robustness and effectiveness of the FAS-RIS system.

title: Real-Time Missing Marker Recovery for Optical Surgical Tracking via Dual-Attention Fusion
authors: Tao Xu, Long Shao, Zhao Zheng, Deqiang Xiao, Jingfan Fan, Jian Yang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Optical surgical tool tracking fails when infrared markers are partially occluded and the number of visible markers becomes insufficient for rigid-body pose solving. To mitigate this problem, we study short-horizon marker prediction for binocular optical tracking and formulate it as a unified task covering both pure history-driven prediction and online recovery with partial posterior observations. We propose a dual-attention real-time fusion model that combines a history-driven structural prior with step-wise posterior correction from visible neighbor markers. The architecture consists of a historical encoder, a real-time encoder, a structure-aware module, an interaction-aware module, and a posterior decoder, enabling the model to exploit both temporal dynamics and rigid-body geometry in a causal manner. Experiments on an in-house optical tracking dataset with simulated occlusions show that, under the pure prediction setting without visible future neighbors, the proposed method remains competitive with representative trajectory-forecasting baselines. When one or two future neighbors remain visible, it consistently outperforms representative missing-marker recovery methods across all prediction horizons. Averaged over T1–T5, the proposed method achieves 1.68 mm and 1.38 mm RMSE under N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">vis</inf> = 1 and N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">vis</inf> = 2, respectively, corresponding to 14.1% and 18.8% reductions relative to the strongest recovery baseline, HGNN. These results indicate that historical motion modeling alone is insufficient for accurate long-horizon recovery in this task, whereas explicit fusion of real-time structural observations is critical for maintaining marker continuity and supporting robust downstream tool pose estimation.

title: SADeblur: Speed-Aware Deblurring for Robotic Perception
authors: Sihuang Yu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Motion blur caused by robot motion severely degrades visual perception in mobile meter reading. Existing deblurring methods mainly rely on image content and rarely exploit robot proprioceptive velocity, which is directly related to blur extent. We propose SADeblur, a speed-aware deblurring framework that incorporates robot velocity as a physical prior. Specifically, an Image-Guided Speed Encoder (IGSE) generates FiLM modulation parameters from image features and velocity, while a DirectionalShift (DS) module performs speed-adaptive feature alignment along estimated motion directions. We further build a real-world motion-blur dataset with paired blur-sharp images under six robot speeds. Experiments show that SADeblur consistently improves deblurring quality and downstream meter detection in high-speed robotic inspection.

title: Quantum-Secured Decentralized Autonomous Organizations: A Theoretical Framework
authors: Keshav Gaur, Badiea Abdulkarem Mohammed, Sandeep Saxena, Varun Shukla, Manoj Kumar Misra, Mehul Kumar Das
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAOs) allow for novel collective governance. However, their reliance on conventional forms of cryptography raises fundamental security concerns, as well as paradoxes in their governance. With the emergence of fault-tolerant quantum computers threatening critical IoT-Blockchain ecosystems, which will shatter today's monetary encryption, this study proposes the first holistic, comprehensive, and conceptualization of a Quantum-Secured DAO (Q-DAO). Such entities will have their core functions organically designed around the foundational elements of quantum theory. Q-DAO design conceptualization transcends the mere addition of post-quantum cryptography and addresses the re-invention of the trustlessness paradigm. It will transform current reliance on a computational assumption to a physical guarantee of trustlessness as defined by the immutable and unassailable laws of nature. The designed system conceptualization will revolve around four pillars: The first is quantum-state governance designed tokens exploiting the no-cloning theorem towards Sybil attacks. The second focuses on a secure and private voting stratagem induced by quantum entanglement. The third introduces a hybrid onchain/quantum channel governance system designed to ensure simultaneous transparency and security of communication. Finally, the fourth emphasizes a novel quantum interference for dispute resolution that overcomes the Code is Law rigidity.

title: TS-StackedBoost: An Interpretable Time-Series Stacked Ensemble for Operational Rainfall Forecasting
authors: Aashi Sachdeva, Aashi Gupta, Arun Kumar Choudhary, Meenakshi Choudhary, Vidushi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Rainfall forecasting has become a formidable challenge due to the significant imbalance in meteorological data. A variety of machine learning models, including tree-based ensemble models and neural networks, have been employed in several studies. Numerous methodologies rely on a single model with basic train-test splits for forecasting precipitation events. Consequently, its applicability diminishes the accuracy of operational forecasting in real-world scenarios. This paper presents the Temporal Stacking Boost (TS-StackedBoost) framework, which integrates heterogeneous tree-based models including Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost, via a stacking methodology. This methodology preserves temporal ordering throughout training to enhance outcomes in real-time forecasting scenarios. Results reveal that individual boosting models surpass the linear and kernel-based baselines, whereas stacked models attain high rainfall detection rates across various climates. Model interpretability is enhanced through feature evaluation and SHAP analysis, which illustrate humidity, pressure gradients, cloud cover, and wind patterns. The suggested architecture provides a reliable and resilient approach to rainfall forecasting across diverse climatic conditions.

title: SAFE-DETR: A Scale-Aware Feature Enhanced Detection Transformer for Tiny Pest Detection in Tobacco Storage
authors: Yi He, Xiangming Kong, Tangkun Zhao, Fan Li, Xuan Xiang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the challenges in detecting tobacco storage pests, which are characterized by extremely small targets, complex backgrounds, and dense adhesion of individuals, this paper proposes a Scale-Aware Feature Enhanced Detection Transformer (SAFE-DETR). First, to mitigate the issue of insufficient semantic representation for extremely small targets, a Scale-Aware Feature Fusion (SAFF) module is introduced to enhance the semantic information of high-resolution shallow features. To overcome the limited local receptive field for detecting such small targets, a Context Enhancement Block (CEB) is designed to expand local context modeling capability. Additionally, a Scale Alignment Loss is proposed to explicitly align the feature distributions between shallow and deep layers, thereby optimizing multi-scale feature consistency and significantly improving detection performance for minute targets. Furthermore, a tobacco pest dataset comprising 1,314 images and 16,984 annotated instances is constructed and publicly released, which authentically reflects the complexity of storage environments. Experimental results demonstrate that SAFE-DETR achieves a mean Average Precision (AP) of 31.2% on the self-built dataset, significantly outperforming the baseline model. The proposed method reduces the missed detection rate by 7.4% and the false detection rate by 10.3%, demonstrating promising potential for practical applications.

title: Firefly Algorithm Based Dynamic Modeling for Non-Invasive Treatment Applied to Microwave Hyperthermia
authors: Qingmei Cao, Mengqing Sun
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This paper presents a dynamic modeling and self-optimization framework for a non-invasive microwave hyperthermia system. To capture accurately the nonlinear relationship between surface and underlying tissue temperatures, a hybrid data-driven modeling approach (FA-FFRLS) is proposed. The FA-FFRLS method integrates the Firefly Algorithm (FA) with the forgetting factor recursive least squares (FFRLS) method. By leveraging the global optimization capability of FA and the adaptive estimation property of FFRLS, the proposed approach improves parameter identification and overcomes the limitations of conventional linear techniques. Experimental results show that the FA-FFRLS model achieves superior tracking performance, with the estimated temperature closely following the measured values throughout the entire operating interval. In addition, quantitative analysis shows that FA-FFRLS achieves lower prediction errors and more concentrated residual distributions, effectively suppressing large deviations and improving robustness to noise. These results indicate that the proposed method can accurately characterize the thermal dynamics of the microwave hyperthermia system and support real-time temperature prediction and control. Overall, the FA-FFRLS framework significantly outperforms the STOA-based approach in terms of accuracy, stability, and robustness, highlighting its strong potential for practical biomedical thermal therapy applications.

title: RBC Consensus: An Optimized Consensus Mechanism for High-Performance Cryptocurrency Exchange Model
authors: Akshat Singh Bora, Akriti, Arushi Goel, Devansh Bajaj, Monika Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: This Centralized cryptocurrency exchanges offer very low latency and high throughput transactions but pose a few risks, such as counterparty dependence, lack of transparency, and single points of failure. In comparison, decentralized exchanges enhance security and trust by eliminating a centralized control; they have poor performance since they commonly have inefficient consensus mechanisms, which cause reduced throughput and increased confirmation delays. Byzantine fault tolerant protocols are traditionally faulttolerant protocols with a high level of security, but with a very high communication overhead when the number of nodes increases. Such other methods, like authority-based consensus, are more optimal and have more output, but are less tolerant to error. This paper introduces the proposal of Reliable Byzantine Consensus, a hybridized proposal of consensus aimed to enhance the performance of decentralized cryptocurrency exchanges, at the same time ensuring a high level of security. The forward solution is the authority-based committee choice combined with the optimization of the Byzantine agreement protocol. Reputation-based selection is used to create small validator committees out of a larger pool of authority, cut down the communication overhead and enhance scalability. An execution path that is optimized is also proposed in order to enforce a faster block agreement in normal operating conditions. Simulated analysis has shown that the proposed framework has greater throughput and reduced confirmation latency than the classical Byzantine fault tolerance schemes with significant fault tolerance. The architecture functions as a whole will incorporate transaction submission, order matching, consensus processing, block validation mechanism, ledger updates mechanism, and governance mechanisms to facilitate effective decentralized trading systems. The Reliable Byzantine Consensus framework proposed offers a realistic platform on the basis of high-performance decentralized exchanges and scalable and secure processing of transactions to future blockchain based financial platform interactions.

title: Intelligent Recognition and Computer Modeling of Water Hazards in Coal Mines Integrated with Deep Learning
authors: Yuping Cui, Kangfu Shi, Jing Wu, Yongjun Li, Nan Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: The hidden dangers of water hazards in coal mines are characterized by concealment, complexity and suddenness, and they are an important factor restricting the safe production of mines. Traditional methods for identifying water hazards have problems such as low accuracy, poor real-time performance and insufficient data utilization. This paper proposes an intelligent identification and modeling method for water hazards in coal mines that integrates deep learning. By collecting multi-source data such as boreholes, earthquakes, and electromagnetism through multiple sensors, a convolutional and temporal neural network model is constructed to achieve the identification of risk areas. Then, couple the recognition results with the 3D geological modeling to generate a visual distribution map. In actual mining area applications, this method performs excellently in terms of recognition accuracy and stability, and has a promising engineering application prospect.

title: AI for Real-Time Fraud Detection in Banking and Financial Institutions
authors: Arth Bhardwaj, Bhupendra Kumar, Namita Rajput, Minakshi Tripathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The recent growth in digital banking and realtime payment systems has led to a substantial rise in the vulnerability of financial systems to sophisticated financial fraud. The existing rule-based systems for fraud detection are becoming increasingly less effective due to their low adaptability, high rates of false positives, and inability to function within the constraints of real-time processing. Artificial intelligence (AI) has been identified as a major facilitator of intelligent fraud detection systems using data-driven learning and adaptability. This paper presents an evidence-based analysis of AI-based solutions for real-time fraud detection in banking and financial organizations based on an assessment of recent journal publications on actual implementations. The paper discusses the performance of various AI approaches on typical fraud patterns, trade-offs in performance within operational constraints, and presents practical insights for actual implementation. The paper concentrates on system-level performance, comparative characteristics, and implement ability. The results show that hybrid and multi-stage AI architectures are the most balanced in terms of detection accuracy, latency, scalability, and regulatory requirements. The paper concludes by emphasizing major design and research issues for developing effective and adaptive fraud detection systems in contemporary banking environments.

title: Explicit Periodic and Multi-Scale Spectral Modeling for Time Series Forecasting
authors: Chen Li
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Real-world multivariate time series often consist of overlapping periodic, trend, and transient components whose frequencies, amplitudes, and phases evolve over time. This inherent non-stationarity makes long-horizon forecasting particularly challenging [1]. Models relying on fixed smoothing kernels or static seasonal templates are vulnerable to phase drift and multi-period interference, while frequency-domain approaches that predict spectral magnitudes alone frequently suffer from temporal misalignment during reconstruction due to missing phase information [2], [3]. Consequently, accurately extrapolating recurrent periodic structure while flexibly modeling residual trend and transient dynamics is crucial for reliable long-term forecasting.

title: Thermal Deformation Mechanism Analysis and Intelligent Prediction Model of Thermal Load of Clutch Steel Plate in Wet Dual Clutch Gearbox (DCT)
authors: Zhongyuan Li, Xiaoyan Mi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: When wet dual-clutch gearbox (DCT) is under the working conditions of frequent gear shifting and heavy load starting, due to the combined action of friction heat and viscous shear heat of oil, it is easy to produce uneven temperature field, which induces local thermal stress concentration and leads to warping deformation of the steel plate, which seriously affects the clutch life and transmission reliability. Focusing on the thermal deformation mechanism and thermal load prediction of wet DCT clutch steel plate, this paper constructs a multi-physical field coupling model of steel plate-friction plate-oil, comprehensively considers friction heat generation and viscous shear heat generation of oil, and reveals the strengthening mechanism of oil flow on heat conduction and the contribution of viscous heat to total thermal load. On this basis, a genetic algorithm optimized BP neural network (GA-BPNN) intelligent prediction model is proposed, which takes key operating parameters (speed difference, actuation pressure, sliding time, inlet oil temperature, and cooling oil flow rate) as inputs to quickly predict the maximum temperature and thermal deformation of steel sheets. The experimental results show that the GA-BPNN model has a prediction error controlled within ± 5° C and an mean absolute error (MAE) of 3.82° C, which is significantly better than the traditional BPNN model. It has good generalization ability and real-time performance, and can be used as digital twin tool for TCU thermal management strategy to achieve forward-looking control of thermal failure.

title: Attention-Based Frequency-Spatial Fusion Network for Fingerprint Classification
authors: Xiaolu Cheng
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Accurate fingerprint classification can reduce the search space before large-scale matching and thus improve the efficiency of fingerprint recognition systems. However, models based solely on spatial-domain convolution are limited in their ability to capture the global directional and periodic structures of fingerprint patterns. To address this issue, this study proposes an attention-based fusion network for three-class fingerprint classification, combining a spatial branch to extract local ridge details with a frequency branch to capture global structural information from FFT-transformed spectra. A multi-scale feature extraction module, a sequential cross-domain attention mechanism, and an adaptive gated fusion strategy are introduced to strengthen feature interaction and enhance representation learning. Experiments were conducted on the NIST DB4 fingerprint pattern dataset containing three classes: arch, loop, and whorl. The proposed method achieved superior overall performance, with an accuracy of 94.65% and a precision of 94.79%, outperforming several representative baseline models. Ablation experiments further confirmed that both dual-domain feature learning and the proposed fusion strategy contributed substantially to the performance improvement. These results suggest that integrating spatial and frequency information is an effective and robust approach for fingerprint classification.

title: Application of Reinforcement Learning in Low-Altitude Edge Computing Resource Allocation
authors: Yunyang Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: In order to solve the problem that traditional allocation strategies are difficult to meet the real-time and efficiency due to the strong dynamics of resources and uncertain task requirements in low-altitude edge computing environment, this paper proposes a resource allocation framework driven by deep reinforcement learning. By constructing a multi-dimensional state space that integrates UAV position, edge node load, communication state and task priority, a weighted reward function that considers both response delay and resource utilization is designed, and an action smoothness constraint and priority experience replay mechanism are introduced to improve the stability of the strategy and the adaptability to burst traffic. Furthermore, transfer learning and federated learning are combined to enhance the cross-scenario generalization and privacy protection ability of the algorithm. The experiment is based on a high-fidelity simulation environment with a density of 300 users /km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and a flight speed of 30 m/s. The results show that compared with the traditional heuristic and mainstream reinforcement learning algorithms, the average response delay of the proposed method is reduced by 19.7%, the resource utilization rate is increased by 25%, the task completion rate is increased by 14.3%, and the convergence speed is increased by 37.6%. The success rate of policy transfer reaches 89.4%. The study confirms the superiority and robustness of deep reinforcement learning in resource management of complex low-altitude environment, which provides a feasible technical path and theoretical support for future intelligent low-altitude system.

title: Accuracy and Computational Trade-offs in Metaheuristic-Deep Learning Hybrid Intrusion Detection Systems
authors: Priya Gulati, Udetta Jain, Shweta Jindal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Due to increase in growth and popularity of digital communication and exchange of data, it has become very important to protect and maintain the security of the information. This requires a shift from traditional IDS models to advanced detection systems. The former faces the limitations of high dimensional features, class imbalance and higher computational complexity. Hence, this research has provided a solution of hybrid intrusion detection system using the NSL-KDD dataset. It first starts with implementing Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique to correct the non-uniformity of the dataset by handling the minority class. After it, feature selection techniques are applied, namely Particle Swarm Optimization, Grey Wolf Optimizer, and Genetic Algorithm, in order to compare their results. The deep learning models (CNN and RNN) are then applied on these selected features, instead of traditional machine learning models. Based on the evaluation metrics, the different combinations of optimizer and deep learning models are then compared.

title: Facial Expression Recognition Based on LoRA-Optimized Dynamic Cross-Modal Prompt Fusion in Multimodal Large Models
authors: Xiangyi Yang, Yang He, Yibo Han
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Facial expression recognition plays a pivotal role in human-computer interaction, affective computing, and security systems. Traditional unimodal deep learning methods often fail to capture subtle emotional cues under unconstrained conditions such as illumination changes, occlusions, and pose variations. This paper introduces a LoRA-optimized dynamic cross-modal prompt fusion mechanism within pre-trained CLIP multimodal large models. The framework encodes 48×48 grayscale facial images via Vision Transformer to extract multi-level visual features while generating class-specific text prompts for seven basic emotions processed by the text encoder into semantic embeddings. A dynamic fusion layer leverages visual features as queries to adaptively modulate text prompt weights, with LoRA adapters enabling parameter-efficient fine-tuning. Multi-head cross-attention and feed-forward networks yield joint representations, followed by a classification head. Training integrates cross-entropy loss with multimodal contrastive loss. Experiments on FER2013 demonstrate 78.2% accuracy, surpassing baselines with reduced computational cost. The proposed method further shows strong practical value in mobile banking authentication, where real-time detection of user stress during transactions can trigger adaptive security measures and improve user experience.

title: A Method for Measuring the Thickness of Dielectric Coatings Based on Local Echo Cross-Correlation
authors: Shengkai Yan, Jiajin Li, Ziling Xu, Gaocheng Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: To address the issues of time shift caused by terahertz echo aliasing in multi-layer thin-film coatings and system drift/deformation, this paper proposes a total thickness analysis method based on main peak correction and local waveform cross-correlation. Correction is performed by adaptively locating the main peak and fitting a two-dimensional background surface. After extracting the main peak, the local waveform is cross-correlated with a reference waveform to obtain high-precision relative time delay. A time-delay–thickness conversion relationship is then established using the known thickness of the reference region. Experiments demonstrate that this method achieves sub-micron-level thickness measurement, providing a feasible solution for the non-destructive testing of thin-layer structures.

title: A Hybrid CNN-LLM Framework for Multi-Class Retinal Disease Detection and Context-Aware Medical Reasoning
authors: Abhishek Tripathi, Mohit Mittal, Anoop Maurya, Yashwant Kumar, Addul Rehman
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Retinal disease pathologies such as cataract, diabetic retinopathy, and glaucoma constitute one of the causes of avoidable blindness in the world. Although deep-learning Computer-Aided Diagnosis (CAD) systems have success with image classification, they are frequently considered black boxes, with no ability to give the diagnosis in context or actionable information. To achieve this without high computational cost of monolithic Vision-Language Models (VLMs), this paper proposes a novel hybrid framework that relies on Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) backbone to extract visual features and Large Language Model (LLM) to interpret the semantic features. The suggested architecture uses retinal fundus image to detect the state of the eye as one of the four possible conditions - Normal, Cataract, Diabetic Retinopathy and Glaucoma. On the Eye Diseases Classification Dataset our visual module achieves 86.9 % accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 8 6}$</tex>, and per-class AUCs up to 0.96 (Cataract) and 0.92 (Glaucoma). In addition, the system fills the gap between the quantitative prediction and the qualitative analysis by using the LLM to create contextdependent diagnostic reports, which specify the disease etiology, risk stratification, and mitigation strategies. This multi-modal technique is more likely to increase the interpretability of automated screening devices, providing a scalable and explainable solution to ophthalmic diagnostics in health care settings that have limited resources. This framework is ultimately used to democratize access to expert-level medical advice to underserved groups by harmonizing pixel-level perception with high-level cognitive reasoning.

title: A Dynamic Graph Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Approach for Eco-Safe Mixed Platooning
authors: Zhujin Huang, Zhiyi Ren, Qi Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The coexistence of connected and automated vehicles (CAVs) and human-driven vehicles (HDVs) presents significant challenges for eco-safe platooning due to the unpredictable behavior of HDVs and line-of-sight occlusions. Traditional decentralized control strategies often physically segment platoons upon encountering HDVs, leading to severe communication breakdown and string instability. To address this, we propose a dynamic graph multi-agent reinforcement learning (G-MAPPO) framework. First, a dynamic spatiotemporal graph topology is constructed by logically fusing radar-based sensor edges and V2V communication edges, enabling CAVs to perceive beyond physical HDV occlusions. Second, a graph attention network (GAT) is utilized to extract high-order traffic fluctuation features. Third, a centralized training with decentralized execution (CTDE) architecture is designed with a mixed multi-objective reward function to achieve global string stability and fuel economy. Finally, a one-step kinematic safety supervisor is cascaded to guarantee absolute collision avoidance. Simulation results in SUMO demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms existing baselines, reducing average fuel consumption while effectively dampening traffic oscillations under varying penetration rates.

title: Research on the Integration and Practices of Ideological and Political Education in Foreign Language Courses Empowered by Artificial Intelligence
authors: Tianrong Lu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: With the comprehensive promotion of “curriculum ideology and politics,” integrating Ideological and Political Education (IPE) into foreign language courses has become a critical objective in Chinese higher education. However, achieving a natural and effective fusion remains a challenge. This study explores the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to empower this integration. We propose an Ideological and Political Sentiment Integration (IPSI) algorithm to quantitatively assess and select teaching materials that align with IPE objectives. A 15-week quasiexperimental study was conducted with two university classes <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{N}=100)$</tex> to evaluate the effectiveness of an AI-empowered teaching framework versus a traditional approach. The experimental group utilized an AI platform for personalized content recommendation, sentiment analysis of student discussions, and interactive case studies on Chinese culture. Pre- and post-intervention data on students' IPE awareness, cultural confidence, and critical thinking skills were collected through validated scales and analyzed using SPSS. Results from Independent Samples T-tests, Paired Samples T-tests, and ANCOVA revealed that the experimental group demonstrated statistically significant improvements in all measured dimensions <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{p}<0.001)$</tex> compared to the control group. These findings indicate that AI technology can serve as a powerful tool to enhance the depth and effectiveness of IPE in foreign language teaching, fostering students' national identity and crosscultural competence in a data-driven, personalized, and engaging manner.

title: Deep Learning—Based Framework for Software Log Analysis and Suspicious Activity Prediction (DL-SLAP)
authors: Pramjit Rana, Md Iqbal, Himanshu Tyagi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The Increasing complexity of software systems and their scale has resulted in the creation of a vast amount of diverse and unique log data. This has made it impossible to use manual monitoring or rule-based procedures to quickly detect suspicious activities. This document will describe a system called DL-SLAP, which has been developed to automate the intelligent, proactive analysis of Software Logs for detecting suspicious activities via Deep Learning techniques. DL-SLAP combines four steps, which are Log Ingestion, Pre-processing, Semantic Feature Extraction and Analysis, and the application of Relevant Deep Learning Techniques, as well as using deep-learning-based features to identify both abnormal behaviors and possible malicious intent in the software's behaviors. The architecture of DL-SLAP is hybrid, containing three components. The first is an LSTM AutoEncoder used for the anomaly detection process; the second is a Bi-directional LSTM (Bi-LSTM) to predict the next sequence in the log; the last is a CNN-LSTM Neural Network that allows for the capturing of log pattern information in both spatial-temporal ways. All three components were developed and tested using publicly-available repositories of System Log data, in contrast to current techniques used by most organizations when using machinelearning techniques. The results of the experiments show that DL-SLAP outperformed previous machine learning techniques and state-of-the-art software Log Analysis techniques. In fact, the results of the experiments shows an increase of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 8 \%}$</tex> in detection accuracy compared to the baseline LSTM-based models. An increase of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5. 4 \%}$</tex> in the F1-Score. A reduction of 18.6 % in the number of false-positive alerts produced. A 22%reduction in latency for generating anomaly alerts from DLSLAP compared to the baseline LSTM models, allowing for nearly real-time alert generation. These results indicate that DL-SLAP offers a superior solution for anomaly detection and alert generation when compared to existing systems.

authors: Jitendra Sheetlani, Harsh Pratap Singh, Lokendra Singh Songare, Harshita Vishal Vachhani, Kedar Nath Singh

title: A Method for Doctor's Handwritten Text Recognition by utilizing Deep learning and Ambiguity Handling
authors: Amanullah Khan, Shweta Agrawal, Sanjiv Kumar Jain, Adityavardhan Kumar Jain, Manoj Agrawal
publish: Proceedings of the 7th International Conference on Information Management & Machine Intelligence
