title: Adaptive Unsupervised Anomaly Detection for Low-Quality Multivariate Time-Series Data
authors: Jiayi Liu, Donghua Yang, Jinbao Wang, Hong Gao, Jianzhong Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: How should we perform anomaly detection on multivariate time-series data with missing data, attribute misplacement and concept drift? The majority of existing anomaly detection methods overlook the fact that data are often of low quality. To address this challenge, we propose an adaptive unsupervised anomaly detection method for low-quality multivariate time series data. Our method introduces a self-attention mechanism that integrates masked information and missing length information to enhance the model's capability in handling incomplete data. Furthermore, we design a deep probabilistic adaptive memory network to improve the model's adaptability to attribute misplacement and concept drift. We also discuss the optimal window size for effectively dealing with concept drift. Comparative experiments on multiple real-world datasets demonstrate that our method can effectively detect anomalies in low-quality multivariate time series data. The experimental results further highlight the robustness of our model, proving its ability to maintain high performance in the presence of data quality issues.

title: VigNette: Generation of Vignette Illustrations from Video
authors: Mayu Namai, Koya Narumi, Kwan-Liu Ma, Issei Fujishiro
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: In recent years, various summarization techniques have been proposed to address the increasing size of video data. However, most aim to compress information within the same modality, leading to inherent limitations in compression rates. Thus, to offset these effects, it is necessary to explore conversions to different modalities. In response, this study focuses on vignette illustrations, which are still-image illustrations that refine and visually express the narrative content of entertainment media, such as animated works or games. If video content can be transformed into vignette illustrations, it becomes possible to retain core visual information while achieving a significantly higher compression rate than conventional techniques. To this end, we propose the Video Image Generative Network, Transformed & Expanded (VigNette), a system that generates vignette illustrations from videos, supporting both semiautomated generation that reflects user preferences and automated generation without user input. Our experimental results demonstrate that VigNette can generate vignette illustrations suited to the content of a variety of anime videos. Furthermore, a user study confirmed that the system can visually represent the world perspective of each video. Further, our exploration into extending VigNette to include three-dimensional figure generations suggests such potential applications as the preservation of memorable scenes in a tangible form.

title: CodeAct: Codebook-guided Multi-skill Integration for Dynamic Humanoid Whole-body Control_supp3-3698369.mp4
authors: Ran Song
publish: N/A
abstract: Recent advances in humanoid robotics have showcased the potential of imitation learning (IL) to replicate dexterous whole-body behaviors in unstructured environments. However, conventional IL approaches that integrate multiple skills into a unified policy often suffer from training bias toward dominant or easily learned samples, resulting in a compromised action space that fails to preserve fine-grained dynamics of individual skills. To address this limitation, we propose CodeAct, a novel two-stage IL framework that facilitates multi-skill integration through structured expert code guidance. In the first stage, individual motions are distilled into high-fidelity expert codes, which are used to populate a dynamic codebook. In the second stage, a codebook-guided attention mechanism leverages these structured expert priors to constrain the optimization of the multi-skill policy. Importantly, expert codes serve as structured skill-specific representations, providing fine-grained supervision signals for different skills throughout multi-skill integration, thus helping reduce training bias and enabling seamless skill composition. Extensive experiments in simulation and on a real Unitree G1 humanoid robot demonstrate that CodeAct achieves competitive performance in both single-skill learning and multi-skill integration.

title: From Digital Twins to Autonomous Local Agents: Rethinking AI for Smart Water Networks
authors: Gabriele Restuccia, Domenico Garlisi, Fabrizio Giuliano, Antonino Pagano, Ilenia Tinnirello
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) enabled Water Distribution Networks (WDNs) increasingly rely on Digital Twins (DTs) to support monitoring and decision-making, yet most DTs remain passive dashboards. Deploying Artificial Intelligence (AI) within these critical infrastructures requires efficient, autonomous solutions at the edge, where operational constraints and data sovereignty concerns limit the suitability of proprietary cloud-based services that expose sensitive telemetry beyond internal boundaries. At the same time, Large Language Models (LLMs) impose significant computational and energy demands, making lightweight and locally executable agentic pipelines essential for practical deployment under edge constraints. This work introduces a modular multi-agent framework that provides an on-demand DT service capable of executing complex “what-if” hydraulic analyses directly at the network edge. The system employs an autonomous agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline that enables locally deployed LLMs to interpret user intents, retrieve domain-specific context, generate simulation workflows, and self-correct execution errors without human intervention. This multi-agent design yields robust and trustworthy behavior even under strict resource constraints. Beyond introducing the framework, this work systematically assesses its trustworthiness and feasibility for edge deployment by benchmarking task reliability, hardware requirements, and energy efficiency. Evaluation across a representative benchmark of WDNs shows that the proposed agentic approach improves full-task success rates by up to 30-40% over zero-shot prompting and reduces execution failures by more than 50%. Moreover, 4-bit quantized local models deliver performance competitive with proprietary cloud solutions while lowering peak VRAM usage and energy consumption by over 60%. These results demonstrate the viability of effective, efficient, and trustworthy AI agents operating entirely on edge hardware to securely and autonomously enable advanced IoT infrastructure services.

title: Self-Heterogeneous Knowledge Fusion with Personalized Guidance for Target Fine-grained Classification in Remote Sensing Images
authors: Hao Zhou, Tingjin Luo, Yongming He, Wenxia Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Fine-grained visual classification in remote sensing images is a critical task with broad applications in military reconnaissance, traffic monitoring and ecological investigation. Existing approaches predominantly adopt a “feature fusion + single classifier” paradigm, where multiple semantic features are pooled or concatenated into a unified representation. However, this often leads to insufficient feature utilization and loss of discriminative details, limiting the model’s ability to handle high inter-class similarity and intra-class variance. To address these limitations, we propose SHKF-PG, a novel Self-Heterogeneous Knowledge Fusion with Personalized Guidance framework that adopts a “self-heterogeneous knowledge fusion + multi-expert ensemble” architecture to better leverage multi-level semantic features. Specifically, to focus on extracting discriminative information from unique feature combinations, our self-heterogeneous knowledge fusion network integrates shallow local, middle-layer attention, and deep global features to form distinct heterogeneous representations, each directed to a specialized expert classifier. To prevent collaborative errors among experts, we introduce a personalized guidance mechanism that dynamically suppresses the hardest negative class for each expert, steering the model toward correct optimization. Finally, compared with the existing state-of-the-art methods, the extensive experimental results on three benchmarks FGSC-23, FGSCR-42 and Aircraft-16 demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of our proposed SHKF-PG. Our code will be made publicly available.

title: Research on Ultrasonic Guided Wave Structure Damage Localization Method Using Two-Dimensional Image Fusion and Dual-Channel Heterogeneous Neural Networks
authors: Yong Chang, Haoyuan Liu, Nana Li, Yihang Du, Zongyao Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In order to achieve accurate localization of structural damage, a novel ultrasonic guided wave detection method based on two-dimensional image fusion technology and dual-channel heterogeneous neural network is introduced in this study. Firstly, the method innovatively combined the GAF and MTF techniques to convert one-dimensional guided wave timeseries signals into two-dimensional images with complementary features. In this way, the spatiotemporal texture information and dynamic statistical features of guided wave detection signals are better preserved, providing high information content data samples for deep learning model. Secondly, based on the different image features of GAF and MTF, a dual-channel heterogeneous neural network architecture is constructed to achieve efficient recognition of spatiotemporal and statistical information features of images. Finally, a convolutional block attention module is introduced, which applies adaptive attention weighting to the dual-channel features to achieve deep fusion and enhance the damage characteristics. The results of experiments on aluminum plate damage detection demonstrate that the proposed method enables high-precision localized structural damage detection, providing an effective tool for ultrasonic guided wave detection.

title: Laser HeartBeat: Feasibility of a Contactless Optical Gating Method for Cardiac MRI
authors: Qiannan Gao, Yingen Zhu, Xingxing Zhang, Jiayu Zhang, Haimiao Mo, Wenjin Wang, Zhekang Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Robust cardiac gating is critical for cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR) imaging; however, electrocardiography (ECG) and other contact sensors are often limited by electromagnetic interference and practical setup constraints. Hence, this paper presents Laser HeartBeat (Laser HB), a contactless gating method using defocused laser speckle imaging to track chest-wall micro vibrations. Specifically, Laser HB comprises (i) an MRI compatible optical acquisition module that projects and records defocused speckle patterns without contacting the subject, (ii) an integrated real-time processing and triggering pipeline, which incorporates our proposed Laser HB trigger algorithm to extract a robust cardiac-motion surrogate signal from speckle imaging and generate gating trigger signals in real time. To evaluate its feasibility in realistic occlusion scenarios with electromagnetic interference, we conducted two out-of-bore studies and one in-bore feasibility study. Comparative experiments show that Laser HB achieves 97.6% availability, with beat-wise precision/recall/F1 = 0.946/0.946/0.943. The extracted triggers exhibit an average 22.6 ms delay relative to the ECG R-peak. Moreover, integration of the Laser HB system decreased the phantom MRI signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) by 2.8 compared with the baseline condition. These results indicate that Laser HB is an MRI-compatible, low-latency, fully contactless gating method that can reduce setup burden, improve workflow efficiency, and enhance patient comfort.

title: YOLO-Trash: Advancing Recycling with YOLO-Based Trash Classification for Smart Reverse Vending Machine
authors: Chuan-Yu Chang, Abida Khanum, Po-Yu Su, Sheng-Gui Su, Yu-Hsin Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Manual trash classification is often inaccurate, hazardous to health, time-consuming, and labor-intensive, reducing recycling efficiency. Recent advances in artificial intelligence have revolutionized trash classification, providing more precise, efficient, and reliable solutions for effective waste management. Traditional barcode-based recycling systems face significant limitations, including the need for constant database updates to recognize new or damaged barcodes and the inability to process non-barcode items such as PET and non-PET. To overcome these challenges, a computer vision approach based on the YOLO (You Only Look Once) architecture is used. It is designed to accurately classify trash, making it a practical solution for real-time industrial applications. We improved the YOLOv5 model and introduced it as the YOLO-Trash model, incorporating two innovative strategies that greatly enhance its efficiency and effectiveness. YOLO-Trash, a modified YOLOv5 model, integrates the Ghost network and C3Ghost as the backbone and neck for lightweight deployment and enhanced feature extraction in complex backgrounds. This reduces reliance on commercial barcode databases, expanding the range of recyclables recognized and improving overall recycling efficiency. However, we still need a comprehensive dataset for its unique waste challenges. To address this, we collected a Recycle Trash Dataset (RTD) to represent realistic scenarios for trash classification. A large, balanced, and labeled dataset for automatically predicting and separating trash is currently unavailable publicly. The Recycle Trash Dataset (RTD) comprises 3,233 images across six categories: (Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET) bottle, non-PET bottle, battery, 9V battery, glass bottle, and other objects), which are used for training and robust feature learning. However, for real-world deployment in a reverse vending machine (RVM), these categories are consolidated into two practical classes: PET and non-PET objects, ensuring consistency between system design, evaluation, and deployment. Our system provides several advantages, making it a highly efficient and reliable solution. Unlike barcode-based methods, it achieves 99.6% identification accuracy without requiring barcode scans. Additionally, the proposed model is optimized for GPU-free industrial computing platforms, achieving a CPU-only inference latency of 1.1 ms per image on the RVM system, thereby enabling efficient, cost-effective real-world deployment. Through extensive image annotation and training, our model maintains robustness under practical deployment conditions, ensuring stable recognition performance despite challenges such as changes in bottle orientation and, physical deformation. The real-time reverse vending machine (RVM) has been successfully deployed at Taipei Medical University-Shuang Ho Hospital, demonstrating its effectiveness in practice.

title: RULEDROID: LLM-Augmented Synthesis of Static Security Detection Rules for Android Apps
authors: Zhentao Xie, Mingyang Chen, Yaqi Gao, Shishuai Yang, Wenrui Diao, Xiangyu Liu, Kehuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract:
Android’s vast ecosystem and expansive API surfaces pose a serious challenge to static application security testing (SAST) tools. Mainstream tools such as MobSF, APKHunt, and AUSERA mainly rely on manually crafted rules. Crafting these rules demands considerable effort, yet they still cannot cover every security issue. When Android introduces new APIs, changes its permission model, or revises other security policies, the rules soon fall behind. Without constant maintenance, false positives grow, and true vulnerabilities slip through. Recently released LLM-based detectors are easy to use and potentially support a wide range of vulnerability types, but their findings often lack clear explanations and suffer from high false-positive rates.

In this paper, we present RULEDROID, a new framework that leverages LLMs to automatically generate Semgrep-compatible static detection rules from up-to-date official Android security documentation. RULEDROID tackles the limits of pure LLM detection by combining (i) Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), which grounds model outputs in trusted documents, and (ii) a modular workflow that decomposes rule synthesis into welldefined stages. The resulting rules are then applied with proven static-analysis techniques, ensuring consistent and explainable results. We evaluated RULEDROID on three public benchmark datasets. Based on its large and precise rule set, RULEDROID achieved higher coverage and accuracy than traditional SAST tools, and sharply reduced false positives compared with direct LLM scanning. When applied to real-world apps, RULEDROID discovered multiple new vulnerabilities, resulting in 57 CVE IDs being assigned. These results show that RULEDROID combines the broad vulnerability coverage of LLMs with the precision of static analysis, delivering a fully automated docs-to-rules solution for Android security testing.

title: Distributionally Robust Physical-Layer Security for Satellite Communication via Aerial Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface
authors: Zhaole Wang, Xiao Tang, Naijin Liu, Jinxin Liu, Qinghe Du, Lei Chen, Tingwu Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: Satellite communications are envisioned as a key enabler for ubiquitous coverage in future 6G networks, yet the broadcast nature renders them vulnerable to eavesdropping, especially given the long-distance transmissions and associated high uncertainties. In this paper, we propose the physical layer security enhancement for multi-beam satellite communications with the assistance of an aerial reconfigurable intelligent surface (ARIS). Considering the high dynamics and uncertainties of channels, we characterize the channel distribution with moment-based ambiguity sets. Accordingly, a distributionally robust secrecy rate optimization is formulated through joint design of transmit and reflection beamforming. We then introduce a conditional value-at-risk-based reformulation to convert the probabilistic constraints into deterministic forms. An alternating optimization framework is subsequently employed to iteratively update the transmit and reflective beamforming vectors until convergence. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed distributionally robust scheme significantly enhances secrecy performance, and maintains reliable performance across various channel error distributions.

title: Generalized Face Recognition with Occlusion
authors: Dengwen Zhang, Yuxi Liu, Guibo Luo, Zhenyu Weng
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Face recognition under occlusion remains challenging due to masks, glasses, and other real-world obstructions that partially conceal facial information. Existing approaches typically rely on training with one or more predefined occlusion types, which limits their ability to generalize to unseen scenarios. In this letter, we propose Generalized Face Recognition with Occlusion (GFRO), an occlusion-robust framework that generalizes to diverse occlusion patterns without requiring occlusion-specific training data. GFRO is trained on partial facial views cropped from complete face images using a cross-entropy loss to learn generic representations across different partial views. A dual mixture-of-experts aggregator is then introduced to refine and integrate features from multiple branches, each handling a specific partial-view representation. Optimized with a clean–noisy contrastive loss, the aggregator aligns partial-face features with complete-face features, where each branch contains experts specializing in complementary partial-view information. Extensive experiments on multiple datasets demonstrate that GFRO generalizes effectively to both real and synthetic occlusion scenarios and achieves state-of-the-art performance compared with methods trained on occlusion-specific data under the same occlusion conditions.

title: Single-Port Reflection-Type Amplitude-Phase Modulator With Simultaneously and Independently Tunable Full-Range Amplitude and 360° Phase
authors: Meicheng Liu, Yuefeng Hou, Zhenshuai Fu, Guangcan Qin, Liqi Yang, Jin Wu, Tao Chen, Daodan Deng, Kaixue Ma
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter proposes a design method for the single-port reflection-type amplitude-phase modulator with simultaneously and independently tunable full-range amplitude and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$360^{\boldsymbol {\circ }}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> phase. First, the principle of single-port amplitude-phase tuning is analyzed mathematically. The independent amplitude and phase tuning paths are selected and utilized to reconstruct the impedance distribution on the amplitude-phase plane. Second, based on the reconstruction concept, two topologies of single-port reflection-type amplitude-phase modulators are proposed to control the amplitude path and phase path simultaneously and independently, covering the reconstructed theoretical impedance distribution. The topologies consist of one 3-dB coupler and three tunable devices, exhibiting simplicity and high integration. Then, the enhanced hybrid integrated suspended line (eHISL) technology is employed in this design to reduce the insertion loss and further expand the amplitude tuning range. To verify the design strategy, two proof-of-concept amplitude-phase modulators were fabricated and measured. The first topology achieves a phase shift range of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$383^{\boldsymbol {\circ }}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and an amplitude modulation range of from −1.4 to −33.1 dB at 2.45 GHz. The second topology exhibits a phase shift range of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$381^{\boldsymbol {\circ }}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and an amplitude modulation range of from −2.8 to −19.6 dB at 2.45 GHz.

title: AI-Enabled Communication Assistance for Hearing Impaired Individual's
authors: Ranveer Kumar Sharma, Sudhanshu Tripathi, Anil Saroliya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The present work proposed an edge-AI based communication assistant design-DRISHTI to mitigate critical communication barriers faced by the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (DHH) community particularly in low resource and multilingual domains. The available speech to text solutions are mostly unsuitable for real-world Indian settings due to their dependencies on internet connectivity poor performance in noisy environment and in ability to effectively handle codeswitch speech (e.g. Hinglish). These limitations are address by purposed system Drishti by a integrating Bhashini AI models with a noble multi model fusion frame work which jointly processes Audio and visual speech cues, thus enhancing robustness to aquatic noise. The system have response latency less than 300 ms and supports all 22 officially recognise Indian languages will native code-switching capability. The purpose system operates in full offline mode on standard smart phone. Furthermore a new evaluation metrics namely communication success rate (CSR) has been introduce to access real world usability field experiment source 40 % improvement in CSR over the cloud based baseline and the noisy conditions. The purposed system archives accuracy of 96 % for clean speech and 87% for noisy code-switch speech with a compact model size of approximately 85 MB per language. The present work which is the bridges the gap between national <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A I}$</tex> infrastructure and available assistive technology's, offering a adoptable and scalable solution for approximately 67 million hearing impaired people in India.

title: SafeStruct: An IoT Based Approach for Real-Time Risk Analysis and Structural Collapse Detection
authors: A. Hemlathadhevi, Ashwanth A, Devadharshan L, L. Jabasheela, V. Subedha, V.Anitha Moses
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Structural collapses are sudden, dangerous, and frequent, thus the need for identifying the risk as early as possible, especially for the modern civil engineer. Conventional monitoring systems are either too expensive, too limited, or not fast enough for the task. SafeStruct is an IoT-based system that is intended for the continuous, real-time monitoring of the structural health of the building or the structure, thus providing an estimate of the risk of structural weakness or collapse. The proposed system uses a set of IoT sensors for the detection of the structure's vibration, tilt, stress, and environmental conditions. The data analysis, using smart algorithms, estimates the probability of structural weakness or collapse. The proposed SafeStruct is capable of detecting the weakness of the structure as early as possible, thus providing an alarm for the people inside the building for the purpose of evacuation or for the maintenance crew for the purpose of performing the necessary preventive maintenance work. The proposed SafeStruct has already demonstrated its capabilities for the detection of structural weaknesses under simulated stress conditions as well as under environmental changes. The results show that the proposed SafeStruct is capable of effectively extracting the early warning signal, has minimal false alarms, and has an extremely fast response time.

title: AttENet: An Attention-Oriented Feature Enhancement Network for Automatic Chest X-Ray Report Generation
authors: Zhiqiang Zheng, Yuxin Zhang, Zhi Weng, Xueran Zhang, Enhe Liang, Zhihong Wang, Jun Chai, Jianqiang Sui
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: The automated generation of chest X-ray medical reports holds significant practical importance in aiding radiological diagnosis and alleviating pressure on healthcare resources. However, existing methods often struggle to adequately represent medical image features and suffer from low inference efficiency. These limitations lead to critical defects in generated reports, such as missing lesion information and suboptimal diagnostic accuracy. To address this issue, we propose an Attention-Oriented Feature Enhancement Network (AttENet), aimed at enhancing the precision and detail richness of report generation. AttENet employs an attention mechanism as its core guiding principle. It utilises group convolution strategies to reduce parameter redundancy while enhancing feature transformation diversity, thereby strengthening the capture of fine-grained pathological textures. Simultaneously, by expanding the receptive field, it balances local detail with global context, improving the model's accuracy in locating critical lesion areas.To validate AttENet's performance, we conducted evaluations on two publicly available chest X-ray report datasets (IU-X-Ray and MIMIC-CXR). Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of AttENet, which achieves a CIDEr score of 0.490 on the IU-X-Ray dataset and a BLEU-4 score of 0.119 on the large-scale MIMIC-CXR dataset. Qualitative and quantitative analyses confirm AttENet's effectiveness in bridging the semantic gap between radiological imagery and text generation.

title: Short-Term Load Forecasting under Extreme Weather Based on WMD-MSTCN-BiGRU
authors: Ying Cuan, Yurun Tian
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Extreme cold wave events can cause sudden temperature drops and sharp increases in electricity load, significantly reducing the stability and predictability of the load. To address the problems of difficulty in capturing abrupt nonlinear load features and poor prediction accuracy under extreme cold wave scenarios, this paper proposes a load forecasting method based on WMD-MSTCN-BiGRU. This method employs High-dimensional Ordinary Least Squares Projection (HOLP) to screen key features, and uses Wavelet Multi-scale Decomposition (WMD) to decompose the load sequence into multiple frequency bands to reduce noise interference. Meanwhile, it combines a Multi-scale Temporal Convolutional Network (MSTCN) with a Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU) to construct a temporal modeling framework, enabling multiscale feature extraction and bidirectional temporal dependency modeling, and achieves refined fusion of multi-frequency information through a multiscale prediction head. Simulation results show that the proposed model achieves high accuracy in extreme weather load forecasting tasks, providing certain technical support for intelligent dispatching of power plants under extreme climate conditions.

title: HoneyExfil: A Rule-Based Adaptive Deception Framework for Detecting and Diluting Ransomware Data Exfiltration
authors: K S Ksheeraj, Shabari Shedthi B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: Data exfiltration is the most common stage in modern ransomware in which the data is exfiltrated before encryption. Traditional honeyfile systems detect ransomware only when files are changed or encrypted, ignoring read-only exfiltration operations. This paper provides HoneyExfil, a new proof-of-concept framework with three architectural innovations, which are, Exfiltration-centric honeyfile identification based on read events rather than write events, deterministic file-tonetwork correlation rules that connect honeyfile readings to outward data transfers, and adaptive ghost file injection that dynamically dilutes exfiltrated datasets. Rather than proposing a production-ready solution, HoneyExfil assesses the feasibility of this architectural paradigm using a lightweight proof-of- concept implementation. Simulations of attack scenarios show that read-to-transfer correlations, dynamic decoy insertion, and basic behavioral profiling can all be detected with low system overhead. This work illustrates that an exfiltration-centric, rule- based deception pipeline is both experimentally lightweight and theoretically solid.

title: Intelligent Deep Learning Framework for Automated Aerial Image Classification and Object Detection: A Novel Sikkim Mountain Region Dataset
authors: Priyanka Sahani, Ajay Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: The recent development of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) and drone technology has changed how aerial surveillance and monitoring is used, especially in difficult mountainous regions. The present paper presents a state-of-theart, deep learning model integrating convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and attention mechanisms in the context of effective classification of the aerial image in the Himalayan areas. We introduce a new hybrid architecture through hierarchical vision transformer (Swin Transformer) with the combination of optimal preprocessing algorithm, namely Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT), Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) and Fast Fourier Transform (FFT). The trained framework is tested on our recently created SAIOD (Sikkim Aerial Image Dataset of Object Detection) of 12,500 annotated images of 10 object classes of the Himalayan terrain taken by DJI Mavic Air drone. The accuracy of classification and inference time of the framework on SAIOD dataset are 93.16 percent and 0.0049 seconds per frame, respectively. We also solve important problems in target tracking under start-stop and nonlinear motion using the adaptive motion models and sensor fusion algorithms with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 9. 3 \%}$</tex> success rate in motion discontinuity.

title: Modular Architecture Design of Word Automatic Report Generation System in Enterprise Production Scenarios
authors: Changyu Yang, Xuehong Li, Lihui Zu, Wei Wang, Zhanping Tang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: With the acceleration of enterprise informatization, the efficiency and accuracy of report generation in production data processing have become important factors influencing system operation. The traditional manual watchmaking method has problems such as long time consumption, high error rate and difficult maintenance. In response to this situation, this study proposes a modular architecture of a Word automatic report generation system for industrial production scenarios. The system adopts a hierarchical and decoupled design, modularizing functions such as data interfaces, template management, report generation, and permission security, achieving efficient and stable report generation. In terms of technical implementation, Word's automation and template-driven mechanism are utilized to achieve dynamic data filling and format control. Through experimental comparison and verification, this system outperforms the manual method in terms of generation time, accuracy and stability. It can effectively support large-scale data processing and automated report output and has a good prospect for engineering deployment.

title: FPGA-Based Implementation of DWT and Adaptive Filtering for EMG Signal Processing
authors: Shengxi Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Low-Altitude Airspace and Artificial Intelligence (LAAI)
abstract: Adaptive filtering and Discrete Wavelet Decomposition are widely regarded as two dominant denoising methods for electromyography (EMG). DWT decomposes original signals into high-frequency and low frequency bands, allowing noise components to be suppressed through thresholding before reconstruction to obtain a cleaner signal However, due to its limited frequency resolution, DWT is not suitable for eliminating narrowband noise such as powerline interference. In contrast, adaptive filtering performs well in removing smallrange frequency since its strong correlation between reference signals and the output. The paper combines two methods and constructs a hybrid denoising system based on FPGA to gain more comprehensive denoising. By comparing two different combinations and considering the performance on FPGA, the result demonstrates that the new system effectively suppresses both broadband white noise and narrowband 50 Hz powerline interference. This hybrid approach highlights a promising direction for non-stationary biomedical signal processing and supports the development of the integration of wearable medical devices.

title: Enhanced Mask2Former for Rebar Instance Segmentation in Construction Site Images
authors: Bin Tan, Xi Zhao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Accurate instance segmentation of rebars is a fundamental prerequisite for downstream construction inspection tasks such as rebar counting and spacing analysis. However, rebar images captured in construction sites are characterized by dense layouts, elongated shapes, frequent overlap, and complex background interference, which pose significant challenges to existing instance segmentation methods.To address these issues, this paper proposes an enhanced Mask2Former framework for rebar instance segmentation. Without introducing a new segmentation paradigm, the proposed method focuses on strengthening feature representation and structural modeling within the Mask2Former architecture. Specifically, a ConvSwinMerge module is introduced to enhance multi-scale contextual feature fusion, a BiFormer-based decoder with bi-level routing attention is employed to improve spatial dependency modeling under dense distributions, and a local feature enhancement branch (PENet) is designed to refine boundary perception and elongated structure representation.Extensive comparative experiments demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms mainstream two-stage and one-stage segmentation approaches, particularly at higher IoU thresholds, indicating superior boundary modeling capability. Furthermore, ablation studies verify the independent effectiveness of each proposed module and their collaborative contribution to performance improvement. The proposed approach achieves substantial gains in overall mAP, mAP50, and mAP75 on a public rebar dataset, with notable improvements observed for both straight and hoop rebar categories.These results validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method for high-precision rebar instance segmentation and highlight its practical applicability in complex industrial visual inspection scenarios.

title: Parameter-Adaptive Genetic Algorithm Based Cooperative Optimization Method for Distributed Radar Jamming
authors: Jiahang Luo, Jifei Pan, Zheng Wang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: This paper proposes a cooperative optimization method for distributed jamming against phased array radar sidelobe cancellation (SLC) systems using a parameter-adaptive genetic algorithm. First, a jamming platform spatial deployment optimization model is established, with azimuth angle and distance as optimization variables, maximizing the jamming-to-signal ratio (JSR) at the radar receiver as the objective, while considering practical constraints such as main-lobe avoidance and safety spacing. Second, a temporal cooperative optimization model for jammers is constructed, minimizing the radar output signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) by optimizing each jammer's blink period, duty cycle, initial phase, and jitter amplitude. An improved parameter-adaptive genetic algorithm is adopted, which dynamically adjusts crossover and mutation probabilities to balance global exploration and local exploitation, and incorporates elitism and early stopping to enhance solution efficiency. Simulation results show that the optimized spatial deployment improves the average JSR by 17.71 dB compared to random deployment, effectively disrupting the adaptive cancellation capability of SLC. In terms of temporal cooperation, the optimized blinking strategy significantly reduces the radar output SINR and maintains a stable advantage under different SLC degrees of freedom. Moreover, robustness analyses confirm the method's resilience to practical uncertainties: when the path loss exponent varies from 2 to 4 (simulating multipath effects), the JSR of the optimized spatial configuration remains at least 17.5 dB above the random baseline; under random frequency offsets up to 0.02, the optimized blinking timing still achieves an average SINR 4.7 dB lower than random jamming. These results demonstrate the practical applicability of the proposed approach.

title: Kinematics Analysis and Research on the Tobacco Leaf Picking Manipulator
authors: Jian Guo, Yiming Zhou, Dingzhong Tan, Shibo Sun, Zhuo Yang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Perception and Autonomous Control (IPAC)
abstract: To address labor shortages and low efficiency in manual tobacco leaf picking, mechanization of this process is imperative. Considering the real-time and accuracy requirements of tobacco leaf picking, this paper designs the robotic arm and end-effector of a tobacco leaf picking robot. An improved D-H (Denavit-Hartenberg) parameter model is established for the 6-DOF (Degree of Freedom) robotic arm, with the end-effector fixed to the sixth joint and integrated into modeling. And workspace analysis via the Monte Carlo method verifies that the arm's configuration fully covers the picking area, confirming the rationality of its design. To resolve numerical instability near singular points, an inverse kinematics solution combining the Jacobian inverse matrix and DLS(Damped Least Squares) method is proposed. Experiments show the solution takes less than 0.1 seconds, meeting real-time picking demands. Finally, an experimental platform is built for motion control accuracy tests, which demonstrate that the end-effector position accuracy and single/multi-point repeat positioning accuracy of the arm satisfy tobacco picking requirements <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\leq 5 ~\text{mm})$</tex>. This research lays a foundation for improving non-destructive picking and success rates of intelligent tobacco leaf picking robots.

title: Quantum Enhanced Blockchain Legal Intelligence: a Framework for Probabilistic Evidence Evaluation and Secure Sentencing Optimization
authors: Anand Singh Rajawat, Mohammad Zunnun Khan, Mahmoud Khalifa, Mohd Muqeem, Zubair Ashraf, Mahfooz Alam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: This paper introduces a Quantum and Blockchain Integrated Legal Intelligence Framework (QBLIF) for the probabilistic assessment of evidence and the optimization of sentencing in computational jurisprudence. The proposed Quantum Probabilistic Sentencing Optimization Algorithm (QPSOA) employs quantum amplitude estimation and variational quantum circuits to enhance Bayesian evidence aggregation and decision uncertainty models. Quantum subroutines can compute likelihoods faster than classical Bayesian networks and reduce inference time by up to 65 %. To ensure data integrity, the framework incorporates a permissioned Hyperledger blockchain layer to securely store evidential data, algorithm parameters, and decision outputs as tamper-evident, using cryptographically signed smart contracts. This blockchain implementation ensures nonrepudiation, transparency, and decentralized verification of all steps involved in judicial inference. The system is optimized at the system level to perform multi-objective functions that balance fairness constraints, moral compliance, and consistency in sentencing, leveraging quantum-enhanced probabilistic reasoning. Simulated legal data experiments demonstrate that the system improves accuracy (92.4 %), bias mitigation, and auditability while maintaining a low latency of 2.7 ms per transaction. With the help of quantum computing and clear blockchain records, QBLIF offers a new way for AI to make legal decisions that guarantee fairness, accountability, and ethical handling of digital justice processes

title: Integrating Multi-Model Data with Explainable Machine Learning for Early Multi Disease Detection
authors: Janvi, Drishti Singh, Anand Shah, Aman Kumar Verma, Yogendra Narayan Prajapati
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: This study presents a multi-modal hybrid deep learning system that can be used to identify Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (T2DM) and cardiac complications related to it before they occur. The system improves the predictive weaknesses of other, single-source diagnostic tools by using a combination of three different data streams, namely, Electronic Health Records (EHR), ECG waveforms, and retinal fundus photography. The basic architecture involves a fused CNN-Transformer model, wherein the Convolutional Neural Network does localized spatial pattern extraction in images, where the Transformer searches and learns large-scale global relationship across tabular and sequential data. In order to achieve the maximum performance as well as transparency, the framework involves the application of stringent feature selection methods such as Chi-Square, ANOVA, and Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE). Thorough testing based on the F1-Score and AUC-ROC metrics shows that our hybrid model obtains a better accuracy of 95.3 and a 0.96 AUC-ROC. Such numbers are significantly higher than unimodal models such as standalone Transformers (91.6%) or CNNs (90.8%). In addition, the results of ablation show that skipping one of the retinal inputs or ECG signals leads to a performance drop of 45 percent, which proves the importance of multimodal integration. Last but not least, SHAP-based analysis proves that key clinical markers, namely, BMI, cholesterol, fasting glucose, HbA1c, and ECG intervals, are the major driving force of the model predictions, indicating high feasibility of clinical decision-support systems.

title: High-Precision Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Enhanced Question Answering Systems
authors: Zheng Dai
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Intelligent question answering (QA) systems based on large language models (LLMs) have seen increasing application, especially with the integration of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) techniques. These systems have proven valuable in areas such as intelligent customer service and virtual assistants. However, traditional chunking methods often fail to capture the semantic and structural relationships within documents, resulting in fragmented context. This paper proposes HRAG-QA, a novel high-precision retrieval-augmented generation approach that leverages structured document storage to enhance retrieval and response performance. Simulation-based evaluations demonstrate that HRAG-QA outperforms existing techniques in retrieval recall, mean reciprocal rank, and answer accuracy, highlighting the potential for improved QA system performance and scalability. The implementation code and datasets used in this study are available at https://github.com/Thedaizheng/rag_system.

title: A Plant Pest and Disease Image Recognition Method Based on Data Augmentation and Model Enhancement
authors: Xinyang Zhang
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Automated and high-precision identification of plant pests and diseases is of significant importance for reducing labor costs in agricultural production, improving pest and disease control efficiency, and ensuring crop yields. Existing convolutional neural network (CNN)-based methods for plant pest and disease recognition still face risks of overfitting, particularly under complex backgrounds and limited sample conditions, and the model's generalization ability needs further enhancement. To address these issues, this paper proposes an improved CNN model architecture, combined with data augmentation and regularization strategies, to enhance the model's ability to represent various pest and disease features and improve the stability of the training process. Experimental results based on the Rice Leaf Disease Image Samples dataset have shown that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 94.96%, which is a significant improvement over the baseline model's accuracy of 78.82%, validating the effectiveness and feasibility of this method for plant pest and disease recognition tasks.

title: FEM-YOLO: A Lightweight Feature-Enhanced Detection Framework for Monorail Hoist Defect Inspection in Underground Mining Environments
authors: Chuangchuang Gui, Laigong Guo
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Monorail hoist systems are indispensable transportation infrastructure in underground coal mines, where I-beam rail surfaces are continuously subjected to mechanical wear, corrosive atmospheres, and vibration-induced fatigue. Timely and accurate detection of rail defects is critical for preventing catastrophic failures in confined underground tunnels. However, existing deep learning-based defect detection methods face three key challenges when deployed in underground monorail inspection scenarios: (1) surface defects such as micro-cracks and localized corrosion are inherently small-scale with low contrast against metallic backgrounds, resulting in frequent missed detections; (2) confined tunnel environments introduce severe imaging degradation—including uneven illumination, dust occlusion, and cluttered metallic backgrounds—that substantially elevate false alarm rates and (3) the computational constraints of onboard edge devices demand lightweight models that existing high-accuracy detectors fail to satisfy. To address these challenges, we propose FEM-YOLO, a lightweight detection framework built upon YOLO11n and augmented with a novel Feature Enhancement Module (FEM). Specifically, FEM employs a multi-branch architecture with directional dilated convolutions to expand the receptive field without increasing spatial resolution loss, enabling enhanced sensitivity to both horizontal flange cracks and vertical web cracks on I-beam surfaces. A channel reduction strategy with residual scaling further compresses the module to less than 3% additional parameters over the baseline. Extensive experiments on a rail defect dataset demonstrate that FEM-YOLO achieves 70.5% mAP@50 with only 2.8M parameters, outperforming ten state-of-the-art methods while maintaining real-time inference capability suitable for underground edge deployment.

title: A Knowledge-Enhanced Metaphor Recognition Method Based on Consistency and Inconsistency
authors: Kun Bi, Yuan Li, Tao Zhang, Ke Zhao
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Intelligent Computing and Signal Processing (ICSP)
abstract: Metaphor recognition is an important research topic in natural language processing, aiming to identify whether words in a sentence are used literally or metaphorically. Existing studies have failed to simultaneously consider the consistency between different domains and the inconsistency between descriptions with the same content, yet from a linguistic perspective, both are equally important. To address this issue, this study proposes a knowledge-enhanced metaphor recognition model based on consistency and inconsistency. The model first constructs descriptive word sets, inter-word relation sets, and subject–object descriptive word sets from a conceptual knowledge base. It then utilizes a hierarchical relational concept graph network to extract conceptual knowledge of inter-word relations. By employing a consistency mapping function and simultaneously designing an inconsistency mapping function, the model learns consistent information from inter-word relations and inconsistent information from subject–object relations. Finally, it integrates the knowledge representations to construct target semantic scenes for metaphor recognition. Experimental results show that the proposed model outperforms existing mainstream methods on three datasets and exhibits strong generalization capability.

title: Optimized Pilot Reuse and Beamforming for STAR-RIS—NOMA Using a Bounded Multi-Source Shortest Path Framework
authors: Veeraswamy Ammisetty, Mahesh Babu Ammisetty, Santosh Pavada, Krishna Veni Sahukara, Viswanath Veera Krishna Maddinala, Rajendra Prasad D
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Systems in Engineering, Secured Systems and Cybersecurity (ICISESSC)
abstract: This study presents a novel pilot scheduling and beam selection methodology for STAR-RIS-assisted NOMA systems, employing a reinterpretation of the Bounded Multi-Source Shortest Path (BMSSP) algorithm within the context of wireless channel estimation. The suggested framework treats the STAR-RIS elements, or user nodes, as vertices in a weighted graph. The edges show possible pilot assignments or RIS-tile-user pairings, with costs based on the expected rise in normalized mean square error (NMSE) or interference. The BMSSP logic is used to improve channel estimation coverage from different anchor points (high-SNR users or RIS elements that have already been trained) while keeping the total estimation cost for each group below a certain error threshold. This enables dynamic RIS beam training utilizing the Energy Splitting (ES) protocol and facilitates efficient multi-user pilot reuse, significantly diminishing training overhead and pilot contamination. The simulation results indicate that the suggested BMSSP-based scheduling works better than the usual heuristic grouping and random selection methods in both theoretical and practical ES-NOMA channel models. It results in lower NMSE and more fairness for users with the same amount of work.

title: SecureAFL: Secure Asynchronous Federated Learning
authors: Anjun Gao, Feng Wang, Zhenglin Wan, Yueyang Quan, Zhuqing Liu, Minghong Fang
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Asia Conference on Computer and Communications Security
abstract: None

title: FlexVNE: A LLM-Powered Flexible Framework for Virtual Network Embedding in Cloud-Network Convergence
authors: Fan Pan, Le Tian, Dan Li, Pengshuai Cui, Yu Wang, Xuanyan Song, Menglong Li, Xinglong Pei, Yuxiang Hu, Xiaobo Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: In cloud-network convergence environments, Virtual Network Embedding (VNE) confronts three fundamental challenges: resources heterogeneity, services dynamicity and policies diversity. Traditional exact algorithms, (meta)heuristic methods and learning-based approaches are respectively con strained by computational complexity, limited generalization capability and data dependency, rendering them inadequate for addressing these challenges effectively. Although Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate remarkable capabilities in semantic understanding and reasoning, their direct application to VNE still suffers from difficulties in cross-modal coordination, insufficient solution reliability and the inability to quantify business intents. To address these limitations, we propose FLEXVNE, an LLM-driven flexible VNE framework that incorporates three key innovations: (i) a coordinated feature encoder that fuses network graph structure with natural language descriptions to precisely characterize heterogeneous resources; (ii) a VNE head equipped with dynamic masking and hierarchical pol icy networks, embedding hard constraints directly into the decision process to ensure feasible and reliable embedding solutions; (iii) an intent-driven policy controller that transforms ambiguous natural language requirements into optimizable objective weights, guiding policy adaptation to diverse business preference. We conduct comprehensive evaluations of FLEXVNE on a simulation platform. Experimental results demonstrate that across topologies of varying scales and constraint intensities, FLEXVNE significantly outperforms all baseline schemes across all evaluation metrics. Furthermore, FLEXVNE successfully accommodates various user-defined intents, offering a promising paradigm for building intelligent and adaptive cloud-network resource orchestration systems.

title: Detail++: Training-Free Detail Enhancer for T2I Diffusion Models
authors: Lifeng Chen, Jiner Wang, Zihao Pan, Beier Zhu, Xiaofeng Yang, Chi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Recent advances in text-to-image (T2I) generation have led to impressive visual results. However, these models still face significant challenges when handling complex prompts—particularly those involving multiple subjects with distinct attributes. Inspired by the human drawing process, which first outlines the composition and then incrementally adds details, we propose Detail++, a training-free framework that introduces a novel Progressive Detail Injection (PDI) strategy to address this limitation. Specifically, we decompose a complex prompt into a sequence of simplified sub-prompts, guiding the generation process in stages. This staged generation leverages the inherent layout-controlling capacity of self-attention to first ensure global composition, followed by precise refinement. To achieve accurate binding between attributes and corresponding subjects, we exploit cross-attention mechanisms and further introduce a Centroid Alignment Loss at test time to reduce binding noise and enhance attribute consistency. Extensive experiments on T2I-CompBench and a newly constructed style composition benchmark demonstrate that Detail++ significantly outperforms existing methods, particularly in scenarios involving multiple objects and complex stylistic conditions.

title: Machine Learning Approaches to Received Signal Strength Modelling in a Complex Environment
authors: Vasileios P. Rekkas, Sotirios P. Sotiroudis, Panagiotis Sarigiannidis, Zaharias D. Zaharis, George K. Karagiannidis, Christos. G. Christodoulou, Sotirios K. Goudos
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Electromagnetics, Antennas and Propagation
abstract: The accurate prediction of received signal strength indicators (RSSI) remains a vital element in the planning and optimization phases of the wireless network. Additionally, complex environments like forests pose more difficulties that cannot be addressed by common path loss (PL) empirical models. In this context, measurement data can be used to build a prediction model. In this work, we modeled propagation in a forest environment using measurement data from the literature taken using a wireless sensor network in a 2000 km2 area. Given recent technological progress and the promising potential of artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML) is being considered as an alternative to traditional modeling approaches, which can incorporate complex and nonlinear environmental dependencies into RSSI prediction. Therefore, this study seeks to bridge this gap by exploring various ML models and evaluating their potential to engineer an ML-driven model to predict RSSI in a complex forested environment. Specifically, three ML models, namely extremely randomized trees (ERT), natural gradient boosting (NGBoost), and evolutionary forest (EF), are utilized. Among the examined models, EF technique is acknowledged as a novel ML model applied to a propagation problem for the first time. Two XAI approaches were adopted, namely the Shapley additive exPlanation (SHAP) framework and the accelerated model-agnostic exp(XAI)lanation (AcME) approach, to interpret results from the optimal performing model, notably the evolutionary forest. The proposed ML techniques exhibit excellent accuracy in predicting RSSI values within forest environment.

title: LCL Resonance Analysis and Damping in Single-Loop Grid-Forming Wind Turbines
authors: Meng Chen, Yufei Xi, Frede Blaabjerg, Lin Cheng, Ioannis Lestas
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: A common assumption in both grid-following (GFL) and grid-forming (GFM) control systems is that they are open-loop (OL) stable in the vicinity of high-frequency resonances. Hence, classical loop-shaping approaches are often used for establishing stability margins and designing active damping (AD) strategies. This article shows that single-loop GFM (SL-GFM) control schemes incorporating a widely used class of reactive power (RAP) control, referred to as droop-I control, can lead to OL unstable poles. This finding reveals a novel instability mechanism resulting in a reduced stability margin and robustness at high frequencies. The sensitivity of this phenomenon to both RAP and electrical parameters is analyzed in detail. An AD design that explicitly accounts for the newly identified instability mechanism is proposed. We also provide a comparison between such SL-GFM and well-studied GFL control schemes, highlighting quite different resonance features between them. Validation is performed through experiments.

title: Twining Stems Inspired Soft Actuators with High Aspect Ratio: Design, Characterization, and Application_supp2-3697813.avi
authors: Jingjing Feng
publish: N/A
abstract: Inspired by the helical growth strategy of natural twining stems, this study introduces a pneumatically driven Soft Twining-stem-inspired Actuator (STA) with high aspect ratio (> 20:1) and thin-walled structures. Fabricated via an integrated dip-molding and fiber-reinforced process, the STA achieves 3D spatial curling deformation with only a single pneumatic chamber, eliminating the need for multi-chamber coordination. The establishment of mechanical model identified the key parameters governing its deformation, which was then validated through performance testing and analysis. This revealed that fiber thread density regulation enable up to 67.1 mm radial contraction (33.6% length) and 1021° rotation at 80 kPa. Practical applications demonstrate STA’s versatility across multiple domains. The actuators function effectively as artificial muscles to drive a robotic elbow, and demonstrate a snap-through mechanism which facilitates rapid bidirectional motion switching. Beyond actuation applications, soft universal grippers composed of 6 STAs achieve non-destructive grasping of both common objects and irregularly shaped targets, highlighting the system’s multi-functional versatility. Furthermore, a pipe-crawling robot was constructed utilizing STAs, showcasing the ability to generate effective anisotropic friction for locomotion within confined pipelines. Overall, this study aims to establish STAs as a versatile actuator design, offering insights for simplified and high - performance soft robotics.

title: Helical Guided Wave Mode Decomposition with Local Peak Constraints for Aluminum Cable Sheath Health Monitoring
authors: Bin Zhang, Zhuyun Chen, Jingyan Xia, Junyu Qi, Weiguo Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Corrosion detection of high-voltage cable aluminum sheaths is of paramount importance as power systems continue to evolve. Ultrasonic guided waves technology, renowned for its high sensitivity, robust penetration capabilities, and comprehensive damage condition feedback, has been widely utilized in non-destructive pipeline testing. However, traditional axial and circumferential guided wave detection techniques fall short in addressing the complex structure of cable aluminum sheath. To tackle this challenge, this paper introduces a mode identification and separation method for helical ultrasonic guided wave. Firstly, the mechanism of the propagation of helical ultrasonic guided waves in cable aluminum sheaths is thoroughly investigated, and the feasibility of this approach is validated through simulation. Following this, a full-modal peak-frequency modulation dictionary for helical ultrasonic guided waves is constructed, grounded in the modal characteristics of these waves within the cable aluminum sheath. Lastly, a local peak constrained-based orthogonal matching pursuit method is developed to effectively extract and identify pertinent modes. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can accurately extract the damage modes with an impressive modal identification accuracy of 99.48 ± 0.041%. This performance significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, highlighting its superiority in modal analysis.

title: HyperCap: A hyperspectral land-cover captioning dataset for vision–language models_supp1-3693613.pdf
authors: Antonio J Plaza
publish: N/A
abstract: We introduce HyperCap, the first large-scale hyperspectral captioning dataset designed to enhance model performance and effectiveness in remote sensing applications. Unlike traditional hyperspectral imaging (HSI) benchmarks, HyperCap integrates spectral data with pixelwise textual annotations, enabling deeper semantic understanding. This dataset enhances model performance in tasks like classification and feature extraction, providing a valuable resource for advanced remote sensing applications. HyperCap is constructed from four benchmark datasets and annotated through a hybrid approach combining automated and manual methods to ensure accuracy and consistency. Empirical evaluations using state-of-the-art encoders and diverse fusion techniques demonstrate significant improvements in classification performance. These results underscore the potential of vision–language learning in HSI and position HyperCap as a foundational dataset for future research in the field. The code and dataset are available at <ext-link ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://github.com/arya-domain/HyperCap" xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/arya-domain/HyperCap</ext-link>.

title: Decoupling Method for Simultaneous Online Tj and Bond-Wire Aging Monitoring of SiC MOSFETs
authors: Dan Zheng, Tao Fan, Xuhui Wen, Puqi Ning, Zhijie Qiu, Hongyang Li, Xiaofeng Jiang, Wenyuan Ouyang, Guofeng Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Junction temperature (Tj) monitoring using thermal sensitive electrical parameters (TSEPs) has been widely investigated. However, most TSEPs are susceptible to aging effects, making the decoupling of Tj and aging a critical challenge for the online condition monitoring of power modules. Based on the chip structural characteristics of MOSFETs, this letter proposes a novel TSEP to achieve decoupling of Tj and bond-wire aging. To this end, a dual-sampling and modulation strategy synchronized with the zero-vector is presented to achieve non-intrusive online measurement. The proposed decoupling method features a clear physical interpretation and an analytical model. Experimental results demonstrate that, for Tj monitoring, the proposed method is insensitive to bond-wire aging and achieves higher accuracy over the full operating range. For aging assessment, it enables converter-level online identification of bond-wire aging states.

title: Stable Equivariant Reinforcement Learning for High-Precision Wheel Loader Unloading
authors: Hainan Zhang, Changchun Hua, Jiannan Chen, Xi Luo, Jing Wei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: The autonomous loader-unloading task demands high-precision pose control. However, conventional deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods often face the dual challenges of low sample efficiency and training instability in high-precision control scenarios. To overcome these limitations, this article proposes a novel framework, stable Euclidean-augmented reinforcement learning (SEARL). First, a Euclidean data augmentation (EDA) technique is introduced, applying Euclidean symmetry transformations to state data to generate abundant cost-free, physically consistent new samples, thereby improving the utilization of collected experience and enhancing sample efficiency in high-precision learning. However, this augmentation also introduces a critical challenge of model instability, arising from the sample churn effect in DRL and causing the learning process to oscillate. To address this challenge, SEARL further proposes the value and policy churn chain effect suppression (CHAIN) mechanism. This mechanism employs a regularization term to constrain the update magnitude of the value and policy networks, ensuring stable and efficient absorption of the rich geometric information from EDA, suppressing destructive interference and achieving stability at the model level. Finally, joint simulations in a high-fidelity physics engine and experiments on a real-world loader validate the effectiveness of the SEARL framework. The results demonstrate significant improvements in sample efficiency, final pose accuracy, and training robustness, offering a new and effective path for solving high-precision robotic control problems.

title: Energy Efficiency Optimization for MC-DSSS Satellite-Terrestrial Integrated Network
authors: Yiyang Zhang, Ziyi Yang, Gaofeng Pan, Shuai Wang, Jianping An
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: With the rapid development of global satellite Internet and the growing demand for seamless connectivity, the Satellite–Terrestrial Integrated Network (STIN) has become a key architecture for achieving ubiquitous communication coverage. However, STIN faces critical energy efficiency challenges, including the high peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) that degrades high power amplifier efficiency at the physical layer and frequent satellite handovers that increase network overhead. To address these issues, this paper proposes a mobility-aware model of Multicarrier Direct-Sequence Spread Spectrum (MC-DSSS) STIN. At the physical layer, the Orthogonality-Based Generalized Multicarrier Constant Envelope Multiplexing (CEMIC) technique is adopted, and an energy efficiency maximization problem is formulated under constant-envelope constraints. A joint power allocation algorithm is developed based on the Dinkelbach method and the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) to solve the non-convex problem efficiently. To overcome the limitations of single-layer optimization, a twolayer collaborative framework is further proposed. At the network layer, an improved binary particle swarm optimization (IBPSO-HO) algorithm is employed to optimize satellite handovers. This joint design enables two-layer energy efficiency optimization. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly enhances overall energy efficiency across both layers, providing robust theoretical support for the large-scale green deployment of STIN.

title: Mach-Zehnder Interferometer Based on 3D Printed Microfiber Waveguide in C-shaped Fiber for Temperature and Refractive Index Measurement
authors: Qipeng Huang, Liangyi Lin, Shanmei Zeng, Jingxian Cui, Lin Htein, Hwa-Yaw Tam, Xiaoyong Chen, Chuanxin Teng, Xuehao Hu, Hang Qu
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: We report a compact in-line fiber Mach-Zehnder (M Z) interferometer enabled by a femtosecond-laser two-photon polymerization (TPP) printed microfiber in a C-shaped fiber. The C-shaped segment is fusion-spliced between two standard single mode fibers, providing a mechanically robust platform, while the printed polymer microfiber bridges the two cores and supports multimode propagation to generate intermodal interference. The open cavity of the C-shaped fiber ensures strong evanescent-field interaction with the surrounding medium, resulting in high sensitivity. The device exhibits a high temperature sensitivity of 361 pm/°C and an air refractive-index sensitivity of 18300 nm/RIU. The sensor also demonstrates good stability and sub second response. Compared with conventional tapered fiber M-Z interferometers, the proposed M-Z interferometer represents a promising and scalable solution for high-sensitivity, multi parameter sensing, with potential applications in environmental monitoring, gas sensing, and biochemical detection.

title: EmotionReasoner: Emotion-Explanation-Oriented Reinforcement Learning for Explainable Multimodal Emotion Recognition
authors: Sicheng Zhao, Hongrui Zhang, Daiqing Wu, Duo Huang, Weicheng Hong, Jianyang Sun, Yifeng Chen, Yu Zhou, Guiguang Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition (MER) is a cornerstone task in affective computing, aimed at discerning emotional clues from multimodal content. Recently, the emergence of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) has catalyzed a shift from MER to explainable multimodal emotion recognition (EMER), which promises not only accurate emotion prediction but also explainable reasoning. Despite notable progress achieved by reinforcement learning (RL) based methods, significant challenges persist in EMER. Existing models often produce lengthy, diffuse reasoning chains that obscure key spatiotemporal cues, and prevailing evaluation protocols offer only coarse-grained assessments of explainability. In response, we propose a two-component solution, each targeting a specific challenge. On the one hand, we introduce EmotionReasoner, a RL-based model optimization framework that integrates an explanation-aware reward and an emotion-polarity-oriented loss weighting strategy. The proposed reward jointly evaluates structure compliance, outcome correctness, and explanation fidelity, while the weighting strategy adaptively amplifies emotionally salient and polarity-critical tokens during loss computation. Collectively, the framework facilitates emotion-centric optimization toward more faithful, transparent, and explainable reasoning. On the other hand, we draw inspiration from related literature and augment existing evaluation protocols with three complementary metrics—MEER-E, LACE-E, and UTAI-E—that assess explanation fidelity, clarity, and practical utility, thereby providing a more comprehensive and fine-grained evaluation of EMER systems. Through extensive experiments on nine representative datasets, we demonstrate that the proposed framework outperforms existing ones in MER. Further evaluations also confirm its superiority in EMER, along with qualitative analysis that highlights the effectiveness and rationale behind the proposed evaluation metrics. Together, these contributions establish a unified pathway toward RL-driven EMER systems with comprehensive multimodal evaluation.

title: Unveiling the Power of Multi-modal Template Update in RGBT Tracking
authors: Lei Liu, Chenglong Li, Andong Lu, Yabin Zhu, Shoufei Han, Xinye Cai, Changhe Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Template update is essential for improving the adaptability of tracking algorithms to target appearance variations. While previous methods have leveraged the spatio-temporal complementarity of multi-modal templates for RGBT tracking, a comprehensive analysis of the template update mechanism remains underexplored. In this work, we propose a novel prototype-based framework that decomposes the multi-modal template update process from the perspective of prototype learning into four key components: multi-modal prototype, prototype integration, prototype evaluation, and prototype update algorithm. Our findings highlight that the multi-modal prototype is the most critical factor in enhancing tracking adaptability to appearance variations, leading to more robust target representations. While prototype integration is less crucial when the target representation is already robust, it still contributes to learning a more discriminative representation. Additionally, the accuracy of template updates is strongly influenced by prototype evaluation, which controls the accuracy of the update process. Finally, the prototype update algorithm, which determines when and how template updates occur, is key to maintaining tracking robustness. Building on these insights, we introduce the Multi-modal Prototype RGBT Tracker (MPTrack), which adapts dynamically to appearance variations through prototype learning. MPTrack combines a fixed template from the first frame with both modality-shared and modality-specific templates, forming a robust multi-modal prototype representation. It incorporates a prototype evaluation module that guides updates based on template reliability, and an adaptive update algorithm to manage templates effectively. Additionally, a prototype-guided cross-modal integration module enhances the discriminative power of multi-modal relation modeling. Experimental results on five challenging RGBT tracking benchmarks demonstrate that MPTrack consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods, setting new performance records. The experimental data and source code will be made publicly available at: https://github.com/mmic-lcl/Datasets-and-benchmark-code.

title: Nanocrystalline stator core with small saturation magnetostriction
authors: Z. Tang, C. Freemantle, M. Ganbold, R. Parsons, N. Ito, K. Suzuki
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Fe-based amorphous and nanocrystalline alloys enable substantial core loss reduction in electric motors. However, their typically large saturation magnetostriction (λs) can enhance dynamic losses and cause assembly-induced degradation. In this study, an Fe-Nb-B based nanocrystalline alloy with a small λs of +2.9 ppm was investigated as a stator-core material. Compared with non-oriented Fe–3wt% Si steel with a thickness of 0.2 mm, the nanocrystalline alloy exhibits an order-of-magnitude reduction in core loss over a wide frequency range due to its low coercivity and small saturation magnetostriction. The Fe-Si stator core of a commercially available permanent magnet motor was replaced with a stator made of the Fe-Nb-B based nanocrystalline alloy. The efficiency of the motor is enhanced by 10% by this replacement and the power loss separation carried out for the motor suggests that a reduction of the iron loss is the origin of the efficiency enhancement.

title: Edge-Aware PDE Semantic Fields for Zero-Shot Medical Image Classification
authors: Ayan Sar, Tanupriya Choudhury, Ajith Abraham
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Chest X-rays (CXRs) are difficult to diagnose pediatric tuberculosis (TB) because of limited annotated pediatric data. The existing models, which are trained with adult CXRs, tend to be ineffective when applied to pediatric cases. This letter projects a new Partial Differential Equation (PDE)-based semantic field architecture for zero-shot pediatric TB detection. Here, we combine an image encoder that is self-supervised and trained solely on adult images, textual radiological prompts, and an edge-preserving diffusion-reaction PDE to produce spatially consistent abnormality maps. Trained on the massive VinDr PCXR dataset, and without participation of pediatric labels, this model scores an Area under the Curve (AUC) of 0.856, a significant improvement over the state-of-the-art baseline by 15.9%. It is also very specific to non-TB pediatric pneumonia to a great extent (91.3%). The proposed solution is a highly accurate, interpretative, and label-efficient approach to pediatric TB screening.
